requirements.description: The system has the ability to unify AP, GL, and procurement functionality, eliminating the need for manual interfacing
The system has the ability to display invoice and payment status in real time via dashboard or queue
The system has the ability to allow users to drill down from GL entries to invoice and supporting documentation
The system can drill-down to view scanned invoices
The system has the ability to route invoices for approval and allow approvers to add annotations, comments, or electronic notes directly on the invoice record.
The system has the ability to provide three-way matching (invoice/order/receipt)
The system has the ability to mark "On hold" designation on invoices
The system has the ability to detect and flag duplicate invoices
The system has the ability to accept invoices without associated purchase orders
The system must allow auditable tracking of corrections (e.g., reclassifying mischarged expenses) and generate configurable alerts for discrepancies or unusual activity.
The system has the ability to manage recurring and scheduled payments
The system has the ability to split general invoice amounts into detailed categories
The system has the ability to suspend invoices by vendor, invoice, or line
The system has the ability to make negative invoice line entries (e.g. trade discount)
The system automatically returns invoices to unpaid status if associated checks are voided
The system tracks and reports on received goods/services not yet invoiced, supporting accrual accounting and period-end liability reporting
The system has the ability to provide debit and credit memos for invoices/accounts
The system allows entry of multiple distribution lines for non-PO invoices
The system has the ability to create invoices to be paid out in future periods
The system has the ability to designate invoices to post in a separate period from payments
The system has the ability to adjust or correct posted invoices and payments without requiring full reprocessing, while retaining a complete audit history.

The system has the ability to apply additional costs (e.g., freight, surcharges, handling) from AP invoices to inventory items so that those costs update item valuation and flow into financial and asset reporting.
The system has the ability to store pre-populated GL utilities  templates for specific vendors
The system has the ability to support multiple outbound payment methods including ACH, wire, check, and Virtual Payment Card (VPC)
The system has the ability to support system checks and manual checks
The system has the ability to include P-Card, wire, and ACH payments into a central payment history view
The system has the ability to provide vendor provided reference numbers on payments
The system provides flexible selection criteria for payment processing, including by voucher range and open unpaid invoices
The system has the ability to review invoices and payments prior to check issuance, with the option to pull back, correct, or cancel transactions before final posting and payment execution.
The system has the ability to distribute invoices to multiple accounts/locations
The system has the ability to store invoices in a secure electronic archive with automated retention policies
The system has the ability to provide user-level access to invoice images, comments, and processing history.
The system retains all invoice field data with audit trail of changes.
The system has the ability to manage flexible invoice processing (one-to-many, many-to-one)

The system must validate and reconcile payment details (check, ACH, wire, virtual card, P-card) against original invoices, and standardize field labels across payment methods to ensure reporting consistency
The system supports vendor-specific payment rules, allowing either single checks per invoice or consolidated payments across multiple invoices, with configurable options.
The system has the ability to track document expiration dates for vendor compliance (e.g., W-9, insurance)
The system has the ability to ensure no new vendors are allowed at invoice entry
The system has the ability to prevent inactivation of vendors with unpaid invoices
The system has the ability to set up vendor types and tags for reporting purposes
The system has the ability to manage hierarchical vendor setups with parent accounts and multiple remit-to addresses.
The system has the ability to configure workflows specific to approval designation for non-PO items (approval authority required).
The system has the ability to generate automated reminders and escalate unapproved invoices through workflow
The system has the ability to route invoices for approval automatically based on business rules and PO linkage
The system has the ability to handle out-of-office delegation with proxy approver setup
The system has the ability to support workflow related to varying payment terms
The system has the ability to capture and retain annotations, comments, or processing notes on invoices, tied to user identity and date/time stamps.
The system has the ability to track contract amounts and alert users when nearing thresholds
The system supports 1099 processing workflow, including print, mail, submit to IRS
The system has the ability to generate reports based on vendor classification for regulatory or internal policy needs
The system has the ability to provide invoice aging reports
The system has the ability to provide 1099 reporting
The system has the ability to generate reports identifying vendors with no payments for a specified period and provide options to inactivate them.
The system supports electronic capture of invoices submitted through vendor portals
The system supports electronic capture of invoices submitted through emails with attachments
The system supports electronic capture of invoices submitted through OCR-scanned documents
The system supports electronic capture of invoices submitted through EDI transactions.
The system automatically routes invoices for approval based on configurable business rules, including vendor, amount thresholds, cost center, project, or grant code
The system must flag unmatched or exception invoices, automatically route them to designated resolution queues, and retain a full audit trail of actions taken.
The system generates payment proposals or batches automatically for approved invoices based on due dates, discounts, or cash flow rules
The system should provide full audit trails for all billing transactions, including user actions and timestamped changes
The system shall support automated generation of monthly accruals, with the ability to account for data that becomes available after the billing period ends.
The system should flag incomplete data sets and allow for scheduled reprocessing or adjustment.
The system has the ability to calculate charges based on percentage of reported sales, such as a CRDC (Central Receiving and Distribution Center)
The system must support highly configurable invoice templates that accommodate varying customer-specific backup requirements.
The system should support recurring billing schedules with configurable frequencies (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual)
The system must support configurable CPI escalation rules tied to contract-specific terms, including multiple CPI indices, effective date offsets, caps/floors, and compounding methods.

The system shall support complex billing rules, including CPI, Airline Cost Sharing, FMV, and tiered rent updates, which vary by contract. Billing logic should be configurable without requiring hardcoding or IT intervention.
The system has the ability to configure dollar calculations to pull volume data and convert into dollars
The system has the ability to incorporate utility billing (water, gas, & electricity meters), both from online or manually read meters.

The system should support a tariff or rate catalog that can be maintained by authorized users and applied to marine and terminal services, including calculation of dockage, labor, water, and waste fees.
The system shall support configuration of billing rules that accommodate fixed rate agreements
The system shall support configuration of billing rules that accommodate variable rate agreements based on volume, sales, or usage
The system shall support configuration of billing rules that accommodate combinations of fixed and variable charges
The system shall support configuration of billing rules that accommodate tiered or stepped pricing

The system must support tracking of cumulative amounts billed against the total value of a contract or lease, including fixed and variable billing components, to ensure accurate monitoring of contract fulfillment and financial exposure.
The system has the ability to manage invoice templates with customizable text, PO fields, contact blocks, and generic sections by billing type
The system has the ability to modify invoice templates to support unique customer requirements
The system has the ability to provide invoice delivery preferences including email, SMS, print, or portal

The system must support the ability to associate multiple distinct billing locations or service sites under a single customer account, with flexibility to configure separate invoice recipients, terms, and billing schedules per location.
The system has the ability to automatically generate invoices with attached supporting backup (e.g., utility statements, concession sales reports, detailed billing descriptions)
The system has the ability to generate manual invoices via batch creation and attach billing instructions or notes
The system should provide the ability to generate one-time or ad hoc invoices without requiring full customer account setup
The system should allow reprinting invoices without creating duplicate transactions
The system should support generation of pro forma invoices for review and approval before final issuance

The system must have the ability to record, track, manage, and report on security deposits, including cash deposits and letters of credit (LOCs), associated with leases, contracts, or customer accounts.
The system has the ability to generate configurable alerts to prompt billing staff about upcoming events (e.g., CPI updates, lease expirations), based on contract terms
The system should provide configurable approval workflows for billing adjustments, credit memos, and manual invoices
The system should provide the ability to apply late fees or interest charges automatically based on overdue criteria
The system should provide integration with online payment portals to capture payments sent

The system should integrate with ArcGIS to provide map-based visualization of properties with direct access to tenant, lease, and billing data from mapped facilities for visualization and access to billing-relevant property and lease data
The system has the ability to import external data from sources such as ship registries, vendor invoices, utility meters, and maintenance tickets
The system has the ability to ingest self-reported revenue data (e.g., from PDFs or forms) and automate population of billing records
The system should provide integration with external tax engines or authorities where required (e.g., for aviation taxes)
The system has the ability to integrate with an EAM system to generate billing from work orders or services
The system must support integration with operational and access control systems (e.g., SAFE Badging, Gatekeeper).
The system has the ability to track, report, and audit Passenger Facility Charges (PFCs) by airline monthly per FAA requirements, including separate activity and payment tracking
The system should provide automated generation of FAA-compliant Passenger Facility Charge reports and support external audit processes

The system has the ability to classify, benchmark, and compare lease and customer data for profitability, rate consistency (e.g., rent per square foot), and audit insights at property, location, and customer levels

The system must have the ability to track, store, and report Marine and Airline operational statistics (e.g., vessel calls, passenger counts, cargo tonnage, aircraft operations) that are used as the basis for billing calculations.
The system has the ability to create, manage, and version operating budgets, with configurable workflows for department input, revisions, and approvals.
The system has the ability to accommodate one-time and supplemental budgets, maintaining separate ledgers for each fiscal adjustment cycle
The system has the ability to budget for income, expenses, and liabilities
The system has the ability to perform capital budgeting and integrate with project management tools to track funding, commitments, and expenditures throughout the project lifecycle
The system has the ability to support budget adjustments
The system has the ability to assign budgets and accounts to a department
The system has the ability to assign budgets to a location
The system has the ability to enable budget entry and monitoring at cost center, business unit, and project level
The system has the ability to control budget visibility by role and project
The system has the ability to allow multiple users/departments to budget across business units and object accounts without separate input tools
The system has the ability to allow classification and tagging of budget items for shared prosperity / mission alignment tracking, filtering, and reporting
The system has the ability to tie vendor and contract line items to specific budget accounts to improve reporting and reconciliation to actual transactions in the general ledger
The system has the ability to support multiple funds - General, Construction, Aviation Revenue
The system has the ability to determine the allocated amount for the current period's budget for specific accounts
The system has the ability to provide user-defined budget allocation codes
The system has the ability to provide user-defined budget allocation codes
The system has the ability to do budget allocations across units or divisions
The system has the ability to allocate overhead costs across defined cost pools and organizational units (e.g., departments or divisions), with support for detailed tracking and reporting
The system shall support automated pro rata budget distribution based on user-defined rules and drivers

The system shall enable distribution of total budget amounts proportionally across time periods, cost centers, projects, or other dimensions, according to configured percentages or weighting factors (e.g., headcount, square footage, revenue share)
The system shall allow recalculation of distributed amounts when the total budget changes, and support manual override with audit trail
The system has the ability to support inter-departmental transfers - time entry allocation at burden rate
The system has the ability to provide budget worksheet and planning tools
The system has the ability to generate budgets using historical data and projections
The system has the ability to support manual adjustments and annotations during budget review and forecasting cycles
The system has the ability to enable capture of budget rationale, assumptions, and key decisions as metadata for traceability
The system has the ability to mandate budgets for selected accounts before finalizing a budget
The system has the ability to support submission of budget package with ability to select and approve components (program budgeting)
The system has the ability to create final budget document compliant with OR law
The system has the ability to support pre-populating from prior year
The system has the ability to enable zero-based budgeting
The system has the ability to create what-if and forecast budgets
The system has the ability to support scenario modeling (e.g., target-based budget scenarios, multi-year forecasting)
The system has the ability to automate forecasting of rent revenue and CPI/FMV-based adjustments from lease contracts
The system has the ability to enable mid-year budget revisions and forecasting updates based on actuals and known variances
The system has the ability to provide cash-flow-based budget versions excluding non-cash adjustments (e.g., GASB 87/96 entries)
The system has the ability to support forecasting and tracking based on business volume data and associated to dollars / budgets - budget to actual
The system has the ability to review budget tolerances and overrides
The system has the ability to compare budgets vs actuals and forecasts
The system has the ability to track supplemental budgets and changes across fiscal years using additional ledger periods (e.g., period 13/14).
The system has the ability to automate import and refresh of actuals for budget vs. actual reporting, including pre- and post-audit views.
The system has the ability to support variance analysis workflows, including configurable spot-check thresholds prior to accounting close or review cycles.
The system has the ability to reconcile differences between adopted and forecasted budgets for audit and reporting purposes
The system has the ability to notify users of budget adjustments
The system has the ability to generate system alerts for critical budgeting milestones (e.g., target overruns, allocation gaps, capital caps).
The system has the ability to support Excel-based budget template imports and bulk uploads
The system has the ability to support configurable data validation and audit trail functionality for Excel-based imports
The system shall support the creation of automated budget review packages for department and executive-level evaluation
The system shall support comparisons to performance targets and historical budget data as part of budget review packages
The system shall support collaborative review workflows and version tracking
The system has the ability to track stages of budgets in process of adoption with version history
The system has the ability to create and monitor 5 year capital budget plans with caps
The system shall allow entry of capital spending caps at portfolio, program, or division levels, and provide variance reporting against these caps
The system has the ability to compare actual additions to the capital budget and calculate variances
The system has the ability to compare approved funds requests to capital budget and actual additions
The system has the ability to allow capital spending caps to be entered and compared against actuals at a portfolio and division level
The system shall support assignment of multiple funding sources (e.g., debt, grants, cash) to capital projects, and track spending against each source
The system has the ability to integrate with capital project management tools (Masterworks) to align project data and budget performance
The system has the ability to track and report on multi-year capital projects, including configurable roll-up views (e.g., rolling forecasts over configurable timeframes such as 5 years)
The system shall link capital budget items to asset records, enabling tracking of capitalization, depreciation readiness, and project-to-asset handoff
The system has the ability to provide financial statements with budget linkage
The system has the ability to create Interactive and real-time budgets
The system has the ability to consolidate budgets
The system has the ability to provide centralized access and reporting tools for division managers responsible for multiple business units
The system has the ability to enable real-time reporting and visualization of budget to actuals, variances, and spending trends
The system has the ability to support personnel budgeting - capture roles, along with cost structure items (including all components)
The system has the ability to create and monitor 5 year capital budget plans.
The system shall compare actual capital expenditures to budgeted amounts and calculate variances.
The system shall compare approved capital funding requests to budgeted and actual expenditures to support variance analysis.
The system shall support aggregation of project budgeted costs by project phase, time period, and funding source.
The system shall support visualization of budget roll-ups using Gantt charts or equivalent time-phased views.
The system shall support aggregation of project budgeted labor hours by project phase, time period, and funding source.
The system shall provide interactive visualizations of 5-year budget plans and spending caps, with drill-down capability by fiscal year, grant, project, and funding source.
The system shall track actual and projected spend against budget caps, broken out by category (e.g., design, construction, contingency), and flag variances or overruns for planning and compliance.
The system shall provide predictive analytics to identify at-risk budget categories before thresholds are exceeded and configurable alerts to stakeholders.
The system has the ability to roll over and adjust grant budgets annually
The system has the ability to support grant budgeting, accruals, and forecasting in alignment with capital planning and annual financial reporting such as Schedules 425, 270, and 271.
The system must enable clear mapping between project costs and reimbursable amounts, with ability to attach and verify supporting payment documentation.
The system shall present open invoices with due dates and associated cash requirements in a summarized and detailed view, supporting forecasted cash flow management.
The system has the ability to provide daily visibility into cash balances, inflows/outflows, and transaction history across multiple bank accounts.
The system has the ability to track and differentiate across custodians, trustee accounts, and operating bank accounts (e.g., Wells Fargo, U.S. Bank, Principal).
The system shall provide the capability to identify and display detailed records of outstanding disbursements, including both uncashed checks and unsettled ACH payments.
The system shall enable users to sort and filter open invoices by expected payment date and other criteria, to support payment prioritization and cash forecasting.
The system has the ability to support restricted cash tracking and unspent balance rollovers tied to specific bond issuances.
The system shall automatically generate corresponding cash accounting entries upon the entry or posting of invoices.

The system shall have the ability to integrate and record cash transactions across multiple funds, divisions, business units, and projects, including appropriate due to/due from entries to ensure balanced financial reporting.
The system shall have the ability to support multiple bank accounts and allocate cash receipts and disbursements by fund, division, or project, with appropriate reconciliation and reporting.
The system has the ability to track bond proceeds usage by project and maintain monthly expenditure and drawdown records for reporting and compliance.
The system shall generate a point-in-time static report of bond expenditure balances, enabling accurate calculation of interest owed or accrued as of a specified date.
The system has the ability to generate reports showing spending by funding source and source balances across capital projects and assets.
The system has the ability to facilitate eligibility tracking of project expenses to funding sources (e.g., debt-funded vs. ineligible cash-funded components).

The system has the ability to support the allocation of costs originating from a single payment account or funding source across multiple companies, in accordance with predefined allocation rules that are maintained and traceable within the system.
The system has the ability to allow segregation of interest income related to bond proceeds for compliance with IRS rules.
The system has the ability to allocate interest earnings from invested bond proceeds proportionally across projects and funding series, with IRS-compliant reporting.
The system has the ability to enable configuration of separate investment portfolios per bond series and track amortization, purchases, sales, and interest earned.
The system shall record and report on imported investment transactions as part of the investment module, including amortization, purchases, sales, and interest earned.
The system has the ability to offer optional summary or detailed posting of investment and debt transactions, depending on ERP performance and data storage capacities.

The system shall support manual and automated import of journal entries from external portfolio management tools (currently SymPro, soon to be DebtBook), including validation, reconciliation, and audit trail of imported data.
The system has the ability to integrate with the fixed asset module to carry funding source metadata through to the asset register for audit readiness.
The system must support integration with the DebtBook budgeting platform, as well as other comparable third-party budgeting applications
The system shall provide the ability to generate the Statement of Cash Flows in configurable formats, including the ability to define templates and reporting periods.

The system shall support automated classification of transactions into cash flow categories (operations, financing, investing) based on account group configuration and user-defined mapping rules, with the ability to review and adjust classifications prior to final reporting.
The system has the ability to capture and reconcile journal entries from treasury systems into the general ledger with detail-level or summarized posting options.
The system shall provide automated matching of imported bank transactions to ledger entries to support treasury reconciliations, with exception handling and audit trail reporting.
The system shall maintain an audit log of all edits and overrides to funding source allocations, cash, debt, and investment transactions, including user, timestamp, and before/after values.

The system shall provide configurable alerts and exception notifications when transactions exceed defined thresholds or violate funding source constraints (e.g., overspending of restricted bond proceeds).
The system has the ability to link invoice numbers to G/L journal entries and make these linkages navigable through drill-downs or reporting views.
The system has the ability to support a complex chart of accounts with over 200 business units, enabling P&L reporting and budgeting at the business unit level.
The system has the ability to manage reporting segmentation (e.g., General Fund and PDX Airport) within a single legal entity for both internal management and external financial reporting.
The system has the ability to allow configuration of additional closing periods beyond standard 12-month fiscal calendar, to support “period 13/14” style adjustments.
The system has the ability to enforce dual control over journal entry creation, approval, and posting, with full audit trail visibility and user permissions.

The system has the ability to generate summarized journal entries from high-volume invoice activity (e.g., daily or batch postings), with configurable aggregation rules (e.g., by account, vendor, or business unit).
The system has the ability to create and schedule month-end accrual entries, including support for auto-reversing journal entries.

The system must support configuration options that allow reorganization of financial reporting hierarchies (e.g., departments, business units) without losing historical reporting accuracy or requiring full business unit recreation.

The system must allow tagging of business units or GL accounts with configurable attributes (e.g., 'Federal Eligible', 'Airline Cost Center', 'Port Cost Center') to support eligibility tracking, internal cost models, and grant reporting.
The system must integrate AP and AR with the general ledger, allowing users to drill down from GL entries to related transaction records (e.g., invoices, receipts) and supporting documentation.
The system must support compliance with current and future GASB accounting standards, including flexible configuration and reporting tools that can accommodate new pronouncements.

The system must support compliance with GASB 68, 75, 87, and 96 and provide reporting and configuration options that allow these entries to be tracked separately from operational financials (e.g., separate schedules, adjustments, or layers).
The system has the ability to maintain audit-ready attachments on journal entries, including supporting documents, remittance records, and reviewer sign-offs.
The system has the ability to attach supporting documents and remittance advice to AR entries for audit and traceability, such as scanned checks, remittance advice, and correspondence.
The system must allow users to drill down from financial statements into the underlying transaction details (e.g., journal entries, invoices, receipts) and access supporting documentation.

The system has the ability to distinguish financial reporting views with and without non-cash GASB entries (for example, for airline cost calculations and grant reimbursements) for both internal analysis and external reporting formats.
The system must provide detailed audit trails showing who created, approved, and posted each journal entry, including associated supporting documentation.
The system must allow tracking of project costs and budgets, with hard or soft budgetary controls maintained within the ERP.
The system has the ability to differentiate and manage both operating and capital projects within the GL, allowing flexible reporting structures.
The system has the ability to create and manage work breakdown structures (WBS) for capital and environmental projects, and associate them with GL accounts.

The system should support creation of non-budget-impacting “cost-only” or internal tracking projects to capture time or effort for non-capital activities (e.g., internal meetings, training, planning sessions).
The system should support creation of cost-only or internal effort tracking projects for non-capital activities, without transferring costs from home departments.
The system must allow optional budgeting directly within project records, with support for hard or soft stops when spending thresholds are exceeded.
The system should support automation of substantial completion and project closeout processes, including notifications and transfer of project costs to assets.

The system must support generation of grant-specific compliance reports (e.g., federal or state funding requirements) including tracking of reimbursable vs. non-reimbursable costs, audit schedules, and expenditure categorization by funding source.
The system must support encumbrance accounting to reserve budget for open commitments (e.g., purchase orders), with automated release upon invoice or closeout.
The system must support fund accounting, including tracking of fund balances, restrictions, and interfund transfers in compliance with GASB standards.
The system must support allocation of costs across accounts, business units, or funds using fixed percentages, rules, or drivers.

The system shall automate interdepartmental labor transfer journal entries using employee time data, applying labor rates, burden/additive calculations, and predefined account rules to ensure accurate financial postings.
The system has the ability to perform sequential, rule-based overhead and cost allocations that can be executed in a specific order, with dependencies.
The system must support dependent, sequential allocations with configurable rules, audit trails, and rollback functionality to support complex overhead and airline cost allocation models.
The system must generate both financial (debit/credit) and statistical labor transfer entries, with separate account logic for each.
The system must translate work orders to business units or project-task codes based on integrated lookup logic.
The system has the ability to support complex, multi-step, and formula-based allocations including dynamic rules for overhead and indirect cost recovery.
The system has the ability to automate recurring and year-end allocations, including overhead distribution and residual airline cost-sharing models.
The system must maintain roll-ups of project, capital, and environmental costs to appropriate income statement or balance sheet accounts based on project classification (e.g., capital vs. operating).
The system must support formula-based cost models for airline revenue agreements, including flexible, rule-based calculations driven by actual expense data.

The system shall support simulation and scenario testing of allocation rules (e.g., labor distribution, overhead models, airline cost-sharing) prior to posting, allowing HR/Finance users to assess impacts on budgets, departments, or projects before committing results.
The system has the ability to age receivables by invoice/due date.
The system must allow users to record and view collection notes tied to customer accounts and outstanding receivables.
The system has the ability to generate and automate aging reports, statements, and delinquency/dunning letters based on configurable criteria (e.g., days past due, customer type).
The system must support tracking of customer security deposits (e.g., cash, check, letter of credit), including receipt, refund, and association with customer accounts across departments.
The system has the ability to accept and reconcile payments across multiple channels, including ACH, credit card, wire, check, and lockbox integration
The system supports flexible correction of AR transactions, including the ability to unapply and reapply misposted payments through automated or manual processes.

The system shall provide a secure, customer-facing portal for invoice lookup and payment submission (e.g., ACH, credit card, digital wallet), accessible via desktop and mobile devices, with automated invoice matching.
The system has the ability to capture and record parking charges revenue, including automated integration from point-of-sale (POS) or parking systems into the general ledger.
The system must allow for transfers of assets between companies, funds, or business units while preserving historical depreciation and reporting integrity.
The system should support assigning and tracking depreciation of asset subcomponents (e.g., HVAC units within a terminal) to enable accurate lifecycle tracking and write-offs.
The system has the ability to link capital projects to resulting fixed assets for automatic asset creation, depreciation setup, and audit trail management.
The system should allow assignment of non-standard useful lives to assets based on engineering or operational assessments, to support accurate depreciation and asset planning.

The system has the ability to automate unclaimed or unmatched AR entries with auto-match logic and rules for common payer patterns including configuration of payer match logic (e.g., partial name match, reference number) and exception reporting for manual follow-up.

The system must support automated bank reconciliations using imported bank data (e.g., ACH, wire, lockbox), with configurable matching rules, tolerance thresholds, and exception-based review workflows.
The system must support multi-bank cash reconciliations across 4–6 active accounts, including automated matching and exception reporting for unmatched or out-of-tolerance transactions.
The system has the ability to track and automate transfers of assets between business units, while maintaining accurate depreciation and historical records.
The system has the ability to produce automated annual financial statements and standard reporting packages.

The system must support automated receipt processing across ACH, credit card, wire, and lockbox channels, including the ability to match payments to open invoices using configurable rules (e.g., amount, reference number, customer ID), with exception-handling workflows for unmatched items.
The system must allow manual entry of invoices, cash receipts, and adjustments through secure, auditable workflows.
The system must support configuration, scheduling, and automated posting of recurring journal entries, such as monthly accruals, amortizations, and interdepartmental allocations.
The system must support high-volume processing through batch imports or direct data entry for invoices, receipts, and adjustments.
The system must integrate with the Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) system to synchronize asset master data, work order costs, and asset lifecycle events for financial reporting and capitalization.
The system must integrate with the Human Capital Management (HCM) system to support labor cost allocation by project, grant, or business unit, and ensure accurate payroll-related journal entries.

The system must support both batch and real-time integrations with the Human Capital Management (HCM) system for labor cost allocations, with built-in monitoring, error logging, and alerting when transactions fail or are delayed.
The system must support two-way integration with the ArcGIS platform to reference asset location and spatial attributes in financial, project, and grant reporting.
The system must support integration with DebtBook for automated synchronization of debt service schedules, principal/interest payments, and investment balances.
The system must support integration with MasterWorks to receive budget data, project milestones, and forecasted spend for use in financial and grant reporting.
The system must support integration with the Property Management system to enable lease, license, and real estate billing data to generate AR invoices and revenue recognition.

The system must provide configurable integration monitoring tools for the Property Management system (e.g., lease billing feeds), including reconciliation reports and automated alerts for failed or mismatched transactions.
The system must integrate with Trimble eBuilder to capture construction project costs, funding sources, and milestone status for capitalization and grant reimbursement purposes.
The system shall support automated import of trustee files from sources such as US Bank or DebtBook into the system, eliminating the need for manual data entry.
The system must integrate with timekeeping and payroll systems to retrieve actual hours worked and apply appropriate burden/additive rates for labor cost distribution.
The system must integrate with ground transportation (gatekeeper), ABRM, Uber & Lyft  (internal), and SAFE badging.
The system has the ability to retain historical grant records across fiscal years enabling backward-looking audits and compliance lookbacks.
The system has the ability to maintain a centralized register of all grants including potential, pending, awarded, and closed grants with status, funding amount, and agency details.
The system has the ability to store grant-specific information including NOFO details.
The system has the ability to store grant-specific terms and conditions and contact details
The system has the ability to store grant-specific reporting requirements and compliance obligations.
The system has the ability to associate GL activity and transactional detail with specific grants for financial reporting, audit readiness, and reimbursement calculations.
The system shall support configuration of each grant’s fiscal year, including start and end dates, and enable budget tracking aligned to the grant’s fiscal year rather than the Port fiscal year.
The system has the ability to track key grant milestones such as application deadlines, award notifications, start and end dates, and reporting due dates.

The system has the ability to generate reports showing staff time charges down to the individual time entry level, including both the dollar amount associated with the time entry and any comments entered describing the work performed.
The system shall provide a lifecycle view/dashboard showing status of each grant across pre-award, award, management, reimbursement, compliance, and closeout phases.
The system must support multi-level project hierarchy (e.g., program → sub-projects) and segregation of funding by responsible parties (PRPs).
The system has the ability to support tagging of actual grant revenue receipts including non-operating revenue accruals against budget and claims.
The system has the ability to support reconciliation of actual grant revenue receipts including non-operating revenue accruals against budget and claims.
The system has the ability to distinguish grant cash receipts from actual receivables and allow alignment with accounting treatment across fiscal periods.
The system shall enable generation of reimbursement reports and supporting documentation reflecting the appropriate labor cost rate per funding source.

The system shall support automated identification of expense accruals for grant-eligible components by detecting open POs with incurred but unbilled costs (including partial receipts and services rendered), with configurable rules for auto-reversal once invoices are received and exception alerts for unmatched or overdue accruals.

The system should allow users to flag, review, and report on accruals by funding source, grant ID, and project phase, with workflow routing for review/approval and audit trail of adjustments to ensure compliance with reimbursement timelines.
The system shall tag and classify project expenditures by funding eligibility to enable filtered reports for drawdown and audit purposes, with the ability to modify tags after initial assignment.
The system has the ability to track and manage grant reimbursements status and history.
The system has the ability to track and manage grant reimbursement documents including submission templates, supporting documentation, and agency-specific forms.

The system shall manage asset-level tracking for federally funded projects, including grant program name, percentage funded, and ongoing inventory compliance needs, and shall allow creation of fixed assets that are excluded from depreciation but tracked for grant purposes.
The system has the ability to track labor charges per grant using multiple wage calculations including base rate, burdened rate, and full additive rate depending on funding source requirements.
The system has the ability to associate multiple funding sources such as FAA, ARPA, Connect Oregon, and local funds to individual projects and track drawdown against each.
The system has the ability to track grants that fund only a portion of a larger project and differentiate reimbursable versus non-reimbursable project costs.
The system has the ability to flag and store grant-specific exceptions or ineligibilities such as fringe benefit restrictions for federal grants.
The system has the ability to manage compliance checklists and documentation such as Davis-Bacon wage tracking, DBE requirements, procurement compliance, and inventory records for capital grants.
The system has the ability to track ongoing compliance requirements post-closeout including long-term reporting deadlines and asset retention periods.
The system shall allocate lease costs to grant funding sources when applicable and track compliance with grant restrictions.
The system shall track grant-related encumbrances including committed amounts against each grant funding source.
The system shall display real-time balances of grant funds including budgeted, encumbered, and actual expenditures.
The system has the ability to track encumbrances against the grant fiscal year as well as the Port fiscal year.
The system has the ability to support collaborative editing and workflow routing for grant applications, award documentation, and compliance forms.

The system shall provide a grant opportunity pipeline management feature, including the ability to track potential funding sources, anticipated submission deadlines, probability of award, and assigned owners.
The system shall maintain version history of grant application documents, including ability to identify final submitted versions and retain draft iterations.

The system shall support collaborative scoring and prioritization of potential grant opportunities, including configurable evaluation criteria (e.g., probability of award, funding potential, strategic alignment)
The system shall support creation of pre-award budgets for grant applications, enabling comparison to final awarded budgets.
The system shall support pre-award compliance checklists for each application, including status tracking and sign-off workflows.

The system has the ability to support configurable workflows for different types of grants including FAA formula, competitive discretionary, state, federal passthrough, and multi-agency or multi-round applications.
The system has the ability to configure automated alerts associated to grant tracking dates.
The system has the ability to support internal collaboration via notes, comments, and shared task lists for grant management processes.
The system should include validations and alerts for misclassifications (e.g., charging to wrong project code) and track corrections.
The system has the ability to integrate grant tracking with Masterworks (project and portfolio management) to enable visibility of project-to-grant relationships.
The system has the ability to integrate with payroll or labor time tracking systems to facilitate federal cost recovery and accurate grant cost allocation.
The system has the ability to integrate with eBuilder.

The system has the ability to integrate or export financial data directly into agency-specific reimbursement formats (e.g., FAA Form 425, SF-271, or equivalent state templates) with automated population of required fields, validation of data against agency rules, and audit logging to minimize Excel manipulation and manual reentry.
The system shall provide grant budget status reports showing budgeted, encumbered, and actual expenditures by funding source.
The system has the ability to store and report on supporting documentation for compliance such as labor detail, procurement history, and receipts and attach it to the grant or reimbursement entry.
The system has the ability to accommodate sub-recipient tracking and reporting requirements including award amounts, monitoring history, and pass-through terms.
The system has the ability to track capital grants through to the fixed asset register maintaining grant metadata such as award percentage, fund source, and compliance horizon.
The system has the ability to generate real-time dashboards and reports showing grant balances, drawdown status, cost category detail, and pending reimbursements.
The system has the ability to track and report on in-kind match contributions, grant-specific cost-sharing arrangements, and allowable versus disallowed expenses.
The system has the ability to generate reimbursement request packets with automated population of expenditure data based on configured rules.

The system shall support subrecipient performance monitoring, including configurable compliance checklists (e.g., site visits, progress attestations), automated reminders for reporting deadlines, and documentation storage tied to each subaward record.
The system shall track financial disbursements alongside performance monitoring to ensure ongoing accountability for subawards.
The system shall track disbursements to subrecipients, including payment dates, amounts, match requirements, and cumulative balances against each subaward.
The system shall provide workflow for review and approval of subrecipient payment requests.
The system shall retain supporting documentation for subrecipient disbursements (invoices, certifications, progress reports).
The system has the ability to support decentralized contract administration models by enabling departments to manage deliverables, track milestones, and initiate amendments within a shared system.

The system has the ability to accept initial service or procurement requests via intake portals or forms with configurable manual and automated workflow defined routing to a centralized inbox and/or specific individuals.
The system must provide the ability to designate whether a requisition includes federal funding.
The system must support a general request functionality that is distinct from requisition processing; these should be two separate and independent workflows.
The system has the ability to automatically generate requisitions ( through configurable fields) from requests while enforcing separation of duties and expenditure authority validation.

The system has the ability to track solicitation requests from intake through solicitation development, publication, evaluation, award, and contract fulfillment through the use of custom defined fields.
The system has the ability to process technical requisitions with long item descriptions and complex line structures, including nested components or bundled items.

The system has the ability to enable a shared solicitation workspace for authoring and revising solicitation documents with role-based editing, clause library access, and version control along with the ability to assign owner to a workspace, with the workspace owner being someone within the Contracts & Procurement Team.
The system has the ability to manage multiple documents and package them together in portable document format (e.g. pdf or otherwise).
The system has the ability to maintain a language bank for alternative solicitation terms & conditions that can be imported into templates.
The system has the ability to allow in-system commenting and conversation threads tied to each solicitation draft.
The system has the ability to support centralized and distributed evaluation models with configurable visibility of vendor data.
The system has the ability to configure evaluation matrices with weighted criteria and assign evaluators.
The system has the ability to automatically convert awarded solicitations into purchase orders or contracts.
The system has the ability to allow for custom editing of the contract either internally or after export.
The system has the ability to support electronic evaluation scoring (both draft and final) from Excel or form-based templates connected to an eProcurement platform (currently PlanetBids).
The system has the ability to log, route, and track vendor protests through a structured workflow.
The system has the ability to publish solicitations widely by NIGP commodity code or to a limited group with the ability to produce record of who was notified.
The system has the ability to track access to documents and produce bidders list.
The system has the ability for the vendor to create and maintain their profile that contains contact information and their NIGP commodity codes.
The system has the ability to do line item quotes and bids.
The system has the ability to track amounts bid and tabulate bids.
The system has the ability to make comments and indicate if a bid, quote, or proposal was responsive and accepted or rejected.
The system has the ability to cancel solicitations.
The system has the ability to post notices for comment or information.
The system has the ability to score and evaluate proposal with ability to assign roles and permissions to segment for confidentiality.
The system has the ability to provide a viewable and downloadable summary of all solicitation data.
The system has the ability to configure and publish solicitations using structured templates (e.g., RFPs, RFQs, IFBs).
The system has the ability to schedule and document pre-proposal conferences, including attendance tracking and meeting minutes.
The system has the ability to support electronic bid submission through a vendor portal, including attachments and timestamping.
The system has the ability to log and timestamp all bid openings, tracking receipt and evaluation readiness.
The system has the ability to manage and publish solicitation addenda and vendor Q&A with full visibility.
The system has the ability to log and archive all outbound solicitation-related communications in a centralized record.

The system has the ability to support multiple contract types (e.g., general purpose, IGAs, construction, grant-related), with the ability to apply distinct templates, approval workflows, metadata fields, and compliance rules to each.

The system has the ability to centralize and manage a contract template library, including dynamic templates, exhibits, and usage notes with metadata tagging and legal-approved standard language, including predetermined alternate terms and conditions and usage notes for each.

The system has the ability to associate funding sources (federal, local, grants) with contracts and purchase orders along with dynamically adjusted required clauses or forms based on agency (e.g., FEMA, EPA, FAA).

The system has the ability to manage and version contract templates and exhibits in a centralized, role-accessible repository, reducing single-person dependency and enabling enterprise-wide visibility.
The system has the ability to track and preserve redlines and draft versions of contract documents with version control and audit trails.
The system should allow documentation of rationale for deviations from standard contract language in a structured or comment-based format.

The system has the ability to support in-system negotiation and redlining with internal and external collaborators (e.g., Legal, vendors), including shared editing, version control, and comment tracking.

The system has the ability to support structured review and approval of contract documents by external parties (e.g., vendors, partner agencies), with  secure access, role-based permissions, audit trail logging, and version control.

The system has the ability to support collaborative document review and editing by both internal stakeholders (e.g., Legal, Risk, IT) and external parties (e.g., vendors), using shared workspaces with commenting, version control, and access control.
The system has the ability to log legal sufficiency approvals and track whether legal review has occurred prior to contract execution.
The system has the ability to notify and track requested review actions from person to person with dates and detailed communication/requests.

The system has the ability to assign unique contract numbers, which may include numbering/nomenclature conventions to designate different categories of contracts (e.g. cooperative, construction, personal services, etc.)
The system has the ability to enable multi-step contract approval routing with thresholds and digital signatures including multi-party signatures.

The system has the ability to create parent contracts or price agreements with catalog and punch out functionality with the ability to set ceiling thresholds and issue child orders centrally or decentralized.
The system has the ability to tie Task Orders back to the Parent Contract.
The system has the ability to track basis of award and tie to solicitation number and requisition.
The system has the ability to issue contract from non-competitive basis of awards such as small dollar or exceptions.

The system has the ability to manage contract expiration with differentiated statuses (e.g., final year vs. option years remaining, evergreen) Including easily viewable data surrounding the current expiration date, the Ultimate Completion Date, and COI expiration dates.
The system has the ability to alert users based on contract status and timing.
The system has the ability to manage contract disposition decisions (e.g., renew, re-solicit) based on predefined rules and timelines.
The system has the ability to generate time-in-process metrics, highlighting how long contracts remain in each stage and with which department or user.

The system has the ability to support updates to contract owner assignments, reflecting changes in project or business ownership and clearly indicate the form of payment (e.g. P-card or payment system/check).
The system has the ability to manage amendments, task orders, and change orders within the contract lifecycle and tie them to the base contract record.

The system has the ability to register and manage subcontractors under each contract, including role classification, compliance tagging (e.g., DBE status), invoice-level allocation, and setup of subcontractor-specific payment terms by Port or prime contractors.

The system has the ability to process change orders on existing purchase orders or contracts, including updates to amounts, dates, quantities, scope, or coding, with full audit trails and approval routing.
The system has the ability to create new purchase orders or contracts by cloning prior records, preserving structure, vendor details, and line-item formats while allowing date and funding updates.
The system has the ability to carry forward purchase orders or contract encumbrances across fiscal years, maintaining visibility and compliance for multi-year obligations.

The System is capable of tracking specific characteristics of contract clauses, such as termination for convenience, payment terms, limitation of liability, cyber liability insurance requirements, and soft or hard Small Business Enterprise (SBE) goals.
The system has the ability to decentralize the processing of task orders or purchase orders.
The system has the ability to assign an owner(s) (Port/department owner that has requested and uses the contract).
The system has the ability to maintain vendor and contractor scorecards with performance metrics and store them for historical reference and analysis.
The system has the ability to initiate contract performance workflows.
The system should support a digital contract file by linking executed documents to structured contract records, enabling review of terms and related files from a central contract record.
The system should support a configurable contract dashboard or homepage for tracking contracts, key terms, and obligations.
The system has the ability to automatic creation of contract history records whenever key contract data fields are changed.
The system must support tracking of contract closeout dates and the calculation of disposition eligibility dates.
The system has the ability to manage multiple and single master contracts with multiple task orders and track cumulative spend against defined contract ceilings.
The system has the ability to pool multiple vendors for one RFP and track group spending to identify where one contract might be under utilized.
The system has the ability to maintain a centralized vendor master record accessible across procurement, contract, and financial workflows.
The system has the ability for a vendor to submit and upload W9 and new vendor forms.
The system has the ability to define vendor types, business classifications, and status values (e.g., active, inactive, pending approval).
The system has the ability to search or produce "approved vendors" database for clients or categorized vendors.

The system has the ability to track vendor certifications and designations (e.g., Disadvantage Business Enterprise, Minority/Women Owned Business Enterprise, sustainability) with expiration dates and supporting documentation.
The system has the ability to store multiple points of contact for each vendor, including role, department, and communication preferences.
The system has the ability to detect and prevent creation of duplicate vendor records based on configurable rules (e.g., TIN, name, address).
The system has the ability to support vendor profile change workflows with configurable approval, audit trail, and effective dating.
The system has the ability to associate vendors with contracts, bids, solicitations, purchase orders, and compliance records.
The system has the ability to track vendor performance including cure notices, disputes, and contract-related behavior, to inform future sourcing decisions and potential preclusion from awards.
The system shall enable workflows to provide and incorporate feedback on vendor performance.

The system has the ability easily modify or make vendor changes to Purchase Orders, Task Orders or Contract when vendors are acquired or otherwise change ownership, or TINs in addition for AP to have access to these modifications.

The system has the ability to generate finalized purchase order documents using system templates, populated with PO data and selected supporting attachments (e.g., vendor quotes, scope of work) with such attachments stored within the system.
The system has the ability to adjust PO as to be seen, along with the ability to hide line items within a report.
The system has the ability to assign unique PO numbers.
The system has the ability to assign unique Requisition numbers.
The system has the ability to issue PO from non-competitive basis of awards such as small dollar or exceptions.
The system has the ability to use OCR or file parsing to ingest vendor quotes into requisition line items.
The system has the ability to support dynamic PO templates with routing rules, expiration tracking, and metadata tagging.
The system has the ability to enable multi-step PO approval routing with thresholds and digital signatures including multi-party signatures.
The system has the ability to enable multi-step PO approval routing with out-of-office delegation handling.
The system has the ability to automate delegation of authority for both requisition and PO approval workflows.
The system has the ability to store internal notes or instructions on PO records viewable only by authorized staff.
The system has the ability to include optional vendor-facing notes or instructions on PO records, controlled by staff permissions.
The system must have the ability to classify different purchase order types, and allow different terms and conditions to be attached to each type.
The system must have the ability to cancel, close, or reopen PO in whole or by line.
The system must have the ability to modify or add to PO under a change control and with an audit trail.
The system has the ability to restrict unauthorized purchasing by vendor, item category, or user profile.
The system has the ability to retrieve purchase information using criteria such as PO number, requisition number, department name, or other user-defined fields.
The system has the ability to flag contracts requiring review by specific stakeholders based on a configurable rules matrix (e.g., contract type, dollar amount, federal funding source).
The system supports peer or supervisory review routing based on staff role, level, or experience threshold, with compliance tracking.
The system has the ability to route P-card transactions to reviewers based on department, manager, or custom proxy. All of which can be set permanently or temporarily based on a date range.
The system has the ability to manage a centralized queue of procurement requests and support rules-based or manual assignment to procurement staff based on workload, request type, or business unit.
The system has the ability to modify a requisition after submission and reroute requisitions back to approver or submitter for more information, with notes and tracking.
The system has the ability to enforce completion of prerequisite reviews and approvals prior to enabling electronic signature.
The system has the ability to route contracts and supporting documents for internal review via configurable workflows including legal, risk, IT security, and other required stakeholders.
The system has the ability to trigger real-time alerts when contract or solicitation approvals are stalled beyond defined time thresholds.
The system has the ability to log idle time at each workflow step to support bottleneck analysis and process improvement.
The system supports end-to-end procurement workflows from requisition through solicitation and purchase order with no duplicate data entry or manual rekeying between steps.
The system has the ability to support configurable invoice approval workflows tied to contracts and payment terms.

The system must support visibility and status tracking for both construction change orders (managed in the CMS) and standard contract amendments (within the ERP), providing a unified workflow view with cross-referenced records, milestones, and approval status.

The system has the ability to automatically create contract records from solicitation results or direct awards, with configurable workflows depending on source (e.g., RFP vs vendor-supplied document).
The system integrates procurement planning with capital project schedules and IT planning tools to ensure alignment with organizational priorities.
The system has the ability to trigger workflow for planning rebid or amendment upon reaching spend or term thresholds.
The system has the ability to distribute final awarded contracts or amendments to all stakeholders (vendor, PM, CA, etc.).
The system has the ability to configure and establish a standardized naming convention along with configurable data validation rules.
The system has the ability to provide visibility of invoices such as: payment status, invoice numbers, check numbers, and dates payments were sent out.
The system has the ability to support secure collaboration on contracts that include confidential or environmental content, with appropriate access controls.

The system has the ability to create, maintain, and edit the official contract file, as one file, to include 6 main folders (Planning, Pre-Solicitation, Solicitation, Evaluation, Award, and Contract Administration) with the ability to include multiple subfolders within each area.
The system has the ability to capture and preserve negotiation history, rationale for changes, and approval notes for future audits or contract renewals.
The system has the ability to maintain version control for all contract documents during drafting, review, and external collaboration phases.
The system has the ability to associate documents and files with POs, requisitions, and solicitations using workflow-based triggers.
The system has the ability to globally update provisions that span multiple templates without requiring individual file updates.
The system has the ability to attach electronic documents (word processing, spreadsheet, scanned image files) to requisition and purchase order transactions.
The system has the ability to store files in the system/cloud directly instead of from a file path, limiting the possibility of files no longer being accessible.
The system has the ability to generate real-time compliance reports by contract attributes such as year, vendor type, funding source, and agency, with filtering and export capabilities.
The system has the ability to enforce document classification, retention policies, and scheduled purging by contract or transaction type with configurable security controls.
The system has the ability to centralize documentation of contract disputes, administrative meetings, and performance issues for audit and legal readiness.
The system allows departments to submit planned procurements with estimated value, timeline, and complexity for procurement forecasting.
The system provides procurement staff with a configurable dashboard of forecasted procurements, filtered by type, timeline, and department.

The system has the ability to flag and manage compliance reporting data such as small business participation, Disadvantaged Business Enterprise/Airport Concessions Disadvantaged Business Enterprise program metrics, and prompt payment tracking.
The system provides supervisory dashboards to monitor procurement staff workload, including assignment status, due dates, request type, and stage in the process.
The system has the ability to track process milestones and deadlines across solicitation and contract workflows, and to generate alerts or logs for delays and bottlenecks.

The system has the ability to chart and dashboard standard procurement and contracting KPI's such as spend by department, by contract type, contracts/PO issued by period, count and by type of solicitation completed per period per buyer along with additional configurable  metrics.
The system has the ability to report on P-Cards, including a column  specifically  for "email" and "Approval  queue" for transaction reports

The system has the ability to support partial or complete receiving of goods and services against POs within the EAM system, with updates passed to ERP for invoice matching and financial reconciliation.
The system has the ability to integrate with eProcurement platforms (e.g., PlanetBids) for public posting, Q&A, RSVP tracking, and bid submissions.
The system has the ability to integrate with the ERP system to support seamless processing of expense transactions, accounts payable activity, and invoice reconciliation.
The system has the ability to integrate with MasterWorks to exchange capital project and funding data for project-related procurement activities.
The system has the ability to integrate with electronic signature platforms (e.g., DocuSign) to enable digital contract execution.

The system has the ability to integrate with B2GNow to exchange data for contract goal tracking, Disadvantaged Business Enterprise/Airport Concessions Disadvantaged Business Enterprise compliance, vendor classifications, and reporting. This includes sending awarded contract data and receiving compliance updates for use in procurement planning.
The system has the ability to integrate with US Bank Application  for P-card reconciliation including receipt uploads, GL coding, and approval workflows.
The system has the ability to synchronize contract terms (amounts, dates) across financial systems to avoid data mismatches and reconciliation errors.
The system has the ability to transmit purchase orders and receive invoices via EDI using standard formats (e.g., ANSI X12, cXML) to and from vendor systems.
The system has the ability to integrate with Accounts Payable to manage first invoice setup, enforce payment holds based on approval status, and track full payment lifecycle.

The system has the ability to support integration between the EAM system and procurement workflows, enabling creation of requisitions and purchase orders from asset-related work orders, with status updates and cost tracking synchronized between systems.
The system has the ability to pull additional information from EAM system, such as priority and material required by dates and ability to push req/PO status to EAM for work planning.
The system must support configurable controlled vocabularies, a centralized data dictionary, and toggled enforced naming conventions for assets across all divisions.

The system shall allow assignment of asset classifications to at least Uniformat Level 5 (or CSI) from a pre-populated, relational list linking assets to repair deficiencies, with ability to add custom items.
The system's asset classification must support cost estimating, reporting, and grouping, and include building, site, linear, and other configurable elements.
The system supports asset tracking by key attributes including asset type, custom categories, asset number, business unit, and ownership status (owned, leased, or subleased).

The system shall support user-defined asset status classifications (e.g., trackable, repairable, surplus) applicable to both assets and work orders, enabling status-based filtering and reporting, with creation and editing tied to permissions.
The system shall support unique identification and hierarchical grouping of assets and sub-assets, with display down to the sub-asset level (minimum nine levels), including linear assets.
The system's supported data hierarchy  should facilitate unlimited properties, buildings, and assets.
The system allows linear asset records to be listed, categorized, and displayed in the data hierarchy with other assets.
The system has the ability to assign components as part of tracked linear asset. (e.g. joints, valves, catch basins, etc)

The system shall store and manage asset metadata including location, manufacturer, install date, asset class, expected useful life, condition rating, and operational statistics, with the ability to perform calculations using install date and operational data.
The system shall store the asset installation year and use the estimated useful life to automatically calculate a recommended replacement year.
The system has the ability to track subcomponents of complex assets such as HVAC components or airfield lighting systems.

The system has the ability to store and associate supporting documentation (e.g. GIS data, photos, O&M manuals, warranties, commissioning records, procedures, and safety protocols) with individual assets, accessible via search and hierarchy.
The system supports assignment and linkage of accounting, performance, and maintenance asset identifiers, enabling tracking and reporting across three levels of asset definition.

The system supports configuration of asset hierarchies with parent-child relationships maintainable by users, user-defined attributes at each level, and inheritance logic for classification and cost rollups.
The system supports the ability to create user defined fields.
The system shall allow creation of user-defined drop-down lists.
The system shall provide asset field templates compliant with ASTM E2018-24 Facility Condition Assessment standards, with the ability to configure custom fields where industry standards differ.

The system shall calculate a Facility Condition Index (FCI) and System Condition Index (SCI) per recognized industry standards and display them for each asset, with values such as current replacement cost calculated automatically from cost estimate tables.

The system shall calculate data used in the FCI and SCI, such as the asset current replacement value, automatically from internal data, and allow the calculated FCI and SCI values to be manually adjusted.
The system shall calculate and display the FCI percentage at both the system and building hierarchy levels.
The system supports automated notifications and approval workflows for asset lifecycle changes, including placement into service, retirement, and record creation or edits.
The system supports exception-based workflows, allowing users to review and act on asset records that fail validation or business rules.

The system shall support structured work order execution, including approvals, reassignment, completion verification, and electronic signature with timestamping, in compliance with Part 139 and SMS requirements.
The system has the ability to support outage coordination with flags, required notifications, and cross-department scheduling.
The system supports users to conduct multiple inspections at multiple points on an asset or infrastructure.
The system has the ability to support interim asset handover processes in phased project rollouts or partial construction completions.
The system shall provide the ability to perform mass edits and manage multiple records simultaneously.

The system must support ArcGIS enterprise/Online Web Maps, Feature Services, and Dashboards for location-aware analytics using data from EAM (assets/work orders), BIM (as-built extracts), IoT/telemetry, and external sources; includes joins/relations to non-spatial tables and scheduled refresh.
The system supports ArcGIS identity, allowing users to leverage their existing ArcGIS login credentials for EAM access.

Provide first-class ArcGIS Feature Service integration (hosted or federated), including attachments, related tables, editor tracking, versioning/branching, and optional support for OGC API – Features/WMTS for open interop.
The system must maintain compatibility with current and N-1 ArcGIS Enterprise; publish a version policy; provide a pre-prod validation window before Port upgrades.

The system shall support working with 3D GIS data (Z values), allowing users to view work activity data against z-enabled GIS features in ArcGIS applications such as ArcGIS Indoors, ArcGIS Scene Viewer, and ArcGIS Pro.

The system shall provide GIS-centric applications and workflows that incorporate GIS data and functionality throughout processes such as asset management, work orders, inspections, and remote monitoring.
The system must support Esri GDB, GeoJSON, FileGDB, FlatGeobuf, CSV, and legacy Shapefile for import/export; preserve attachments/relations where supported; expose schema export for automation.

The system must leverage ArcGIS Utility Network, Network Analyst, GeoEvent/Stream layers, and Indoors in asset workflows (condition/risk layers, routes, alerts, indoor positioning), configurable without vendor custom code.

The system has the ability from any GIS view, allow users and mobile crews to create, assign, and update work orders tied to the selected AssetID or linear segment, honoring security/role gating and offline sync.

The system has the ability to align assets to LocationID and infrastructure layers (parcels, buildings, floors, rooms, utility networks, linear routes with from/to measures). Store both AssetID and LocationID on records and enforce referential integrity.
The system supports  offline/poor-connectivity field editing with preplanned map areas, background sync, conflict resolution, high-accuracy GNSS/RTK, barcode/QR capture, and MDM app distribution.

The system has the ability to provide delta-based sync (scheduled and webhook/event-driven) between EAM assets and ArcGIS layers; handle schema evolution, change detection, and error reconciliation reports.

The system has the ability to visualize condition, criticality, work order backlog, risk index, and outages as thematic layers; support near real-time joins to EAM tables and time-enabled playback for incident review.
The system allows for map users to automatically sign into EAM solution, and vice-versa, EAM to map. f.k.a.-single sign-on.
The system has a map interface that allows users create, read, update, and delete the GIS features in a geodatabase using REST API.

The system shall provide a routing map tool uses a geospatial routing service (e.g. Routing ArcGIS) for work orders.  The routing tool should allow users to add locations to the map and then navigate to those locations with turn-by-turn directions, and the path highlighted on the map.
The system has the ability to publish time-enabled project boundaries, planned outages, and asset replacement schedules; analyze spatial/temporal dependencies to deconflict work and tenant impacts.

The system has the ability to provide geofences and zone entry/exit alerts with privacy controls (aggregation/anonymization), audit logs, and quiet hours, feeding EAM, Safety, or SOC channels as configured.

The system has the ability to reference authoritative cadastral and permit layers; enforce constraints in work routing/approvals; retain legal map-of-record links and retention metadata on the work order.
The system has the ability to support streaming layers for high-velocity feeds (e.g., IoT, RTLS) and P95 map load < 4s for standard operational web maps; document service SLOs and scaling approach.

The system has the ability provide spatial overlays and event hooks to integrate with airfield operations systems (e.g., lighting, inspections) using standard APIs/events, without hard vendor lock-in.
The system shall seamlessly integrate with Esri ArcGIS, leveraging Esri technology for its GIS-centric cloud and mobile applications.
The system has persist from/to measures, stationing, and Utility Network associations for linear assets; when geometry changes, update measures and sync EAM LocationRef with audit.
The system has the ability to store building/floor/space refs (e.g., ArcGIS Indoors) and render floor-aware maps; link each EAM AssetID to its floor/space.
