The system has the ability on GIS geometry change (e.g., valve relocation) run approval workflow, update LocationID/linear measures, and propagate to EAM with lineage.

The system shall ingest asset data from external sources such as capital project systems, spreadsheets, or procurement platforms into the asset registry, and populate or update associated asset record fields, including user-defined fields.

The system shall support BIM visualization and asset candidate extraction from IFC 4.x (incl. 4.3) and COBie; publish generalized geometry (rooms/levels/major systems) to GIS and maintainable assets to EAM with lineage.

The system integrates with Autodesk authoring tools (e.g., Revit, Civil 3D) via standards-based exports (IFC/COBie, schedules) and APIs; support phased/partial handover and back-write of EAM AssetIDs to turnover packages.
The system integrates with the ERP to support asset-to-project cost mapping, capital versus operating expense classification, and depreciation category alignment.
The system has the ability to integrate labor time tracking from maintenance work orders directly with the HCM system to support compensation and cost allocation.
The system integrates with the HCM system to share technician schedules, availability, and shift assignments, enabling coordinated maintenance planning and coverage visibility.
The system integrates with the LMS to track technician certifications, training completion, and compliance requirements, including gating work assignments based on qualification status.
The system has the ability to ingest real-time telemetry data from sensors (e.g., runtime hours, vibration, temperature) to inform predictive maintenance and utilization reporting.
The system has the ability to integrate with GIS platforms to spatially visualize assets, maintenance work orders, and service coverage areas through interactive maps with GIS locators.

Enable controlled bi-directional updates: GIS edits update location/geometry; EAM edits update asset attributes/status with enforced authoritative boundaries, conflict rules, approvals, and full audit trail.
The system supports integration with image capture or media storage systems (e.g., drone footage, field inspection photos) for asset condition documentation.
The system shall integrate with the hazard reporting system in compliance with SMS requirements.
The system shall integrate with MyEkos for fleet and transportation management and tracking.

The system supports validation rules and workflow checkpoints to confirm required asset data fields are complete before an asset record is finalized, with the ability to bypass based on role-permissions.

The system supports creation and tracking of planned or future asset records based on budgeted projects or forecast data, including fields for estimated cost, acquisition source, and in-service timeline.

The system shall integrate with the Wildlife Information System and Cannon Control system to centralize wildlife hazard tracking, mitigation activities, and compliance reporting.

The system shall support ingestion and aggregation of energy usage and emissions data from diverse sources (e.g., utility feeds, fuel purchases, charging stations) to enable greenhouse gas reporting and climate goal tracking.
The system has the ability to link related cases to each other for reference or precedent.
The system has the ability to reopen closed case with full audit tracking (e.g. access, changes, etc.)
The system has the ability to modify, correct, or enhance existing case records post-closure.
The system has the ability to track a variety of case types, including performance issues, workplace concerns, and accommodations.
The system has the ability to allow bulk actions (e.g., status changes, reassignment, archiving) on multiple cases Via spreadsheet.
The system has the ability to allow creation of configurable case types and custom fields without vendor intervention.
The system has the ability to differentiate between active and former employees and resolve name duplication issues.
The system has the ability to pull employee workgroup and representation status for classification and analysis.
The system supports manual entry or override of employee demographic data when needed (e.g., for former employees or non-integrated records).
The system has the ability to maintain case history tied to individual employee records, including cases where the employee has left the organization.
The system has the ability to capture members of the public or contractors (non-employees) within system.
The system has the ability to set reminders and due dates on cases.
The system has the ability to assign, reassign, and track case status, history, and aging.
The system has the ability to support adding and maintaining internal case notes, comments, and collaboration threads that are associated with each individual case.
The system has the ability to route cases through defined workflows, from intake to reassignment and resolution.
The system has the to Integrate fed/retained demographic data  (importing employee records).
The system has the ability to attach and store supporting documentation within each case file.
The system has the ability to update or add documents after case creation or closure.
The system has the ability to generate canned reports for open/closed case counts, staff assignment, and volume by category.
The system has the ability to support custom reports analyzing trends by department, issue type, and representation status.
The system has the ability to include a configurable dashboard to show key operational metrics such as open cases by type, aging, or assignment.
The system has the ability to provide configurable demographic reporting that needs to match central system.
The system should be configurable to operate in an internal-only mode, with no external (employee-facing) intake, submission, or access features enabled by default.
The system has the ability to restrict case access based on user roles (e.g., HR-only, Legal-only).
The system has the ability to allow fully private case creation and handling by legal personnel.
The system has the ability to provide full audit logging of user activity, including record creation, edits, access, and deletions.
The system has the ability to support case retention policies, including the ability to set auto-archive or purge rules based on case type or age.
The System shall maintain historical schedule records for a minimum of five (5) to ten (10) years to support grievance processes, union compliance, and legal record retention.
The system supports tracking of historical schedule data and role assignments to enable accurate retroactive pay calculations and auditing.
The system maintains a record of each employee’s standard work schedule (e.g., 4/10s, 9/80s) for visibility, planning, and compliance with leave and benefit reporting.
The system supports real-time visibility into approved schedules and exceptions (e.g., out-of-class assignments, overtime triggers) for reconciliation with timecards and payroll.
The system shall support a configurable probationary period based on actual hours worked (excluding vacation and leave time), with notifications tied to time and leave tracking.
The system shall account for and track both continuing education requirements and specific certifications.

The system shall integrate scheduling with timekeeping to ensure accurate transfer of hours worked and schedule variances for payroll and reporting, and support vacation requesting and approval with integration to employee leave bank balances.
The scheduling system integrates with the core HCM system to access job class, certification status, union affiliation, and employee status for scheduling decisions.
The scheduling system integrates with the payroll systems to transmit validated time and schedule data, including shift hours, premiums, and leave types.

The scheduling system has the ability to integrate with  Vector Solutions and InTime to exchange schedule, assignment, and availability data. ( This will be needed if Public Safety scheduling requirements are not sufficient to replace InTime and Vector.)
The scheduling system integrates with the LMS to validate training completion and certification status for shift eligibility enforcement.
The system shall integrate with first responder software and applications.
The system shall integrate with the EAM to communicate employee absences, manage call-out schedules, and flag scheduled work as incompletable  when resources are unavailable.
The system has the ability to allow employees to request time off or submit shift trades directly within the platform.
The system has the ability to allow supervisors to approve or deny time-off requests and shift trades through a rule-based approval workflow.
The system shall enforce minimum rest periods between shifts and track cumulative hours worked to ensure compliance with fatigue management policies, with the ability to override when necessary.
The system supports the creation and publication of staff schedules across multiple departments, including operations, public safety, and facilities teams.
The system has the ability to support 24/7 coverage scheduling with varying shift lengths and start/end times.

The system shall assign full teams or squads (e.g., fire crew, police detail) to shifts as a single unit for efficient scheduling and coverage tracking, including management of required overtime for backfilling (e.g., wildland response with state reporting on coverage), and support breaking crews or squads into subgroups.

The scheduling system shall provide functionality to create and track special missions and associated specialized vehicles/operations (SVO), ensuring qualified personnel and resources are assigned, available, and auditable.
The system has the ability to create or modify schedules dynamically in response to emergency incidents, critical events, or mutual aid deployments.
The system supports scheduling rotating command duty officers (e.g., battalion chief, watch commander) to ensure leadership coverage during all operational hours.

The system shall support crew-based scheduling by assigning firefighters in teams to specific apparatus (e.g., engine, truck, rescue unit) with shift-based rotation and location tracking, and allow assignment of individuals to multiple apparatus.
The system shall forecast vacation hours to support union processes such as annual vacation bidding (Critical for Fire & Police shift bidding).
The system shall support Shift bidding (for Public Safety Workers).
The system allows designated personnel (e.g., team leads, watch commanders) to enter time or attendance on behalf of other employees, with audit trails for delegated entry.
The system has the ability to support flexible shift patterns, including rotating schedules, split shifts, and fixed assignments.
The system has the ability to accommodate seasonal or operational mode-based schedule variations (e.g., dredge season, snow events, fire response cycles).
The system has the ability to support self-scheduling within parameters defined by policy or supervisor rules.
The system supports assignment of specialized duties such as court appearances, investigations, inspections, and training events within the shift schedule.
The system prevents scheduling conflicts between specialized duties (e.g., court) and regular shifts or overtime assignments.
The system supports employee-initiated shift trades or swaps, with configurable rules for supervisor approval, eligibility, and role matching.
The system allows designated personnel to enter role-based time and shift assignments (e.g., acting Lieutenant, upgraded Fire role), with validations based on certification and pay rules.
The system shall allow submission of scheduling requests, including leave, overtime, and timesheets, from mobile or remote access.
The system has the ability to flag overlapping shifts, overtime triggers, and excessive hours for compliance and fatigue management.
The system has the ability to enforce union-negotiated rules for shift assignments, bidding eligibility, overtime rotation, and call-out procedures.
The system tracks mandatory holdovers and voluntary shift fills, and ensures equitable distribution across staff in compliance with overtime rules or union agreements.
The system tracks and balances overtime assignments to ensure fair distribution based on applicable union provisions or department-specific policies.
The system restricts shift assignments based on required certifications or training status (e.g., CPR, firearms, snow equipment operation, radio communications).
The system supports automated shift and vacation bidding processes, including configurable rules for seniority, eligibility, and bidding windows.
The system supports definition of blackout periods or restricted dates during which time-off or shift trading is limited or prohibited.
The system enforces minimum staffing levels and role-specific coverage rules per shift based on department-defined thresholds.

The system calculates fatigue risk indicators based on configurable rules (e.g., consecutive night shifts, shift length, limited off-duty time), and provides alerts to mitigate scheduling-related fatigue risk.
The system shall provide functionality to track and enforce contractually defined limits on union leave usage, including leave for union meetings.
The system has the ability to alert staff to open shifts and allow staff to volunteer or be assigned based on rules and qualifications.
The system has the ability to manage on-call scheduling and provide alerts for on-call staff activations.

The system identifies and alerts when qualified personnel are needed for specialized roles such as SWAT, HAZMAT, technical rescue, or paramedic response, and ensures these roles are staffed per shift.
The system has the ability to initiate mass notifications for staff recall during emergencies, track responses, and automatically populate emergency rosters.
The system automates callout notifications for vacant shifts using configurable rules for rotation, seniority, role qualifications, and response tracking.
The system shall provide configurable notification to managers as time thresholds are being reached.
The system has the ability to generate coverage dashboards that update dynamically based on time-off, call-outs, or emergency  events.
The system has the ability to display real-time views of coverage and ensure no gaps or double-bookings in shift assignments.
The system shall track employee leave bank balances.
The system shall capture occurrences and events in the personnel file for minor disciplinary  action.
The system has the ability to support roster-based scheduling, allowing supervisors to assign employees based on predefined templates or rotation cycles.
The system has the ability to allow supervisors to modify schedules using a drag-and-drop interface.
The system has the ability to forecast future staffing needs based on seasonal patterns, historical data, or special events.
The system has the ability to restrict scheduling permissions by role (e.g., line supervisor vs. department manager).
The system has the ability to handle multiple work locations and track where employees are assigned per shift.
The system supports offline time entry for field-based personnel during network outages, with automatic synchronization once a connection is restored.
The system displays scheduled training events in employee calendars and prevents scheduling conflicts with assigned shifts.
The system supports configuration of multiple scheduling periods (e.g., weekly, bi-weekly, monthly) to align with operational and payroll cycles across departments.
The system supports scheduling employees based on project assignments or milestone dates, with visibility into expected start/end dates and resource requirements.
The system allows employees to view their schedules online and on mobile devices, and supports printable formats for posting if needed.
The system provides authorized users with cross-departmental access to view schedules for operational coordination and staffing validation.
The system notifies employees of schedule changes in real time via configurable channels (e.g., email, SMS, in-app alert).
The system has the ability to produce reports showing staffing levels by role or location.
The system has the ability to produce reports showing overtime trends linked to specific reasons.
The system has the ability to produce reports showing variances between scheduled and actual attendance.
The system has the ability to produce reports showing unfilled shifts.
The system shall produce reports showing trends in sick leave.

The system shall allow employees and managers to view up-to-date Total Rewards summaries, including compensation, pension, paid time off, and benefits contributions, with the ability to add non-payroll items via configurable fields.
The system supports automated issuance of Total Compensation Statements at configurable intervals (e.g., onboarding, annual refresh) with minimal manual effort.
The system has the ability to store and apply step increases to employee compensation.
The system shall manage and apply Cost of Living Adjustments (COLA), support calibration outside of standard metrics, and generate printable compensation statements.
The system shall apply compensation changes resulting from promotions or role changes and prompt updates to bonus eligibility targets when employee changes are made.
The system supports off-cycle compensation adjustments, such as retention, or one-time pay changes, outside of standard pay schedules.
The system has the ability to facilitate compensation modeling and pay scenario planning.
The system supports rule-based eligibility for compensation programs (e.g., bonuses, incentives) based on employee role, union status, or tenure.
The system can manage multiple compensation plans concurrently, including union and non-union pay structures.
The system shall support spot bonus workflows, including routing for approvals, linkage to division-level budgets, and validation to prevent awards that exceed available funds.
The system allows HR users to simulate compensation changes and model impacts on budgets or pay equity before applying them.
The system supports alerts and audit trails for compensation changes to ensure transparency and compliance.
The system supports centralized storage and versioning of compensation plans, grids, and ranges, with effective dating.
The system has the ability to support manager and employee self-service for initiating and managing HR-related changes.
The system has the ability to support employee self-service for address changes and emergency contact updates.
The system has the ability to support employee self-service for Direct Deposit setup.
The system has the ability to support employee self-service for access to pay stubs, W2s, ACA forms, and any other tax related documents.
The system has the ability to support employee self-service for employee-initiated resignations to trigger full termination workflows.
The system has the ability to support manager self-service for termination processing triggering full termination workflows.
The system has the ability to offer mobile access to employee records, forms, and self-service tools (paystubs, tax forms, Open enrollment).

The system shall allow employees to digitally acknowledge receipt of documents (e.g., handbooks, policy updates, compensation letters, employment status changes) with timestamped tracking, leveraging functionality similar to an LMS.
The system shall allow employees to view and download their complete personnel records through self-service, in a format that meets Oregon legal requirements for personnel file access upon request.
The system allows employees to submit and track life event changes (e.g., marriage, birth, divorce) that trigger benefits, tax, and emergency contact updates.
The system shall provide the ability for managers to see emergency contact and personal contact information for their employees.
The system shall provide the ability for manager (including department or division head) to name their team if they desire it to be different than their business unit’s name.
The system shall provide the ability for manager or delegate to assign a schedule for their employees.
The system allows employees to view their job title, department, supervisor, and location as maintained in the HR system.
The system allows managers to view and initiate job requisition requests based on vacancies or approved budgeted positions.
The system supports self-service requests for employment verification letters, including configurable templates.
The system supports peer-to-peer and manager-to-employee recognition, including ability to assign award titles, reasons, and messages.
The system supports a configurable points-based reward program, allowing employees to earn and redeem points for recognition activities.
The system allows HR to create and manage recognition campaigns tied to Port values, events, or milestones (e.g., Safety Week, event attendance, retirements).
The system allows employees to view their recognition history and current points balance through self-service.
The system supports automated recognition triggers based on events (e.g., work anniversaries, certifications, retirement).
The system supports anonymous or named employee engagement surveys with configurable questions, schedules, and audience targeting.
The system includes analytics on recognition and engagement activity, including participation rates, usage by department, and impact trends.
The system can integrate development plans with assigned learning, skills tracking, and career paths to guide employee growth.
The system has the ability to alert supervisor when an employee is out of compliance in training or certification
The system has the ability to maintain and track required employee certifications (e.g., CDL, DPSST) with effective dating and pay linkage.
The system has the ability to allow departments to update and monitor certifications relevant to their staff.
The system supports assigning and tracking learning activities by role, certification requirement, or performance plan.
The system allows employees to access a personalized learning portal, with upcoming deadlines, assigned training, and certification expirations.
The system integrates with external learning content platforms (e.g., LinkedIn Learning, state LMS), including completions and scores.
The system shall provide an internal content library at minimum.
The system supports training delivery in multiple formats: eLearning, instructor-led, blended, and on-the-job.
The system enables HR or training staff to configure learning paths, assign training by cohort, and run completion reports.
The system provides dashboards for certification status, overdue training, and role-based learning compliance by team or department.
The system supports a skills matrix or internal talent marketplace, enabling visibility into employee qualifications, certifications, and interests for project or role alignment.
The system can maintain and manage skills inventories to inform talent development and deployment.
The system has the ability to support succession planning by identifying potential internal candidates for key roles.

The system has the ability to track probationary periods automatically based on hire or status change dates, trigger alerts to managers or HR, and link completion status to performance or pay workflows.
The system has the ability to support manager self-service for promotion and pay step workflows
The system shall automate performance management processes, including goal-setting, reviews, calibration, visibility to timelines, and review storage.
The system allows employees and managers to track goals, link them to organizational objectives, and monitor progress throughout the review cycle.
The system supports continuous feedback and performance notes that can be logged throughout the year by managers or peers.
The system enables employees to self-assess or submit pre-review notes as part of the performance evaluation process.
The system supports differentiated review cycles or criteria based on employee type (e.g., union, non-represented, probationary).
The system allows HR to report on talent readiness or bench strength by role or division.
The system shall define and manage job types (e.g., limited duration, sunset, regular, probationary) in addition to job classifications, with associated rules and effective dating.
The system has the ability to support a modern classification system that allows easy access to class specs and job-related information.
The system supports controlling/tracking transfers of positions for employees.

The system has the ability to support position control and job reclassification workflows in a standardized and trackable way including integration with budgeted positions, supervisory hierarchies, and org modeling tools for proactive planning.
The system has the ability to facilitate organizational work design and structure change workflows.
The system has the ability to support reorganization planning through tools like drag-and-drop org charts with associated workflow approvals.

The system has the ability to display a unified employee lifecycle timeline, showing key events such as hires, promotions, evaluations, training, and compensation changes in a single, chronological view.
The system shall store and manage job descriptions and position requirements in a centralized library, linked to job classifications and employee assignments, with the ability to archive information.
The system has the ability to track vacant positions to support proactive hiring and staffing decisions.
The system supports associating personnel and organizational data with GIS-defined zones, enabling map-based visualizations and planning dashboards for HR, safety, and operational leadership.
The system has the ability to support union contract rules and employment condition nuances (e.g., seniority calculations).
The system allows configuration of pay step progressions, premiums, or differentials based on union contract terms.
The system supports union-specific leave accrual policies and eligibility conditions tied to bargaining agreements.
The system tracks employment conditions such as probationary periods or time-in-grade, with automated alerts and effective-dating to reflect contractual requirements.

The system shall support automated calculation and processing of retroactive pay adjustments, including but not limited to retroactive step increases, pay step changes, and other compensation adjustments.
The system must allow HR/payroll staff to configure retro pay rules and apply them based on effective dates, ensuring accurate recalculation of wages from the retroactive date to the present.
The system has the ability to capture and track leave-of-absence events, including unpaid leave, and automatically calculate benefits premium adjustments and repayment schedules upon return.

The system shall provide the ability to track employee FMLA and OFLA leave usage, including start and end dates, intermittent leave schedules, and leave status (e.g., pending, approved, active, completed) with or without a Third-Party Admin. This tracking is for informational and payroll/benefits coordination purposes only, and does not need to replace the Third-Party Administrator’s entitlement management.

The system shall provide employees with the ability to request paid time off (vacation, sick, and other PTO categories) through a self-service portal, with routing based on union, department, role. This should inlcude automatic routing to managers for approval and workflow-based updates to leave balances.
The system should allow Leave Delegates to approve time off in absence of a manager.
The system shall calculate wages for leave scenarios with multiple pay sources and configurable rules to ensure correct pay without manual intervention.
The system has the ability to manage contingent, seasonal, and contract workers with distinct profiles, onboarding workflows, access rights, and assignment tracking.
The system supports role-based access and training assignments for non-employees, ensuring compliance with required certifications and policies.
The system provides workforce dashboards and reports across all worker types, supporting total workforce visibility and planning.
The system shall allow configurable alerts for HR employees on tenure, assignments, expiration dates, and renewal status for contract or temporary workers.
The system shall allow HR to assign role-based or manual delegates for performance and compensation workflows to ensure continuity during manager absences or departures.
The system shall support org chart modeling, including drag-and-drop reorgs, contingent workers, and printable charts.
The system has the ability to capture and analyze predictive retention indicators (e.g., engagement trends, compensation positioning, performance history) to support workforce planning.
The system has the ability to capture and report equal pay for similarly situated jobs.
The system can report on job classification distribution, including headcount, pay range utilization, and assignment trends.
The system can identify and report on reclassification activity, including volume, timing, and organizational impacts.
The system supports reporting on workforce demographics such as age, tenure, employment status, job family, and location, with filtering and trend analysis capabilities.
The system supports reporting on training and certification compliance, including upcoming expirations and overdue requirements.
The system supports reporting on leave compliance (e.g., FMLA/OFLA tracking, usage patterns, eligibility status).
The system includes reporting on policy or process compliance, including time entry completion, document acknowledgments, or termination processing timelines.
The system has the ability to produce dashboards and insights related to workforce composition, compensation trends, job classification patterns, and hiring activity over time.
The system includes built-in benchmarking tools or supports import of external benchmark data such as market comp data.
The system has the ability to support configurable forms and templates for HR actions.
The system has the ability to automate key employee transactions such as promotions, transfers, pay changes, address changes, and job reclassifications.

The system supports offboarding workflows for terminated employees, including task assignment and tracking for activities such as system access removal, final documentation, asset return, and exit survey completion.
The system has the ability to track pay step progressions and automatically alert HR or managers to upcoming increases or probation completions.
The system has the ability for authorized HR users to configure and modify forms, approval paths, and workflow logic without requiring vendor or IT intervention.

The system has the ability to support delegated or proxy access to personnel records and associated workflows, allowing designated users to perform actions or review records on behalf of others (e.g., during absences).
The system has the ability to digitally store employee personnel files including ECNs (Employee Change Notice), resignation letters, and related documents.
The system has the ability to manage personnel records through secure, centralized, and role-based access.
The system has the ability to manage personnel file retention in accordance with exit dates and legal retention policies.

The system has the ability to consolidate employee data into a system of record and support integrated document/content management features. This should be configurable to store data in separate subfolders under the employee main folder.

The system shall support system-to-system feeds for benefits data (e.g., deferred compensation from Lincoln Financial) and shall integrate with PERS, including the ability to generate and transmit required ADAT files.
The system supports integration with the ERP to transmit payroll results for financial posting and reconciliation.
The system supports integration with the EAM system to receive time entries submitted against work orders.
The system supports integration from the ERP to provide budgeting and position funding data to the talent acquisition process.
The system supports integration from the LMS to the EAM system to validate employee training or certifications required for specific work order assignments.
The system supports integration with the ArcGIS platform to associate employee locations, organizational units, or assignments with spatial data for analysis or planning.
The system supports integration with the ERP to transmit employee cost allocation data by project, grant, or organizational unit.
The system has the ability to capture and track leave-of-absence events, including unpaid leave, and automatically calculate benefits premium adjustments and repayment schedules upon return.
The system automatically accrues leave balances based on eligibility rules, bargaining unit, tenure, and employment status.
The system allows employees, managers, and HR to view leave balances, history, and pending requests in real time.
The system provides alerts for insufficient balances, overlapping requests, and conflicting entries.
The system supports visibility into protected leave status provided by a third-party administrator, including ability to flag impacted pay periods or timecards for coordination.
The system supports audit trails and reporting for leave transactions, including requests, approvals, and adjustments.
The system allows employees to submit leave requests in advance through self-service, with visibility into eligibility, balances, and prior requests.
The system calculates earnings and deductions accurately during periods of paid or unpaid leave, including partial pay, offsets, and deferred payments.
The system shall provide FMLA and wage calculations based on state and federal leave laws.
The system shall provide the ability to generate specific reports by group or role with flexibility.

The system has the ability to process bi-weekly & weekly payroll with support for varying pay period schedules across represented and non-represented groups, including differences in start and end dates by union contract or business unit.
The system has the ability to support unique payroll configurations for uniform allowance.
The system shall support unique and configurable multiple payroll rules to accommodate pay types such as bonus payments, retro payments, premium pay, etc.
The system allows payroll administrators to configure pay codes and rules without vendor or IT dependency.
The system has the ability to assign employees to rule groups that define pay calculations, accrual logic, and eligibility based on union affiliation, job classification, or employment type.
The system has the ability to support configuration and enforcement of payroll rules for both exempt and non-exempt employees.
The system supports platoon-based scheduling and payroll cycles for public safety staff (e.g., 24-on/48-off).

The system supports equalized salary calculations for shift-based employees whose scheduled hours exceed a standard 80-hour pay period, ensuring consistent pay regardless of shift alignment or pay period length.
The system has the ability to process payroll for active, inactive, seasonal, and terminated employees where appropriate.
The system has the ability to process multiple concurrent job assignments and apply blended or stacked pay rates based on position, union agreement, and hours worked within each role.

The system has the ability to configure and apply earnings codes, deductions, and benefit calculations across all employee types, with support for multiple rule sets based on group, job class, or assignment status.
The system has the ability to track and allocate employee payroll costs across departments, projects, grants, or capital activities based on time worked or assigned labor codes.
The system has the ability to manage tax withholding setup, updates, and compliance across federal, state, and local levels.
The system supports out-of-class pay processing, including override of job codes and pay rates based on temporary assignments, with audit tracking and retroactive eligibility.
The system supports future-dated changes for pay rates and benefit deductions with automatic application based on effective date.
The system supports configuration of auto-pay codes (e.g., stipends) based on eligibility, assignment status, or schedule logic.
The system provides secure access to direct deposit information for authorized users to support reversals and audit processing, with configurable visibility controls.
The system tracks employee schedules (e.g., 9/80, 4/10, platoon) and provides visibility for payroll, timekeeping, and leave tracking purposes.
The system shall support limitations on stipend payments during leave periods.
The system shall provide the ability to stop accruals on leave banks with hard caps.
The system shall provide the ability to notify employees when leave or accrual caps are almost reached.
The system supports configuration of multiple union rule sets with exceptions by employee group, work schedule, classification, or location.
The system has the ability to apply union rules and contract terms for pay differentials.
The system has the ability to apply union rules and contract terms for overtime.
The system has the ability to apply union rules and contract terms for leave earning rules.
The system has the ability to apply union rules and contract terms for probation and step progression.
The system supports automated retroactive pay recalculations based on prior time worked, updated rates, and rule-based eligibility.
The system supports automated calculation and processing of leave balance payouts upon separation, based on eligibility and accrual rules.
The system has the ability to process garnishments and third-party deductions with flexibility and legal compliance.
The system has the ability to support off-cycle checks and emergency payments.
The system has the ability to enforce accrual caps and automatically stop accruals at threshold with employee visibility.
The system supports retroactive pay adjustments resulting from labor contract settlements, including rate changes over time, partial eligibility, job history, and blended assignments.
The system supports retroactive pay adjustments for non-contractual corrections, such as late approvals, incorrect rates, or misclassified time.
The system supports bulk uploads or mass entry of payroll adjustments during off-cycle and regular payroll processing.
The system supports payroll reversals and corrections after processing, including secure workflows for approval, audit trail logging, and employee notification.
The system generates retroactive pay audit summaries showing time worked, original vs updated rates, and resulting pay differences for employee and payroll review.
The system supports allocation of payroll costs by project for General Ledger posting.
The system supports allocation of payroll costs by department for General Ledger posting.
The system supports allocation of payroll costs by object code for General Ledger posting.
The system supports allocation of payroll costs by other chart of accounts elements for General Ledger posting.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system supports online benefits enrollment, including new hire, qualifying events, and selection of services.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system supports a secure mobile experience for viewing and changing benefits selections.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system allows employees to view and download historical benefits elections and contributions across plan years.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system supports real-time eligibility checks and rules-based validation during benefits selection (e.g., dependent age, coverage duplication).

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system supports open enrollment configuration by HR users, including plan setup, eligibility rules, communications, and testing.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system has the ability to trigger additional benefit enrollment based on employee activities or work group.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system shall provide the ability to create employee groups for benefit enrollment.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system supports pre-population of prior year benefits elections during open enrollment, with employee review and override or the ability to check a box that states something simialr to "No Changes".

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system allows employees to manage dependent and beneficiary information via self-service, including eligibility verification and documentation upload.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system provides confirmation statements, reminders, or alerts to employees during benefits enrollment and life event changes.

requirements.subCategory: Benefits Self Service and Employee Experience
requirements.description: The system shall provided self-service for special payroll forms repayments (eg. Global entry, cell phone subsidy, ergonomic stipend, ERG stipend, etc).
The system supports uploading of documents required for benefits enrollment with tracking and validation workflows.
The system supports benefits enrollment and change workflows including review, approval, and effective dating of benefit elections.
The system has the ability to track employee benefits eligibility and coverage tiers over time, including plan history and changes.
The system maintains audit history for changes to eligibility status, with integration to benefits enrollment logic.
The system supports automated premium adjustments for unpaid leave and generates catch-up schedules upon return.
The system can comply with Secure 2.0 calculations for retirement plans.
The system has automatic age notifications (eg. Kids turning 26, or turning 65)
The system can create reports for benefit billing by vendor and be able to reconcile the bill based on the Port rules.
The system shall provide the ability to transfer or terminate leave banks.
The system has the ability to manage payroll calendars and ensure accurate processing timelines (e.g., calendar-year vs fiscal-year cycles).
The system has the ability to run pre-payroll and post-payroll audits and generate exception reports.
The system has the ability to retain payroll records in compliance with legal and internal retention policies.
The system has the ability to maintain full audit trails for payroll changes, approvals, and overrides.
The system has the ability to generate detailed payroll reports for finance, HR, and union contract compliance.
The system has the ability to track and calculate benefits eligibility based on hours worked (e.g., ACA compliance).
The system has the ability to store, configure, and apply multiple payrate codes.
The system integrates with the timekeeping system to import employee time entries for payroll processing, with support for project codes, labor distribution, and audit details.
The system integrates with benefits administration systems to process payroll deductions, including health plans, deferred compensation, and third-party contributions.
The system supports generation and transmission of payroll data extracts to third parties (e.g., unions, benefits carriers, retirement systems) in configurable formats.
The system supports outbound integration to the ERP System for payroll cost allocation by project, department, object code, and other chart of accounts elements.
The system shall integrate with LCPtracker to exchange certified payroll, compliance, and labor reporting data, reducing manual entry and ensuring consistency between systems.
The system should have the ability to push time entries from EAM to timekeeping system.
The system supports self-service and delegated time entry across employee types, including hourly, exempt, union, and seasonal workers.
The system supports mobile and web-based time entry with support for multiple codes per day.
The system provides configurable validations (e.g., total hours, shift logic, CBA rules) and validations error rules, such as warnings vs. blocking processing at the point of entry.
The system supports project-level and work order real-time visibility rules for time entry, allowing employees to view and select only cost codes or account strings they are authorized to charge.
The system shall provide the ability to limit pay codes available to be used on an employee’s timecard to those that are allowed for use on their timecard

The system supports automated prepopulation of timesheets based on assigned schedules or default job roles, with the ability to convert approved schedules into editable timecards for employee review and override.
The system shall provide the ability to enter comments on timecards and activity tracking.
The system shall provide the ability to adjust timekeeping roles by group or specific employees, with appropriate controls.
The system shall provide the ability to auto-populate holidays time-off or pay by group.
The system sends reminders and alerts for incomplete, unsubmitted, unapproved, or invalid timecards.
The system provides workflow routing for supervisor review and approval of time submissions.
The system enforces time submission prior to payroll processing, including lockouts or exception workflows for incomplete or unapproved timecards.
The system maintains a complete audit trail of time entries, edits, and approvals, with user IDs and timestamps.
The system supports audit reporting and exception tracking for late, missing, or inconsistent time entries.

The system provides configurable error handling when timecard entries do not align with approved schedules or position eligibility, including alerts, validation messages, and routing for supervisor resolution.
The system supports reconciliation reporting between scheduled and actual time worked, including exception flagging for early departures, missed shifts, or unscheduled hours.
The system allows supervisors or designated approvers to override prepopulated timecards derived from schedules, with an audit trail and optional resubmission workflow.
The system allows timekeeping codes and policies to be configured without vendor or IT support.
The system integrates with EAM platform for work order scheduling.
The system has the ability to initiate a new hire request, capturing fields such as: new vs. replacement, prior incumbent, and budget status.
The system has the ability to support group-based approvals and delegated approvers.
The system has the ability to support multi-hire requisitions (i.e., hiring multiple candidates under a single job posting).
The system shall validate the position status (budgeted, vacant, approved) before requisition submission.
The system has the ability to support multi-step requisition approval workflows.
The system has the ability to generate interview preparation materials (e.g., job description, schedule) within the platform.
The system has the ability to store and link historical recruitment documentation (e.g., interview guides, advertisements) for reuse.

The system shall support talent pipelines and proactive candidate relationship management, including the ability to create talent pools, automate outreach campaigns, and track candidate engagement history.
The system shall provide configurable, branded career sites that allow dynamic job postings, candidate self-service, and mobile responsiveness.
The system has the ability to assign recruiters to requisitions and configure job-specific workflows and interview stages.
The system shall provide configurable dashboards for hiring managersto track the status of requisitions, offers, background checks, onboarding, and provisioning tasks.
The system has the ability to track and facilitate multiple interview rounds (e.g., virtual initial round, in-person panels).
The system shall allow configurable rules for timing and sequencing of candidate notifications, including templates and an option to require manual approval before dispatch.
The system has the ability to store interview evaluations directly in the system, eliminating the use of spreadsheets.
The system has the ability to prevent previously failed applicants from reapplying or appearing in future interview pools for evergreen postings.
The system has the ability to mask or anonymize candidate identifying information during screening to reduce bias.
The system has the ability to tag and flag repeat applicants across multiple requisitions.
The system has the ability to merge candidate  information when applying (e.g., candidates having multiple emails). - having something specific for HR hires and access control
The system has the ability to enforce Veteran’s Preference rules in both scored and unscored evaluation processes.
The system has the ability to validate the presence of required documentation (e.g., veteran documents) during application.
The system can automatically populate application fields using information extracted from the applicant’s submitted resume.
The system shall provide configurable compliance reporting capabilities, including EEO, veteran, and diversity reporting, and maintain audit logs of requisition and candidate activities.
The system has the ability to send automated interview scheduling links to applicants for self-selection of times.
The system has the ability  to upload and store interview materials (questions, interviewer notes, interviewer scoring, etc.).
The system integrates with Outlook to streamline interview scheduling by automatically adding interviews to employees’ calendars once applicants select a time.
The system has the ability to lock applicant schedules and notify recruiters of changes in real time.
The system has the ability to support two-way SMS functionality for communication with candidates.
The system has the ability to display real-time candidate status updates throughout the hiring process via an applicant portal and/or automated communications.
The system has the ability to coordinate offer approvals with compensation and generate customizable offer letters.
The system shall support electronic offer letter generation with configurable templates, version control, and integrated e-signature functionality.
The system has the ability to manage background checks, drug testing, and contingencies before offer finalization.
The system has the ability to deliver onboarding tasks, forms, and checklists digitally to new hires prior to new hire's start date.
The system has the ability to support additional onboarding processes expandable by department
The system has the ability to manage onboarding workflows tailored by classification (e.g., union vs. admin).
The system has the ability to automate onboarding notifications and provisioning tasks for IT, Facilities, and Payroll.
The system has the ability to record and use a new hire’s preferred name (e.g., for setting up email accounts).
The system has the ability to provide document upload capabilities and support permission-based access to sensitive onboarding content.
The system shall provide role-based and configurable access provisioning templates (e.g., network drives, applications, equipment) that can be automatically triggered during onboarding.
The system shall automatically populate core HR and payroll records from the accepted offer details.
The system shall enable support onboarding and provisioning workflows for contingent workers, project staff, and non-employee personnel.

The system shall support assignment and tracking of required training modules, policy attestations, and certifications as part of onboarding workflows, with completion status visibility to HR and hiring managers.
The system has the ability to support onboarding media material such as welcoming videos
The system integrates with benefits platforms and programs.
The system has the capability to activate HR-required permissions during the onboarding process.

The system has the ability to generate configurable, automated notifications to relevant stakeholders (e.g., Hiring Manager, HR, IT, Facilities) upon completion of approvals or requisition status changes.
The system shall support bi-directional integration with the LMS to retrieve, validate, and sync training completion records, including bias training specific to employee's department/role.
The system shall integrate with Finance and Position Control modules to provide real-time visibility into position funding and status.
The system shall integrate with core HR and Payroll modules to automatically populate employee records upon offer acceptance.
The system shall integrate with third-party background check and drug testing providers to automate initiation, tracking, and completion of pre-employment checks.
The system shall support integration with employee wellness and benefits enrollment platforms (e.g., Sprout) to enable seamless data exchange.

The system shall track customer master data including lease terms, balances, and security deposits, as well as property taxes (R numbers, Port- or tenant-paid taxes, drainage fees) and certificates of insurance (COI).
The system has the ability to track customer billing activity across locations and business units with rolled up and drill-down views.
The system shall provide a customer portal for viewing billing information and for viewing or downloading active agreements and amendments.
The system should provide payment capabilities within the customer portal.
The system has the ability to track contact details and roles (e.g., legal contact, billing contact, facilities lead) associated with each tenant or lease, and log communication history.
The system has the ability to manage broker records, including tracking commission terms, associated lease deals, and payment milestones.
The system has the ability to track tenant and broker interactions over time tied to specific property locations.
The system has the ability to track leads, leaving notes or comments to added documents.
The system shall support tracking and reporting of Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method (BREEAM) scores for applicable assets or facilities.
The system has the ability to manage lease options (e.g., renewal, expansion, early termination), including key dates, conditions, and stakeholder visibility.
The system has the ability to configure building units and lease spaces with structured hierarchies and clearly defined spatial relationships.
The system has the ability to set lease and billing details by location and by property within a location (e.g. Terminal 2, and specific properties within Terminal 2)
The system has the ability to manage dual address records for each lease, including billing and notice addresses.
The system supports high-volume lease creation through batch entry, import templates, or pre-configured setup rules for leases with multiple units or recurring structures.
The system supports sequential leasing of units to multiple tenants across time without data conflicts or reuse restrictions.
The system supports overlapping occupancy of a unit or space by multiple tenants during transitions or short-term arrangements, including temporary reuse scenarios.
The system supports lease transitions where a new tenant begins occupancy before the prior tenant’s contract has fully terminated.
The system supports termination of only part of a lease, such as a subset of rooms or square footage, while keeping the rest active.

The system supports classification of leases by business unit, lease type (e.g., ground, terminal, easement), and configurable tags to assist with reporting, workflow assignment, or complexity scoring.
The system has the ability to handle perpetual or long-term leases with special statuses (e.g., easements, year-to-year occupancy).
The system supports definition and tracking of landlord vs. tenant maintenance responsibilities using configurable rules or assignment tables per lease or asset.

The system shall allow configuration of custom fields and document metadata by lease type, property class, or business unit without vendor customization, and support searching by specific fields (e.g., operating agreements, leases of improved space).
The system has the ability to route contract documents and drafts to legal, risk, environmental, and finance teams with audit trails and status tracking.
The system has the ability to manage and track parallel contract drafts with placeholders, redlines, and version histories.
The system has the ability to manage standard and custom lease templates, including clause libraries and pre-approved exhibit structures.
The system has the ability to initiate leases from both  business development (ingestion of CRM records) and tenant renewal workflows.

The system shall support internal pre-approval workflows for lease proposals, including structured deal analysis, stakeholder routing, and executive sign-off (e.g., via BATS - Business Analysis Transaction Sheets), with support for executive summaries, signatures, NDAs, and LOIs.
The system has the ability to initiate contract drafts with minimal required fields and progressively require additional information based on contract status (e.g., Draft, Legal Review, Execution).
The system shall generate or display structured lease abstracts summarizing key terms, dates, obligations, financial conditions, and location details including street and building numbers.
The system shall allow assignment of Port signatories and tracking of delegated authorities.

The system shall define and manage properties, units, and leases to support integrated financial reporting by location and asset, including links to billing activity, maintenance history, capital planning needs, and the responsible property manager.
The system has the ability to track available properties, office suites, and land parcels in a centralized, searchable database with availability timelines.
The system has the ability to determine and display property availability based on current lease end dates, renewal status, termination status, and planned vacancy periods.

The system shall distinguish between lease statuses such as 'closed,' 'terminated,' 'month-to-month,' 'year-to-year,' 'start and end dates,' and 'pulled over,' particularly when finance activities are still pending.
The system supports definition and tracking of CAM (Common Area Maintenance) program rules, and generation of tenant cost estimates for reconciliation, in coordination with ERP billing processes.

The system shall forecast Common Area Maintenance (CAM) fees for prospective or renewing leases based on historical billing, square footage, or other allocation methods, and support management of reserve funds for CAM.
The system has the ability to display historical CAM cost allocations at the unit or tenant level to assist with lease negotiation and validation.
The system has the ability to support scenario-based planning and cost-benefit analysis for asset retention or reinvestment decisions.
The system has the ability to track and report capital expenditures and depreciation schedules by property or asset location.
The system has the ability to recognize revenue by accounting period based on lease terms and amortization rules.
The system shall provide data analytics capabilities to support capital forecasting and planning, financial analysis, and pro forma analysis.
The system shall automate rent escalation schedules, including fixed increases, percentage-based increases, and CPI or flat-rate adjustments, with effective dates and audit history.

The system has the ability to allocate indirect or shared costs (e.g., utilities, staff time, insurance, taxes) across properties based on configurable drivers (e.g., square footage, lease terms, usage).

The system has the ability to allocate shared utility and maintenance costs (e.g., stormwater bills) across multiple tenants or units using configurable cost allocation methods such as square footage, percentage share, fixed fee, or usage-based inputs.

The system has the ability to route calculated tenant allocations for internal review and approval prior to invoicing, including visibility into allocation methods and editable overrides where permitted.
The system has the ability to initiate invoice requests or generate tenant billing entries based on approved allocation results, and to link those entries to the originating shared expense.
The system has the ability to store contract drafts and executed agreements in a centralized, searchable repository.

The system supports categorization and storage of supplemental lease-related documents such as environmental cleanup reports, lease closeout records, and right-of-first-refusal documentation, with searchable metadata and access controls.
The system has the ability to document and retrieve historical lease terms, clauses, and unique provisions for future reference and reuse.
The system shall provide version control capabilities with audit trails.
The system shall support reporting of lease information, expirations, and terminations to County assessors for property tax processing, including tenant responsibilities and drainage fees.
The system shall log and route property tax statements, track Port and tenant payment responsibilities, and integrate tax account details into finance to ensure compliance and timely payment.
The system has the ability to provide mobile access to lease documents, unit details, and obligations for field-based staff or inspections.

The system has the ability to track internal lease workflow progress and task ownership, including contract negotiation, legal review, and amendment routing, with status visibility, document attachments, and assignment history. Assign port signatories and track delegated authorities
The system has the ability to perform bulk updates to leases, units, or billing records, with role-based approvals and audit logs.
The system has the ability to track lease execution milestones, including final approvals, legal sufficiency, and effective dates, with audit trail support.
The system supports contract and amendment prioritization through structured task queues or dashboards that reflect current status, responsible parties, and key dates, with audit trails for updates.
The system has the ability to support multi-step lease or unit setup workflows, including validations and approvals.

The system has the ability to attach, manage, and route lease exhibits with version control, supporting department-specific routing (e.g., CAD, Legal, Environmental) and configurable checklist validation per lease type.
The system has the ability to manage large-format or oversized exhibits by storing them externally and embedding reference links within the lease record, when direct attachment is not supported.
The system has the ability to manage lease modifications (amendments, extensions, space adds) through a governed change workflow.
The system has the ability to support user-defined cost allocation models across tenants or units (e.g., based on square footage).
The system has the ability to identify tenant-billable maintenance and generate invoice requests based on work order details.

The system shall capture and track lease-related obligations (e.g., insurance, reporting, maintenance, and EHS compliance) using structured lease logs that support deadlines, categories, free text, automated reminders, and assignment of tenant responsibilities.
The system shall allow notifications to be sent to both the tenant and the property manager.
The system has the ability to generate system-generated alerts based on workflow changes, field updates, or task assignments, with user subscription and routing rules.
The system has the ability to notify Property Management when tenant billable maintenance work is completed and trigger billing workflows.
The system has the ability to receive invoice requests and generate tenant invoices based on integrated maintenance records from the EAM system.
The system has the ability to integrate with Salesforce to support lead tracking, tenant interaction history, and transition of selected records into lease initiation workflows.
The system has the ability to integrate with ESRI GIS to support property-level visualization, location-based queries, and parcel-based lease management.
The system supports integration with the EAM system to consume asset condition data and forecast capital planning needs for lease decision-making.
The system supports integration to update EAM asset information (Business Units, contracts, etc.) based on active lesses.
The system has the ability to request or retrieve customer IDs from the ERP as part of lease setup workflows, supporting automated or prompted record creation when a new tenant is entered.

The system has the ability to integrate with the ERP system to transmit structured tenant billing requests, including customer ID, amount, description, charge type, and associated lease or allocation references.
The system shall integrate with budgeting tools to support forecasting by location.
The system shall support terminal use agreements that are tied to a use rather than a property.

AP01.comments: Workday's single data model unifies AP, GL, and procurement, removing the need for manual interfacing. Transactions automatically update the General Ledger in real time upon approval, facilitating coordination between purchasing, accounts payable and accounting to ensure proper unity. All Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level.

AP04.comments: Workday streamlines invoice review and editing with field annotations and interactive highlights in scanned PDFs. Users can easily compare the scanned file and the Workday invoice using the pop-out attachment or split-screen view. Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level. Unlike other cloud-based financial software packages, this action does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

AP06.comments: Workday supports 3-way invoice matching to purchase orders and receipts. Matching rules and conditions can be defined to govern when 3-way match is required. Workday can perform a three-way match between the Purchase Order, Receipt, and Supplier Invoice. Matching enables you to identify exceptions on supplier invoices, ensuring compliance across your procurement activities.

AP07.comments: Supported. Workday helps you identify and manage disputed invoices, invoices in collection, and invoices on hold. Invoices can be marked as on hold, in dispute, in collection, or as any combination. When you view a customer invoice, you have visibility into all hold, collection, and dispute information.

AP09.comments: Supplier invoices can be entered manually, copied from an existing invoice, or created from a purchase order, contract, or receipt. Invoices can also be submitted by a supplier via self-service or created from a Workday customer invoice. Additionally, invoices can be imported from other systems or via EDI or OCR.

AP10.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework provides auditable tracking and, with audit always on capabilities, date and time stamps are always recorded. Workday's Expense Protect functionality, an intelligent auditing tool leveraging machine learning, reviews 100% of the expenses in real-time and provides insights on those expense lines or reports with anomalies requiring special attention. Validation rules create warnings/errors for discrepancies and can be configured with conditional logic.

AP11.comments: Workday fully supports the creation of recurring supplier invoices, either fixed or variable amounts, with all the relevant supplier and invoice information including frequency type and first/last invoice date or total amount. You can also define supplier invoice contracts which will generate invoices according to a user-defined schedule. With Workday Contracts you can automate billing using templates for various types of products or services, including fixed amount, prepaid amount including visibility to utilization, recurring billing, fixed term, subscription, usage based billing, variable amount where the contract amounts represent estimates, project fixed fee, project Time & Expense, value based project, alternate contracts, set worktag dimension as a billing rate rule and create a billing rate rule using contract rate sheets. You also define the billing schedule as single, recurring with equal or custom amounts or milestone-driven. You can create a consolidated billing schedules across all customer contract lines.

AP12.comments: Workday provides the ability to allocate the cost of each line item on a business document to multiple accounting distribution lines through our line split details. Splits can be allocated by quantity or spend amount/percentage.

AP13.comments: Workday enables you to hold supplier invoices from payment. You can specify reasons to hold supplier invoices and supplier invoice adjustments from payment.

AP14.comments: You can define, at the supplier contract level, a discount percentage applicable on the supplier catalog. When creating a purchase order, the price will be derived from the contract, including the discount. For prompt payment, discounts may be calculated using Payment Terms. Discounts may also be entered as a negative value (-) for specific lines within the purchase order.

AP15.comments: Supported. When you Void/Cancel a payment in Workday the related Supplier Invoices are set back to Unpaid/Approved status so a new payment can be issued.

AP16.comments: Workday supports automated accruals for Purchase Order lines and Supplier Contract receipt lines that have not been invoiced. Receipts that have not been invoiced are fully reportable and visible on dashboards for both the Accounts Payable and Procurement teams.

AP17.comments: Supported. You can create an invoice adjustment referenced to the original invoice to change the amount due from the customer and create the required accounting. You can decrease the amount due as a credit memo (e.g., for returns, damaged merchandise, price adjustments, and so forth). You can also increase the amount due as a debit memo.

AP18.comments: Workday provides the ability to allocate the cost of each line item on a business document to multiple accounting distribution lines through our line split details. Splits can be allocated by quantity or spend amount/percentage.

AP19.comments: Workday gives you the ability to create prepaid invoices and have the system automatically offset billable transaction invoices against the prepayment amount; this functionality is only utilized for project time and expense and usage-based contract line types.

AP21.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to make accounting adjustments to correct worktags on paid supplier invoice, supplier invoice adjustments, expense reports, and procurement card verification transactions. Customers can perform accounting adjustments to transfer costs on operational transactions for supplier invoices, supplier invoice adjustments, expense reports, and procurement card transaction verifications. Like the operational transactions, adjustment transactions follow a business process with a journal entry posting upon completion of the process with full audit tracking.

AP22.comments: Supported. Workday includes allocated freight, tax, and other charges for the on-hand value of purchased items. Workday also provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created.

AP23.comments: Workday provides multiple options for import/upload of data from external sources. Workday will produce the required template needed to complete a spreadsheet upload of virtually any type of transaction into the system, e.g., purchase orders/supplier invoices, journal entries, or any other type.

AP24.comments: Workday supports multiple payment methods including checks, ACH, EDI, and ISO200022 protocols. Additionally, Workday supports the SWIFT (ISO 9362) financial messaging document standard. While Virtual Payment Card payment type requires the use of a third-party payment provider, customers also have the ability use a credit card to pay outstanding invoices directly through the Workday Customer Portal.

AP25.comments: The system provides the ability to print a sequence of checks for payments and allows you to change the check number if the Workday check number and the number on your preprinted check stock get out of sync. You can also create manual checks.

AP26.comments: Workday provides a unified settlement engine to manage settlement for all transaction types including Procurement Card Transactions and Bank Account Transfers. This enables you to gain visibility to all approved and unpaid items across multiple organizations, transaction types, and currencies and settle all payments from one place streamlining your process.

AP27.comments: Supplier Invoices can be generated from and matched against purchase orders, supplier contracts, receipts, timesheets, and task logs. Payments typically would be created through the Workday Settlement, which is a single engine that enables you to manage all incoming and outgoing payments in one place across your entire business. Payments can be consolidated, and automatically calculate any discounts eligible. You can also make ad hoc (i.e., discrete) payments. You can pay in full or make partial payments. There are several Payment Statuses that are possible, which will be updated by the Workday Business Process Framework.

AP28.comments: Workday provides a unified settlement engine to manage settlement for all transaction types, which enables you to gain visibility to all approved and unpaid items. Workday also allows for multiple currencies and companies to be settled within a single settlement run. It also allows you to run more efficient reports by searching on all currencies at once, settlement run dates, companies, or payment purposes.

AP29.comments: Supported. If there was an error made on entry, an invoice can be changed with a related action any time before it is paid and will re-enter the business process upon submission. When you Void/Cancel a payment in Workday the related Supplier Invoices are set back to Unpaid/Approved status so a new payment can be issued.

AP30.comments: Supported. You can print an invoice with attachments using both Workday Docs for Layouts and BIRT to mail to a customer or the system can automatically create a PDF-formatted version and send it to the customer as an attachment via email. Workday enables you to BOTH email and mail customer invoices to multiple recipients.

AP31.comments: You can add scanned images, as well as any other type of document or file, to the supplier invoice. You can also use Workday’s delivered web services and integration tools to integrate with third party document management/imaging solutions or leverage Workday's in-built Invoice OCR capability.

AP32.comments: You can add scanned images, as well as any other type of document or file, to the supplier invoice. In this way, the image of the invoice is available to anyone with access to the invoice, including those involved in the approval process. You can also configure a user's access to delete or change supplier invoice attachments.

AP33.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system including: Transaction entry and approval such as journal entries and supplier invoices; Reference data such as customers, cost centers, and expense types; Hierarchy/organization structures; Business process/workflow definitions; Edit and validation rules and Security definition and role assignment. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility over things such as the Vendor or Item Primary records, companies, organizational structures, account roll-ups, etc. Auditing is always on.

AP34.comments: Workday can consolidate customer invoices as well as provide support for customer hierarchies and make it easier to create, print, and email consolidated invoices. Workday also supports direct intercompany transactions where one company is a supplier to another company without the need to create a supplier for each company.

AP35.comments: Supplier Invoices can be generated from and matched against purchase orders, supplier contracts, receipts, timesheets, and task logs. Payments are created through the Workday Settlement, which enables you to manage all incoming and outgoing payments in one place across your entire business. Workday supports multiple payment methods including checks, ACH, EDI, and ISO200022 protocols. Additionally, Workday supports the SWIFT (ISO 9362) financial messaging document standard.

AP40.comments: On each supplier you can set Supplier status, segmentation, or supplier risk. There's also the option to set tags, such as 'preferred'. Filter on these settings to select, for example, all 'preferred' suppliers. Additionally, you can create supplier custom fields within the supplier profile to further assist with supplier categorization.

AP41.comments: Workday supports multiple alternate supplier connection names for a single supplier, enabling you to process payments for suppliers with different remit-to locations, payment types, and unique remit-to names. Workday supports consolidated billing and provides support for customer hierarchies and make it easier to create, print, and email consolidated invoices. Payment processing supports payment against consolidated invoices for customer hierarchies, enabling users to select invoices for customer hierarchies to select invoice at both the parent and the child customers.

AP42.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework supports configurable approval workflows, including those for non-PO items. Approval steps can be based on amount, spend categories, project, cost center, or any number of other related business attributes.

AP43.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework is highly configurable and helps prevent business processes from being delayed if an Approver isn't available or doesn't respond within a time range.
