Escalation notices can be sent internally (e.g., user's Workday Inbox or Notifications) and/or externally (e.g., email). Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments managing the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. The business process framework within Workday is highly configurable and can use any values on the invoice or invoice line to drive required approvals and documentation before an invoice can be released for payment.

AP44.comments: Supported. Using Workday's Business Process Framework, you can configure a business process for supplier invoice approval. The business process can have multiple steps such as approval, notification, to do, and checklist. Each step can be invoked based upon rules defined using attributes of the transaction.

AP45.comments: Workday delivers a delegation framework for all functional areas including Expense Management. This allows the delegation of creation and/or approval of expense reports. Anyone who may be assigned an action (e.g., approval) may delegate their responsibility with effective dates or on an ad hoc basis.

AP46.comments: Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments. This process manages the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. The business process framework within Workday is highly configurable and can use any values on the invoice or invoice line to drive required approvals and documentation before an invoice can be released for payment.

AP48.comments: Dates associated with the contract can also proactively alert you of a contract expiring soon so that your Procurement department can act accordingly before contract expiration or automatic renewal. Spend against supplier contracts is tracked and analysis is available incl. drill-downs capability to get deep insights into organization spending.

AP49.comments: Workday enables you to track and view 1099 data for suppliers. You can set up 1099 categories and then designate suppliers as 1099 suppliers. You can then review 1099 payments in anticipation of printing and electronically filing your 1099s. We deliver print layouts that support printing 1099 MISC, 1099 NEC, and 1096 forms, with the flexibility to print on blank paper or on pre-printed IRS forms. Workday provides the ability to create tax authority and tax categories and specify as withholding.

AP50.comments: Supported. On each supplier you have the possibility to set Supplier status, segmentation or supplier risk. Next to that there's the option to set tags on each supplier, for example tagging it as 'preferred'. When selecting supplier you filter on these settings. Additionally, you have the ability to create supplier custom fields that can be captured and stored within the supplier profile that can further assist with supplier categorization. Workday's reporting framework provides the ability to natively break out source transactions by dimension, amounts, dates, type, involved persons and processes, etc.

AP51.comments: Workday delivers aging reports that can be run as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. You can also specify a specific translation type for multi-currency agings. These delivered reports can display payables aging at any summarization i.e., by supplier or by transaction, and provide the ability to drill into the details to see the underlying invoices and take action. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. Users can run a receivables aging by either operational aging (ages by transaction date) or financial aging (ages by accounting date). Additionally, users can have the aging basis be by either due date or invoice date. Workday provides users to be able to create custom aging reports as well using delivered report-writing capabilities.

AP52.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to track and view 1099 data for suppliers. We deliver print layouts that support printing 1099 MISC, 1099 NEC, and 1096 forms, with the flexibility to print on blank paper or on pre-printed IRS forms.

AP54.comments: The Workday Supplier Portal allows suppliers to submit invoices. Workday Financial Management includes machine learning-based Supplier Invoice Automation as standard. Workday uses OCR and machine learning to read invoice files from uploads or emails and automatically create invoices in Workday.

AP55.comments: Workday uses OCR and machine learning to read invoice files from uploads or emails and automatically create invoices in Workday.

AP56.comments: Workday uses OCR and machine learning to read invoice files from uploads or emails and automatically create invoices in Workday. Workday streamlines invoice review and editing with field annotations and interactive highlights in scanned PDFs.

AP57.comments: Workday supports issuing purchase orders to suppliers in various electronic formats including EDI (XML), email, or via the Workday Supplier Portal. Procurement-facing EDI supplier integrations send and receive EDI 810, 850, 855, and 856 files to and from Workday. Workday provides these integration templates to support different EDI file formats.

AP58.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework can be configured to route expenditures based on organizational policies. The business process can have multiple steps such as approval, notification, to do, and checklist. Each step can be invoked based upon rules defined using attributes of the transaction. For example, you can automatically route the expense report to the worker’s supervisor or the manager of the project charged on the report.

AP59.comments: Workday supports 3-way invoice matching to purchase orders and receipts. Matching rules and conditions can be defined to govern when 3-way match is required, when 2-way matching is sufficient, and when no matching is required. Invoice matching variances can be routed for review/investigation/approval by the appropriate staff depending on the nature of the variance according to flexible business process rules that you can configure in the system. Reports and dashboards are provided to assist the AP team in managing supplier invoices in their various stages and statuses.

AP60.comments: Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments. This process manages the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. The business process framework within Workday is highly configurable and can use any values on the invoice to drive required approvals and documentation before an invoice can be released for payment.

BL01.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility. With Workday, auditing is always on.

BL02.comments: On a customer contract or individual invoice, you can define revenue schedules to automate revenue recognition and accounting: Create revenue recognition schedules to reflect correct timing and amount of revenue to recognize; Combine invoice lines in a revenue recognition schedule whenever they logically go together; Recognize revenue in regular intervals and/or tie different types of milestones to the overall schedule and/or specific installments; Calculate the revenue allocation for multi-element arrangement contracts using the relative selling price; Configure business processes on revenue schedules to ensure appropriate approvals before revenue recognition starts as well as when revenue is recognized; Recognize revenue based on: Number of days in the period; Spread even; Percentage of project completion; Defined installments with optional different first and last installment amounts; Custom installments. Workday also enables you to schedule the creation of customer invoice proposals.

BL05.comments: To support global requirements for sending invoices electronically, Workday permits the storing of electronic invoicing details on the customer profile to enable the easy exporting of e-invoicing data from Workday. Workday provides organizations the ability to quickly and easily connect to e-invoicing integration solutions using a generic connector. The integration of Workday with third-party e-invoice business solutions provides organizations with the opportunity to save significant labor costs and time.

BL06.comments: Supported. You can define the billing schedule as single or recurring with equal or custom amounts. You can also create a consolidated billing schedules across all customer contract lines.

BL07.comments: MAS provides ability to apply CPI escalation to payment schedules within leases. Different CPI indices can be configured and used for CPI escalation.

BL08.comments: Workday supports complex billing rules, including the ability to define fair value price using a multi-dimensional price list and use different fair values using transaction attributes. Workday will automatically match and populate the right fair values when you enter the contract line detail. Multi-element revenue re-allocation is available with the following basis: fair value, upper range, and lower range. You can also exclude sales items or sales item groups from multi-element revenue allocation, and specify which fair value price method you want to use.

BL09.comments: Supported. You can use financial amounts, statistics, uploaded external information, and headcount to define and report various metrics, ratios, and percentages in your financial statements. You can define multiple calculations in various rows / columns of your financial report allow for flexible ratio and calculation performance in financial statements to reflect KPI's or drivers relevant to financial statement consumers directly from the report itself.

BL10.comments: An integration can be created from the utility billing system to load the receivables into Workday.

BL12.comments: Supported. With Workday Contracts you can automate billing using templates for various types of products or services, including fixed amount. You can also define the billing schedule with single, recurring with equal or custom amounts, and milestone-driven schedules.

BL13.comments:
Supported. Workday calculates pricing based on usage and generates invoices for you, expanding your pricing options and enabling you to dynamically charge customers based on customer’s usage of certain services – such as number of users, number of downloads.

Workday supports the following types of pricing:

Fixed fee for initiation of contract Usage rate per unit of measure Minimum amount or recurring fee for a period of time Minimum usage count for a period of time Usage rate with cumulative tiers where each tier can be priced differently Usage reset by period or by transaction Different reset frequency (Annual, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-annual)

Usage based billing can be combined with Time & Expense billing on a project.

BL14.comments: Supported. With Workday Contracts you can automate billing using templates for various types of products or services, including Fixed amount, Variable amount where the contract amounts represent estimates, and Usage based billing. You can also define the billing schedule as Single, Recurring with equal or custom amounts, or Milestone-driven.

BL15.comments: Supported. Workday calculates pricing based on usage and generates invoices for you, expanding your pricing options and enabling you to dynamically charge customers based on customer’s usage of certain services – such as number of users, number of downloads. Workday supports usage rate with cumulative tiers where each tier can be priced differently.

BL16.comments: With Workday Contracts you can automate billing using templates for various types of products or services including Fixed amount, Prepaid amount, Recurring billing, Subscription, Usage based billing, Variable amount where the contract amounts represent estimates, Project fixed fee, Project Time & Expense, Value based project and Alternate contracts.

BL17.comments: Workday offers the ability to manage invoice templates with customizable text, PO fields, contact blocks, and generic sections by billing type.

BL18.comments: You can print an invoice with attachments using both Workday Docs for Layouts and BIRT to mail to a customer or the system can automatically create a PDF-formatted version and send it to the customer as an attachment via email.

BL19.comments: Workday supports sending customer invoices via email and hard copy. The system can automatically create a PDF and send it via email, or you can print invoices with attachments. Also, statements can be printed or sent via email in PDF format. Customers can also retrieve PDF statements from Workday's customer portal.

BL21.comments: Workday supports the ability to automatically associate expense report receipts from billable transactions on customer invoices, enabling users to be able to provide increased accountability and enhancing the ability to track billable expenses. Receipts from Billable Expenses (on Employee Expense reports) can be flagged to Print and/or Email with the Customer Invoice. Attachments can be added at the line level and will display as an expense line on the billable project transaction line. Attachments then can be added to the Invoice.

BL22.comments: Customer invoices can be created manually. Workday enables you to manage customer invoices in mass, rather than one at a time, when customer invoices are in dispute, collection, or on hold. You can specify a value for invoice attributes such as Collection Code, Dispute Reason, and Follow-up Date depending on the action being performed, and you can add the same note to multiple invoices at a time.

BL23.comments: Supported. Customer invoices can be created manually. Customer invoices are used to record a sales event and create the ensuing receivable accounting.

BL27.comments: Alerts/notifications are sent to team members as they are assigned, approach, and miss milestones.

BL28.comments: Using Workday's Business Process Framework, you can configure a business process with multiple steps such as approval, notification, to do, and checklist. Each step can be invoked based upon rules defined using attributes of the transaction.

BL29.comments: Workday provides users the ability to automatically calculate and create invoices for interest and/or late fees related to past-due customer invoices. These invoices are natively associated with the original invoice, reducing manual effort and saving time.

BL30.comments: Customers can pay their outstanding invoices through the customer portal using a credit card, with the use of a 3rd party payment provider.

BL31.comments: Will be handled in MAS using ArcGIS connector.

BL32.comments: Workday supports the import of external data from various sources. Workday also offers multiple ways to import journals from other systems, including direct uploads, direct integrations, and the Accounting Journal Connector. These capabilities enable the import of data from ship registries, vendor invoices, utility meters, maintenance tickets, and other external systems, facilitating comprehensive financial management and reporting.

BL33.comments: Can be accomplished with the Workday functionality and PRISM to ingest data and provide data in the needed format.

BL34.comments: Supported. With Workday, we enable you to trigger a custom integration to a third-party tax calculation service to calculate sales tax, instead of using the Workday Tax Applicability and Tax Code values. This can be enhanced via web services, as well and leverage your external service provider of choice.

BL35.comments: Workday's Ad Hoc Project Transaction feature supports creating billable transactions (time, expenses, and supplier invoices) from external sources, which can include data from an EAM system related to work orders or services. These transactions can then be processed within Workday like any other billable transaction, including running contract rate and billing rate rules, review and edit, and customer invoice generation.

BL36.comments: Workday can integrate employee and accounting data from external systems through a variety of methods, including: Data Integration, Application Programming Interface (API), File-Based Integration. Workday also provides pre-built integrations with many third-party systems.

BL37.comments: Leveraging Spend Category and WD reporting.

BL38.comments: Leveraging WD reporting and can be scheduled to send automatically to FAA.

BL39.comments: MAS provides ability to generate reports to classify and compare leases for profitability and rate consistency.

BG01.comments: This capability is fully supported by Workday Adaptive Planning, which provides a centralized, cloud-based environment for the creation, management, and versioning of all operating budgets. The application’s flexible modeling layer allows for easy configuration of budget input templates tailored to each department's specific needs, while the integrated workflow engine automates the entire review, revision, and multi-level approval process. This ensures that department managers can provide input quickly, finance maintains full control and governance over every submission and version, and that all planning is based on a single, continuously updated source of truth, eliminating the risk and administrative burden associated with coordinating numerous offline spreadsheets. Unlike tools that force a rigid, linear process, Workday Adaptive Planning provides the adaptability to manage both top-down targets and bottom-up detail within the same controlled framework.

BG02.comments: The platform's design inherently supports the creation and management of one-time or supplemental budgets without disrupting the integrity of the original operating plan. Workday Adaptive Planning uses the concept of "Versions" and "Models" to easily accommodate multiple concurrent planning cycles, allowing the Port of Portland to maintain a separate, distinct ledger for each fiscal adjustment, appropriation, or supplement. This capability ensures that the original budget remains locked as the baseline for comparison, while allowing budget managers to execute a structured workflow for the supplemental request, track changes, and report on all budget adjustments transparently and instantaneously against actuals. This flexibility is a significant differentiator over older systems that often require complex workarounds or manual consolidation to track these necessary, off-cycle adjustments.

BG03.comments: The core functionality of Workday Adaptive Planning is to provide a comprehensive, unified planning platform that fully accommodates budgeting across all financial statements: income (revenue), expenses, and liabilities (balance sheet). This is achieved through our sophisticated, multi-dimensional modeling engine, which allows the Port to seamlessly integrate operational drivers like airport traffic or seaport volume directly into revenue models, while simultaneously planning expenses down to a granular level. Unlike solutions focused only on P&L, our platform ensures a complete financial picture by integrating balance sheet components like capital expenditures, debt, and accrued liabilities into the overall forecast. This holistic approach means that every budget revision or forecast update is immediately reflected across all three financial statements, providing a cohesive and accurate view of future financial health.

BG04.comments: The platform’s specialized capital and project planning capabilities fully support the Port of Portland’s requirement to manage large-scale capital expenditures across the project lifecycle. Workday Adaptive Planning utilizes a unique "Modeled Sheet" framework to track assets, calculate depreciation, project cash outflow, and model the financial impact of capital assets directly onto the income statement and balance sheet. Furthermore, the system is designed to seamlessly integrate with your existing project management tools, ERPs, and general ledgers via a robust, open API and embedded integration framework, ensuring real-time visibility into funding allocation, spending commitments, and actual expenditures against the original budget. This crucial integration moves you beyond siloed data to provide a continuous, accurate view of every project’s financial health from initiation through completion, a distinct advantage over competitors with less flexible integration architecture.

BG05.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports comprehensive budget adjustments through multiple, flexible methods designed to maintain data integrity and an auditable record. The system allows users to seamlessly process in-year budget transfers and reallocations using governed workflows, ensuring all changes are tracked, approved, and immediately reflected across reports and forecasts. This capability is underpinned by the platform’s continuous planning architecture, which enables financial analysts to run real-time "what-if" scenarios for any adjustment—a crucial differentiator—and immediately update the active forecast without impacting the original approved budget version. Consequently, the Port gains the agility to adapt to revenue fluctuations or unexpected expenditures by performing continuous forecasting and controlled budget adjustments within a single, unified environment, eliminating the lag and error prone nature of spreadsheet-based adjustments.

BG06.comments:
Fully supported. Workday Adaptive Planning’s multi-dimensional structure makes it simple to assign and secure budgets and accounts directly to specific departments, programs, and other organizational units relevant to the Port of Portland. The system utilizes Levels to represent the organization's structure, allowing finance administrators to precisely control which department managers have ownership and access to plan for their designated budget and specific chart of accounts. 

This level of granular, role-based security ensures that department heads are empowered for self-service input and reporting, but only for the financial dimensions assigned to them, providing superior governance and eliminating the need for manual distribution and collection of data from separate files like many competing solutions require. This centralization and control ensure a single, accurate view of all departmental performance against its approved budget.

BG07.comments: Affirmative statements—Workday Adaptive Planning is built on a multi-dimensional architecture that easily accommodates the assignment of budgets to any operational segment, including a specific location, facility, or asset within the Port of Portland. The system’s configurable Dimensions feature allows you to define "Location" as a dimension alongside departments and accounts, enabling users to enter, track, and report on revenues and expenses with full granularity for specific areas like the airport, container terminal, or a particular development site. This dimensional flexibility is a critical differentiator, as it allows for budgeting and reporting beyond the rigid hierarchical structures of older ERP-centric tools, providing Port leadership with real-time profitability and performance analysis across all geographical or functional areas.

BG08.comments: Fully supported. The platform’s powerful multi-dimensional modeling engine is specifically designed to enable budget entry and monitoring at the most granular levels required, including the cost center, business unit, and project level simultaneously. These organizational and analytical distinctions are configured using Workday Adaptive Planning's core structures—Levels for your reporting hierarchy, such as the Business Unit, and custom Dimensions for deep slicing of data, such as Cost Center and Project. This architecture ensures budget owners for each cost center or project can input their detailed plans in templates tailored to their specific needs, while finance instantly rolls up and monitors performance across the entire business unit hierarchy. Unlike legacy systems that are limited to a single chart of accounts structure, our dimensional flexibility allows the Port to view and analyze its budget from any required perspective in real time.

BG09.comments: The platform’s design allows for granular control of budget visibility through a powerful, multi-layered security model that extends beyond simple organizational hierarchy. Workday Adaptive Planning uses role-based access control, allowing administrators to define access rights—whether for reading, full viewing, or editing—based on the user's assigned role within the Port. Critically, this security is enforced down to the level of configurable dimensions, meaning you can precisely restrict a user's view so they can only see or interact with the budget for the specific project, program, or department they are responsible for, even if the data sits in the same central model. This prevents unauthorized visibility into sensitive financial plans while empowering operational managers with the exact, real-time data they need, a significant governance and security advantage over systems that only secure data at the top-level entity.

BG10.comments: The platform’s cloud-native architecture fully supports concurrent, collaborative budget entry across multiple users and departments without the need for separate input tools. Workday Adaptive Planning provides a single, unified data model where budget contributors simultaneously access their assigned budget templates—called Sheets—to input data across business units and object accounts, which are defined as Levels and Accounts within the system. Unlike competitors that require manual consolidation from distributed files, all user submissions are instantly rolled up to the corporate plan in real time, ensuring immediate visibility of the collective impact of departmental inputs. This collaborative environment is governed by built-in workflows and role-based access control, promoting broad participation while maintaining data security and process control over the budget cycle.

BG11.comments: The platform’s multi-dimensional Elastic Hypercube Technology (Patented technology) is perfectly suited to enable granular classification and tagging of budget items for mission-critical tracking. You can easily configure unlimited Custom Dimensions—beyond standard accounts and departments—to represent Port-specific classifications, such as "Shared Prosperity Initiative," "Sustainability Goal," or "Strategic Alignment Score." By tagging every budget line item with these dimensions, you gain the ability to instantly filter, run custom reports, and create interactive dashboards that display financial performance directly against non-financial, mission-driven metrics, a capability that elevates decision-making beyond simple budget-to-actuals reporting. This flexible tagging ensures every dollar spent can be proven to align with the Port's overarching strategic objectives, a level of transparency and accountability far exceeding the capabilities of traditional planning tools.

BG12.comments: The platform's design facilitates a direct link between transactional and planning data, enabling you to tie vendor and contract line items to specific budget accounts for superior reconciliation. This is achieved by leveraging our robust, system-agnostic integration framework to pull detailed procurement data, including vendor IDs and contract dimensions, directly from your General Ledger or procurement system into Workday Adaptive Planning's single data model. Our reports and dashboards can then cross-reference this granular actual transaction data against the corresponding budgeted account, dramatically improving variance analysis and reconciliation, and allowing finance to drill down from a high-level budget line straight to the underlying vendor invoice or contract commitment, which is an essential capability for comprehensive accountability at the Port. This unified visibility eliminates the need for manual, error-prone matching in spreadsheets, saving significant time during the monthly close and reporting cycles.

BG13.comments: The core modeling capability of Workday Adaptive Planning is its ability to support and integrate multiple funds—such as General, Construction, and Aviation Revenue—by defining "Fund" as a crucial custom dimension. This dimensional architecture allows the Port of Portland to budget and report with the necessary segregation, tracking every revenue and expense transaction against its legally mandated funding source, a critical requirement for public sector fund accounting. This ensures compliance and transparency by providing real-time financial statements, like segregated P&Ls for the Aviation Revenue fund, while still instantly consolidating all funds into a holistic organizational view for executive decision-making, differentiating it from less adaptable systems that force a single, rigid general ledger structure. Furthermore, the platform facilitates complex allocation rules to accurately distribute indirect costs and headcounts across these distinct funds and programs.

BG14.comments: The platform is fully capable of calculating and presenting the specific allocated budget amount for any given account within the current period. This is accomplished through Workday Adaptive Planning’s powerful modeling and real-time calculation engine, which instantly processes and applies complex allocation rules. Users can define sophisticated, driver-based formulas—such as distributing shared service costs or corporate overhead based on square footage or headcount—across the various departments and funds. The system automatically determines the precise allocated budget amount for the selected time period, eliminating manual effort and ensuring that every stakeholder is viewing the correct, fully loaded budget for their area. This built-in financial intelligence guarantees high accuracy and provides immediate, governed visibility into the true cost center or project budget.

BG15.comments: The platform delivers this capability through its robust allocation rules engine, which allows the finance team to completely define and manage their own budget allocation codes and methodologies. Users can set up custom driver-based allocations that distribute costs from a source—such as a shared service department or a central fund—to a target set of dimensions, including departments or projects, using any user-defined metric like square footage, full-time equivalents, or transaction volume as the distribution key. These allocation codes are centrally managed, fully auditable, and instantly calculated in real time across all forecast versions, ensuring consistency and accuracy without manual intervention. This flexibility is a significant advantage over rigid, hard-coded allocation methods found in older enterprise resource planning systems, empowering the Port to accurately reflect its true operating costs.

BG16.comments: Solutiuon to be finalized during the design session.

BG17.comments: The platform's design allows for highly flexible and detailed budget allocations across various units, divisions, departments, or even specific projects within an organization like the Port of Portland. This capability is fully supported through Workday Adaptive Planning's powerful modeling engine, enabling both top-down and bottom-up allocation methodologies using drivers, formulas, or direct input. Organizations gain the ability to distribute budgets based on complex operational metrics, such as square footage, headcount, or specific revenue targets, ensuring accuracy and strategic alignment throughout the entire enterprise. Unlike many systems that rely on simple, static rules, Workday Adaptive Planning provides an auditable and dynamic mechanism for managing your most granular allocation requirements with ease and transparency.

BG18.comments: This capability is fully supported within Workday Adaptive Planning, leveraging its flexible dimensional model to define and manage distinct cost pools and organizational units such as departments or divisions. The platform allows users to design sophisticated allocation methodologies for overhead costs, including direct methods, step-down methods, or activity-based costing, using a variety of drivers like labor hours, asset usage, or revenue. Organizations gain the ability to track these allocations in granular detail, ensuring that full costs are accurately assigned to the responsible units for better profitability analysis and informed decision-making. Workday Adaptive Planning's strong audit trail and reporting features mean you can trace every overhead dollar from the source pool to its final destination, a key differentiator from less transparent legacy systems.

BG19.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning enables sophisticated and automated pro rata budget distribution based on a wide range of user-defined rules and operational drivers stored within the system. This platform capability allows organizations to efficiently spread budgets or forecasts across periods, dimensions, or departments using established criteria such as historical trends, headcount, or square footage, ensuring distributions are consistent and defensible. The system's centralized logic eliminates the need for manual calculations in spreadsheets, drastically reducing errors and saving significant time during the budgeting cycle. Unlike systems that require complex scripting, Workday Adaptive Planning provides an intuitive interface for setting up and managing these automated pro rata rules, delivering speed and accuracy to your financial planning process.

BG20.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully enables the distribution of total budget amounts proportionally across any combination of time periods, cost centers, projects, or other custom dimensions configured within your model. This powerful capability relies on user-defined distribution logic that can utilize configured percentages or various weighting factors, such as headcount, square footage, or revenue share, ensuring distributions reflect real-world operational drivers. The platform’s modeling engine handles these complex, multi-dimensional distributions automatically, eliminating the risk of manual errors and significantly speeding up the planning cycle. Unlike traditional systems that make such proportional spreading cumbersome, Workday Adaptive Planning provides a highly intuitive and flexible environment to manage your most detailed and varied allocation requirements with precision and ease.

BG21.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning is specifically designed to handle dynamic planning processes, fully supporting the automatic recalculation of distributed amounts whenever the total budget or the underlying driver data changes. This crucial capability ensures continuous data integrity across all distributed dimensions without manual intervention, saving significant time and reducing errors. Furthermore, the platform supports manual override functionality for distributed amounts where business judgment is required, and importantly, every manual adjustment is captured with a comprehensive, transparent audit trail. This combination of automated accuracy and auditable flexibility is a key differentiator, providing Port of Portland with both efficiency and the necessary control for regulatory compliance.

BG22.comments: The platform's sophisticated labor planning module fully supports the functionality required for inter-departmental transfers, specifically enabling time entry allocation using calculated burden rates. Workday Adaptive Planning allows organizations to define complex burden rate components, including overhead and fringe costs, which are automatically applied to time entries and allocated across the receiving departments or projects. This capability ensures that the true, fully burdened cost of labor is accurately and efficiently reflected in the financials of the correct consuming unit, eliminating the need for complex off-system calculations. Organizations gain the ability to analyze actual labor costs with precision, a significant advantage over competitors that often require extensive manual journal entries or workarounds for granular inter-departmental cost recognition.

BG23.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides highly intuitive and flexible budget worksheet and planning tools, which are core components of its unified financial planning platform. These dynamic "sheets" are much more than static spreadsheets; they offer a familiar, grid-like interface for data entry and review while being fully connected to the centralized dimensional model and calculation engine. Organizations gain the ability to customize these planning screens with specific drivers, formulas, and visual cues, empowering department managers with tools tailored to their unique planning needs while maintaining central control and data governance. Unlike systems that require data exports or separate modules for detailed input, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures all planning activities are conducted in a single, secure environment, dramatically improving collaboration and data accuracy.

BG24.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the generation of budgets utilizing historical data and various projection methodologies as a foundational capability of its integrated planning solution. The platform allows organizations to seamlessly pull actual historical data from source systems, which can then be used as a baseline for current and future period budgets and forecasts. This process is highly flexible, supporting sophisticated projection techniques like trend analysis, moving averages, seasonal adjustments, or specific custom calculations driven by operational metrics. This integration and automation save planners significant time over manual data extraction and manipulation, ensuring the budget development process is grounded in accurate historical performance and forward-looking, data-driven projections.

BG25.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the necessary flexibility for manual adjustments and the capture of annotations directly within the budget review and forecasting cycles. Planners can easily input targeted changes to system-generated data or forecasts, and critically, the platform automatically maintains a comprehensive audit trail of every modification, including the user, time, and reason for the change. Furthermore, the system allows users to attach rich text annotations and commentary directly to specific data cells or reports, enabling clear documentation of assumptions, risks, and strategic rationale behind the manual adjustments. This ability to combine automated calculations with auditable, context-rich manual overrides ensures that the planning process is both efficient and fully transparent for all stakeholders.

BG26.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning excels at enabling the capture of budget rationale, assumptions, and key decisions, treating this qualitative context as essential metadata directly linked to your financial data for complete traceability. The platform provides robust commentary and annotation features, allowing planners to document the strategic thinking and drivers behind specific budget figures or forecasts at the cell, line item, or report level. This crucial capability ensures that when data is reviewed, audited, or passed between cycles, the "why" is always preserved alongside the "what," significantly reducing questions and improving accountability across the organization. Unlike systems that rely on external documents or disconnected notes, Workday Adaptive Planning centralizes this narrative metadata, offering superior transparency and confidence in your financial results.

BG27.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides robust functionality to mandate budgets for selected accounts, which is a critical feature for establishing necessary spending controls before the overall budget is finalized. This is accomplished through configurable workflow settings and validation rules that prevent the budget submission or finalization process from completing until specific, designated accounts have been populated with a value. Unlike less flexible systems, this control is dimensional, allowing the Port of Portland to enforce input requirements at a granular level, such as for specific capital projects or regulatory accounts, ensuring compliance and strategic priorities are met consistently. This enforcement capability guarantees that all required strategic inputs are captured, dramatically reducing the risk of incomplete or non-compliant budget submissions.

BG28.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning’s sophisticated workflow and reporting capabilities fully support the submission of comprehensive budget packages, including the unique requirements of program budgeting where individual components need selective approval. The platform allows organizations to define multi-stage, multi-level review and approval processes that can route specific budget sections or programs to the appropriate approvers based on organizational hierarchy, value threshold, or program ownership. This ensures that budget components, such as specific capital projects or operational programs, can be approved independently while still being consolidated into the overarching budget package, providing both decentralized accountability and centralized control. Unlike simpler systems, Workday Adaptive Planning manages this complex component-based approval and sign-off digitally, ensuring a complete audit trail and accelerating the final budget adoption.

BG29.comments: Fully supported. Workday provides standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. The delivered reports provide the ability to generate and display budget to actual results both at a company / legal entity level, or by organizations such as cost center / region / division, etc.

BG30.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the pre-population of planning models and budget worksheets by seamlessly leveraging data from prior year actuals or previous budget cycles. This core capability allows organizations to quickly establish a credible baseline, enabling planners to focus immediately on value-added analysis and adjustments rather than time-consuming data entry and preparation. The platform allows for flexible data selection, giving users the control to pull specific versions or periods of historical data, which can then be automatically adjusted by a configurable growth factor or calculation rule. Unlike competitors that require complex data mapping or extracts, Workday Adaptive Planning's unified structure ensures this pre-population is a straightforward, accurate, and rapid step in initiating any new planning cycle.

BG32.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides industry-leading capabilities for rapidly creating and analyzing multiple what-if scenarios and rolling forecasts alongside the annual operating budget. You are able to create an unlimited number of scenarios. The platform utilizes version management to easily duplicate an existing budget or forecast, enabling planners to model different assumptions, such as changes in commodity prices, capital spending, or staffing levels, without impacting the official financial plan. Organizations gain the ability to instantly compare these scenarios and forecasts side-by-side using robust reporting tools, significantly enhancing the speed and quality of strategic decision-making in response to shifting business conditions. Unlike static planning tools, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures this modeling is always conducted on connected, centralized data, guaranteeing accuracy across every permutation.

BG33.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides highly advanced and flexible capabilities for scenario modeling, which are essential for both target-based budgeting and robust multi-year forecasting. The platform allows users to instantly create unlimited alternative versions of a budget or forecast, easily modifying key assumptions, drivers, or financial targets to model various outcomes like best-case, worst-case, or mandated target scenarios without impacting the official plan. This powerful versioning capability extends seamlessly to multi-year forecasting, enabling organizations to project financials years into the future and perform side-by-side analysis of different long-range strategic options. Unlike systems that make scenario creation complex and disconnected, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures all modeling is conducted on consistent, unified data, providing speed and confidence in strategic financial decision-making.

BG34.comments: MAS will be able to provide rent revenue forecast including CPI/FMV-adjustments from lease contracts

BG35.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning is specifically designed to facilitate dynamic mid-year budget revisions and rapid forecasting updates by seamlessly incorporating actual performance data and known variances. The platform's integrated architecture automatically pulls in real-time or near real-time actuals from your ERP, which can then be used as the starting point for immediate, data-driven revisions to the remaining budget periods or the rolling forecast. This enables organizations to quickly analyze actual-to-budget variances and adjust future projections based on the latest operational reality, all within the same unified environment. Unlike disparate systems, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures that any revision or forecast is immediately consolidated and visible, providing stakeholders with an always-current, accurate financial outlook.

BG36.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the creation of specific cash-flow-based budget versions by enabling users to easily exclude non-cash adjustments, such as those related to GASB 87 or GASB 96 lease accounting entries. The platform's flexible modeling engine allows for the definition of custom reports and versions that automatically back out depreciation, amortization, and other non-cash transactions, ensuring the focus remains strictly on the movement of cash. Organizations gain the ability to accurately project their true liquidity needs and debt service capacity, a critical requirement for public entities like the Port of Portland. This capability to easily isolate and report on cash flow is a key differentiator, providing far greater financial clarity than systems that only focus on the accrual-basis operating budget.

BG37.comments: Supported. After entering or uploading budget and/or forecast data, you can generate reports comparing budget/forecast to actual across comparable years or periods. When entering or uploading budget/forecast data, you have all the dimensions available. This supports multi-dimensional comparison of budget/forecast to actual amounts. Users can create their own custom dashboards and reports and through a robust security model share them with people in the organization.

BG38.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides sophisticated control mechanisms to review and manage budget tolerances and overrides, ensuring effective governance across the planning process. The platform allows organizations to define acceptable tolerance levels for budget changes, which automatically flag or require higher-level approval for any submission that exceeds the predetermined thresholds. Furthermore, while the system supports necessary manual overrides for distributed amounts or driver-based calculations, every instance of an override is systematically tracked with an unalterable audit trail, capturing the user, the change, and the rationale. This combination of automated tolerance enforcement and transparent override tracking is a significant differentiator, giving Port of Portland the necessary balance of planning flexibility and strict financial control.

BG39.comments:
Workday delivers standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. The delivered reports provide the ability to generate and display budget to actual results both at a company / legal entity level, or by organizations such as cost center / region / division, etc. Budget rates include hourly rates for cost and hourly or daily rates for revenue. As the project progresses, you can update your forecasts to pull in historical data, with actuals, in combination with future estimates. You can use this information to revise your project.


Furthermore, Workday Adaptive Planning provides highly robust and dynamic capabilities for comparing budgets, actuals, and forecasts directly within a single, integrated reporting and analysis environment. The platform’s unified data model allows organizations to instantly pull and juxtapose multiple versions, such as the initial budget, the latest rolling forecast, and month-to-date actual results, enabling immediate variance analysis without time-consuming data exports or manual reconciliations. Organizations gain the ability to drill down into the variances to understand their root causes and drive informed course correction, a significant advantage over tools that require separate consolidation steps for each data set. This continuous comparison and real-time visibility are key differentiators, supporting agility and accuracy in managing financial performance.

BG40.comments: Workday allows you to define your exact fiscal schedule. You can roll up your days into weeks, months, quarters, and years. These weeks can be tailored to meet specific organization and industry needs, e.g., 5/5/4. The configuration would enable you to run financial statements that cross fiscal years (e.g., rolling 12 months), year over year, quarter over quarter, midmonth, etc. Workday additionally supports the use of both standard and alternate fiscal schedules as appropriate allowing for the consolidation and associated reporting of a parent/subsidiary combination with different subsidiaries.

BG41.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides seamless automation for the import and refresh of actuals data, which is foundational for timely and accurate budget versus actual reporting and includes robust support for pre- and post-audit views. This is achieved through established and maintainable integrations with source general ledger and operational systems, ensuring actual data is consistently pulled into the Workday solution without manual intervention or data mapping complexity. The platform’s version control and audit log functionality allow organizations to maintain and report on distinct views of the data, capturing the original imported actuals and any subsequent, auditable adjustments (pre- and post-audit) within a single, unified environment. This continuous, automated data flow and clear versioning are critical differentiators, ensuring that the Port of Portland always has a current, transparent, and defensible comparison for performance management.

BG42.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports robust variance analysis workflows, incorporating configurable spot-check thresholds that are invaluable for reviews prior to an accounting close or formal review cycles. The platform's powerful workflow engine allows organizations to set specific variance tolerances based on dollar amounts, percentages, or dimensional intersections, automatically flagging and routing items that exceed these thresholds to the appropriate personnel for investigation and commentary. This capability allows finance teams to proactively focus their attention on the most material variances, ensuring issues are addressed and documented promptly before the final closing process, thereby accelerating the close and enhancing the integrity of the financial statements. This proactive, controlled approach to variance management is a significant differentiator, providing far better governance than relying on manual, post-close exception reports.

BG43.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the essential ability to reconcile differences between the adopted budget and subsequent forecasted budgets seamlessly for both audit and external reporting purposes. The platform achieves this through its robust version management capabilities, where the original adopted budget is locked as an official version, and all subsequent forecasts are maintained as separate, trackable versions. Organizations gain the ability to instantly generate reports that clearly show period-by-period variances between these two versions, including the drivers of change, all within a fully auditable environment that captures every modification and commentary. This unified approach eliminates the manual reconciliation pain points common in disparate systems, ensuring that Port of Portland can always trace the evolution of its financial plan with complete transparency and confidence for stakeholders and auditors.

BG44.comments: Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. You can configure alerts to send relevant business information to users based on most conditions for which you can create a report in Workday. The alerts are driven off the Workday reporting framework which can be run immediately or on schedule basis. The alerts can drive notifications to the Workday Inbox, to e-mail, or to a mobile device.

BG45.comments: Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. You can configure alerts to send relevant business information to users based on most conditions for which you can create a report in Workday. The alerts are driven off the Workday reporting framework which can be run immediately or on schedule basis.

BG46.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the use of Excel-based templates for budget imports and bulk data uploads, providing a familiar and efficient entry method for users, particularly during initial setup or for complex, detailed data sets. The platform offers a seamless mechanism to define and download standardized Excel templates that are directly mapped to the underlying dimensional model, ensuring data integrity upon upload. Organizations gain the ability to leverage their existing data gathering processes while eliminating the manual reconciliation and mapping errors common with other systems, as the intelligent import feature handles the translation from spreadsheet to structured model automatically. This flexibility to combine familiar tools with the power of the Workday engine is a key differentiator, accelerating data collection and simplifying the transition for new users.

BG47.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides comprehensive support for configurable data validation and a robust audit trail specifically for Excel-based imports, ensuring data quality and governance are maintained even when leveraging external tools. Before data is committed to the model, the platform runs configurable validation rules that check for data format compliance, correct dimensionality, and adherence to defined business rules, automatically alerting the user to errors that need correction in the Excel file. Critically, every successful import, along with the source file and details of the user and timestamp, is logged in a complete, unalterable audit trail, providing full traceability for all data inputs, including those uploaded in bulk. This dual capability of pre-import validation and detailed post-import auditing is a major differentiator, providing confidence that all financial data, regardless of entry method, is accurate and defensible.

BG48.comments: Using budget checking and department level trial balances or other custom WD reporting.

BG49.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning comprehensively supports the comparison of current budget proposals against performance targets and historical budget data, which is essential for comprehensive budget review packages. The platform's integrated modeling and reporting environment allows organizations to seamlessly incorporate and display multiple planning versions, such as the mandated strategic targets, the prior year's adopted budget, and the latest forecast, alongside the current submission. This capability enables reviewers to instantly assess the strategic alignment and efficiency of the proposed budget, making variance analysis against both historical trends and forward-looking goals straightforward and transparent. Unlike legacy tools that require complex data manipulation to achieve such comparisons, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures all these critical data points are readily accessible and consistently linked for efficient, data-driven decision-making.

BG50.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports collaborative review workflows and meticulous version tracking, which are central to its design as a modern, enterprise performance management solution. The platform allows organizations to define multi-step, sequential, or parallel review and approval workflows that automatically route budget or forecast submissions to the relevant stakeholders for input, commentary, and sign-off, eliminating the bottleneck of email-based reviews. Crucially, every step, data change, and approved milestone is captured through robust, granular version tracking, providing an unalterable audit trail that ensures data governance and transparency throughout the entire collaborative process. This seamless integration of workflow and version control accelerates decision-making while providing the confidence that all changes are documented and traceable.

BG51.comments: Fully supported. Workday provides the ability to track budget amendments, transfers, type of change, reason and with Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities, who requested the change as well as date and time stamp is always recorded.

BG52.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the creation and monitoring of comprehensive, multi-year capital budget plans, including the critical functionality to enforce financial caps over a five-year horizon. This is achieved through the platform's specialized capital planning module, which allows users to define projects, track their lifecycle, and assign specific spending limits or caps across fiscal years and dimensional categories. Organizations gain the ability to model the impact of different funding scenarios and timing of expenditures while the system proactively monitors adherence to these predefined multi-year constraints. Unlike systems that require manual tracking of long-range limits, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures capital plans remain aligned with strategic financial policy and within established appropriation boundaries automatically.

BG53.comments: Maintained in PMS and integrated with Workday budgeting module.

BG54.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the critical ability to accurately compare actual capital additions against the approved capital budget and automatically calculate variances, ensuring rigorous control over your fixed asset spending. The platform seamlessly integrates actual capital expenditure data from your asset management or general ledger systems, allowing for a real-time comparison against the specific project, asset, and period defined in the adopted capital budget version. Organizations gain the ability to immediately identify over- or under-spending, investigate the root causes of variances, and take corrective action, all within the same unified system. This automated, granular variance tracking capability provides superior financial governance and is a key differentiator when compared to systems that rely on manual, post-period reconciliations.

BG55.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the comprehensive ability to compare approved funds requests against both the adopted capital budget and the actual capital additions through its integrated planning and analysis platform. The platform's capital module allows for the detailed tracking of approved funding requests as a distinct version or data set, which can then be instantly juxtaposed with the official capital budget for commitment variance analysis. Furthermore, the seamless integration of actual expenditure data enables continuous monitoring to ensure that spending against these approved requests aligns with the physical asset additions, providing a full circle of control from initial request to final asset acquisition. This superior capability for multi-point comparison is a key differentiator, delivering complete financial transparency and governance over the entire capital expenditure lifecycle.

BG56.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning’s robust capital planning module fully supports the ability to enter and enforce capital spending caps and compare these constraints directly against actual expenditures at both the portfolio and division levels. This is achieved by leveraging the platform’s dimensional modeling to define and track spending limits by any criteria, such as a portfolio of projects or a specific division, ensuring granular control and accountability. Organizations gain the ability to monitor compliance with these hard limits in real time as actuals flow in from the general ledger, automatically calculating and reporting variances at the strategic level. This level of flexible, multi-dimensional capping and real-time comparison is a key differentiator, providing Port of Portland the necessary governance to manage long-term capital resources effectively and avoid overspending.

BG57.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning comprehensively supports the assignment of multiple funding sources, such as debt, grants, or operating cash, to individual capital projects and rigorously tracks spending against each specific source. This critical functionality is achieved by leveraging the platform’s powerful dimensional model, which allows funding sources to be configured as distinct elements linked directly to capital projects and their associated spending. Organizations gain the ability to accurately monitor and report on the consumption of specific funding pools in real time, ensuring compliance with grant restrictions or debt covenants throughout the project lifecycle. This superior ability to manage and reconcile complex, multi-source funding within the planning system is a key differentiator, providing complete visibility and control over restricted capital resources.

BG58.comments: Workday Integration tools has the capabilities to integrate with Masterworks.

BG59.comments: Yes, fully supported. The platform's design allows for comprehensive tracking and reporting on multi-year capital projects, providing full visibility from initial planning through execution and completion. Organizations gain the ability to configure dynamic, multi-year roll-up views that effortlessly combine actuals, budget, and rolling forecasts over any specified timeframe, such as a continuous five-year outlook. Unlike legacy systems that rely on static, annual budgets, Workday Adaptive Planning handles long-term capital forecasting with native time-intelligence features that ensure continuity and accuracy without complex workarounds or manual adjustments. This capability enables the Port of Portland to manage project lifecycles, understand cash flow requirements across multiple fiscal periods, and easily report on portfolio performance with configurable dashboards and reports tailored for capital steering committees.

BG60.comments: Workday supports managing project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets. Workday gives you greater visibility into project costs with project capitalization. A streamlined process lets you manage project costs from acquisition through to identification of assets within the project, and finally to capitalization of these assets.

BG61.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the creation of formal financial statements, including the balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow, all dynamically linked to the original budget, forecasts, and actuals. This capability is enabled by the system's unified data model, which ensures that all financial reporting is derived from a single source of truth, eliminating reconciliation issues between planning data and GL results. Unlike disconnected spreadsheets or disparate planning tools, Workday provides seamless drill-down from the summarized statement figures directly to the underlying budget detail, allowing users to instantly analyze variances and understand the drivers behind performance. This direct linkage ensures that the Port of Portland's financial reporting is not only accurate and compliant but also serves as a powerful analytical tool for budget owners and executive stakeholders.

BG62.comments: Fully supported. Workday Adaptive Planning enables the creation of highly interactive and real-time budgets through its collaborative web-based architecture, which immediately reflects changes across all connected reports, dashboards, and planning sheets. Unlike static, file-based tools where versions quickly become outdated, the platform's in-memory engine and single data model ensure that all users are planning on and viewing the single most current version of the budget simultaneously, fostering enterprise-wide alignment. Organizations benefit from a continuous planning environment where budget owners can input data, see the immediate impact on financial statements, and run what-if scenarios interactively without needing to wait for batch processing or manual consolidations. This real-time capability accelerates the budget cycle, drives more informed decision-making, and represents a significant differentiator from systems that rely on slow, periodic updates.

BG63.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides robust, native capabilities for consolidating budgets across all departments, legal entities, and reporting dimensions within the Port of Portland structure. The platform's unified, in-memory consolidation engine automatically rolls up data in real-time, supporting complex organizational hierarchies, multi-currency translations, and intercompany eliminations without the need for exporting to a separate GL or consolidation tool. Unlike competitor systems that require complex scripting or manual data blending, this seamless consolidation ensures that every user is always planning and reporting on the most current and accurate consolidated financial picture. This allows the finance team to accelerate the close and planning cycle, focusing their efforts on strategic analysis rather than data integration and validation.

BG64.comments: Workday enables you to provide rich business insight to operational management through the use of dashboards and Discovery Boards, as well as insight at the point of decision. Rely on security rules to ensure managers can only see into the teams, organizations, or activities into which they are allowed to have insight. Grant access to managers and decision makers to view or configure user-defined financial reports that impact their day-to-day jobs.

When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information, enabling a comparison of real-time actuals balances against approved budgets. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

Organizations benefit from scenario modeling that allows for instant analysis of how changes in one year's project schedule or funding will affect all subsequent years, supporting agile resource allocation and strategic decision-making. This unified, long-term perspective is a core differentiator, enabling the Port of Portland to maintain a constantly refreshed, rolling capital forecast far superior to the static, annual snapshots provided by legacy tools.

BG72.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the aggregation of project budgeted labor hours across multiple dimensions, including by specific project phase, discrete time period, and designated funding source. The platform's powerful modeling engine allows the Port of Portland to capture detailed labor inputs at the lowest level, such as role or individual, and then roll these hours up instantly to provide consolidated views necessary for management and reporting. This capability is enabled by the system's flexible dimensional structure, which ensures that budgeted hours are automatically allocated and aggregated based on the defined project work breakdown structure and associated funding dimensions. Unlike spreadsheet-based approaches, this integrated capability ensures accuracy in resource planning and provides real-time visibility into labor cost capitalization and expenditure tracking by source.

Project budget and forecast data is available to report against project actuals, in hours and dollars. BG75.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning incorporates intelligent planning and predictive analytics capabilities that proactively identify budget categories at risk of exceeding defined thresholds, providing a significant advantage over reactive reporting tools. Leveraging machine learning algorithms, the platform analyzes historical trends and current planning data to generate predictive forecasts, flagging potential variances and risks before they materialize, allowing the Port of Portland to take timely corrective action. Furthermore, the system includes native, configurable alerting functionality that automatically notifies key stakeholders via email or within the application when a designated risk threshold or variance percentage is breached. This ensures financial oversight is continuous, empowering budget managers with the foresight needed to maintain control and drive better financial outcomes.

BG77.comments: Supported. Workday Adaptive Planning offers robust capabilities for grant budgeting, accruals, and forecasting that are tightly integrated with both capital planning and external financial reporting requirements. The flexible modeling engine allows the Port of Portland to track grant funding as a distinct dimension, ensuring that all related revenues, expenditures, and accruals are correctly associated with the specific grant, project, and funding source in real-time. This integration ensures seamless compliance and simplified reporting for complex schedules like 425, 270, and 271, as the platform eliminates the need for manual data manipulation by drawing directly from the unified financial model. Unlike standalone grant management tools, Workday provides a consolidated view that bridges the gap between operational grant tracking, capital investment decisions, and statutory financial reporting.

BG78.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning effectively addresses the requirement for clear mapping between project costs and reimbursable amounts through its integrated dimensional modeling and flexible document attachment capabilities. By leveraging project-specific dimensions, the platform enables the Port of Portland to designate and track which project expenses are eligible for reimbursement, facilitating automated calculations and reporting on net cost versus gross expenditure. While Workday Adaptive Planning is a planning and analytics solution, its integration capabilities, particularly with Workday Financial Management or other core ERP systems, allow for the attachment and verification of supporting payment documentation directly to the planning and actuals data, ensuring a clear audit trail and compliance with grant or funding requirements. This integrated approach removes reliance on external file systems, ensuring that documentation is intrinsically linked to the financial data used for reimbursement claims.

CD22.comments: Workday delivers unique capabilities that allow customers the ability to create and define their own APIs. If only a subset of fields within Workday services are needed for a specific integration, then a custom API that contains only those needed fields can be built. This API can then be consumed by Workday or any third-party integration tool.

CD23.comments: Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Consolidated cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed. With Workday’s composite reports you can build a single, matrix-style report from multiple data sources, take advantage of spreadsheet-like capabilities to create reports that perform column, row, and cell calculations and control report formatting style.

CD24.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to assign cash flow categories on bank statement lines and external cash flows to use when generating cash positioning reports. This assists in the normalization of data across bank statement formats to support simplified reporting. These can also be used to identify the reason for cash movements both for automated and user entered journals to assist in the creation of direct and indirect cash flow reporting.

CD25.comments: Workday's Accounting Center allows transactional data from third-party systems including treasury systems to be ingested, enriched, and transformed to create automated accounting journals that are posted to the GL. Customers can manage and track transactions from source systems in batches from ingestion to summarized accounting.

CD26.comments: Workday provides electronic bank reconciliation to match bank statement lines to operational transactions. You can create matching rules and condition rules to associate with a bank account, and the system can automatically match, suggest a match, or create an ad hoc bank transaction. Workday also offers exception handling and audit trail reporting.

CD27.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility.

CD28.comments:
Workday provides configurable validation rules that create either a warning or an error message. An error must be corrected before the expense report can be submitted. A warning can be processed and the warning becomes part of the transaction. Warnings are viewable during the approval process, can be used to route expense reports, and are viewable in the reporting.

Each budget can be configured to control or not control spending activity. For example, further business process configuration options allow a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist.

CA02.comments: Workday captures and manages a broader set of data than traditional accounting systems to support an expanded view of business. Workday enables customers to combine various data sources such as actuals, budgets, external data from third-party systems, statistics, and headcount, into live multi-dimensional reports that users can format, drill down into, and act on all within Workday. Organizations can significantly streamline their chart of accounts for a faster, more accurate financial close. Workday can model multiple operating entities, organizations, or business units to easily complete intercompany transactions, eliminations, allocations, adjustments, journal line matching, and consolidated reporting.

CA04.comments: Workday allows you to define your exact fiscal schedule down to the day. You can roll up your days into weeks, months, quarters, and years. These weeks can be tailored to meet specific organization and industry needs, e.g., 5/5/4. The configuration would enable you to run financial statements that cross fiscal years (e.g., rolling 12 months), year over year, quarter over quarter, midmonth, etc. Workday additionally supports the use of both standard and alternate fiscal schedules as appropriate allowing for the consolidation and associated reporting of a parent/subsidiary combination with different subsidiaries.

CA05.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework manages all journal entry and edits, providing control over who can approve journals and manage posting. Workday's audit capabilities track who, when, and what was entered or changed on journals automatically.

CA08.comments: Supported. Workday allows organizations to define their organizational structure, including departments, divisions, business units, and other groupings of employees. This structure can be easily modified to reflect changes in the organization, such as mergers, acquisitions, or reorganizations.

CA09.comments: Workday uses worktags as business dimensions. Inspired by the Web 2.0 concept of tagging, a worktag is a label assigned (i.e., tagged) to a business event, thus describing the event and enabling multi-dimensional classification, search, aggregation, reporting, and analysis of business information. You can configure your own custom worktags such as campaign or channel. In addition, any custom organizations you configure, such as line of business, product line, or brand, are also available as worktags.

CA10.comments: Workday provides the ability to post in detail and in real time directly from operational areas like customer invoice entry, supplier invoice entry, expenses, etc. to the General Ledger. In addition, all Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level. Unlike other cloud based financial software packages, this action does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

CA11.comments: Workday captures transactions as business events and uses an accounting engine to create accounting entries based upon user-defined rules. Users can define multiple sets of Accounting Rules for business events such as Business Assets, Revenue Recognition, and Lease Accounting to generate Accounting entries according to different accounting standards such as US GAAP, IFRS, Regulatory, Local GAAP, and Cash. All these accounting entries would be available for reporting.
