The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and will alert the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensures the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA31.comments: Workday supports multi-level or dependent allocations. The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and alerts the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensuring the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA33.comments: Workday can allow you to find the data for Work Orders but does not provide functionality of Work Orders.

CA34.comments: Workday supports a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules. We enable you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required. A single allocation definition can be used to group multiple worktag types allowing allocations to be performed by worktags.

CA35.comments: Workday enables you to define allocations and organize them by how often and in what order they should run. We support a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules.

CA36.comments: Workday gives you greater visibility into project costs with project capitalization. A streamlined process lets you manage project costs from acquisition through to identification of assets within the project, and finally to capitalization of these assets. Workday also automates the WIP/CIP and other accounting entries along the way. This enables you to identify your project costs and delay recognition of these expenses by recording them as long-term assets.

CA38.comments: Workday lets you run allocations in a “draft” status to view the financial impact through your reports WITHOUT having to post/un-post the transactions. You can also run pro-forma allocations at any time to provide visibility into fully loaded numbers on demand.

CA39.comments: Supported. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. Users can run a receivables aging by either operational aging (ages by transaction date) or financial aging (ages by accounting date). Additionally, users can have the aging basis be by either due date or invoice date.

CA40.comments:
Workday provides the ability to add unlimited notes and attachments to a customer’s record. We also break up the security capability to add a note (versus add and/or update the customer record) and another permission to edit any notes entered.

When you view a customer invoice, you have visibility into all hold, collection, and dispute information.

CA41.comments: Workday provides Dunning Letter capabilities to assist organizations with late customer invoice management by providing auto-generated reminder letters, which can be emailed. Workday provides users the ability to automatically calculate and create invoices for interest and/or late fees related to past-due customer invoices. The delivered customer statement can be run in two formats. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories.

CA42.comments: While the provided text does not explicitly mention security deposits, Workday's robust functionality around customer payments, cash application, and receivables control accounts suggests that it can be configured to track customer security deposits. The ability to record deposits, apply cash across ledgers, and manage receivables control accounts provides a foundation for tracking security deposits. Furthermore, the system's document ID generation capabilities can be used to create unique identifiers for each security deposit transaction.

CA43.comments: Workday supports the following payment methods: cash, check, direct debit, EFT, wire, and credit/debit card. Workday also provides a Customer Payment Lockbox integration to import Bank Administration Institute (BAI) lockbox data into Workday from an external endpoint.

CA44.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. You can also offset multiple invoices and adjustments that aren't tied in a single transaction by recording zero-amount customer payments.

CA45.comments: Yes - Extend & integration with customer provided web ecommerce platform

CA46.comments: Workday does not provide the POS system but can certainly bring the data in.

CA47.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. Workday even delivers a specific business process to govern and audit assets transferred.

CA49.comments:
Supported. You can manage project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets.

Project assets are buckets that are specific to each capital project, which capture separate, ongoing costs of a capital project that is in progress. You can associate multiple projects assets with a project to track cost over the life of a project. When all costs are in from transactions tagged to the capital project, you can convert each project asset to a business asset to realize cost and start depreciation. Each asset may have a different depreciation schedule.

CA50.comments:
Workday enables you to correct the useful life of an asset if the asset is capitalized with an incorrect life or there's a change in its estimated useful life. The transaction effective date when you correct useful life is always the acquisition date. The transaction effective date to change the useful life of an asset can be any date during the life of an asset.

The Asset Useful Life Update Event business process routes the transaction for review and approval. Workday also creates depreciation adjustment transactions for applicable useful life changes when ledger periods have processed depreciation and depreciation expenses should be a different amount.

CA51.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. Workday enables you to apply large volumes of payments automatically against multiple invoices based on remittance information via a system of payment application rules. You can configure customer payment rule sets and link them to active bank accounts, and then schedule payment jobs for eligible bank accounts with unreconciled bank statement lines. Workday supports the ability to configure invoice prefixes, customer mapping tables, and multiple bank accounts, among others, to populate associated instances. This automates the process of recording customer payments with remittance advice and customer deposits from the bank statement lines.

CA52.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Reconciliation rules are configured to tell Workday how to reconcile the items using condition rules. A condition rule consists of one or more logical statements used to check for matching entries using logical comparison operators AND/OR. Condition rules are then specified in reconciliation rules, which specify the reconciliation reason and the action to take. The action could be to automatically match, suggest a match or create an ad hoc bank transaction for a first notice item (e.g., bank fees). This reduces manual effort as you only have to take care of the exception not covered by the reconciliation rules which provides more flexibility, transparency, and accuracy.

CA53.comments: Supported. With Workday, you can automatically import electronic bank statements in order to reconcile bank statement lines with system transactions for multiple bank accounts. Delivered integrations support both the BAI2 and ISO 20022 statement formats. If the bank does not support BAI2 or ISO 20022, you can build a custom integration to import the statement data. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Additionally, exception reporting is available for unmatched or out-of-tolerance transactions.

CA54.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday also provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. Workday delivers a specific business process to govern and audit assets transferred.

CA55.comments: Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Accounting "workpaper" reports such as trial balance, ledger detail, account analysis, and reconciliations provide analysis and control. Consolidated profit and loss (P&L), balance sheet, and cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed.

CA56.comments: Workday supports automated receipt processing across various channels, including ACH, credit card, wire, and lockbox. Our system provides the ability to match payments to open invoices using configurable rules such as amount, reference number, and customer ID. For unmatched items, Workday offers exception-handling workflows to ensure proper resolution.

CA57.comments: Workday supports manual entry of invoices, cash receipts, and adjustments. For customer invoices, you can create them manually, load them directly into Workday, copy existing invoices, or create them from customer invoice proposals. Supplier invoices can also be created by manual entry or by copying existing invoices. Journals can be created by manual entry or copying an existing journal.

CA58.comments:
Supported. Workday allows you to create a template for a recurring journal with a start and end date and generate new journals using this template for a user-specified timeframe. You may also specify a reversal date on recurring journals. Please note that with this template, once it has been populated the recurring journals will automatically get recorded in the appropriate timeframe that are specified in the template. Different from other solutions that recommend using a spreadsheet and uploading recurring journals each period, in Workday users do not need to remember to upload the journals each period as journal generation will happen automatically.

Custom validations can also be added to recurring journal entries to ensure that all requirements for data input are met prior to posting.

CA59.comments: Receipts can be processed efficiently for goods, services, project-based services and contingent worker time sheets on purchase order and supplier contract lines. Workday supports both a decentralized and centralized receiving process on both the desktop and mobile application. New accounts can be loaded into the system via a spreadsheet if the volume warrants it. The Ad Hoc Project Transaction feature allows users to create billable (time, expenses, and supplier invoices) transactions from external source.

CA63.comments: To minimize the movement of data, latency, and reconciliation that goes with it, Workday's technology includes a reporting and analytics platform in the same environment you consolidate, transact, and reconcile. Unlike point solutions, our approach allows you to load, transform and manage source data directly in the Reconciliation and Consolidation environment. In Workday, this provides you seamless access to financial details at the level of granularity defined by what YOU need, without maintaining drill paths and security between multiple third-party environments.

CA64.comments: Workday can integrate very well with other systems as long as the other system can be integrated with.

CA65.comments: Workday provides dynamic and flexible integration capabilities that enable customers to extend the functionality of the platform. With open APIs, web services and delivered integration templates, Workday facilitates sharing information with systems that are relevant for maintaining and managing your projects. An integration with MasterWorks, a third-party system, can be configured to meet this requirement.

CA67.comments: Workday is a very open system for integrations. Integrations are typically supported as long as the external system can be integrated with.

CA68.comments: An integration would need to be configured to bring costs to projects for additional capitalization.

CA69.comments: Workday supports integration for organizations and accounts that provide multiple benefits, including automatic creation of bank transactions in Cash Management for bank reconciliation purposes and automatic creation of related General Ledger journals.

CA70.comments: Workday allows you to cost project time directly from payroll, where payroll is allocated across projects charged in the timesheet. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data where you choose which compensation components to include in the cost rate and you choose what burdening percentage (if any) to uplift the rate by. Burdening percentage is defined by the same project rate rules.

CA71.comments: Workday Integration tools and architecture enables us to accomplish these integrations.

GM01.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities ensures all history is tracked in the system. This includes grant records across fiscal years, enabling backward-looking audits and compliance lookbacks.

GM06.comments: Workday allows you to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. You can also reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds.

GM07.comments: Workday supports defining multiple fiscal schedules, allowing you to configure each grant’s fiscal year with specific start and end dates. This enables budget tracking aligned to the grant’s fiscal year, independent of the Port's fiscal year.

Stakeholders will get visibility into next steps, milestones yet to be completed, when they were completed and will be notified along the way. Everything within Workday can be tracked, captured and reported on in our reporting part of the solution, leveraging graphs, charts and dashboards which is all available in real time.

GM11.comments: Workday provides a mechanism to allocate funding from the top-level portfolio to the children projects and project portfolios. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds.

GM15.comments: Workday provides three ways to cost project labor: standard cost rates, burdened cost rates, and direct payroll. Standard cost rate sheets allow you to cost labor according to three methods: named worker (most specific), project role (least specific), and project rate rule. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data. Workday allows you to cost project time directly from payroll, where payroll is allocated across projects charged in the timesheet.

GM16.comments: Workday supports automated accruals for Purchase Order lines and Supplier Contract receipt lines that have not been invoiced. With receipt accruals, you can easily accrue all supplier contract or purchase order lines that aren't fully invoiced giving you better insight into the goods and services your organization has received, but that have not yet been invoiced. Accrual entries can be set to automatically reverse in the next accounting period. Receipts that have not been invoiced are fully reportable.

GM17.comments: Workday provides multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. With Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities, all changes are tracked with date and time stamps.

GM18.comments: Workday provides the ability to tag reports. You can apply tags to individual reports and to report groups. When you generate an individual report or report group, Workday applies the output tag to each individual report. Tagging reports enables you to easily filter and access reports. You can have tags that are ‘fixed’ (i.e., user defined) and tags that are ‘variable’ (i.e., ‘period’ would tag the report with the specific period you are running the report for), giving you flexibility to configure them to your specific business needs. Worktags are configurable. Each customer can configure up to 15 custom worktags. Additionally, Workday delivers over 100 worktags out of the box. For each of the delivered worktags, Workday presets where you can use it; therefore, you do not have to sort through inapplicable worktags when entering data. You can configure how to use related worktags so that the related worktag types available make sense for your business.

GM22.comments: Workday provides three ways to cost project labor: standard cost rates, burdened cost rates, and direct payroll. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data and you choose which compensation components to include in the cost rate and you choose what burdening percentage (if any) to uplift the rate by. Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements.

GM23.comments: Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source.

GM24.comments: Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. GM25.comments: Workday provides the ability to define validation rules to ensure compliance with expense reporting policies. Validation rules can result in a warning or they can prevent the user from submitting the expense report until the policy violation is corrected. Expense reports that contain validation warnings/errors are flagged so you can monitor policy non-compliance and optionally route for additional review and audit.

GM28.comments: Workday provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created. Lease accounting in Workday automatically generates lease transactions from supplier contracts. You can then create financial reporting, which can help with compliance.

GM29.comments: Workday provides enhanced commitment accounting on procurement card transactions by automatically liquidating encumbrances on purchase orders and contracts associated with procurement cards. This increases the accuracy of spend obligation balances and reduces manual processing. Your organization can configure budget check to run at any point, or at multiple times, in the procurement card transaction verification business process.

GM30.comments: Workday provides standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. Reporting in Workday is based on real-time reporting on transactions. When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information, enabling a comparison of real-time actuals balances against approved budgets. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

GM31.comments: Workday provides various ledgers to accommodate pre-encumbrances, encumbrances, and actuals.

GM33.comments: Opportunity Projects enable you to start planning for projects before they are sold. Opportunity Projects allow you to plan for future staffing, generate financial forecasts, and convert them later to sold standard projects. Workday allows you to distinguish between opportunity projects and sold projects for ease of prioritizing resources and understanding project pipeline.

GM38.comments:
Workday's Business Process Framework supports any number of approval levels for a given transaction or activity. Approval and review steps can be configured based upon your organization's business needs and roles such as Cost Center Manager, Grant Manager, Manager, Management Chain as well as numerous other roles.

Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required.

GM39.comments: Alerts/notifications are sent to team members as they are assigned, approach, and miss milestones. Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. You can configure alerts to send relevant business information to users based on most conditions for which you can create a report in Workday. The alerts can drive notifications to the Workday Inbox, to e-mail, or to a mobile device.

GM40.comments: Workday provides a Message Centre facility to allow Sourcing Managers to send messages to suppliers, suppliers to submit questions to the sourcing team, and sourcing managers to respond to supplier questions. Throughout the lifecycle of the Sourcing project, a Team Chat facility is provided to allow internal stakeholders and the procurement team to communicate and collaborate, with all messages retained against the project for audit purposes. During active sourcing events, a live chat facility is provided to allow sourcing managers to communicate quickly with suppliers. During RFx events and other projects, sourcing managers can define project milestones and assign them to stakeholders so everyone knows what they are expected to do, by when, and the completion status can be tracked.

GM41.comments: Workday provides configurable validation rules that create either a warning or an error message when charging to the wrong project code. Validation rules can result in a warning, or they can prevent the user from submitting the expense report until the policy violation is corrected. Expense reports that contain validation warnings/errors are flagged so you can monitor policy non-compliance and optionally route for additional review and audit. Workday provides the ability to track project time and expense.

GM42.comments: Workday provides dynamic and flexible integration capabilities that enable customers to extend the functionality of the platform. APIs are also provided to integrate with external Project Management solutions

GM44.comments: Workday provides data integration capabilities that allow organizations to integrate data from external systems. This can be accomplished through standard integrations or custom integrations, using tools such as Workday Studio and Workday Cloud Connect.

GM46.comments:
Workday delivers standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. The delivered reports provide the ability to generate and display budget to actual results both at a company / legal entity level, or by organizations such as cost center / region / division, etc. Additional reports can also be configured where desired. Existing results can be copied and leveraged as a starting point for new reports.

Reporting in Workday is based on real-time reporting on transactions. When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information, enabling a comparison of real-time actuals balances against approved budgets. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

GM47.comments: Workday's reporting framework provides the ability to natively break out source transactions by dimension, amounts, dates, type, involved persons and processes, etc. Flexible reporting prompting settings and drilldowns allow for flexible querying of activity and transactions to answer relevant questions relative to spending or spend attributes such as those above.

GM48.comments: Workday's reporting framework provides the ability to natively break out source transactions by dimension, amounts, dates, type, involved persons and processes, etc. Flexible reporting prompting settings and drilldowns allow for flexible querying of activity and transactions to answer relevant questions relative to spending or spend attributes such as those above. Workday delivers dashboards and Discovery Boards around supplier accounts and procurement as well as standard reports that cover everything from requisitions to requests for quotes, purchase orders, invoicing, and payments that cover the full scope of spending activity.

GM50.comments: Workday provides real-time reporting on transactions. When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

GM52.comments: After a worker has submitted an expense report and it has been approved (or one has been submitted and approved on the worker's behalf), Expense Disbursement Specialists can pay the expense reports via the centralized settlements infrastructure and can run the settlements on a frequency of your organization's choosing. Workers receive payment for their expense reports based on their payment elections.

GM56.comments: Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments. This process manages the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. The business process framework within Workday is highly configurable and can use any values on the invoice or invoice line to drive required approvals and documentation before an invoice can be released for payment.

CP01.comments: Contracts in Workday can have start and end dates, whatever the duration of the contract. They can be amended at any point to extend the life of the contract. They can also have multiple lines for different goods or services. Receipts can be processed efficiently for goods, services, project-based services and contingent worker time sheets on purchase order and supplier contract lines. Workday supports both a decentralized and centralized receiving process on both the desktop and mobile application. Workday Strategic Sourcing workflow management capabilities helps organizations seamlessly move through the various stages of the Source to Contract workflow.

CP03.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework is flexible and allows for configuration based on various conditions. This enables you to configure internal policies and procedures for approvals, including those related to federal funding.

CP05.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework supports any number of approval levels for a given transaction or activity. Approval and review steps can be configured based upon your organization's business needs and roles such as Cost Center Manager, Grant Manager, Manager, Management Chain as well as numerous other roles. Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required.

CP06.comments: Workday Strategic Sourcing workflow management capabilities helps organizations seamlessly move through the various stages of the Source to Contract workflow. Also, with Workday, you can create custom fields for suppliers, purchase orders, and supplier contracts to meet your organization's specific needs. By leveraging our tools feature for creating custom objects, you can define custom fields and custom prompt lists.

CP07.comments: Workday supports robust detailed item level descriptions. Workday provides the ability to allocate the cost of each line item on a business document to multiple accounting distribution lines through our line split details. Splits from requisitions carry over to the purchase order line-level, and during purchase order creation, Workday provides the ability to add, remove, or modify line-level splits.

CP11.comments: Workday Strategic Sourcing provides a number of mechanisms designed to facilitate internal and external collaboration around sourcing events and other procurement activities, including a Message Center, Team Chat, Supplier Chat and Milestones.

CP13.comments: When publishing your RFP you will have the option to build out your evaluation, you can add sections, scoring questions, long answer questions, short answer questions, multiple choice questions. Once the RFP has been returned your sourcing team can score while looking at supplier responses, ie, looking at pricing and financials while scoring on the same page through a side bar. Once scoring has been completed the system will calculate a percentage across all suppliers aggregated the opinion of all your stakeholders to show you the results from your scoring side by side to ultimately make your decision.

CP18.comments:
Workday enables you to create commodity codes that group similar types of goods and services, and associate spend categories as well as purchase and catalog items to commodity codes. This helps to ensure data integrity and improve search for items based on standard commodity code structures.

Workday Strategic Sourcing enables you to create request for quote (RFx) business documents so that suppliers can bid on procurement goods and services. An email is sent to suppliers inviting them to participate in your RFx event. We also have public bidding options which allow for a link to be discovered by any prospective supplier and proactively sign up for Workday Strategic Sourcing.

CP19.comments: You will have access to see when the supplier accessed the bid, submitted the bid and/or revised the bid.

CP20.comments: The Workday Supplier Portal allows suppliers to review and maintain contact details and supplier classifications. You can create commodity codes that group similar types of goods and services. You can also define your own structure or use an industry-based standard taxonomy, such as the National Institute of Governmental Purchasing Commodity Services Code (NIGP).

CP24.comments: RFx can be edited, canceled, and closed with some restrictions at each stage.

CP26.comments: The Workday Strategic Sourcing application allows Customers to grant users access to their projects and contracts in pre-defined security policies by assigning them to various security groups. Security Groups can grant either full or read-only access to bids and  contracts.

CP29.comments: While Workday does not have embedded pre-conference functionality, you can track this data as part of the project (internal tracking) that is associated with the bid.

CP58.comments:
Change orders can be processed against a purchase order at any point during the purchase order life cycle, up until the purchase has been marked as Closed. From a purchase order, the user can select the related action of Create Change Order to enter the desired changes and submit the change order for processing. Once submitted, change requests are routed to an internal user for review and approval before being implemented. Additionally, Customers have access to an audit log in the form of changes for projects and contracts. There also is a Supplier Profile history log.

Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required, such as exceeding a threshold.

CP99.comments: Workday gives you control to specify which goods/services are presented and thus available to users during requisitioning. You can grant access to goods and services based on criteria related to the worker as well to the worker's location to ensure that requesters are presented with options that are relevant to their job and their location when creating requisitions.

CP100.comments: Workday enables users to search for purchase information using PO number, requisition number, department name, and other user-defined fields (Worktags). Worktags are custom dimensions that can be defined to meet specific reporting and tracking needs, offering flexible criteria for information retrieval.

CP125.comments: Attachments may be added to requisitions. The maximum attachment size is 30720 KB (30 MB), and the maximum image size is 10240 KB (10 MB). The organization's Workday tenant can be configured to either allow all file attachment types or allow ONLY specific file attachment types (e.g., allow only .PDF, JPG, etc.).

CP138.comments: Yes, however Workday does offer own sourcing module called Strategic Sourcing.

CP142.comments: Yes, we offer a public API for resources within Workday Strategic Sourcing that would allow for integration with 3rd party systems. The API supports both import and export. Please see https://apidocs.workdayspend.com/index.html for further information about our API.

AM01.comments: Maximo Application Suite (MAS) provides ability a configurable centralized data dictionary that can be used to add, modify different field types (list, boolen, numeric, boolean etc). MAS also provides ability to configure asset naming conventions across sites and divisions to ensure uniformity and consistency in asset names.

AM02.comments: MAS provides ability to create industry classifications like uniformat level 5 or CSI , OSCRE, OMNI/Uniclass. These classifications can be linked to assets to identify classify and repair deficiencies

AM03.comments: MAS's asset classification fully supports cost estimating, reporting, and grouping across building, site, linear, and other configurable assets. Classifications provide a structured way to categorize and define asset attributes, which can then be used to manage costs, analyze performance, and create customized reports.

AM04.comments: MAS provides all the key attributes like asset type, number, ownership status, operational status and others for tracking status out of box. Additional fields specific to customer's requirements can be easily added through data dictionary.

AM05.comments: MAS provides flexibility to add, modify or delete classification values based on customer's requirement only by system administrators.

AM06.comments: MAS provides ability of hierarchical grouping of assets and sub-assets including linear assets to nth sub-asset level. It is recommended to keep the hierarchy levels from 9 to 12 sublevels.

AM07.comments: MAS provides ability to manage hierarchies for unlimited number of properties, buildings and assets.

AM08.comments: MAS linear asset application allows managing linear assets like roads, pipelines, and railways. This feature allows organizations to record defects, perform work, and take meter readings at specific points or segments along a linear asset, integrating with other Maximo applications and offering capabilities like dynamic segmentation and potential GIS integration.

AM09.comments: MAS linear asset application allows physical features or association of other assets/components on different points of linear assets.

AM10.comments: MAS provides ability to provide comprehensive set of asset data including but not limited to location, manufacturer, install date, asset class, useful life and others. Additional fields can be added to asset through centralized data dictionary. All these fields can be used to perform mathematical, date and string calculations using the functions provided within Maximo.

AM11.comments: MAS provides the ability to store installation year, estimate useful life and calculate recommended replacement year.

AM12.comments: MAS provides ability to track all subcomponents of complex assets.

AM13.comments: MAS Doclinks capability provides ability to store associated documentation, photos, O&M Manuals, warranty documentation, procedures, safety protocols and other information related to assets.

AM14.comments: MAS  provides ability to tag different identifiers to asset records to enable asset tracking and reporting across multiple levels

AM15.comments: MAS provides ability to maintain asset and location hierarchies with parent-child relationship and inheritance. These hierarchies are used for cost roll up.

AM16.comments: MAS provides ability to define user defined fields within data dictionary.

AM17.comments: MAS provides ability to create field types of list types with users able to define drop-down lists. MAS also provides ability to define dynamic list fields wherein list values in the secondary field are driven by the selection of a value in the primary field.

AM18.comments: MAS's Facility Condition Assessment (FCA) capability supports the ASTM E2018-24 standards for Property Condition Assessments (PCAs) by providing a framework to collect and analyze facility assessment data, including the use of a Facility Condition Index (FCI).

AM19.comments: MAS FCA module provides ability to capture FCI for properties and building systems as per the industry standards, and has ability to calculate replacement cost based on cost estimate data  like RS means.

AM20.comments: MAS FCA module provides ability to adjust system calculate FCI and SCI values to be adjusted with override reason.

AM21.comments: MAS provides ability to updated FCI values at both building and building component levels.

AM22.comments: MAS provides ability to send out notifications and trigger automated approval workflows for asset lifecycle changes using configurable approval engine.

AM23.comments:
MAS enables exception-based workflows, automatically flagging asset records that fail validation or business rules, so users can efficiently review and resolve issues.
This streamlines oversight by focusing user attention on records needing action, helping maintain asset data integrity.

AM24.comments: MAS Health safety and environment (HSE) module has built-in features that directly support Part 139 and SMS requirements, with specific configurations recommended for airport operators. The Regulatory Compliance application allows tracking specific external and internal regulations, associate them with assets and work procedures, and manage certification details. MAS facilitates proactive hazard and risk management by linking work orders to risk assessments, incident management, and investigations. This is critical for the hazard identification and risk management components of an SMS. The system provides robust documentation and audit capabilities, enabling Port to stay "audit-ready" and demonstrate compliance to regulatory bodies. It provides an auditable history of maintenance activities, approvals, and regulatory requirements.

AM25.comments: Maximo facilitates outage coordination by using flags, automated notifications, and integrated cross-department scheduling, ensuring all stakeholders are informed and activities are synchronized. This enhances communication and efficiency during planned or unplanned asset outages.

AM26.comments: MAS empowers users to perform multiple inspections at various points on a single asset or infrastructure. The platform streamlines scheduling, recording, and managing these inspections, ensuring comprehensive coverage and accurate documentation of all critical areas. This capability enhances visibility into asset conditions and supports timely maintenance interventions.

AM27.comments: MAS supports interim asset handover during phased rollouts or partial construction completions by enabling documentation and tracking of asset transfers between stakeholders. This ensures transparent and efficient management throughout the project lifecycle.

AM28.comments: MAS enables users to perform mass edits and efficiently manage multiple records at once, streamlining bulk updates and administrative tasks across assets, work orders, and other key records.

AM29.comments: MAS integrates with ArcGIS Enterprise/Online, supporting Web Maps, Feature Services, and Dashboards for advanced location-aware analytics using EAM, BIM, IoT, and external data. The platform enables joins to non-spatial tables and supports scheduled data refresh for up-to-date insights.

AM30.comments: MAS supports ArcGIS identity integration, enabling users to access EAM features using their existing ArcGIS login credentials for seamless authentication and user management.

AM31.comments: MAS delivers robust integration with ArcGIS Feature Services (hosted or federated), supporting attachments, related tables, editor tracking, versioning/branching, and optional interoperability with OGC API – Features/WMTS for enhanced data sharing and management.

AM32.comments: MAS maintains compatibility with current and N-1 ArcGIS Enterprise versions, publishes a clear version support policy, and offers a pre-production validation window prior to Port upgrades to ensure smooth transitions.

AM33.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM34.comments: MAS supports GIS-centric applications and workflows, embedding GIS data and capabilities across asset management, work orders, inspections, and remote monitoring to enhance spatial decision-making.

AM35.comments: MAS supports import and export for Esri GDB, GeoJSON, FileGDB, FlatGeobuf, CSV, and legacy Shapefile formats, preserving attachments and relationships where supported, and exposes schema export for automation workflows.

AM36.comments: MAS leverages ArcGIS Utility Network, Network Analyst, GeoEvent/Stream layers, and Indoors to enrich asset workflows—enabling integration of condition/risk layers, routes, alerts, and indoor positioning, all configurable without vendor custom code.

AM37.comments: MAS enables users and mobile crews to create, assign, and update work orders directly from any GIS view, linked to the selected AssetID or linear segment, with full security/role enforcement and robust offline synchronization.

AM38.comments: MAS aligns assets to LocationID and infrastructure layers—including parcels, buildings, floors, rooms, utility networks, and linear routes with from/to measures—storing both AssetID and LocationID on records while enforcing referential integrity.

AM39.comments: MAS provides offline and poor-connectivity field editing with preplanned map areas, background synchronization, conflict resolution, support for high-accuracy GNSS/RTK, barcode/QR code capture, and efficient MDM app distribution.

AM40.comments: MAS enables delta-based synchronization—via scheduled jobs or webhook/event-driven processes—between EAM assets and ArcGIS layers, with built-in support for schema evolution, change detection, and error reconciliation reporting.

AM41.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM42.comments: MAS enables seamless single sign-on, allowing map users to automatically sign into the EAM solution and reciprocally, EAM users to access mapping tools without separate authentication steps.

AM43.comments: MAS provides a map interface that enables users to create, read, update, and delete GIS features in a connected geodatabase via REST API integration.

AM44.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM45.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM46.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM47.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM48.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM49.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM50.comments: MAS seamlessly integrates with Esri ArcGIS, leveraging Esri technology to power GIS-centric cloud and mobile applications within the EAM environment.

AM51.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM52.comments: MAS integrates with GIS systems to provide spatial visualization and management of assets, enabling real-time updates between asset records and geographic data. This integration enhances decision-making by linking asset information with precise map locations.

AM53.comments: MAS integrates with GIS to trigger approval workflows when geometry changes—such as relocating a valve—updating LocationID and linear measures, and seamlessly propagating these updates to the enterprise asset management (EAM) system while maintaining change lineage.

AM54.comments: MAS can ingest asset data from external sources like capital project systems, spreadsheets, or procurement platforms, updating its asset registry and associated asset record fields—including user-defined fields. This ensures comprehensive, up-to-date asset information for better management and decision-making.

AM55.comments: MAS provides built in integration with Revit to import BIM models and COBie files to create assets, building systems and equipment and associate them with locations. MAS also provides the ability for bi-directional information updates between Revit BIM models to update status, location, ownership and other attributes.

AM56.comments: MAS provides built in integration with Revit to import BIM models and COBie files to create assets, building systems and equipment and associate them with locations. MAS also provides the ability for bi-directional information updates between Revit BIM models to update status, location, ownership and other attributes.

AM57.comments: MAS integrates with ERP systems to facilitate asset-to-project cost mapping, capital versus operating expense classification, and alignment of depreciation categories. This integration supports accurate financial management, reporting, and compliance with accounting standards.

AM58.comments: MAS integrates labor time tracking from maintenance work orders directly with HCM systems, supporting accurate compensation and cost allocation. This ensures seamless transfer of labor data, improving payroll accuracy and enabling better cost management.

AM59.comments: MAS integrates with Human Capital Management (HCM) systems to share technician schedules, availability, and shift assignments. This integration enables coordinated maintenance planning and improves visibility into workforce coverage for optimized resource allocation.

AM60.comments: MAS integrates with Learning Management Systems (LMS) to track technician certifications, training completions, and compliance requirements. The system can gate work assignments based on qualification status, ensuring only suitably qualified technicians are assigned to appropriate tasks.

AM61.comments: MAS ingests real-time telemetry data from sensors (runtime hours, vibration, temperature) to enable predictive maintenance and asset utilization reporting, improving operational reliability. This allows for proactive management and informed decision-making based on live asset conditions.

AM62.comments: MAS integrates with GIS platforms to provide spatial visualization of assets, maintenance work orders, and service coverage areas using interactive maps with GIS locators. This capability enhances operational insight, asset management, and geographic analysis for more informed decision-making. It can integrate with GIS platforms to enable spatial visualization of assets, maintenance work orders, and service coverage areas on interactive maps. GIS locators enhance real-time geographic insight, supporting more effective asset management and operational planning.

AM63.comments: MAS supports controlled bi-directional updates between GIS and EAM systems, allowing GIS edits to update asset location/geometry, while EAM edits update asset attributes and status. Authoritative boundaries, conflict rules, approval processes, and a full audit trail ensure data integrity and accountability. MAS enables controlled bi-directional updates, where GIS edits can modify asset location/geometry and EAM edits can update asset attributes/status. The system enforces authoritative boundaries, conflict resolution, approval workflows, and maintains a full audit trail for transparency and compliance.

AM64.comments: MAS enables integration with image capture systems and external storage, allowing users to attach and manage inspection photos and drone footage for asset records. This supports efficient, real-time documentation and stronger asset condition tracking.

AM65.comments: MAS can integrate with hazard reporting systems to meet Safety Management System (SMS) requirements, enabling seamless documentation and tracking of hazards within regulatory guidelines. This ensures proactive risk management and compliance throughout asset operations.

AM66.comments: MAS can integrate with MyEkos to streamline fleet and transportation management, allowing real-time tracking and coordination of vehicles and assets. This integration enhances operational efficiency and centralizes transportation data within Maximo’s platform.

AM67.comments: MAS enforces validation rules and workflow checkpoints to ensure all required asset data fields are completed before record finalization. Role-based permissions allow authorized users to bypass these checks when necessary, supporting both data quality and operational flexibility.

AM68.comments: MAS supports creating and tracking planned asset records using budgeted projects or forecast data, including capturing estimated cost, acquisition source, and in-service timelines. This feature enhances strategic asset planning and lifecycle management.

AM69.comments: MAS enables the initiation of new asset records directly from maintenance workflows, such as upgrades or component replacements. This ensures accurate documentation and seamless updating of asset inventories as changes occur during maintenance activities.

AM70.comments: Maximo allows users to configure multiple start/stop dates and define distinct lifecycle phases for individual assets. This flexibility supports detailed tracking of asset usage, project timelines, and status changes throughout the asset’s lifecycle.

AM71.comments: MAS tracks lifecycle costs for each individual asset, recording expenses related to rebuilds and usage cycles. This enables comprehensive cost analysis and helps optimize asset investment decisions and maintenance strategies.

AM72.comments: MAS enables detailed tracking of maintenance, repair, and operational costs for each asset throughout its lifecycle. Users can view real-time cost data to inform budgeting and asset management decisions.

AM73.comments: MAS supports staged or partial activation of assets—such as by component, location, or zone—allowing for phased operational handoffs. This enables flexible deployment and management of assets as they become operational in stages.

AM74.comments: MAS accommodates tenant-installed, legacy, or modified Port assets by offering flexible asset identification and lifecycle tracking. This ensures comprehensive management and visibility of diverse asset types throughout their lifecycle.

AM75.comments: MAS analyzes repair frequency, cumulative repair vs. replacement costs, and preventative maintenance data to identify failure trends and condition degradation curves by asset class or vendor. This insight supports proactive maintenance decisions and asset lifecycle optimization.

AM76.comments: Maximo health and Predict functionality can be enabled in the future after the system has been in use for at least 3 years. Maximo health and predict requires historical data for enabling this feature.

AM77.comments: MAS can configure and maintain a flexible Criticality Framework tailored to industry-specific models, leveraging AI/ML and historical data. But this will be enabled in the future for Port while implementing MAS Predict.

AM78.comments: MAS can calculate and model current and future risks, service levels, KPIs, and failure probabilities using advanced techniques like Monte Carlo simulations. This empowers predictive asset management and supports strategic decision-making to reduce operational risk. This will be enabled in the future with MAS predict.

AM79.comments: MAS portfolio management module provides ability to create and compare investment alternatives of based on risk, service levels financial investment and benefits over time.

AM80.comments:
MAS enables definition of forecasts and constraints, and optimizes asset portfolios to deliver lowest-cost, risk-mitigating solutions. The system provides scenario visualizations—covering financials, risks, benefits, and service levels—to support informed planning and strategic asset management.
Maximo supports the definition of forecasts and constraints, portfolio optimization to achieve lowest-cost and risk-mitigating solutions, and visualization of scenarios showing financials, risks, benefits, and service levels. This enables data-driven asset portfolio decision-making and capability analysis.

AM81.comments: MAS provides ability to create deferred maintenance opportunities including asset renewal, renovation, repair for assets across time horizons. These opportunities can be evaluated against the available budget/funding based on priority, timeline, impact and other factors.

AM82.comments: MAS supports regulatory lifecycle milestones by tracking key events such as inspection due dates, re-certification, and end-of-permit life. This ensures ongoing compliance and proactive management of regulatory requirements throughout an asset’s lifecycle.

AM83.comments: MAS facilitates forecasting and prioritization of asset renewal or replacement by analyzing condition, risk, and criticality factors. This enables data-driven decisions to maximize asset performance and minimize downtime.

AM84.comments: MAS enables condition scoring and importance ranking of assets through customizable scales (e.g., Failed to Excellent, High/Medium/Low), enhancing prioritization and targeted asset management. This capability supports more effective decision-making and resource allocation.

AM85.comments: MAS's capital planning module that provides ability to capture deferred maintenance replacement projects and funding requests with multiple sources.

AM86.comments: MAS's capital project module provides ability to integrate with RSMeans for updating cost data for different project activities and cost summaries at a budget level.

AM87.comments: As part of MAS's portfolio management module, scenario based modeling can be prepared to compare renewal or cost investment strategies across multiple funding levels, risks, thresholds.

AM88.comments: MAS provides ability to calculate and notify replacement date for asset based on the asset installation/provisioning date and useful life provided by manufacturer.

AM89.comments: MAS has the ability to capture location adjustment factors across years and apply it to cost calculations.

AM90.comments: MAS Facility condition assessment module has ability to create opportunities across timeline based on identified deficiencies or improvements. The opportunities can capture scope duration, milestone and benefits. The cost for these opportunities can be calculated based on cost tables and pre-loaded into the system.

AM91.comments: MAS calculates system-level asset replacement costs using a combination of inputs, including building square footage and other asset-specific details. Useful life is tracked and used to help prioritize facility maintenance and replacement opportunities, but it is one of several data points, not the sole calculation method.

AM92.comments: MAS has the ability to integrate with RSMeans that allows for data updates in RSMeans to trigger/recalculate estimates for system replacements and identified deficiencies

AM93.comments: MAS allows users to manually add custom cost line items, such as cost adders, which can be used to increase the cost of work to account for specific situations or needs not covered by national averages.

AM94.comments: MAS has the ability to define conditions preventing overwrites to manual cost adjustments

AM95.comments: MAS provides robust reporting features that allow users to generate custom and interactive reports on asset costs. Facility Condition Assessment module allows tracking the existing and future condition of facilities, systems, and assets. It determines the immediate and long-term costs associated with assets and helps prioritize maintenance needs. The capital projects module lets creation detailed budget records and anticipate costs throughout a project's lifecycle. It enables forecasting potential changes to a project's cost and proactively manage stipulated budgets.

AM96.comments: MAS provides a user-friendly table view for queried assets or systems, allowing users to filter by asset area, asset type, and other attributes. This streamlined interface enhances data accessibility and enables efficient asset management workflows.

AM97.comments: MAS is designed to track an asset's lifecycle stages and supports multiple classification categories from a central source. The software offers standard functionality that can be customized to meet specific business needs. MAS also provides a central, hierarchical classification system that can be used for assets, spaces, and other records. This system helps organize and standardize information across Port's entire portfolio.

AM98.comments: MAS enables allocation of costs to various financial accounts, including expense, inventory balance, revenue, asset, liability, and capital accounts. This functionality supports comprehensive financial tracking and integrated asset accounting within the system.

AM99.comments: MAS allows direct charging of labor, materials, and maintenance costs to specific asset records for accurate accounting and cost attribution. Configurable controls ensure that only authorized users can assign charges, supporting secure and transparent financial management.

AM100.comments:
MAS Capital Project Management module is purpose-built to manage funding for capital projects and programs. Funding source records for various types of resources like Grants, 
Bonds, Federal and state awards, Private donations, Internal funds. The platform includes a secure, built-in audit trail that logs data changes made to a record. An "Audit" tab can be enabled on a business object form to display a record of all data changes, including who made the change, when it was made, and a reason for the change if required.

AM101.comments: MAS enables the linking of events, work orders, and problem codes to individual assets or asset groups, enhancing operational visibility and diagnostics. This integration supports proactive issue identification, streamlined workflows, and improved asset performance analysis.

AM102.comments: MAS can generate reports on the impact of funding levels on asset condition using its Facility Assessment application, which allows users to track asset conditions, identify needs, and plan for capital projects by linking funding requests to facility assessments and then using Facility Condition Index (FCI) and System Condition Index (CI) calculations for comparative analysis and reporting

AM103.comments: MAS Facility Assessment and Capital Projects modules  is capable of generating reports that show required capital renewals and replacements over a specified period, broken down by building, campus, or portfolio.

AM104.comments: MAS generates data reports that calculate asset information at each hierarchy level, including renewal costs, useful life metrics, forecasts, and Facility Condition Index (FCI).

AM116.comments: Maximo can generate work orders from user requests, tenant reports, scheduled tasks, or system-detected asset performance conditions.

AM117.comments: Maximo provides a mobile interface for maintenance teams to log service activity and complete field tasks.

AM118.comments: Maximo allows offsite data collection and access via mobile devices, with data synchronized to the database upon reconnection.

AM119.comments: Maximo supports mobile-enabled surveys to gather asset condition data, capture photos, and record locations.

AM120.comments: Maximo supports mobile entry, enabling technicians to receive, update, and close work orders from field devices.

AM121.comments: Maximo enables technicians to document work performed, materials used, and time spent directly within the work order.

AM122.comments: Maximo allows photos and videos of assets to be captured via mobile devices and automatically linked to asset records, supporting both live data connectivity and offline cache mode.

AM123.comments: Maximo enables authorized users to create and update asset records from mobile devices, with built-in validation and role-based review to ensure data accuracy and control.

AM124.comments: Maximo can push real-time work order and maintenance schedule updates directly to technicians’ mobile devices, ensuring timely communication and task management.

AM125.comments: Maximo supports mobile asset assessments by allowing individual asset records to be locked during evaluation, while keeping all other data and system functions accessible to other users at different locations.

AM126.comments: Maximo enables assignment of inspections to users based on asset type, location, or system type.

AM127.comments: Maximo enables creation of system deficiencies during mobile inspections, associating them with Life Cycle Asset Management and treating them separately from maintenance work orders.

AM128.comments: Maximo enables users to submit new and follow-up work requests directly from mobile devices.

AM129.comments: Maximo allows users to access asset records and open work orders by scanning barcodes or QR codes linked to assets.

AM130.comments: Maximo enables users to access asset documentation directly from a mobile device.

AM131.comments: Maximo updates asset records upon inspection completion, with an option for delegated review prior to record update

AM132.comments: Maximo enables users to capture notes in the main asset record field, supporting extended narratives and ongoing updates over time.

AM133.comments: Maximo allows multiple users to simultaneously view, create, and update different records.

AM134.comments: Maximo updates asset records after maintenance completion, capturing technician comments and statistics, with an option for delegated review or confirmation.

AM135.comments: Maximo supports workflow-driven asset updates—including additions, edits, and retirements—during maintenance or work order completion, with appropriate change controls

AM136.comments: Maximo enables end users or non-maintenance staff to generate and track service requests via an accessible portal interface.

AM137.comments: Maximo can prepopulate job steps, safety protocols, required materials, and estimated durations for repeatable maintenance tasks.

AM138.comments: Maximo supports calculation and reporting of total work order costs—including labor, materials, external services, and overhead—with detailed breakdowns by task or technician.

AM139.comments: Maximo can assign and monitor service level agreements (SLAs) for work orders—including response time, completion deadlines, and automated alerts for violations.

AM140.comments: Maximo supports creation and scheduling of recurring work orders for routine inspections, compliance checks, or preventive maintenance.

AM141.comments: Maximo can automatically route service requests based on location, asset type, or priority to the appropriate maintenance team.

AM142.comments: Maximo can prevent closing a work order based on the status of the associated asset.

AM143.comments: Maximo can record the final asset disposition status before closing a work order.

AM144.comments: Maximo allows relevant documents to be attached directly to work orders, including pre-populating attachments based on job or work type.

AM145.comments: Maximo enables attachment of relevant documents directly to assets.

AM146.comments: Maximo supports assignment of structured failure codes to work orders and enables grouping and reporting by asset or asset class.

AM147.comments: Maximo supports shift handover logging for in-progress work orders, capturing technician notes, asset status, and pending actions.

AM148.comments: MAS supports dynamic scheduling of preventive and corrective maintenance tasks. This functionality is enabled through the Maximo Scheduler and Maximo Optimizer modules, which use real-time data, and advanced algorithms to manage and prioritize work.

AM149.comments: MAS supports emergency work by enabling the rescheduling of planned work orders through Graphical scheduling and assignment applications, while advanced scheduling tools such as cron jobs and workflows help maintain compliance for time-sensitive tasks.

AM150.comments: Maximo  Graphical Assignment application has the ability to provide a real-time calendar  and dispatch view(Assignment and work view) of open work orders and technician assignments.

AM151.comments: Maximo supports multi-trade or multi-technician work orders using specific applications and features within the platform. The core functionality for this involves the use of assignments, labor records, crews, and the Maximo Scheduler applications.

AM152.comments: Maximo system supports automated technician assignment based on a wide range of defined rules and criteria, such as craft, certification, location, shift, and availability. Maximo Scheduler and Maximo Optimizer are the primary tools used for automating work assignments based on specific criteria. The optimizer automates scheduling to achieve goals, like assigning work to the best-suited technicians and completing high-priority tasks first.

AM153.comments: Maximo system allows for the scheduling of work orders with respect to specific service windows and access restrictions through the use of several integrated applications and features. This functionality is handled primarily by the Maximo Graphical Scheduler  and Assignment applications and related configurations within Maximo Manage.

AM154.comments: Maximo system integrates technician availability, time off, and shift schedules using the Calendar and Graphical Assignment applications. This provides dispatchers and planners with a graphical overview of resource availability to make informed assignment decisions.

AM155.comments: Maximo Graphical Assignment application and other Graphical Scheduling applications within the Maximo Scheduler module provides Gantt-style view to manually assign, reassign, and monitor technician workloads by day, shift, or crew. These tools provide the interactive, drag-and-drop calendar and Gantt-style views for managing technician workloads.

AM156.comments: In Maximo, balancing technician workloads based on assigned work orders, labor hours, and task complexity is handled through the combination of Maximo Scheduler and Maximo Optimizer. While Maximo Manage includes manual assignment features, the Scheduler and Optimizer modules provide the advanced tools for automated, data-driven workload distribution and resource leveling.

AM157.comments: Maximo allows technician assignments based on qualifications and certifications which requires a multi-step configuration process involving the Qualifications, Job Pans, Work Order Tracking and Labor applications.

AM158.comments: Maximo enables the creation of preventive maintenance (PM) routes for a single asset type across multiple locations using its Routes and Preventive Maintenance applications. A Maximo Route is a predefined list of assets, locations, or both, that a maintenance crew can visit to perform related tasks—such as inspections—which can then be associated with a PM record to schedule and manage recurring work.

AM159.comments: Maximo enables the creation of preventive maintenance (PM) routes for a single asset type across multiple locations using its Routes and Preventive Maintenance applications. A Maximo Route is a predefined list of assets, locations, or both, that a maintenance crew can visit to perform related tasks—such as inspections—which can then be associated with a PM record to schedule and manage recurring work.

AM160.comments: Maximo has the ability to phase out work orders and manage progression statuses which is handled primarily through the Work Order Tracking application. A work order's status can be changed manually, through a workflow, or via automation, with a detailed history of status changes maintained in the system.

AM161.comments: Maximo has the ability to overschedule  which is handled by modifying resource availability and manually making assignments within the Graphical Scheduling application. The system does not prevent over-allocation by default; instead, it uses visual cues to alert planners when an assignment exceeds resource capacity.

AM162.comments: In Maximo, the integration of maintenance schedules, inventory levels, and procurement lead times for long-range planning and forecasting is a function of several applications within the Maximo Application Suite. This capability is most robustly supported through the use of Maximo Predict, Maximo Inventory Optimization, and Maximo Optimizer. This will be enabled in later phases when predict has enough data to analyze.

AM163.comments: In Maximo, users can create material requisitions for non-inventory items using the Create Desktop Requisition application within the Self-Service module. Instead of reserving items from a storeroom, users specify the required item by description, which initiates a direct purchase from an external vendor.

AM164.comments: Maximo offers advanced parts staging and proactive alerts for material shortages as part of its integrated inventory, planning, and work order management features. This functionality ensures required parts are available before a work order is scheduled, helping organizations prevent delays and enhance maintenance efficiency.

AM165.comments: Maximo supports this requirement through its Inventory Optimization (MIO) module and core functionalities, dynamically forecasting inventory demand based on scheduled maintenance and upcoming work orders. By leveraging AI-driven analytics, Maximo shifts MRO inventory management from a reactive to a prescriptive approach, enhancing forecasting accuracy and operational efficiency.

AM166.comments: Ability to commit inventory to work orders in advance for future demand visibility is managed through inventory reservations and pick lists, forming part of Maximo’s broader strategy to balance spare parts availability with inventory carrying costs.

AM167.comments: In Maximo, inventory items can be linked to maintenance schedules and set up for usage-based reordering by configuring Job Plans, Preventive Maintenance (PM) records, and Inventory applications. This approach applies to both standard stock items and non-stock items required for work orders. Specialized add-ons like Maximo Inventory Optimization can provide more advanced forecasting.

AM168.comments: IBM Maximo can detect and flag conflicts between scheduled maintenance and inventory availability through its robust reservation system—a core component of its Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) capabilities. This functionality helps organizations avoid costly delays and downtime resulting from shortages of necessary parts.

AM169.comments: In IBM Maximo, the system can automatically delay work orders when parts are backordered and provide updates on their expected availability. This functionality is supported through inventory reservation types, work order status management, and system configuration settings.

AM170.comments: In IBM Maximo, pick lists are generated from material reservations linked to work orders, grouping items by storeroom to streamline the process for storeroom personnel and facilitate efficient issuance of materials required for maintenance activities.

AM171.comments: Maximo tracks labor and material costs applied to individual assets serviced through route-based or multi-location work orders.

AM172.comments: Maximo can allocate labor and material costs evenly across all assets in grouped or route-based work orders.

AM173.comments: Maximo tracks work order-level costs, including labor, parts, tools, and external services.

AM174.comments: Maximo enables invoicing vendors or tenants for responsible work and supports a maintenance matrix during work request creation.

AM175.comments: Maximo provides functionality to generate quote documents for estimated repair costs.

AM176.comments: Maximo can use historical cost data and external sources to estimate costs.

AM177.comments: Maximo supports maintenance inspections and surveys using checklists, photos, and geotags.

AM178.comments: Maximo enables association of maintenance tasks with asset records, including hierarchical equipment structures.

AM179.comments: Maximo provides tracking of deferred maintenance and backlog by facility, asset, or system type.

AM180.comments: Maximo enables tracking of condition assessment data, including inspector ratings and facility condition indices (FCI).

AM181.comments: Maximo stores historical maintenance records by asset to support lifecycle analysis and capital planning.

AM182.comments: MAS supports predictive maintenance by managing triggers such as vibration readings, usage meters, and performance thresholds. MAS predict will be enabled in the future.

AM183.comments: Maximo enables mobile or automated capture of condition-based monitoring data for predictive maintenance triggers and alerts. MAS predict will be enabled in the future.

AM184.comments: Maximo automatically generates alerts and predictive insights based on usage trends, failure history, and sensor data thresholds. MAS predict will be enabled in the future

AM185.comments: Maximo provides the ability to create and manage preventive maintenance hierarchies for organizing related maintenance activities.

AM186.comments: Maximo allows configuration of preventive maintenance schedules based on asset class, condition, usage, or lifecycle indicators.

AM187.comments: Maximo supports assigning estimated repair costs to assets using historical work order data, benchmark references, or manual inputs for planning.

AM188.comments: Maximo enables external vendors or contractors to receive and complete work orders

AM189.comments: Maximo records and tracks warranty coverage details and enforces vendor service obligations.

AM190.comments: Maximo records and tracks warranty work separately from internal labor, capturing warranty hours, labor, and parts sent out for reimbursement to support accurate reporting and recovery.

AM191.comments: Maximo enables tracking and management of multiple warranties per asset, including coverage terms, expiration dates, service providers, and warranty-specific work order routing.

AM192.comments: Maximo supports multiple maintenance groups or divisions, enabling distinct workflows, asset ownership, and reporting requirements for each.

AM193.comments: Maximo provides visibility into asset utilization and availability to inform maintenance planning and operational readiness.

AM194.comments: Maximo manages permits, lockout-tagout (LOTO), and other safety protocols associated with high-risk maintenance activities.

AM195.comments: Maximo supports structured failure mode and root cause analysis workflows, linking failure events to asset classes, preventive actions, and historical repair outcomes.

AM196.comments: Standard MAS functionality that can be utilized for Preventive and Corrective maintenance of Assets

AM197.comments: MAS supports fleet asset classification by type using industry standards like NAFA and custom categories, enabling tailored district-level preventive maintenance plans, reporting, and accurate cost allocation.

AM198.comments: MAS enables assignment of fleet assets to user groups, individuals, or projects, and generates detailed reports on costs associated with each assignment for optimal resource management.
