AM199.comments: MAS offers configurable vehicle inspection templates—such as pre-trip, post-trip, or seasonal—with mobile data entry, photo capture, and automatic follow-up actions for flagged issues to streamline compliance and maintenance.

AM200.comments: MAS facilitates preventive maintenance scheduling by leveraging meter readings and calendar intervals, automatically notifying users when services are due or overdue to ensure timely asset upkeep. MAS enables preventive maintenance scheduling using meter readings like mileage or engine hours, as well as calendar intervals, and provides automated alerts for due or overdue service tasks.

AM201.comments: MAS tracks license expirations, insurance renewals, safety certifications, and compliance tasks, providing proactive alerts and comprehensive reporting to support regulatory adherence and operational continuity.

AM202.comments: MAS tracks operator or driver assignments by vehicle and date to ensure accountability and aid in diagnostics for usage or damage, while enabling vehicle scheduling even when an assigned driver is off duty.

AM203.comments: MAS provides functionality to track driver hours, supporting compliance, workforce planning, and operational oversight for fleet management.

AM204.comments: MAS integrates with telematics to automatically gather mileage, engine diagnostics, and location data, enhancing maintenance planning and asset utilization analysis for fleet operations

AM205.comments: MAS offers reporting and dashboards on fleet utilization by vehicle, category, business unit, and usage type (such as idle versus in-service), enabling data-driven decisions for operational efficiency.

AM206.comments: MAS manages shared-use or pooled fleet vehicles by enabling reservation scheduling, maintaining usage logs, and tracking key assignments to optimize fleet availability and accountability. MAS supports shared-use or pooled fleet vehicles by offering reservation scheduling, detailed usage logs, and key assignment tracking to enhance fleet management efficiency.

AM207.comments: MAS enables creation of external fleet management databases using identification processes similar to internal forms, while supporting tailored data fields for specialized public safety service management, such as Police and Fire departments.

AM208.comments: Maximo tracks detailed maintenance activities for each asset, including actual maintenance events, labor hours, parts used, and associated maintenance costs. This granular data visibility supports informed decision-making and enhances asset performance management throughout its lifecycle.

AM209.comments: Maximo generates maintenance reports and dashboards featuring key performance indicators like PM completion rate, mean time between failures (MTBF), backlog, overdue work orders, and labor efficiency. It also supports user-defined KPIs, enabling organizations to tailor analytics to their specific operational goals and monitor performance in real time.

AM210.comments: Maximo enables tagging of assets by business unit or cost center, facilitating precise internal cost attribution and usage tracking. This feature supports detailed reporting, enhances budget management, and streamlines accountability across the organization.

AM211.comments: Maximo provides real-time status updates, estimated completion times, and follow-up notes for work orders, ensuring transparent communication and enhanced visibility into maintenance progress. These capabilities help keep stakeholders informed and support proactive management of service activities.

AM212.comments: Maximo supports compliance and regulatory reporting needs, such as fire/life safety, elevators, and ADA requirements, by logging documented completion histories and key operational variables. This ensures organizations can demonstrate regulatory adherence and maintain comprehensive audit trails for inspections and certifications.

IW01.comments: Bulk inventory items can be imported using either the MIF application's import/export option or the MXLoader import function.

IW02.comments: Maximo's Company and Company Master applications are used to manage vendor information and contacts. Purchase Orders and work order applications are used for order history. Maximo's KPs are used for performance monitoring.

IW03.comments: Maximo can achieve multi-dimensional part classification for inventory items using a combination of the Classifications application, attribute specifications, commodity codes, and the Multisite feature.

IW04.comments: Maximo supports this capability through a hierarchy of organizations, sites, and storerooms. The Bin field can be used to track detailed location information such as aisle, bin, and shelf.

IW05.comments: Serial number tracking in Maximo is enabled by designating a part as a "rotating item," which generates a unique asset record upon receipt. Warranty coverage for parts can be managed through warranty contracts and claims applications.

IW06.comments: Item status in Maximo’s Item Master and Inventory applications manages an item’s lifecycle and determines its availability for transactions like work orders, purchases, and replenishment.

IW07.comments: Maximo allows users to link alternate or interchangeable part numbers to assets, making it easier to substitute parts during ordering or stocking. This flexibility helps reduce equipment downtime and streamlines inventory management by ensuring suitable replacements are readily available.

IW08.comments: The Maximo system accommodates multiple units of measure for each item (such as each, box, or case), and includes built-in conversion logic to seamlessly translate quantities between units.

IW09.comments: The Maximo system accommodates multiple units of measure for each item (such as each, box, or case), and includes built-in conversion logic to seamlessly translate quantities between units.

IW10.comments: Maximo enables the classification of hazardous or regulated items via the Safety module, integrated with both the Item Master and Inventory applications.

IW11.comments: In Maximo, configurable inventory classification based on factors such as criticality, part size, and other criteria is supported through the combined use of the Classifications and Organizations applications. For existing items, ABC analysis can be performed using the Inventory application, while for new items, classification rules and attributes can be pre-configured to ensure accurate categorization upon creation.

IW12.comments: In Maximo, a location-based storeroom catalog is provided to users through the use of security groups and data restrictions, which ensure that each user can access only the sites and storerooms relevant to their assigned work areas. This approach enables targeted inventory visibility and enhances operational efficiency.

IW13.comments: In Maximo, inventory management capabilities cover inventory counts, expense tracking, price variances, and transfers, with all transactions being recorded for accounting purposes. This is primarily handled through the Inventory, Count Books, and Inventory Usage applications.

IW14.comments: In Maximo, inventory costs are captured based on the costing method set at the storeroom level, which can vary for different items. While Maximo tracks all inventory transactions, creating "cost averages using historical records for high-moving and high-dollar items, with a configurable timeframe" requires leveraging its reporting and business intelligence features, as this is not a standard built-in function.

IW15.comments: In Maximo, the Asset application tracks asset movement history, including location transfers, dates, and related transactions. This information is stored in the ASSETTRANS database table.

IW16.comments: In Maximo, enriched pick lists are created and managed using the Inventory and Inventory Usage applications, especially within the context of the Pick and Stage process. Mobile users can access and interact with this enriched content through the Maximo Mobile application;

IW17.comments: Maximo Manage supports receiving workflows that reconcile vendor purchase orders (POs) and packing slips, including built-in tolerance settings to account for minor data discrepancies. The system can also be extended with workflows and automation scripts to handle more complex reconciliation needs.

IW18.comments: In Maximo, storeroom-to-storeroom inventory transfers are a standard function accomplished through a structured workflow involving the Inventory Usage and Shipment Receiving applications. This process provides a clear record of transfers, maintains accurate inventory counts, and can include approval steps using Maximo's workflow engine.

IW19.comments: Maximo has built-in capabilities to manage manual inventory adjustments with audit trails and role-based permissions. Maximo is designed to provide transparency, security, and accountability for inventory management processes, including changes to stock levels.

IW20.comments: In Maximo, users can record and track expected delivery dates for items at the purchase order (PO) level and for work order materials, which directly supports inbound planning. The system also allows viewing and planning around these dates through various applications.

IW21.comments: Maximo has the capability to issue inventory tied to a work order or business unit with real-time stock decrement. This is a fundamental feature of its Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) and inventory management functionality.

IW22.comments: In Maximo, the tracking of internal storeroom transfers involves a workflow centered on the Inventory Usage and Shipment Receiving applications. The status is updated automatically as items move from the originating storeroom to the receiving storeroom, where the transfer is confirmed.

IW23.comments: Maximo has the ability to record partial receipts, flag backordered quantities, and track the remaining balance against the original order. This is a standard function of IBM Maximo for managing purchasing and inventory.

IW24.comments: In Maximo, storeroom staff can initiate inventory reorders using two main methods: the manual or automated "Reorder Items" process based on predefined thresholds, or by creating purchase requisitions (PRs) that reference existing Blanket Purchase Agreements (BPAs).

IW25.comments: In Maximo, you can manage inventory items by placing them on hold or reserving them to restrict issuance until a specific condition is met. This is accomplished through reservations, the use of condition codes, and by adjusting the status of the item or its storage bin.

IW26.comments: Maximo has the capability to perform cycle counts and physical inventory at the shelf or bin level using mobile devices. This is accomplished through the Inventory Counting functionality in the Maximo Mobile application.

IW27.comments: Maximo has built-in functionality to automatically flag count variances and includes processes that support validation before final adjustments are made. This is a standard part of Maximo's inventory management and cycle counting features.

IW28.comments: Maximo system has robust capabilities for flagging inventory discrepancies, including those related to physical counts, transactions, and items not returned from work orders. These features are a core component of Maximo's inventory management module, which is designed to help organizations track and maintain inventory accuracy.

IW29.comments: In Maximo, traceable check-out of inventory using digital or scanned methods is achieved by combining several built-in functionalities. Maximo Mobile, the mobile interface for Maximo Application Suite, offers role-based applications that enable technicians and storeroom clerks to perform inventory transactions digitally.

IW30.comments: In Maximo, This requirement can be achieved by  setting up cycle counting with scheduling and prioritization based on item movement through the ABC analysis functionality. This is a standard feature designed to ensure your most valuable or frequently used inventory is counted more often.

IW31.comments: In Maximo, the tracking and reintegration of salvaged or repaired parts is primarily managed using "Rotating Items" and "Assets," which enable full cost attribution and usage restrictions. This functionality distinguishes reparable components from simple, consumable spare parts.

IW32.comments: In Maximo, inventory items can be marked as obsolete and link them to specific assets for traceability using item statuses and the spare parts functionality. The most common approach involves setting an item to a "Pending Obsolescence" status to deplete existing stock, followed by changing it to "Obsolete".

IW33.comments: BM Maximo has the ability to identify slow-moving or obsolete items based on movement history and usage rates, primarily through its Inventory Optimization features. This functionality is central to managing inventory and is accomplished using system-generated metrics, data analysis, and designated item statuses.

IW34.comments: In Maximo, inventory items can be marked as obsolete and link them to specific assets for traceability using item statuses and the spare parts functionality. The most common approach involves setting an item to a "Pending Obsolescence" status to deplete existing stock, followed by changing it to "Obsolete".

IW35.comments: Custom BIRT reports should be developed to automatically generate replenishment recommendations based on usage patterns, safety stock levels, and lead times.

IW36.comments: Maximo can auto-generate and print pick lists based on planned and scheduled preventive maintenance (PMs) or work orders with associated parts. The feature is part of Maximo's comprehensive Inventory Management module, which links parts inventory with work orders to streamline maintenance tasks.

IW37.comments: In Maximo, the system calculates reorder needs by factoring in inventory balances, quantities already on order, and committed quantities (reservations) for scheduled work. The calculation ensures that new orders are placed to bring the available stock above the specified reorder point, taking future demand into account.

IW38.comments: In Maximo, you can track pick status using the inventory and work order management modules, aided by mobile and scan-based technology. To tie material availability to work order scheduling, you will use work order status updates and the Graphical Scheduling application.

IW39.comments: Maximo has robust capabilities for reporting on inventory movement tied to specific equipment or assets to support lifecycle cost analysis. By integrating its Asset Management, Work Order Management, and Inventory modules, Maximo allows you to track the costs of materials and tools used for maintaining each piece of equipment throughout its entire lifecycle.

IW40.comments: In Maximo, the ability to report on fuel inventory by type, volume, usage, and vendor for both fixed and mobile assets is managed through several applications, including the Inventory, Assets, and Fuel Transactions Import modules. Customization and ad hoc reporting features further extend these capabilities.

IW41.comments: Maximo has the capability to generate on-demand reports on inventory usage, reorder trends, and backorder exposure. This is achieved through its core inventory management functions, standard reports, and advanced modules like Maximo Inventory Optimization.

IW42.comments: Maximo has the ability to track and forecast inventory costs, turnover ratios, and lead times by category through its core applications and the specialized Maximo Inventory Optimization solution.

IW43.comments: Maximo has the ability to reassign inventory items to different bin or shelf locations and maintain a history of previous locations for audit purposes. The primary methods for accomplishing this are through the Inventory Usage application and by enabling audit tracking features.

IW44.comments: Maximo can track bin and storeroom capacity and generate alerts when defined thresholds are exceeded. This functionality is typically achieved by using a combination of the core inventory application and related solutions like Maximo Monitor for advanced analytics.

IW45.comments: In Maximo, this requirement can be fulfilled by  leveraging a combination of standard features and configurations, including stock categories, purchasing controls, workflow automation, and specialized add-ons.

IW46.comments: Maximo fully supports inventory scanning at issue points using mobile or handheld devices through its native Maximo Mobile application and integrations with third-party solutions. This feature improves inventory accuracy by simplifying the process of issuing parts and tools to work orders or assets.

IW47.comments: Maximo system has the ability to integrate barcode and RF scanning directly into inventory item setup, lookup, and tracking workflows. This capability is a standard part of modern Maximo functionality.

IW48.comments: Maximo can support barcode scanning on receipt and immediate label generation for storage, but it typically requires integration with mobile hardware and specialized software. While Maximo Mobile applications support scanning out of the box, the process of immediate label printing often requires additional steps or third-party solutions.  Also Barcode label BIRT report can be used and printed.

IW49.comments: In Maximo, configurable inventory label formats are primarily enabled through third-party add-ons or extensions, which offer advanced design and printing features. Maximo does not have native, built-in functionality for creating custom label layouts. Instead, it relies on these external tools to integrate the required capabilities. Also Barcode label BIRT report can be configured used and printed.

IW50.comments: In Maximo, transferring items to other storerooms via a mobile device is done through the Issues and Transfers application. The process involves creating an inventory usage record with a usage type of TRANSFER. For a more streamlined experience, the Maximo Mobile app is recommended, as it allows storeroom clerks to perform this function either online or offline.

IW51.comments: In Maximo, you can issue materials, tools, and rotating assets to a work order through the Inventory and Work Order Tracking applications. These actions track inventory usage and charge the costs to the work order.

IW52.comments: In Maximo, searching for inventory items via a mobile device is possible through the Maximo Mobile application. Maximo Mobile provides users with access to Maximo Asset Management functionalities, including inventory management, on a mobile device. This feature is critical for field technicians and storeroom clerks who need to track and manage parts and supplies while away from a desktop computer.

IW53.comments: In Maximo, user identification by name and signature is primarily accomplished through two different mechanisms: Electronic Signatures (e-signatures) for authentication and auditing, and Real Signature for graphical capture on mobile devices.

IW54.comments: In Maximo, integration with a procurement system for item master records and vendor data is handled by the Maximo Integration Framework (MIF). The MIF uses configurable components to enable both inbound (pulling data into Maximo) and outbound (pushing data from Maximo) data exchanges.

IW55.comments: Maximo has robust capabilities for integrating with vendor portals and web-based ordering systems from major suppliers. These integrations streamline purchasing, improve data accuracy, and automate procurement workflows by connecting Maximo's supply chain features with external vendor systems.

IW56.comments: Maximo can integrate with fuel tracking systems for usage-based charging, particularly through its Maximo for Transportation solution. This capability allows organizations to automate the process of tracking fuel consumption and billing based on actual usage, which is essential for managing fleets and other fuel-intensive assets.

IW57.comments: Within Maximo, automatic integration with vendors to push purchase orders (POs) and pull price sheets can be achieved using its native Integration Framework (MIF). This process allows for automated procurement and keeps vendor information and pricing current.

IW58.comments: Implementing a system that uses P-Card transactions to update inventory in Maximo requires an integration solution and a structured workflow, as there is no out-of-the-box feature for this process. The standard method for updating Maximo inventory involves creating and receiving purchase orders (POs) or performing manual inventory adjustments.

EH01.comments:
MAS has a multi-layered security framework that includes security groups, signature options, and data restrictions that enables role-based access configurations. MAS is designed for multi-site operation, and access can be restricted by site or organizational unit. For EHS records, this means a user's visibility can be limited to the sites or locations they are responsible for. This robust security model allows administrators to configure access based on record type, user role, and organizational unit. 
Workday secures information to the field level. Security permissions are delivered out of the box, but are easily configurable. Workday leverages role-based and user-based security, as well as security based on many other attributes, such as segment, organization, location, and several others. This gives customers the ability to control at the field level, as well as the report level if desired.

EH02.comments: MAS has built-in e-audit feature, which can be enabled for specific objects and attributes in the system. For sensitive environmental data, this includes objects within the Maximo Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) component, which is designed to manage regulatory compliance. Workday provides always-on auditing throughout the entire system, capturing all data changes with details including the user ID, timestamp, and action taken. The audit data is retained in an immutable audit data log in perpetuity.

EH03.comments:
MAS Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) modules of MAS allow organizations to proactively assess, mitigate, and manage risks related to assets, locations, and personnel safety. In MAS Health, custom scoring elements for the health, criticality, and risk of assets and locations can be defined. These scores can be defined based on Port's specific criteria.
Workday delivers robust case management and safety incident tracking that allows all parties relating to a safety incident to be tracked and their status monitored in detail. Safety Incident tracking allows the tracking of detailed information as to the cause(s) of the incident, injury sustained, time away from work, as well as a history of medical treatment, restricted work, etc. Workday also allows the designation of each safety incident as reportable/not reportable. Reportable incidents are then available for incident reporting and inclusion on OSHA reports.

EH04.comments:
MAS HSE Incident application provides a single repository for all HSE incidents, including accidents and near-misses. It allows for the reporting, investigation, and tracking of corrective and preventive actions from a unified location.
Workday Help provides an intelligent HR case management system that allows employees to raise cases of all types, including cases for health and safety incident tracking. On both mobile and desktop, employees are able to select a relevant case type and provide all the necessary details related to the incident (including any relevant documentation) by filling out a contextual form driven by an organizational case template. A case manager is then able to view the case in the context of any relevant data about that employee (driven by security within our single application for Human Capital Management) and take appropriate action in a timely manner.

EH05.comments:
MAS HSE tracks corrective actions from start to finish as part of a larger, integrated workflow for managing incidents, risks, and compliance. The system can be configured to: 
Automatically assign corrective actions based on the type of incident, hazard, or risk identified. Track actions resulting from audits, surveys, incidents, and non-conformances until they are closed. Establish ownership for actions and create review items to ensure follow-through. Escalate issues automatically based on business rules, such as for a root cause analysis. 
The Workday Business Process Framework supports any number of approval levels for a given transaction or activity. The ability to configure multi-level approval is configurable via the business process framework.

EH06.comments:
MAS's dedicated HSE features and underlying workflow engine manage this process through several applications and modules. With the Workflow Assignments application, users get a detailed, centralized view of all workflow-related tasks assigned to them. 
Workday Talent Optimization includes a configurable template for tracking disciplinary actions. Actions can be initiated by a manager, can follow your defined business process, and can include required steps and notifications, as well as e-signatures by the employee and manager.

EH07.comments:
EAM - S - Maximo Application Suite (MAS) specifically with its Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Manager and core workflow capabilities, fully supports the routing of environmental and safety incidents, including those related to FAA Part 139, into a complete corrective action workflow. The system allows for assignment, approval, tracking, and role-based visibility across departments. Maximo's workflow engine can assign tasks to individuals, specific person groups, or roles (such as "Unit Work Coordinator"), based on the relationships defined for a record. This allows tasks to be automatically routed to the correct department or team.
Workday leverages a few different solutions to help meet customers EHS needs (Workday Help, Request Framework and Safety Incidents. There would need to be a step from the safety incident moving into the corrective action workflow.

EH08.comments:
MAS hyperlinks to external SharePoint documents by adding the direct SharePoint URL to a hyperlink control within  Application Designer, which requires obtaining a valid shareable link from the SharePoint document library and configuring it to allow access. These hyperlinks are configured with a target URL and can be dynamically built using bind fields, allowing users to click from MAS directly to the SharePoint file after obtaining the proper SharePoint access credentials. 
Workday does not provide a delivered, out-of-the-box integration specifically for SharePoint to retrieve and display documents directly within Workday records.

However, Workday does offer a feature called Workday Drive, which provides pre-built integrations with other major cloud document management systems:

    ● Box
    ● Dropbox
    ● Google Drive
    ● Microsoft OneDrive for Business

With these delivered integrations, you can link files from your chosen storage provider directly to records in Workday. The documents remain stored in the external drive, but they are accessible in context from the relevant Workday pages.

EH09.comments:
Workday has the ability to do a file upload or API call. Email and other entries items would require a workaround approach. You can still achieve a similar result by using a common workaround:
    1. Create a Custom URL Field:
        ● An administrator can use the Maintain Custom Fields task to add a new field to the relevant business objects (e.g., Asset, Work Order, Incident).
        ● The Field Type for this custom field should be set to Hyperlink.
        ● You can name the field something intuitive, like "SharePoint Document" or "Project Folder."
    2. Link Documents Manually:
        ● Users would then navigate to the specific document or folder in your SharePoint instance and copy its URL.
        ● They would paste this URL into the new custom hyperlink field on the corresponding record in Workday (e.g., on the specific asset's page).
    3. Accessing the Document:
        ● This creates a direct link from the Workday record to the document in SharePoint.
        ● When a user clicks the link in Workday, it will open the document directly from SharePoint in a new browser tab.
Advanced Custom Integration
For a more seamless experience than a manual URL link, a custom integration could be developed using Workday Studio. This would be a more complex solution requiring development resources to build a custom connection between Workday and the SharePoint API, but it could potentially automate the process of linking and retrieving documents.

EH10.comments: This capability is not built within MAS right now. As per IBM's product roadmap this will be available in Q2 of 2026. Therefore we anticipate that this capability will be available when we start system configuration activities for the port in Q4 2026.

EH11.comments: This capability is not built within MAS right now. As per IBM's product roadmap this will be available in Q2 of 2026. Therefore we anticipate that this capability will be available when we start system configuration activities for the port in Q4 2026.

EH12.comments:
IBM MAS’s document management captures and stores metadata—such as submitter identity, timestamp, site, and facility—for all structured data and document submissions. MAS’s audit features maintain permanent records for compliance, using configurable audit tables to log every change, including user, timestamp, change type, and before/after values. Auditing is accessible within the MAS interface for direct review by compliance teams. 
Workday provides a comprehensive audit trail that captures all system changes, including details of who made the add or change, what was changed, and when the change occurred. This feature cannot be bypassed and applies across the entire data model.

EH13.comments: MAS, specifically through its Maximo Spatial Asset Management add-on, fully supports spatial analysis for applications like environmental planning, maintenance prioritization, and emergency response. By integrating with Geographic Information Systems (GIS), such as Esri's ArcGIS, Maximo allows users to visualize, analyze, and overlay multiple datasets to gain a comprehensive geospatial context for their assets. Workday does not deliver spatial analysis to inform environmental planning, maintenance prioritization, and emergency response.

EH14.comments: Yes, MAS HSE (Health, Safety, and Environment) can support migration and or integration with standalone asbestos tracking programs, as it is designed to integrate with other Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) systems to provide a holistic view of HSE and asset management data. While MAS's own Health, Safety and Environment Manager provides tools for managing these processes, integrations can also be built with external EAM or specialized software to connect with other systems and consolidate information into the MAS platform.

EH15.comments:
MAS offers a robust framework for organizations to track hazardous materials such as asbestos using its safety and asset management modules. Users can create hazard records for asbestos within the Hazards application, specifying material types (for example, chrysotile or amosite) and risk categories, and linking these hazards to specific assets and facility locations, such as insulation on pipes or select rooms. MAS facilitates detailed condition and risk assessments, allowing risk scores to be defined according to the asbestos type, condition, and location, as well as the probability of disturbance. The Health module gathers inspection data to monitor the material's degradation over time.
MAS maintains comprehensive historical tracking of changes made to asset and location records, including updates to hazard details and risk ratings. Work order histories related to asbestos inspection, repair, or removal are auditable, supporting compliance and safety protocols. The system integrates metadata about organizations, sites, assets, and locations, ensuring that asbestos records are fully embedded within facility data. Classification systems allow for the precise categorization of hazardous materials by material type or risk category. Reporting and dashboard tools visualize asbestos inventory, risk, and condition trends, enabling organizations to effectively manage hazardous material risks across their operations.

EH16.comments: MAS’s data migration capability enables organizations to efficiently load data from other systems using its robust Integration Framework. This framework supports bulk imports from external sources—including databases, spreadsheets (Excel), and flat files like CSV or XML—through APIs, file upload tools, and connectors. MAS validates and standardizes incoming data, mapping it to its business object structure to ensure consistency. Advanced features allow for scheduled, automated batch migrations and error handling, while metadata and audit trails preserve data integrity. This makes it straightforward to consolidate historical data, asset records, and maintenance information from legacy tools into MAS’s centralized environment.

EH17.comments:
MAS supports spatial tagging of hazardous materials to assets and zones by integrating with Maximo Spatial Asset Management (which uses GIS data) and the Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) module to link hazardous material information to specific locations or assets on a map. Users can associate hazards with locations or assets, define hazardous material details like health risk and flammability, and link them to spatial maps to visualize their location, allowing for better management and safety planning. 
This is not native functionality in Workday and would required configuration and custom objects to make it work.

EH18.comments:
MAS maintains comprehensive hazardous material and environmental constraint data—such as asbestos, remediation zones, seasonal restrictions, and stormwater protection areas—in a centralized, version-controlled repository. This approach ensures data integrity, simplifies regulatory compliance, and supports safe, informed decision-making across operations.
This is not supported by Workday natively

EH19.comments:
MAS enables assignment of data stewardship roles for asbestos and hazardous material records, such as Environmental, Industrial Hygiene, and EH&S. The system maintains robust audit trails for updates, ensuring ownership accountability and transparent management of sensitive compliance data.
Workday does not natively support the assignment of data stewardship roles (e.g., Environmental, Industrial Hygiene, EH&S) for asbestos and hazardous material records, with audit trails of updates and ownership accountability. Some level of security roles could be configured.

EH20.comments:
IBM MAS enables effective compliance tracking for asbestos management, including scheduling and documenting required inspections, monitoring reporting obligations, and managing tenant lease requirements. The platform supports linking lease details to assets or spaces, and facilitates the generation and submission of regulatory reports. Configurable alerts and notifications help users stay ahead of deadlines, inspection schedules, and potential violations, ensuring timely action. Customizable workflows allow adaptation to specific regulatory or lease mandates. Together, these features provide organizations with a centralized, auditable platform to proactively manage asbestos-related responsibilities and maintain strong compliance with relevant legal and contractual obligations.
See EH14

EH21.comments:
MAS supports detailed tracking of asbestos inventories by recording locations, material types, conditions, and associated risks within its hazardous materials management module. This functionality enables comprehensive oversight, risk assessment, and informed decision-making for safe facility operations.
This is not supported by Workday natively. Workday Inventory could possibly be used to allow items to be tracked by lot numbers. Lot numbers and Lot expiration dates can be tracked to minimize the amount of expired goods. The data and supported Workday inventory tasks include: Location, Location Identifier, Lot Identifier, Quantity and more. Supported Workday Inventory Tasks include: Find Inventory Balances, Ad Hoc Inventory (Cycle) Count, and Count Inventory (Cycle Count).

EH22.comments:
MAS enables linking hazardous material monitoring and industrial hygiene records—such as asbestos, lead, and IH exposure reports—to specific locations and individual employees. The system ensures record retention aligns with OSHA requirements, maintaining documentation for the duration of employment plus 30 years to support regulatory compliance and employee safety.
This is not native functionality in Workday and would required configuration and custom objects to make it work.

EH23.comments:
MAS provides a centralized repository for logging and tracking all environmental and safety incidents, such as spills and releases, using structured fields for detailed documentation. This functionality enhances incident management, regulatory compliance, and continuous improvement in safety and environmental performance.
Workday delivers robust case management and safety incident tracking that allows all parties relating to a safety incident to be tracked and their status monitored in detail. Safety Incident tracking allows the tracking of detailed information as to the cause(s) of the incident, injury sustained, time away from work, as well as a history of medical treatment, restricted work, etc. Workday also allows the designation of each safety incident as reportable/not reportable. Reportable incidents are then available for incident reporting and inclusion on OSHA reports.

EH24.comments: MAS supports configurable role-based access for EHS records and workflows, enabling tailored permission controls for hazard reporting, reviews, and corrective action processes. This ensures secure, compliant management of sensitive data for both internal and external stakeholders.

EH25.comments:
MAS captures hazard and incident reports in a structured format, ensuring consistent and reliable data collection across all events. This structured approach facilitates effective analysis, risk assessment, and seamless integration with corrective action workflows to enhance safety management.
Workday delivers case management and safety incident tracking. Employees can raise cases of all types, including health and safety incident tracking, and provide all necessary details related to the incident by filling out a contextual form. A case manager is then able to view the case and take appropriate action.

EH26.comments:
IBM MAS can be configured to support anonymous or confidential hazard submission to help organizations comply with regulatory requirements like FAA Part 139, which mandates mechanisms for reporting safety hazards without fear of reprisal.
Workday delivers robust case management and safety incident tracking that allows all parties relating to a safety incident to be tracked and their status monitored in detail. Safety Incident tracking allows the tracking of detailed information as to the cause(s) of the incident, injury sustained, time away from work, as well as a history of medical treatment, restricted work, etc. Workday also allows the designation of each safety incident as reportable/not reportable. Reportable incidents are then available for incident reporting and inclusion on OSHA reports.

Workday Help provides an intelligent HR case management system that allows employees to raise cases of all types, including cases for health and safety incident tracking. On both mobile and desktop, employees are able to select a relevant case type and provide all the necessary details related to the incident (including any relevant documentation) by filling out a contextual form driven by an organizational case template. A case manager is then able to view the case in the context of any relevant data about that employee (driven by security within our single application for Human Capital Management) and take appropriate action in a timely manner.

EH27.comments: Workday delivers Incident/Injury Tracking. Workday does not deliver pre-configured functionality to track additional Employee Health items in the system. However, you do have the ability to extend the Worker business object with custom fields. We have customers who have used custom objects and fields to track this information. Workday’s Request Framework can also be leveraged to create custom business processes for desired acknowledgements and/or approvals.

EH28.comments:
IBM MAS maintains a fully auditable record of hazard closure actions by logging key details including closure dates, responsible parties, and supporting evidence such as documentation or images. Each resolved hazard can be directly linked to related inspections, maintenance activities, or work orders, providing transparent traceability from initial identification through completion. Robust audit trails ensure all records of edits, updates, and closures are preserved, fulfilling regulatory and internal compliance requirements. This comprehensive documentation enables organizations to demonstrate accountability, meet standards for regulatory audits, and uphold best practices in hazard management and closure tracking.
Workday tracks safety incidents, notes, files and involved parties. Notifications can be sent to the appropriate people.

EH29.comments:
MAS hyperlinks to external SharePoint documents by adding the direct SharePoint URL to a hyperlink control within  Application Designer, which requires obtaining a valid shareable link from the SharePoint document library and configuring it to allow access. These hyperlinks are configured with a target URL and can be dynamically built using bind fields, allowing users to click from MAS directly to the SharePoint file after obtaining the proper SharePoint access credentials.

The advanced search provides additional parameters to find specific changes. Depending on the where the asbestos records are kept will determine if this is possible.

Automated notifications and escalations ensure important deadlines are monitored and addressed proactively.

Recurring inspections and compliance tasks are directly linked to work orders, ensuring timely execution and accurate record-keeping. This approach streamlines compliance management, reduces risks of non-compliance, and supports transparency for audits and regulatory reviews.
In Workday, custom objects and fields could potentially leveraged for this process.

EH38.comments: IBM MAS provides robust support for compliance management and operational scheduling. Organizations can create and track compliance-related deadlines, such as regulatory reporting dates and permit renewals, by leveraging MAS’s work order and notification features. These critical deadlines are easily associated with assets, locations, or compliance records, and automated alerts help ensure key dates are not missed.

EH39.comments:
MAS enables recurring inspection schedules through its preventive maintenance and scheduling modules. Users can set up inspection and compliance tasks with configurable frequencies—such as annual, quarterly, or monthly—and assign responsible parties. Due dates for completion are tracked within MAS, ensuring routine activities are managed effectively and accountability is clearly assigned.
While Workday does not have a dedicated "inspection schedule" feature, you can configure recurring processes and reports to manage inspection schedules effectively. This approach allows you to schedule, track, and notify responsible parties of upcoming and overdue inspections.

EH40.comments:
To further streamline operations, MAS supports highly configurable task frequencies. Scheduling templates allow organizations to tailor activity intervals to meet specific regulatory or operational needs, accommodating changes in compliance requirements or internal policies over time.

EH42.comments: IBM MAS supports the management of environmental datasets across programs like air, water, soil, sustainability, and compliance through its flexible asset management platform. It enables structured data capture, validation, and integration with external systems, while configurable applications and business rules ensure accurate, consistent data recording. MAS provides historical tracking and audit trails for environmental records, linking them to assets and work orders for comprehensive oversight. Robust reporting and dashboard tools allow organizations to analyze trends, support regulatory planning, and make informed decisions. Overall, MAS centralizes environmental data management for improved compliance and sustainability program effectiveness. Workday has several solutions around Environmental, Social, Governance (ESG). The Supplier Sustainability packaged solution was designed to support the Environmental aspect of ESG compliance. This solution enables customers to capture scope 1, 2 & 3 GHG Inventory; survey suppliers to determine if they have science-based targets, what those targets are, and their actual carbon footprint to incorporate sustainability in supplier selection; calculate scope 3 emissions with minimal data movement by combining third-party emissions factors and actual spend data in Workday; prioritize sustainable suppliers by mapping third-party data such as SBTi, EPA carbon factors, ESG risk ratings and emissions for reporting; incorporate sustainability data in supplier decisions and track supply chain sustainability against target with pre-built reports that leverage supplier spend, SBT, and emissions data.

EH57.comments:
IBM MAS can securely receive structured environmental data—such as spreadsheets, forms, and inspection records—from external systems through its robust integration capabilities. Using the MAS Integration Framework, organizations can exchange data in industry-standard formats (XML, JSON, CSV) via web services (SOAP, REST), secure file transfers, or direct API connections. Middleware and ETL tools often help validate, transform, and standardize incoming data, ensuring it fits MAS’s business objects and compliance needs. Security features, including encryption, authentication, and audit logging, help protect sensitive data during transfer and integration. Bulk import tools also allow direct data loads from CSV or XML files. By adopting standardized data templates and automated validation, businesses streamline data flow and maintain integrity. This approach centralizes disparate environmental metrics within MAS, supporting advanced reporting, regulatory compliance, and efficient asset management. Integration solutions are configurable to suit each organization's workflow and security requirements.

Users can either use the Integration Designer to automatically connect to source systems or use the file import utility. The integration framework automatically connects to cloud-based solutions, behind-the-firewall, or local Excel files.

CM02.comments:
Workday tracks notes, files, involved parties, and conversations using safety incidents functionality. Workday Help, our case management tool, facilitates conversations between employees and internal experts to manage the input, allocation, and resolution of HR-related cases.
Throughout the entire Workday platform, all business processes that impact a worker's record capture the full audit trail for each update. This means customers can easily see the date and timestamp of every user and the updates they made for each step in the business process. 
Workday provides the ability to reopen closed cases, and all actions are captured for audit purposes. Here’s a breakdown of how it works:
Reopening a Closed Case
    ● Employee/Case Creator: An employee can reopen a case they created within seven days of it being resolved. If a case creator adds a comment to a resolved case, the assigned Case Solver is notified and can choose to reopen it.
    ● Case Solver: A Case Solver can reopen a case up to seven days after it has been closed.
    ● Automatic Reopening: The system does not automatically reopen a case when a creator adds a new message.
Audit Tracking
Workday maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all case activities. To review the audit history of a case, you can use the following general procedure:
    1. Locate the case in question.
    2. From the related actions menu of the case, select Audits > View Audit Trail.
    3. You can then specify a date range to view all changes, including who made the change and when.
This audit trail includes, but is not limited to:
    ● Case creation
    ● Comments and internal notes
    ● Status changes (e.g., from 'Resolved' to 'In Progress')
    ● Changes to the assigned solver or service team
It's important to note that Workday retains all case data indefinitely for legal and auditing purposes.

CM03.comments:
Workday provides a mechanism to correct records for a business process that has already been completed, such as a closed case. This is done using the Correct action.

It is important to distinguish this from reopening a case. Reopening a case changes its status and allows the regular workflow to continue. The Correct action is an administrative function used to fix erroneous information on a transaction that is already considered complete, without changing its completed status.

CM04.comments:
Workday Help is a case management solution for HR teams consisting of two main elements: the Knowledge Base and the Case Management capability. Cases can be classified according to defined categories and sub-categories, which are managed by the customer. HR teams have access to previous enquiries and can produce reports to identify trends so that they can improve self-service information and add new content. For example, if an employee has an issue not covered by standard COVID-19 information, they can raise a case. Once HR has completed the case, they can decide whether to add those circumstances to the existing documentation. Once the case is resolved, a survey can be sent to measure employee experience and provide qualitative feedback. Workday Talent Optimization includes a configurable template for tracking disciplinary actions. Actions can be initiated by a manager, can follow your defined business process, and can include required steps and notifications, as well as e-signatures by the employee and manager.

Workday Case Management is highly configurable and allows you to create and track a wide variety of case types, including those related to performance issues, workplace concerns, and accommodations.

Key Configuration Components

The system's flexibility comes from the ability to define custom Case Types and Case Categories. This allows you to tailor the case management system to the specific needs of your organization.

    ● Case Types: This is the highest-level classification for a case. You can create distinct case types for different HR functions. For example, you could create case types such as:
        ● Employee Relations
        ● Performance Management
        ● Reasonable Accommodations
        ● Benefits Inquiry
        ● Payroll Discrepancy

CM05.comments:
Workday provides Mass Actions functionality to allow data to be collected and reported in an excel-like Worksheet within Workday, mass updates applied and then leverage the Workday Business Process Framework to trigger any required business process approvals, actions etc. Workday also provides Enterprise Interface Builders (aka EIBs) to provide excel-like templates to allow data to be collected and reported outside of Workday, mass updates applied and then import back into Workday, where the Workday Business Process Framework can again trigger any required business process approvals, actions etc.

Workday does not offer a standard feature to perform a wide range of bulk actions like status changes, reassignments, and archiving on multiple cases simultaneously using a single spreadsheet upload.

However, Workday does have a powerful Mass Actions framework that utilizes Worksheets (a spreadsheet-like interface) to process events in bulk for specific business processes. This capability is primarily used for HCM transactions, such as:

    ● Change Organization Assignments
    ● Terminate Employee
    ● End Contingent Worker Contract
    ● Change Job

While this framework exists, Case Management is not listed as a business process that is enabled for this general Mass Actions functionality.

Alternative for Bulk Approvals

For the specific action of approving cases, Workday does provide a bulk option:

    ● Bulk Approve from Inbox: A user can select multiple approval tasks directly in their My Tasks inbox and approve them all at once.
    ● Approve Business Process EIB: You can use an Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB) to upload a spreadsheet containing the IDs of multiple business process events (like cases) to approve them in bulk. This requires the user running the EIB to be a valid approver for the tasks.

These methods are limited to approvals and do not support other bulk actions like changing a case status to "In Progress," reassigning cases to a different team, or archiving.

CM06.comments:
Workday provides robust functionality for customers to create their own configurable case types and custom fields directly within the tenant, without needing to engage Workday or another vendor for assistance.

This self-service capability is a core part of the Workday platform and is managed through the use of Custom Objects.

CM07.comments: Workday provides a unique employee ID for every employee, and this ID remains an attribute for that employee, regardless of their location, status, or changes to their profile. Workday also offers a Unified Duplicate Management Framework to configure match criteria to surface potential duplicates for a candidate.

CM08.comments:
Workday has extensive capabilities for reporting and analysis on an employee's various group memberships and roles, which can be used for classification and analysis. This is achieved through Workday's flexible organizational structures and its powerful reporting framework.

Tracking Employee Workgroups

In Workday, an employee's "workgroup" can be represented by several different objects, all of which are reportable:

    ● Organizational Roles: Every employee belongs to a Supervisory Organization, and their position is associated with other organization types like Cost Centers, Locations, and Regions. You can report on all of these assignments.
    ● Workteams: You can use Workteams to create and track flexible, cross-functional groups of people that span geographic and organizational boundaries. This is a leading practice for tracking project teams, squads, or Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). You can report on Workteam membership and an employee's status within the team (e.g., Active, Inactive).
    ● Committees: Workday allows you to formally track membership in various types of committees, such as corporate, regulatory, or special subcommittees.
    ● Talent Pools: You can group employees into Talent Pools based on specific criteria for development, succession planning, or special projects. Membership in these pools is fully reportable.

Tracking Representation Status

An employee's representation status is typically tracked through their assigned roles and security group memberships.

    ● Assignable Roles: You can report on the specific roles a worker holds within any organization, such as Manager, HR Partner, or Matrix Manager. The standard report Role Assignments for Worker provides a detailed overview of these assignments.
    ● Security Group Membership: You can easily report on which user-based and role-based security groups an employee belongs to. This is useful for understanding a user's access rights and their functional role within the system.

Reporting and Analysis

Workday provides a robust set of tools to pull this information for analysis:

    ● Standard Reports: Workday offers numerous standard reports to view this data, including: 
    ● Custom Reporting: You can create powerful custom reports using data sources like All Active Workers or Workers for HCM Reporting. 

By combining these objects in a single custom report, you can create detailed datasets for classification, auditing, and analysis.

CM09.comments:
Workday provides the ability to manually enter and override employee demographic data, which is particularly useful for managing records of former employees or individuals who were not part of an integrated data load.

This is primarily managed through the Former Worker object and associated tasks.

CM10.comments:
Workday is designed to maintain a complete history for each employee, and this includes all cases associated with their record, even after they have left the organization.

How It Works

The ability to maintain this history is based on a core concept in Workday's architecture: the persistence of the Worker Profile.

    ● Permanent Worker Record: When an employee is hired, a permanent worker record is created. All transactions and associated data, including any cases created by or for that employee, are linked to this single record.
    ● Termination Process: When an employee leaves the organization, their status is changed from "Active" to "Terminated." The worker record itself is not deleted. It is preserved with its entire history.
    ● Retained Case History: Because the case data is tied to the persistent worker record, the full case history remains accessible. Authorized users, such as HR partners or case managers, can still view and report on cases associated with a terminated employee, subject to their security permissions.

Distinction for Former Workers

It's important to distinguish between employees terminated within Workday and "Former Workers":

    ● Terminated Employees: These are individuals who were active in Workday and were subsequently terminated. Their complete history, including cases, is stored in Workday.
    ● Former Workers: These are individuals who left the organization before Workday was implemented. Typically, only a basic set of their historical data is loaded into Workday's "Former Worker" object. Any case history for these individuals would reside in the legacy system that was in use prior to Workday.

In summary, for any employee who has had an active profile in Workday, the system ensures that their entire case history is maintained and accessible on their record indefinitely, providing a complete and auditable trail of all interactions.

CM11.comments: Workday delivers various options for handling non-employees, including hiring the individual into Workday as a Contingent Worker and setting up their employee sub-type as a non-employee.

CM13.comments: Yes, there are multiple configuration options for cases and Workday Help to align with your business needs. Case Management Flags, Case Statuses, Help Case SLAs and Service Teams can be configured to track case status, history, and aging.

CM14.comments: Workday Help enables HR to receive, process and track enquiries. The employee or HR person can attach attachments, contextual insights or internal notes to the case, as well as tag anyone else who needs to be involved.

CM15.comments:
In Workday, this capability is managed through Workday Help, which leverages the Business Process Framework to control the lifecycle of a case.

You can configure the business process to define steps and routing for case intake, assignment, and resolution.

Workday Help enables HR to receive, process, and track enquiries. Cases can be classified according to defined categories and sub-categories, which are managed by the customer. Workday Help is supported by our Business Process Framework ensuring customer rules and processes are followed in each case.

CM16.comments:
Workday Human Capital Management provides an Integration Framework as part of the unique, adaptable Workday foundation, to allow data to be easily imported and exported between Third Party systems, such as Recruiting/Applicant Tracking systems. Workday provides core connectors and templates to provide a basis for integration to be configured (during deployment), which can therefore allow internal candidates to be provided access with Third Party Recruiting/ATS systems, allow job and compensation data to be shared etc., as well allow for the upload of Candidate Hires into Workday. As the ATS has managed the candidate job application, Workday is able to import the Candidate information as data fields (vs a document file) into a Pre-Hire Worker record with Workday (Workday provides this worker type to specifically manage the stage between Hire and Official Start Date) and automatically converts the Pre-Hire to an Employee worker record upon official start date. Note: When using Third Party ATS systems, all Candidate data, history and documents are retained within the Third Party ATS (not Workday).

Workday is designed to be the single source of truth for all employee data, and it has robust capabilities for importing and managing demographic and personal information. This is a fundamental part of any Workday implementation.

Here’s how Workday handles the integration and import of demographic data for employee records:

Key Integration Methods

Workday provides several methods to import employee data, each suited for different scenarios:

    1. Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): This is the most common tool for bulk data loading.EIBs use a pre-formatted spreadsheet template (based on a corresponding web service) that you populate with your employee data. You then upload this spreadsheet into Workday, and the EIB process creates or updates the employee records. 
    2. Workday Studio: This is a more powerful, developer-oriented tool for complex integrations. Workday Studio is an integrated development environment (IDE) that allows you to build sophisticated integrations with custom logic, transformations, and connections to multiple sources or destinations. 
    3. Core Connectors: These are pre-built integration templates designed for specific third-party systems or standards. Core Connectors provide a pre-configured starting point for common integrations, such as those for benefits carriers or payroll providers. You configure the connector to meet your specific requirements.

CM17.comments: Yes, Workday Help, our case management tool, enables companies to manage the input, allocation and resolution of HR-related cases by facilitating those conversations between employees and internal experts. This is a case management solution that ties in with the full power of the Workday platform. Workday enables the attachment of any electronic document to an employee’s record. There are two means of attaching documents to workers within Workday: Workday enables the attachment of documents to a worker's record as a general attachment. When attached as a general attachment, the document is not saved in association with a specific business process. Alternatively, a document can be attached within a business process. Documents attached within a specific business process will be shown in association with the business process that they support. In either case, all documents will be shown in the worker’s document file and security can be setup by document category to determine who has access to view the document for the worker. There are no limitations on the number of documents on an employee's record.

CM18.comments:
Workday provides a Document Framework, allowing a library of documents to be managed (across various document types) that can be associated with worker profiles and/or included to support any business process. Workday also allows for easy upload of external documents to worker profiles. All Workday documents leverage the always on audit functionality as part of the Workday Business Process Framework. All Documents that a worker views and/or accepts are visible within the worker profile, with the actual document, user login and date/time that the document was added.

Workday Help is designed to allow for the management of documents throughout the entire lifecycle of a case, even after it has been created or closed.

This functionality ensures that a complete and accurate record is maintained, allowing users and solvers to add relevant information or documentation as it becomes available.

How to Manage Documents on a Case

Here are the steps to add or manage documents on an existing case:

    1. Navigate to the Case: Access the relevant case by searching for it or selecting it from a worklet or report.
    2. Select the Documents Tab: On the case record, click on the Documents tab. This will display all documents currently attached to the case.
    3. Add or Manage Documents:
        ● To Add a New Document: Click the Add button. You will be prompted to upload the file from your computer and can add comments or tags if needed.
        ● To Manage an Existing Document: Use the related actions icon next to a document in the list to perform actions such as viewing, downloading, or deleting it (permissions permitting).


Key Considerations

    ● Lifecycle Access: Documents can be added by the case creator, the assigned solver, or any other user with the appropriate permissions at any stage, including when the case is In Progress or Closed.
    ● Audit Trail: All document uploads and changes are tracked in the case history, providing a complete audit trail of who added what and when.
    ● Security: The ability to view, add, or manage documents on a case is controlled by the Functional Areas for Case Management security domain in the System functional area. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access sensitive attachments.

Even after a case is closed and most of its fields (like category or status) are no longer editable, the system still permits adding comments and documents. This is crucial for situations where follow-up information or related documentation needs to be recorded with the original case for future reference.

CM19.comments:
Yes, there are multiple configuration options for cases and Workday Help to align with your business needs. The following can be modified and reported on: Case Management Flags, Case Statuses, Help Case SLAs, and Service Teams. The service team or individual within a service team, user-defined cases statuses (scheduled, unscheduled, etc.) prioritization, and response time based on SLAs may all be reported on to capture who might be responding to cases, their frequency, their volumes, and SLA/response times.

Workday provides a suite of standard, out-of-the-box reports for case management that cover the key operational metrics listed.

These are often referred to as Standard Reports in Workday. They are pre-built and delivered with the Workday Help application to provide immediate value and insight without requiring any initial configuration.

Standard Reports for Case Management

You can expect to find standard reports that cover the following areas:

    ● Open/Closed Case Counts: Reports like "Cases by Status" provide a quick overview of how many cases are currently in an "Open," "In Progress," "Closed," or other custom status. This is fundamental for tracking overall workload and backlog.
    ● Staff Assignment: You will find reports such as "Cases by Solver Group" or "Cases by Assignee" that show how cases are distributed across different teams and individual agents. This is essential for monitoring workload balance and identifying potential bottlenecks.
    ● Volume by Category: Reports like "Cases by Category" allow you to see which types of issues are being reported most frequently. This is a critical input for trend analysis, helping you identify areas where more training, documentation, or process improvement may be needed.

CM20.comments:
Workday delivers the ability to report across all data held in the system. Reports detailing trends by department, issue type, and representation status can be generated as needed in Workday.

With Workday you have the ability to create custom reports that analyze trends across various dimensions is a core strength of the Workday platform. Workday is not limited to pre-defined reports; it provides a powerful, built-in Report Writer that allows you to build sophisticated analyses from the ground up to meet your specific needs.

CM21.comments:
Dashboard functionality is standard in Workday. Workday provides over 100 dashboards with the full Workday solution. In addition, customers can create an unlimited number of custom dashboards. Organizations can easily add new default analytics, called Worklets to any dashboard. Individual users can then configure additional reports onto the dashboard.

A key strength of the Workday platform is its ability to create highly configurable dashboards that provide real-time operational metrics. This is not a rigid, pre-built feature but a flexible framework that allows you to tailor dashboards to the specific needs of your audience, such as HR leaders, legal teams, or case managers.

This is achieved by combining two of Workday's most powerful tools: the Workday Report Writer and Dashboards.

CM22.comments:
Workday Human Capital Management provides country-specific diversity data definitions (such as ethnicity, personal identifiers), as well as gender and compensation grade profiles, to allow for country specific reporting requirements alongside other worker data such as employment type and management level. Reporting compensation in relation to worker demographics (i.e. ethnicity, age group, and gender) is seamless.

Workday is not just a system with a set of pre-built reports; it is a comprehensive reporting platform that allows you to create highly configurable reports to match the specific layouts, data points, and logic of any central system.

Workday's ability to serve as the "single source of truth" relies on its capability to present that truth in whatever format is required for consumption by other systems or for comparison with legacy data.

Here is a breakdown of the tools and concepts that enable this functionality:

1. The Workday Report Writer

This is the core engine for all reporting in Workday. It is a powerful, built-in tool that allows you to create custom reports from scratch without needing to know SQL or have database administration skills.

2. Calculated Fields

This is the most powerful tool for ensuring a report can match the logic of another system. Calculated fields allow you to create new, custom fields within Workday without programming. You can derive data, transform existing data, and apply business logic.

3. Custom Report Layout and Formatting

Once you have the right data (using standard and calculated fields), the Report Writer gives you full control over the presentation:

4. Integration-Readiness

These highly configurable reports are the foundation of Workday's integration capabilities.

    ● Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): You can use a custom report as the data source for an outbound EIB. This allows you to generate a file (e.g., a CSV) with the exact columns, headings, and data formats required by a downstream "central system" and deliver it on a schedule.
    ● XSLT Transformations: For more complex formatting requirements, you can apply an XSLT transformation to the report's XML output to create a file in virtually any text-based format.

By combining the Report Writer, Calculated Fields, and control over output formatting, you can build demographic reports in Workday that are not just similar to, but are a precise, field-for-field replica of, the reports from your central system.

CM23.comments:
Workday delivers a non-employee role with minimized access already configured based on best practices. The customer has the ability to modify this role as needed to meet their organization policies and needs. Non-employee roles might include roles (with appropriate security access/limitations) for contingent workers (contractors, subcontractors, etc.), retirees, external learners, and external applicants.

Workday is designed to be highly configurable and can absolutely be set up to operate in an "internal-only" mode for case management.

By default, Workday's security is restrictive. Access to any task or data must be explicitly granted. Therefore, achieving an internal-only model is a matter of not granting self-service access to the general employee population, rather than having to disable pre-existing features.

Here is how you would configure Workday Help to operate exclusively for internal teams (like HR, Legal, Payroll, etc.) to create and manage cases among themselves or on behalf of employees, without any employee-facing features.

CM24.comments:
Workday leverages role-based security to determine access to different functionalities and information. Security can be configured to trigger against roles and groups to ensure each user has relevant access.

The ability to restrict case access based on user roles is a fundamental and powerful capability of Workday's security framework, especially as it applies to Workday Help.

This is not achieved through a single setting but by combining several components of Workday's security architecture to create a robust, contextual, and role-based access model.

Here is a breakdown of how this is accomplished:

Core Components for Role-Based Case Security

    1. Case Categories: This is the primary element used to drive security. You create distinct categories for different types of cases (e.g., "HR Policy Inquiry," "Payroll Discrepancy," "IT Support Request," "Legal Investigation"). Each category can have its own unique security configuration.
    2. Security Groups: These groups define the "roles" or populations of users. Workday uses many types of security groups, but for this purpose, the most common are:
        ● Role-Based Security Groups: These are dynamic groups that include all users assigned to a specific role (e.g., all "HR Partners," all "Managers," all "Payroll Administrators").
        ● User-Based Security Groups: These are manually created lists of specific individuals. This is ideal for small, static teams like a "Legal Department" or an "Ethics Committee."

    3. Domain Security Policies: This is where the configuration comes together. Domains represent a set of related tasks or data (e.g., View: Case, Manage: Case Documents, Reassign: Case). You configure the security policy for a domain to specify which security groups have access, and you can make this access conditional on the Case Category.

CM25.comments:
Workday has robust security capabilities that allow for the creation and handling of fully private cases, accessible only to designated personnel, such as a legal team.

This is a critical function for managing sensitive employee relations, investigations, or other confidential matters. The privacy is not a separate feature but is configured using Workday's standard, powerful security framework.

CM26.comments: Workday provides "always on auditing" throughout the entire system. All change-effecting actions are tracked within Workday by default, and the audit trails for any object within Workday can be accessed for further review by individuals with the correct security permissions. Workday does not provide a way to “turn-off” audit trails within the system, and Workday customers do not have to take any additional steps to enable and/or maintain the audit trails. They are maintained and provided automatically as part of your Workday subscription.

CM27.comments: Workday enables you to manage the lifecycle of help cases through case purging. This allows you to permanently delete cases to comply with your organization's data retention policies. While Workday provides the tools to define and execute retention rules, the process itself is manual and not fully automated.

SC01.comments:
The Workday platform is fundamentally designed as a system of record, and it fully supports the requirement to maintain historical schedule and time data for long-term periods to meet legal, union, and internal policy needs.

Workday's architecture ensures that this data is not only stored but is also auditable and accessible. The retention period is not limited by the system; data is retained indefinitely unless you explicitly choose to purge it.

Workday provides the flexibility to meet your data retention requirements. Here is a summary of how historical schedule data is managed and how you can configure retention.

Data Retention in Workday

Workday does not impose a maximum retention period for customer data, including historical schedule records. Your organization has control over how long data is stored in the tenant. Data is retained for the lifetime of the tenant unless your organization actively purges it using Workday's data management tools.

This approach allows you to define your own data retention policies in line with legal, union, and grievance process requirements.

SC02.comments:
Workday maintains a full history of all events including position assignment and compensation changes. Workday is an always-on audit application. Also, Workday Time Tracking supports retroactive worked time and time entries, as well as Time Offs and Leave of Absence.

This capability is a cornerstone of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are all part of a single system, it can seamlessly track historical changes and automatically calculate the financial impact of those changes with full auditability.

SC03.comments:
Workday Scheduling provides a range of configurable scheduling settings that allow various warnings and/or errors for various conditions to be triggered. Examples include minimum breaks between shifts, minimum shift length, maximum shift length, maximum daily hours, maximum weekly hours, required meals and breaks, consecutive days and overtime thresholds.

This is a fundamental capability of Workday's Human Capital Management (HCM) and Time Tracking/Absence platform. The system is designed to create, assign, and maintain a detailed and historical record of each employee's standard work schedule.

This functionality is critical, as it forms the foundation for numerous other processes, including payroll, leave accruals, and resource planning.

SC04.comments:
Workday provides visibility for all worker and business process changes as part of the always-on audit controls. Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time and Workday Scheduling which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs actual time reporting. Workday Time Tracking leverages the worker data already held within Workday, including the Core Compensation Pay Rates, Absence Plans and Job Data, which allows this data to be available for base rates natively.

This capability is a core strength of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are not separate systems but are all part of a single platform, it provides unparalleled real-time visibility and seamless reconciliation between scheduled work, exceptions, and final pay.
