This real-time, integrated process eliminates the manual reconciliation work common in systems with separate time and payroll modules, significantly reducing errors and ensuring that employees are paid accurately based on their approved schedules and exceptions.

SC05.comments:
Workday's business process framework supports the complete cycle for probation periods. This includes the ability to configure specific conditions for probationary periods based on contract duration, type of employment, previous employment, region, collective agreements and more. Specific business processes are delivered for Manage Probation Period, Pre-Hire Probation Period Details, and Review Probation Period.

Workday can support this requirement through a combination of standard features and configurable components. While the standard probation period functionality is typically based on a set duration (e.g., 90 days), you can use calculated fields and alerts to create a process based on actual hours worked.

Standard Probation Period Functionality

Workday's native probation period feature allows you to track a period of time from an employee's hire or start date. You can configure alerts to notify managers and other stakeholders when a standard, duration-based probation period is coming to an end.

However, this standard feature is based on a calendar duration (e.g., days, months) and does not natively track a cumulative total of hours worked.

Leading Practice for Hours-Based Probation

To meet the requirement of a probation period based on actual hours worked, you can leverage the power of Workday Time Tracking and Calculated Fields. This approach involves creating a mechanism to count the hours and trigger a notification once the threshold is met.

SC06.comments:
Yes, the system can track continuing education units and certifications within the worker profile in Workday Core HCM. Workday Learning supports configurable Unit Types, which allows customers to define their accreditation units (Professional Development Units, CEUs, Credits, certification units) within the properties of a course with flexibility.

Workday is well-equipped to track both continuing education requirements and certifications. This is primarily managed through the Talent and Performance and Workday Learning modules.

SC07.comments:
Workday Scheduling, Time Tracking, and Absence Management work as a unified system. Time off requests and approvals update balances dynamically. Schedules integrate with timekeeping for variance reporting and accurate payroll.

These capabilities represent a core and fundamental strength of the Workday platform. Workday's single architecture, where HCM, Scheduling, Time Tracking, Absence, and Payroll all exist within one system, is specifically designed to provide this seamless integration and real-time visibility.

Workday's architecture is designed to provide seamless integration between scheduling, timekeeping, and absence management to ensure data accuracy and streamline payroll processes.

Integration of Scheduling, Timekeeping, and Payroll

Workday provides a single, unified system for Workforce Management that includes Workday Scheduling, Workday Time Tracking, and Workday Payroll. This integration ensures a smooth and accurate flow of data.

    ● Schedule to Timecard: When schedules are created in Workday Scheduling, they serve as the baseline for an employee's timecard in Workday Time Tracking. As employees report their time (e.g., via clock-in/clock-out or manual entry), the system automatically compares the actual hours worked against the scheduled hours.
    ● Variance Tracking: This direct comparison allows the system to identify and flag schedule variances in real-time. Examples include:
        ● Overtime: Time worked beyond the scheduled shift or weekly threshold.
        ● Unscheduled Absences: A "no-show" for a scheduled shift.
        ● Early/Late Punches: Deviations from the scheduled start and end times.

    ● Flow to Payroll: Once timecards are approved by a manager, the accurately calculated hours—including all variances, overtime, and shift differentials—flow directly into Workday Payroll. This eliminates the need for manual data transfer, significantly reduces the risk of payroll errors, and creates a clear audit trail.
    ● Reporting: Because all data resides within the same system, you can generate real-time reports and dashboards that provide visibility into scheduled vs. actual hours, overtime costs, and other key labor metrics.

Vacation Requesting and Leave Balance Integration

Workday also provides tight integration between vacation requests, managed in Workday Absence Management, and employee leave balances.

SC08.comments:
Workday supports effective dated tracking of union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. These elements are attributes of the employee and can be used throughout the entire system for compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, and reporting. Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for assigning Workday Scheduling tags based on specific qualifications, assessments, and/or experience. A worker or position may be associated with a wide variety of attributes such as job profile, location, cost center, manager, default weekly hours, shift, and status.

This is a fundamental principle of Workday's architecture and a primary benefit of its unified platform. The scheduling system is not a separate module that needs to be integrated with HCM; it is an intrinsic part of the same system, operating on the same single source of truth.

This seamless access to core employee data is what allows Workday to make intelligent, compliant, and efficient scheduling decisions, rather than just placing names in time slots.

SC09.comments:
Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time, and scheduling, which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs. actual time reporting. Workday can manage shift differential eligibility by setting up standard schedules, for example, based on geography (country) or other criteria. These shifts can have different pay rates based on the shift or base plus a premium for hours worked on a shift or eligible times of day. Shift differential calculations are configured to ensure workers working in the specified timeframes get the right level of pay. Multiple shift differential calculations can be combined, for example, when different pay rates apply from 10pm to 12am and from 12am to 4am.

Workday doesn't just integrate its scheduling, timekeeping, and payroll systems; they are all components of a single, unified system operating on one source of employee data. This distinction is crucial because it means there is no "transmission" or "transfer" of data between separate systems. Instead, it's a seamless, real-time flow of information within one platform.

SC10.comments:
While Workday does not offer pre-built, certified connectors specifically for Vector Solutions or InTime, it provides a robust and flexible integration platform that can be used to build custom connections to these or any other third-party systems.

If you find that the native Workday Scheduling capabilities do not fully meet your Public Safety requirements, you can develop a custom integration to exchange schedule, assignment, and availability data.

Workday Integration Tools

Workday offers several tools to facilitate custom integrations. The choice of tool depends on the complexity, data volume, and real-time needs of the integration.

    ● Workday Studio: This is Workday's most powerful integration tool, providing a graphical development environment for building complex, custom integrations. Workday Studio would be the leading practice for creating a sophisticated, bidirectional integration with systems like InTime or Vector Solutions. It can handle complex data transformations, custom logic, and error handling.
    ● REST and SOAP APIs: Workday provides a comprehensive library of web services that allow external systems to securely access and update Workday data. You can develop a custom solution that calls Workday's APIs to:
        ● Get Data: Pull worker information, availability, leave requests, and other relevant data from Workday to send to the external scheduling system.
        ● Put Data: Push approved schedules, assignments, and actual hours worked from the external system back into Workday for timekeeping and payroll processing.

    ● Workday Cloud Connect: This is a framework for building packaged integrations. While there isn't a pre-built Cloud Connect package for these specific vendors, the underlying technology could be used by a development team to create a reusable connector.
    ● Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): EIBs are a user-friendly tool for getting data into and out of Workday. They are ideal for simpler, bulk data transfers that can be scheduled to run on a recurring basis. An EIB could be used for less time-sensitive data exchanges, such as sending a daily file of approved hours from the external system to Workday.

In summary, if you need to integrate with Vector Solutions or InTime, it is technically feasible. The project would involve a custom development effort utilizing Workday's integration tools, with Workday Studio and the extensive API library being the most likely components of a successful solution.

SC11.comments:
Workday provides a fully integrated solution to validate training and certification status for shift eligibility by leveraging the seamless connection between Workday Scheduling, Workday Learning, and the Talent and Performance module.

Workday ensures employees have valid certifications and correct credentials for their roles by issuing and renewing certifications based on successful completion of learning content. Certification rules allow configuration of required learning content and completion frequency. Workday provides reporting capabilities around active, inactive, and expiring certifications.

SC12.comments:
While Workday does not provide pre-built, certified integrations for the wide array of specialized first responder software and applications (such as Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) or Records Management Systems (RMS)), it is designed with a powerful and flexible integration platform to make these connections possible through custom development.

The leading practice for integrating Workday with specialized third-party systems is to leverage Workday's suite of integration tools to build a solution tailored to your specific needs. This allows you to use best-of-breed applications for specific first responder functions while maintaining Workday as the system of record for all HR, time, and payroll data.

SC13.comments:
Workers absence can be sent through integrations to EAM for EAM to flag work as "cannot be completed"
Yes, Workday is designed to integrate with external systems like an Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) system to facilitate the exact scenarios you've described. While there isn't a pre-built, certified connector for every EAM on the market, Workday's robust integration platform provides the tools necessary to build a custom, seamless connection.

The leading practice is to use Workday as the system of record for all employee data, including absences and availability, and to build an integration that allows the EAM to query this information in real-time.

SC14.comments: Workday provides a delivered task for Request Time Off that can allow workers to easily submit a request for time off and view balances and history. Workday Scheduling provides the ability for workers to swap shifts.

SC15.comments: Workday leverages the Business Process Framework as part of the core platform, with delivered business processes for entry and correction for time and the ability to trigger notifications and/or approvals as needed. Users can approve, deny, delegate or re-assign any approvals that they receive via their Workday My Tasks notifications or via the Review Time Dashboard. Additionally, Workday Scheduling allows for Manager initiated shift-swaps and allows for approvals of Staff member initiated shift-swaps.

SC16.comments: Workday provides hard-stop alerts for Workday Scheduling settings where needed. This can prevent a user from adding a shift if rules are breached. Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

SC17.comments:
Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements. Workday provides a hierarchy of scheduling orgs, which can allow for visibility across various orgs vs specific orgs when needed.

Workday Scheduling is designed to help organizations create, manage, and publish schedules for their workers. Key features that support this include:

    ● Schedule Creation: You can create schedules based on predefined scheduling patterns or build them on an ad-hoc basis.
    ● Position-Based Scheduling: Schedules can be assigned directly to positions, which is useful for roles with consistent shift coverage needs.
    ● Publishing: Once schedules are finalized, they can be published, making them visible to employees through their Workday profiles and the Workday mobile app.
    ● Flexibility: The tool is designed to support the scheduling needs of diverse teams, from hourly workers in retail or facilities to salaried staff with more fixed schedules.
    ● Integration: It is tightly integrated with Workday Time Tracking and Absence Management to provide a complete view of worker availability and automatically calculate hours worked against their schedule.

SC18.comments:
Workday Scheduling can be configured to provide 24/7 coverage with varying shift lengths and start/end times. Configurable scheduling tags allow shift specific role types to be included to support scheduling requirements, ensuring shifts and workers are tagged with appropriate capabilities to support workforce needs. Configurable scheduling settings allow for enforcing of specific organizational rules, such as minimum/maximum shift lengths and required breaks/meals per shift, as well as worker preferences.


Workday's scheduling solutions are designed to handle complex scheduling requirements, including 24/7 coverage with varied shift lengths and start/end times. This is managed through a combination of Work Schedule Calendars, Workday Scheduling, and the optional Workday Labor Optimization add-on.

    ● Workday Scheduling: This solution is ideal for organizations with hourly workers who have relatively static or consistent schedules. It allows you to create schedules manually or automatically based on previous periods. The system validates schedules against business rules, regulatory requirements, and worker availability, preferences, and qualifications.
    ● Workday Labor Optimization: For businesses with more fluctuating labor demands, this AI-powered add-on to Workday Scheduling optimizes schedules to ensure the right workers with the right skills are in the right place at the right time. It helps balance business needs, labor costs, and worker preferences.

SC19.comments:
May want to consider a third-party and may recommend Labor Optimization upon further discovery of your specific needs in this area, but Workday Scheduling is an intelligent scheduling solution that automatically generates schedules and assigns workers to shifts based on worker availability, preferences and qualifications. 

Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements. Workday can be configured to handle pay codes needed for the organization including types such as overtime, double time, and shift premiums. 

Workday's scheduling functionality is centered on assigning schedules to individual workers rather than assigning a shift to a team or squad as a single, collective unit. However, you can achieve efficient team-based scheduling and coverage management with it.

    ● Provides fundamental tools to create and manage schedules
    ● Automatically generates schedules based on prior period schedules. Automation is geared toward schedules that are static or consistent in nature and don’t fluctuate greatly week to week
    ● Assigns shifts by taking into account availability, preferences, regulatory rules but doesn’t automatically adjust the structure of the shifts themselves
    ● Provides tools to allow managers or scheduling administrators to manage/adjust/edit schedules seamlessly
    ● Provides tools for workers to set availability and preferences
    ● Provides tools for workers to open, cover and swap shifts
    ● Provides visibility into gaps in coverage and unassigned shifts
    ● Schedule score and recommended workers are limited to worker preferences and availability

Workday Scheduling provides capabilities to manage coverage and overtime when backfilling is required.

    ● The system can identify unassigned shifts or coverage gaps.
    ● When a manager needs to fill a shift, Workday Scheduling can recommend replacement workers based on their qualifications, availability, and preferences. 
    ● Workers can also view and bid on open shifts through an Open Shift Board, providing another mechanism for backfilling.

SC20.comments:
Workday Scheduling enables the tagging of shifts and workers with appropriate capabilities to support workforce needs. Configurable scheduling settings allow for enforcing specific organizational rules, such as minimum/maximum shift lengths and required breaks/meals per shift, as well as worker preferences.

You could potentially track special missions through the use of Worktags as well. Additional discovery would be advised to better understand your needs.

    ● Workday Scheduling provides the foundational tools to manage schedules while considering various constraints.
        ● It allows for the creation of schedules, either manually or automatically, based on rules, compliance requirements, and worker availability, preferences, and qualifications. This ensures that only personnel with the correct skills are assigned to specialized tasks.
        ● You can schedule workers across multiple positions, work functions, and locations, which is ideal for managing special missions that may occur at various sites.
        ● Workers can view and manage their schedules, swap shifts with qualified colleagues, and pick up open shifts, providing flexibility while maintaining operational readiness.


With the addition of Labor Optimization, you can use AI to generate workers to fit a shift. Workday Labor Optimization is an AI-powered add-on that enhances the core scheduling product.

    ● For organizations with fluctuating demands, it automatically generates optimized schedules to ensure the right workers with the right skills are in the right place at the right time.
    ● This is particularly useful for assigning personnel to SVOs by intelligently suggesting the best-fit workers based on a holistic view of business objectives, labor costs, and compliance rules.

Further discovery is recommended to determine if Labor Optimization is necessary.

SC21.comments:
Workday Scheduling engages workforce managers and operations leads, by providing an easy to use, dynamic scheduling tool to allow schedules to be quickly generated and to handle dynamic schedule changes for emergencies. Managers can use the My Team's Schedule report to make immediate, ad-hoc changes directly on the schedule calendar. This allows them to:

    ● Instantly add or edit shifts to deploy workers to an incident.
    ● Cancel existing shifts to reassign personnel from their regular duties.
    ● Find available and qualified workers to fill critical gaps.

These changes are temporary overrides that don't affect the workers' base schedules. You can use Schedule Tags (e.g., "Emergency Response") on these shifts to simplify tracking, reporting, and applying premium pay for the incident. If additional staff are needed, managers can use the system to identify qualified and available workers. The Replace Worker functionality can suggest suitable replacements for backfilling non-critical roles, considering factors like availability and qualifications.

You can create and apply specific Schedule Tags to these dynamic shifts (e.g., "Emergency Response," "Mutual Aid," "Incident Command"). This is critical for:

    ● Reporting: Easily track and report on all hours and costs associated with a specific incident.
    ● Payroll: Automatically apply premium pay or different pay rules for hours worked during the declared emergency.
    ● Automated Notifications: When a manager adds or changes a worker's shift, the system can automatically send a notification to the worker's mobile device or Workday inbox, ensuring rapid communication of the new assignment. 

Real-time insights also allow workforce managers to react quickly to labor cost and productivity KPI impacts as schedules are being determined and/or worked.

SC22.comments:
Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements.

To ensure leadership coverage during all operational hours through rotating command duty officers, you can leverage Calendar Events in Workday. This approach is effective for creating complex, rotating schedules that do not follow a standard weekly pattern.

Here is a general procedure to set this up:

    1. Create a Calendar: Establish a new calendar specifically for the command duty officer schedule.
    2. Create a Calendar Event: For the first shift in the rotation, create a calendar event. This event will represent the 'on-duty' period for a specific officer or role.
    3. Define Recurrence: Within the calendar event, set up the recurrence pattern. This is the key step to automate the rotation. You can define the cycle length (e.g., every 20 or 30 days) and the duration of the pattern.
    4. Assign Workers: Assign the relevant command duty officers to this calendar. Their schedules will then reflect the rotating events you have created.

This method allows you to create a complete, recurring cycle for the rotating duty, which can be set up with or without a specific end date.

SC23.comments:
Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements. 

Workday supports complex, crew-based scheduling for organizations like fire departments through its Workday Scheduling module, which is part of the broader Workforce Management solution.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system addresses your specific requirements:

    ● Crew-Based Scheduling and Apparatus Assignment: The system allows you to define specific "posts" or "jobs" that can represent an apparatus (e.g., Engine 1, Truck 2). You can then create schedules that assign entire crews or teams to these specific posts for their shifts.
    ● Shift-Based Rotation: Workday Scheduling is designed to handle complex rotations. You can configure rotating shift patterns and apply them to your crews, ensuring that teams are correctly assigned to their apparatus and location according to the established rotation.
    ● Location Tracking: By assigning crews to an apparatus/post, which is tied to a work location, the system effectively tracks where each team is scheduled to be during their operational hours.
    ● Assignment to Multiple Apparatus: The system is flexible, allowing for individuals to be assigned to different posts or apparatus. For example, a firefighter could be scheduled on an engine for one shift and a rescue unit for another, depending on qualifications and operational needs. This is often managed through the Scheduling to Posts functionality.

These capabilities, combined with Time Tracking and Absence Management, provide a centralized solution for managing schedules, tracking time, and ensuring proper staffing levels across all apparatus and locations.

Note: Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

SC24.comments:
Workday can forecast vacation hours to support processes like annual vacation bidding. The system includes a Projected Balance feature. This allows an employee or manager to view a time off balance as of a future date.

When a future date is selected (e.g., the end of the next year), Workday calculates the total time off that will be available on that date. This calculation includes all future accruals that are scheduled to occur between the present and the selected future date.

This gives employees a clear view of their full annual entitlement, allowing them to confidently bid on vacation slots for the entire year, even for time they have not yet technically accrued.

Workday provides visibility for all eligible Time Off plans and associated balances. Time Off/Vacation balances are dynamically updated as time off/vacation is requested/approved and based on effective dates, so a worker could determine what their vacation balance will be as of a future date less already approved vacation dates.

SC25.comments:
With shift bidding, an unlimited amount of workers can bid on the same shift from the Open Shift Board and managers can decide which worker to select to cover the shift.

Workday supports shift bidding, which can be configured to meet the needs of organizations like Public Safety where seniority is a key factor in awarding shifts.

The feature, known as Open Shift Bidding, allows workers to bid on available shifts posted on the Open Shift Board. Managers are then able to review the bids and select a worker to cover the shift.

A key aspect for Public Safety is that when a manager reviews the bids, the system provides details about each worker's seniority. This allows the manager to make an informed decision and award the shift based on established seniority rules. It is important to note that the system provides the seniority information to the manager, who then makes the final selection; it does not automatically award the shift to the most senior bidder.

SC26.comments: Managers and administrators can input time via timesheets for any employee for which they have security access. Workday provides audit trails that record when a transaction was approved on behalf of someone else.

SC27.comments:
Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements.

Workday Scheduling provides the flexibility to create a wide variety of shift configurations to meet different operational needs.

Here’s how it supports the patterns you mentioned:

    ● Rotating Schedules: These are managed using Schedule Patterns. You can define a sequence of work shifts that repeats over a specified number of weeks, and then assign this pattern to workers.
    ● Split Shifts: You can define a Work Shift to include non-contiguous blocks of time. For example, you can create a single shift for a day that includes a work block from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM and another from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM.
    ● Fixed Assignments: This is a standard capability where workers are assigned a consistent schedule that does not change week to week. This can be managed by assigning a simple, non-rotating Schedule Pattern or by directly assigning shifts.

In addition to these, you can also use scheduling tags to identify workers with specific skills for certain shifts and define labor requirements to ensure adequate coverage.

SC28.comments:
Workday Scheduling engages workforce managers and operations leads, by providing an easy to use, dynamic scheduling tool to allow schedules to be quickly generated and updated based on changing organizational needs. Workday accommodates seasonal or operational mode-based schedule variations effectively.

The leading practice is to create separate Work Schedule Calendars for each distinct operational period (e.g., a "Peak Season" schedule and an "Off-Season" schedule).

When the operational mode changes, an administrator or manager uses the Assign Work Schedule task to assign the new calendar to the relevant workers with an effective date. This can be done in bulk for entire teams or locations using an EIB (Enterprise Interface Builder), making the transition efficient and predictable.

Configurable scheduling tags allow shift specific role types to be included to support scheduling requirements, ensuring shifts and workers are tagged with appropriate capabilities to support workforce needs.

SC29.comments:
Somewhat supported. Workday Scheduling provides a worker-first approach that allows worker needs to be included in scheduling decisions. Workers can set their preferences for working hours, locations and/or shift type, set their non-availability, take time-off and have the opportunity to easily pick up, drop or swap shifts when needed.

    ● Workers can use the Change My Schedule Preferences task to indicate their preferred working hours, days, and locations. Managers and the scheduling engine can then use this information when creating schedules.
    ● They can also use the Change My Work Availability task to formally communicate their availability to work.

Further discovery recommended, but setting up worker preferences and availability, coupled with the ability to swap shifts and put a shift on the open shift board may suffice. Further discovery needed to know for sure.

SC30.comments:
Workday handles specialized duties within a shift schedule effectively, primarily by using Schedule Tags. Workday provides configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and/or to workers. This allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements for specialized duties such as court appearances, investigations, inspections, and training events. 

This approach provides three key benefits:

    ● Visibility: The event is clearly labeled on the schedule, showing what the worker is assigned to do and blocking that time from other assignments.
    ● Reporting: You can easily run reports filtered by these tags to track time and costs associated with specific activities.
    ● Payroll: Schedule tags can be used in time calculations to automatically trigger different pay rates, premiums, or overtime rules associated with that specific duty.

Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

SC31.comments: Workday provides hard-stop alerts for Workday Scheduling settings where needed. This can prevent a user from adding a shift if rules are breached. Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

SC32.comments: Yes. Workday Scheduling allows for Manager initiated shift-swaps and allows for approvals of Staff member initiated shift-swaps.

SC33.comments:
Workday Scheduling provides configurable scheduling tags, which can allow workers to be assigned specific shift types to manage Higher Duties. 

Workday Time Tracking also provides the ability to select a higher duties time code for hours worked. Time entry codes describe the types of time workers can enter, such as Worked Time, Training, On Call etc. Time code groups control who can use a set of time entry codes. Workers can use the time entry codes in their time code group, as well as the default time entry code assigned to their time entry template. Based on different dimensions, (e.g. roles, location, company division) different time entry types can be made available.

SC34.comments: Yes, Workday provides delivered business processes for requesting time off and leave of absence, both of which allow for conditional approvals (e.g. can be different by leave type, job role, location, etc.). Managers can review and approve time off requests via any device, including the Workday mobile app, which provides device notifications that users expect of other consumer apps like Facebook and LinkedIn. Workday notifications can also be pushed to email.

SC35.comments:
Workday Scheduling settings allows for enforcing of specific organizational rules, such as minimum/maximum shift lengths and required breaks/meals per shift, as well as worker preferences. Workday Time Tracking managers can proactively track approaching overtime and overtime reached by employees.

Yes, that is a core capability of Workday's workforce management solution, which combines Workday Scheduling and Workday Time Tracking.

Here is how the system addresses those specific compliance and fatigue management needs:

    ● Overlapping Shifts: When creating or editing schedules, Workday automatically flags shifts that overlap for the same worker, helping managers avoid scheduling conflicts.
    ● Overtime Triggers: You can configure scheduling validations and time calculations to manage overtime.
        ● Daily and Weekly Overtime Thresholds: You can set thresholds for the maximum number of hours a worker can be scheduled in a day or week. When a schedule exceeds this limit, Workday flags the excess hours as overtime.
        ● Time Calculations: For payroll purposes, you can create Standard Overtime Calculations in Workday Time Tracking. These calculations automatically identify and tag hours that exceed daily, weekly, or biweekly thresholds, ensuring accurate overtime pay.

    ● Excessive Hours and Fatigue Management:
        ● Maximum Shift Length: You can define the maximum duration for a single shift.
        ● Minimum Rest Between Shifts: You can set a required minimum rest period between scheduled shifts to help prevent worker fatigue.
        ● Maximum Working Days: You can specify the maximum number of consecutive days a worker can be scheduled to work in a week.


These rules are configured as Scheduling Validations and can be applied to specific groups of workers using eligibility rules. When a manager builds a schedule that violates one of these rules, Workday displays a validation error, alerting the manager so they can make corrections.

SC36.comments:
Workday supports the effective dated tracking union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. As attributes of the employee, these elements can then be used throughout the entire system for things like compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, reporting, etc. Workday also provides Workday Scheduling settings that can allow for enforcing of specific Workday Scheduling rules, including maximum hours worked per day (with alerts for associated overtime). Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

Workday provides the flexibility to configure and enforce complex union-negotiated rules through a combination of its scheduling and time-tracking features.

    ● Schedule Validations: This is the core enforcement engine. You can build rules that prevent managers from publishing a schedule that violates union policies, such as assigning a shift to an unqualified worker, scheduling insufficient rest time, or exceeding weekly hour limits.
    ● Shift Assignments and Bidding: Eligibility for shifts and bidding is managed by checking worker attributes. Validations can be configured to ensure a worker has the required qualifications, certifications, or seniority before they can be assigned to or bid on a specific shift.
    ● Overtime Rotation: Workday uses Time Calculations to continuously track accumulated overtime for each worker. Managers then use custom reports, sorted by these hours (e.g., from least to most), to ensure they offer overtime to the correct person in the rotation, adhering to equalization rules.
    ● Call-Out Procedures: Similar to overtime, you can create custom reports that act as the official "call-out list." These reports are configured to order employees based on specific union rules (e.g., lowest overtime hours, last call-out date), ensuring managers follow the negotiated procedure for unscheduled needs.

SC37.comments:
Workday provides a range of configurable scheduling settings that allow various warnings and/or errors for various conditions to be triggered. Examples include minimum breaks between shifts, minimum shift length, maximum shift length, maximum daily hours, maximum weekly hours, required meals and breaks, consecutive days and overtime thresholds.

Workday handles mandatory holdovers and voluntary shift fills by combining schedule tags with custom reporting to ensure fairness and compliance.

    ● Tracking the Events:
        ● Mandatory Holdovers: When a worker is required to stay past their shift, a manager makes an ad-hoc edit to their schedule, extending the shift and applying a "Mandatory Holdover" schedule tag.
        ● Voluntary Fills: When a vacancy is filled by an employee who volunteers, the shift they pick up (often from the Open Shift Board) is marked with a "Voluntary Fill" schedule tag.

    ● Ensuring Equitable Distribution:
The system continuously tracks the hours associated with these tags for each employee. To ensure equitable distribution, you create custom reports that serve as live "equalization lists." These reports rank employees based on the specific union rules, such as:
        ● Lowest total overtime hours.
        ● Fewest mandatory holdovers year-to-date.
        ● Seniority or rotation order.


When a manager needs to assign a holdover or offer voluntary overtime, they consult this ranked report to identify the correct employee. This process provides a clear, data-driven, and auditable method for complying with union agreements. The tags also ensure the hours are paid at the correct rate in Workday Payroll and can also carry over into Workday Finance for visibility into labor costs for the organization (if desired).

SC38.comments:
Workday provides configurable time calculation rules and time totals, which can allow worked time to be split into relevant time codes according to union provisions or department-specific policies. 

Workday provides the functionality to track and balance overtime assignments to ensure fair distribution, although it is not a single, automated feature but rather a configured process combining several Workday modules. The process primarily leverages Workday Time Tracking and Workday Reporting.

SC39.comments:
Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

Workday restricts shift assignments based on certifications and training status by seamlessly integrating employee qualifications from Workday HCM with its Workday Scheduling module.

Here is a brief description of the process:

    1. Maintain Certifications in HCM: All employee certifications and qualifications (e.g., CPR, firearms, specific equipment training) are maintained within the employee's profile in Workday HCM. This includes tracking issuance and expiration dates, ensuring the data is always current.
    2. Define Requirements on the Shift: When creating a schedule, supervisors can define the specific certifications or skills required for a particular shift or role. For example, a shift can be configured to require an active "Firearms Qualification" certification.
    3. Automated Matching and Restriction: The Workday Scheduling engine uses this data to enforce the rules automatically:
        ● During Schedule Generation: The system will only consider and assign employees who hold the necessary, unexpired certifications for that specific shift.
        ● Finding Replacements: If a supervisor needs to fill a vacant shift, the system will only suggest available employees who meet the defined certification requirements.


This ensures that only qualified and compliant personnel are assigned to roles that require specific training or credentials, preventing manual errors and maintaining operational readiness.

SC40.comments:
Workday's Absence Management provides a comprehensive view into a worker's use of various absence plans, ranging from vacation and sick plans to defined leaves of absence. By taking advantage of Workday's robust calculation engine, Workday Absence Management enables organizations to define and manage both their accrued time off and leave of absence plans. Workday’s Absence Management can be used seamlessly with Workday Payroll or as part of Workday HCM.

Workday's Absence Management enables workers to submit absence requests that can follow a defined path for approval. Workers and managers can view worker's leave balances and the history of their leave requests through self-service. Once a request is approved the worker's balance is automatically decremented, and the leave information is shared with payroll for calculation purposes.

Workday's Absence Management provides the following high-level functionality:

    ● Highly flexible and configurable set of rules to define eligibility criteria
    ● Configurable accrual policies and accrual limits
    ● Configurable negative balance limits
    ● Configurable rules for carryover allowances
    ● Flexible calculation period schedules

Workers are able to:

    ● Create absence or time off requests through self-service on a desktop or mobile device
    ● View time off balances through self-service, including calculation of balances as of a future date
    ● View history of time off or absence requests
    ● View approved team time off

SC41.comments: Workday provides block out absence periods, which can restrict team members from being able to request time off during specific time periods when needed (can also provide limits to allow some team members but not all if needed).

SC42.comments:
Workday enforces minimum staffing and role-specific coverage primarily through Schedule Validations.

The process works in two main parts:

    1. Defining Roles and Thresholds:
        ● First, you use Schedule Tags to identify workers with specific roles, skills, or qualifications (e.g., "Paramedic," "Supervisor," "Hazmat Certified").
        ● Next, administrators create Schedule Validation rules for each department or scheduling organization. These rules define the minimum number of workers with specific tags that must be scheduled for any given shift (e.g., "At least 2 Paramedics" and "1 Supervisor" must be scheduled for the Day Shift).

    2. Enforcing the Rules:
        ● When a manager creates or edits a schedule, these validation rules run automatically in the background.
        ● If the manager tries to publish a schedule that violates a rule (e.g., only one paramedic is scheduled), the system will generate a real-time warning or error. This prevents the understaffed schedule from being finalized until the coverage gap is resolved.


This provides managers with immediate feedback and ensures that every shift is published with the correct number and type of qualified staff as defined by the department.

SC43.comments:
Workday has the ability to handle fatigue risk management through configurable rules and alerts within the Workday Scheduling and Workday Time Tracking modules.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system manages this:

    1. Configurable Rules and Validations:
        ● Workday allows you to create a robust set of Schedule Validations and Time Calculations to monitor for conditions that could lead to fatigue. These rules are highly configurable to match your specific safety policies or contractual requirements.
        ● Key rules that can be configured include:
            ● Maximum Hours: Set limits on the number of hours an employee can be scheduled in a day or week.
            ● Maximum Consecutive Days: Define the maximum number of days an employee can work in a row.
            ● Minimum Rest Between Shifts: Enforce a required rest period (e.g., 10 hours) between the end of one shift and the start of the next. This is handled via a Minimum Rest time calculation.
            ● Shift Length: Validate that individual shifts do not exceed a specified duration.


    2. Alerts for Supervisors:
        ● When a supervisor is creating or modifying a schedule, Workday's scheduling engine runs these rules in real-time.
        ● If a proposed schedule violates a fatigue-related rule, the system provides an immediate visual alert directly on the scheduling interface.
        ● These alerts are typically categorized by severity, such as a Warning (yellow) for a soft limit or a Critical (red) alert for a hard limit, making it easy for supervisors to identify and address potential fatigue risks before the schedule is finalized and published.


By using this framework, you can proactively identify and mitigate scheduling-related fatigue risks, ensuring compliance with safety standards and promoting employee well-being.

Workday provides hard-stop alerts for Workday Scheduling settings where needed. This can prevent a user from adding a shift if rules are breached. Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

SC44.comments:
Workday provides robust functionality to track and enforce contractually defined limits on union leave, including specific uses like union meetings. This is managed primarily through the Workday Absence Management module.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system handles this:

    1. Create a Dedicated Time Off Plan:
        ● First, you would configure a specific Time Off Plan for union-related activities (e.g., "Union Meeting Leave" or "Union Business Leave"). This separates it from other leave types like vacation or sick time.

    2. Define Contractual Limits with Accruals:
        ● Next, you create an Accrual rule linked to this plan. This rule defines the contractually stipulated limit—for example, it can automatically grant each eligible union member a balance of 40 hours at the beginning of each year.

    3. Control Eligibility:
        ● Eligibility Rules are used to automatically assign the Time Off Plan and its corresponding accrual only to the specific employees covered by the union contract. This can be based on factors like their job profile, location, or a custom organization assignment for union membership.

    4. Enforce Limits with Validations:
        ● To enforce the limit, you configure a Validation Rule on the Time Off Plan. This rule checks the employee's available balance when they request leave. If the request exceeds their remaining balance, the system can be configured to:
            ● Block the request, preventing the employee from submitting it.
            ● Issue a warning message to the employee and their manager.


    5. Track and Report on Usage:
        ● Managers and HR administrators can view real-time balances and usage on employee profiles. Furthermore, you can create custom reports and dashboards to monitor union leave usage across the entire organization, providing clear visibility for compliance and labor relations management.

SC45.comments: Workday provides Open Shift Board functionality to allow shifts to be backfilled by workers when needed (leveraging the Business Process Framework, requests can be submitted for approval/acceptance by Schedulers). With shift bidding, an unlimited amount of workers can bid on the same shift from the Open Shift Board and managers can decide which worker to select to cover the shift. Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

SC46.comments:
Workday has the capability to manage on-call schedules and can be configured to provide alerts when on-call staff are activated. This is achieved through a combination of Workday Scheduling and the Workday Alerts framework.

Here is a brief explanation of how this is handled:


Handling On-Call Scheduling
You can manage on-call assignments directly within Workday's scheduling tools. The leading practice is to use Work Schedule Calendars to define on-call shifts or rotations.

    ● On-Call as a Time Code: You would typically create a specific Time Entry Code (e.g., "On-Call Standby") to represent the hours an employee is scheduled to be available.
    ● Scheduling On-Call Shifts: Supervisors can then create schedules that include these "On-Call Standby" shifts and assign them to employees, ensuring there is a clear record of who is on-call and when.


Alerts for On-Call Staff Activations
While Workday doesn't have a single "Activate On-Call" button, you can configure a robust notification process to trigger when an activation occurs. The "activation" is typically recorded when an employee, who is scheduled for on-call duty, clocks in or enters actual worked hours.

The process works as follows:

    ● Step 1: Create a Custom Report: First, you would build a custom report that identifies the activation event. This report would be designed to find employees who have both an "On-Call Standby" time block and an overlapping "Regular Hours" or "Call-Out" time block on their timesheet for the same period.
    ● Step 2: Configure an Alert: Next, you use the Configure Alert task to create a notification that is driven by the custom report.
        ● Trigger: The alert is triggered whenever the report finds a new instance of an on-call activation.
        ● Recipients: You can define who receives the notification, such as the employee's manager, a department head, or a payroll administrator.
        ● Message: The notification message can be customized to provide relevant details (e.g., "On-call activation for [Employee Name] at [Time]").


It is important to note that the timing of the alert depends on the scheduled Run Frequency of the alert rule (e.g., hourly, daily), so it provides a near-real-time, automated notification of the activation event.

SC47.comments:
Workday addresses this through the integrated use of Workday HCM (Human Capital Management) and Workday Scheduling.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system accomplishes this:

    1. Tracking Qualifications: Specialized qualifications and certifications, such as Paramedic, HAZMAT, or SWAT, are maintained within the employee's profile in Workday HCM. This creates a central, reliable record of each person's skills. 
    2. Defining Shift Requirements: When building a schedule, supervisors can define the specific qualifications required for each shift. For example, a shift can be designated as requiring a certified paramedic. 
    3. Intelligent and Automated Staffing: Workday Scheduling uses an AI-powered engine that matches the shift's requirements against the employee's qualifications.
        ● When automatically generating schedules, the system will only assign workers who possess the necessary certifications to the specialized roles.
        ● This ensures that each shift is properly staffed with qualified personnel from the outset.

    4. Alerts and Coverage Gaps: If a shift requiring a specialized skill becomes vacant (due to time-off, a call-out, or an unfilled spot), the system identifies this as a coverage gap. It then alerts the supervisor and automatically suggests a list of available and appropriately qualified replacement workers to fill the role, ensuring continuous and compliant coverage.

SC48.comments:
An integration can be built to the Emergency Response system.
While Workday does not have a single, dedicated feature for emergency staff recall, it provides several tools that can be configured to initiate mass notifications, track responses, and facilitate the creation of emergency rosters.

Here is a brief description of the system's capabilities:

    ● Mass Notifications:
        ● Alerts and Notifications: You can use the Configure Alert task to send notifications to a dynamic list of employees based on a custom report. For example, you could create a report to identify all "on-call" certified personnel and trigger an alert to their Workday inbox and work email.
        ● Distribute Tasks: The Mass Distribute Documents or Tasks feature can be used to send a To Do task to a large group of employees, which serves as a direct notification and a call to action.
        ● Announcements: For broader, less targeted communication, an Announcement can be posted to the Workday home page.

    ● Response Tracking:
        ● The most direct way to track responses is by using the To Do task mentioned above. When an employee completes the task (e.g., "Acknowledge Recall" or "Confirm Availability"), the completion is logged in Workday, allowing supervisors to run reports and see who has responded.

    ● Emergency Rosters:
        ● Workday does not automatically populate an emergency roster based on responses. However, the response tracking data provides the necessary information for supervisors to act quickly. A manager can view a report of all employees who have confirmed their availability and then manually assign them to the required shifts or schedules.


For critical emergency communications, especially via SMS, the leading practice is to integrate Workday with a dedicated third-party emergency notification system. In this model, Workday acts as the system of record for employee contact information, feeding it to the specialized service that handles the mass SMS distribution.

SC49.comments:
Workday automates callout notifications by combining a custom report with a configured Business Process (BP).

    1. The "Callout List" Report: First, a custom report is built to act as the live, ranked callout list. This report is the core of the logic. It automatically:
        ● Filters for workers with the required role qualifications (certifications, skills, etc.) for the vacant shift.
        ● Sorts that eligible list based on configurable union rules, such as lowest year-to-date overtime hours (for rotation) or by seniority date.

    2. The Automated Notification BP: When a manager needs to fill a shift, they initiate a specific business process (e.g., "Fill Vacant Shift").
        ● The BP automatically sends a notification to the first person on the ranked callout list, offering them the shift with "Accept" or "Decline" options.
        ● Response tracking is built-in. If the worker declines or doesn't respond within a set time, the BP automatically sends the notification to the next person on the list.
        ● This cycle continues down the ranked list until a worker accepts the shift, at which point the system assigns them the shift and stops the process.


This creates a fully auditable, hands-off process that ensures the correct, most eligible person is always contacted first, strictly following the negotiated rules.

SC50.comments: Workday includes a system of reminders and alerts that can be incorporated into the Business Process Framework. Customers can also generate configurable alerts based on a Custom Report. These alerts can be configured to send relevant business information to users based on any condition for which you can create a report in Workday. The alert can be set to run immediately, or once on a specified date and time in the future, daily, weekly, or monthly, and is sent to Workday My Tasks notifications, via e-mail, or mobile device.

SC51.comments:
Workday provides dynamic, real-time coverage views for managers, primarily through the Time and Scheduling Hub. This hub acts as a live dashboard. The central component is the My Team's Schedule report, which presents a visual, calendar-based view of the entire team's schedule.

Because Workday uses a unified system for Absence, Time, and Scheduling, this view updates instantly when events occur:

    ● Time-Off: When a leave request is approved in Workday Absence, the worker's schedule is immediately blocked off, showing them as unavailable.
    ● Call-Outs: When a manager records a call-out (typically by deleting the worker's shift for the day), that shift is instantly removed from the schedule.
    ● Emergency Events: If a worker is deployed to an incident, the manager's ad-hoc schedule change appears on the calendar in real-time.

This gives managers an immediate, up-to-the-minute picture of their staffing levels. They can instantly see who is available, who is not, and where new coverage gaps have appeared, allowing them to take quick action to find replacements and maintain operational readiness.

SC52.comments: Workday Scheduling provides Schedulers with real-time insights. The Intuitive Scheduler experience provides Schedulers with AI driven suggested worker recommendations, real-time insights, configurable layouts and configurable scheduling tags and alerts to ensure the right workers are assigned to the right shifts. It will give you an error message to ensure no gaps, overtime, or double-bookings appear in shift assignments.

SC53.comments: Workday Absence Management provides the ability for employees, managers and administrators to view leave balances, based on their security permissions. Leave balances can be viewed at a past, present or future date. Furthermore, with the Workday reporting framework we deliver summary reports for managers or HR to get full transparency of vacation balances.

SC54.comments: Workday Talent Optimization includes a configurable template for tracking disciplinary actions. Actions can be initiated by a manager, can follow your defined business process, and can include required steps and notifications, as well as e-signatures by the employee and manager.

SC55.comments:
Workday directly supports roster-based scheduling, allowing supervisors to assign employees to shifts using predefined templates and rotation cycles. This is managed through Work Schedule Calendars.

Here is a brief description of the capability:

    ● Schedule Patterns & Templates: Supervisors can create Work Schedule Calendars that contain predefined Schedule Patterns. These patterns act as reusable templates, defining shifts (start/end times, meals) for a set number of weeks. This is ideal for creating standard weekly rosters or multi-week rotation cycles (e.g., 4 days on, 2 days off).
    ● Rotation Cycles: For more complex or longer rotations, you can configure recurring Calendar Events within a Work Schedule Calendar. This allows you to build out patterns that repeat over many weeks or a specific number of days (e.g., a 10-day rotating schedule).
    ● Assignment: Once a template or rotation is defined in a Work Schedule Calendar, a supervisor can easily assign it to an employee using the Assign Work Schedule task. This allows for efficient and consistent application of rosters across teams. This functionality is a core part of Workday Scheduling.

SC56.comments: Workday provides an intuitive Scheduler experience with drag-and-drop scheduling.

SC57.comments:
Workday provides advanced capabilities to forecast future staffing needs by analyzing historical data, seasonal patterns, and special events. This is primarily accomplished through Workday Adaptive Planning and Workday Labor Optimization.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system supports this:

    ● Workday Labor Optimization: As an add-on to Workday Scheduling, this tool is designed for industries with dynamic staffing needs, such as retail and hospitality.
        ● It uses Artificial Intelligence (AI) to generate optimized schedules based on fluctuating labor demand.
        ● A key feature is its ability to forecast business demand by ingesting external data like historical sales figures, foot traffic, and special event calendars.
        ● By understanding these patterns, the system can accurately predict the number of staff required, ensuring that you have the right people with the right skills scheduled at the right time to meet demand.
        ●  

    ● Workday Adaptive Planning: This is Workday's primary tool for strategic workforce planning.
        ● It integrates data from Workday HCM, Financials, and external sources to create comprehensive forecasting models.
        ● It features a Predictive Forecaster that uses machine learning (ML) to analyze historical time-series data (like past sales, transaction volumes, or foot traffic) to predict future headcount demand.
        ● This allows you to model different scenarios, understand the financial impact of staffing decisions, and proactively plan for recruitment and scheduling based on data-driven insights.


In summary, Workday leverages a combination of strategic planning with Adaptive Planning and AI-driven schedule optimization to provide robust, data-driven forecasting for future staffing requirements.

SC58.comments: Workday Application Security allows you to align security permissions to organizational structures / hierarchies, called Assignable Roles. Role-based security groups are a very essential and powerful way of granting needed access in Workday. Workday delivers a set of Assignable Roles such as Manager and HR Partner out-of-the-box. Customers can adjust these delivered security groups and/or create their own security groups / Assignable Roles.

SC59.comments: Workday provides configurable Scheduling orgs which allows for reporting and management of rostering locations and configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers, which allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements. Workday can automatically capture the default location associated with time punches corresponding to tasks performed, time spent on projects, and locations associated to the worker. If a worker reports to multiple locations, they can choose what location when punching in and geofencing makes sure they are within the range of that location.

SC60.comments:
Partial. Workday Time Tracking does not provide offline connectivity, however leveraging an Amazon Web Services (AWS) in-country tenant and opting in to the downtime exclusion for Time Tracking will provide extensive uptime for users and should reduce the impact and need for offline connectivity.

The Workday Time Kiosk works even when offline so that workers can record their time during local network or Workday scheduled downtime. Data stored on the kiosk when it's offline is automatically transmitted to your tenant when you reconnect the app to the internet. 

Here is a brief description of how it functions:

    ● Offline Operation: The Workday Time Kiosk is an application designed to be installed on a tablet (iOS or Android). It can continue to capture employee check-ins and check-outs even during a network outage or scheduled Workday system downtime.
    ● Local Data Storage: When the kiosk is offline, it securely stores the time clock events as encrypted data directly on the tablet. To use the kiosk in offline mode, a worker must have previously checked in on that device while it was online within the last 30 days.
    ● Automatic Synchronization: Once the tablet's internet connection is restored, the Time Kiosk application automatically transmits the stored offline data to your Workday tenant. This ensures that all time entries are synchronized without manual intervention.

This process provides a reliable method for capturing time for field-based personnel, ensuring that work hours are recorded accurately even when a network connection is unavailable.

SC61.comments: Workday provides configurable Scheduling tags that can be associated with shifts and to workers. This allows for creating various matching combinations of shifts and workers to meet rostering requirements for specialized duties such as court appearances, investigations, inspections, and training events. Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

SC62.comments: Workday Scheduling enables customers to create optimized schedules in seconds through a powerful AI-based optimization engine. Workday is also unique in that the system simultaneously generates the schedule and assigns workers based on business and worker preferences and availability, vs. first creating a schedule and then assigning workers.

SC63.comments:
Somewhat supported. Workday's scheduling tools are designed to manage workforce schedules, including those for project-based work. There are two main components:

    ● Workday Scheduling: This allows for the manual or automatic creation of schedules. It takes into account various factors such as:
        ● Worker availability, preferences, and qualifications.
        ● Business rules and regulatory compliance.
        ● Scheduling across multiple positions, jobs, and locations.
        ● The ability for workers to view and manage their schedules, including swapping shifts. 


Workday's Project Time Entry functionality (within Workday PSA) allows for the tracking of time against specific projects and project plan tasks. 

    ● Time Entry Against Projects: Employees can enter their time directly against multiple projects or specific tasks within a project plan.
    ● Filtering by Assignment: The system can be configured to filter the projects and tasks available for time entry based on the employee's specific project assignments.
    ● Respect for Dates: Time entry respects the start and end dates of resource plan lines and project tasks. You can configure the system to enforce these dates, preventing time from being logged outside of the planned duration.
    ● Approval Workflows: Project managers can review and approve project time, ensuring accuracy and providing oversight.
    ● Reporting and Analytics: Workday provides reporting capabilities to analyze project time, compare scheduled versus actual hours, and monitor project costs.

Here is a summary of the key capabilities that address your specific needs:

    ● Assigning Schedules: You can manually assign work schedules to individual workers or use automated rules. The Assign Work Schedule task allows you to specify start and end dates.
    ● Project-Based Time Tracking: Workers can be restricted to entering time only for the projects and tasks to which they are assigned.
    ● Visibility into Dates: The system provides visibility into project and task start and end dates, and these dates can be used to control time entry.
    ● Flexible Scheduling: Workday supports various scheduling scenarios, from static, recurring schedules to highly variable and project-based assignments.
    ● Reporting: reports to view worker schedules, project time, and analyze variances between scheduled and actual hours.

To implement this, you would typically configure Work Schedule Calendars, Time Entry Templates that are enabled for project time entry, and utilize the project and resource planning features within Workday PSA.

SC64.comments: Workday Scheduling provides a mobile application for workers, allowing them to leverage the same consumer-grade Workday Mobile App and utilize device features such as touch screen and in-app notifications. Workday provides a hierarchy of scheduling orgs, which can allow for visibility across various orgs versus specific orgs when needed. As Workday is a single system, Time Off and Leaves of Absence visibility is held within Workday and visibility is provided natively within rosters/schedules.

SC65.comments: Workday provides a hierarchy of scheduling orgs, which can allow for visibility across various orgs versus specific orgs when needed.

SC66.comments:
Workday automates real-time notifications for all schedule changes. This is a core function of its Business Process Framework.

When a manager makes any change that affects a worker's schedule—such as assigning a new shift, approving a swap, or making an ad-hoc change for an emergency—the system automatically triggers a notification to the employee.

These notifications are delivered through multiple channels, and users can typically configure their own preferences:

    ● In-App Alert: A notification appears in the worker's inbox directly within the Workday web or mobile application.
    ● Mobile Push Notification: An alert is sent directly to the worker's smartphone via the Workday app, which is ideal for frontline staff.
    ● Email: A notification is sent to the worker's registered email address.

Customers can create notification templates to suit their needs, with emails triggered by our Dynamic Business Processes for flexible timing and frequency. Notification Designer enables customization of response appearance. SMS and text messaging is supported by using Workday Messaging or by leveraging a SMS partner.

SC67.comments: Yes. Headcount and organization reports and views are delivered as standard with Workday, giving customers the ability to configure the values included. Additionally, our unique inline analytics will allow your users to pivot information to obtain views from different reference points. Organizational charts within Workday are also configurable. For example, your organization can specify a set of fields that are presented in each organization chart and whether matrix reports, contingent workers, and open positions should be included in the chart view.

SC68.comments: Workday provides consumer grade reporting and insights for leaders, leveraging the unique and adaptable Reporting Framework, with transactions and reporting in the same place, leaders are provided with 3 dimensional drill-down (vs only 2 dimensional available with complex legacy relational data models) to allow leaders to slice and dice the data within Workday (avoiding the need to extract to Third Party Reporting tools) and then also take action.

SC69.comments:
Yes, this functional requirement is supported in Workday.

Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time and scheduling which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs actual time reporting.

SC70.comments:
Yes, this functionality requirement is supported. 

Workday has the ability to produce reports showing unfilled shifts. This is a fundamental feature of Workday Scheduling designed to give managers clear visibility into staffing gaps.

How Unfilled Shifts are Managed and Reported

In Workday, an unfilled shift is known as an Open Shift. These are shifts that are required for operational coverage but do not yet have a worker assigned.

Here are the primary ways to view and report on them:

    1. Manager's Scheduling View:
        ● The most direct way to see unfilled shifts is within the scheduling interface itself. Managers viewing the schedule for their team will see "Open Shift" placeholders on the calendar for any required shifts that are not assigned.
        ● This view provides real-time visibility into coverage gaps, allowing managers to take immediate action, such as assigning an eligible worker or publishing the shift for workers to claim.

    2. Custom Reports:
        ● You can create custom reports in Workday to list all open or unfilled shifts across one or multiple departments.
        ● The report would be built using a scheduling-related data source and would typically filter for shifts that have a status of "Open."
        ● Key fields to include in an "Unfilled Shifts" report would be:
            ● Date of the shift
            ● Shift Start and End Time
            ● Position or Job Profile requiring coverage
            ● Scheduling Organization (e.g., Department, Location)
            ● Required Skills or Certifications for the shift



These reports can be scheduled to run on a regular basis and distributed to relevant managers, or they can be added to dashboards to provide an at-a-glance view of immediate staffing needs.

Workday provides Open Shift Board functionality to allow shifts to be backfilled by workers when needed. Workday also provides reporting of scheduled vs actual time, time exceptions and time anomalies to assist with reporting of potential shrinkage of ability to deliver supply of workers to meet demand.

SC71.comments: Workday provides a full history of all leave of absence records and associated business process steps as part of the Workday Business Process Framework and always-on audit controls. Workday provides configurable Leave of Absence plans with validations rules, balance tracking and cascading leave functionality to allow multiple leave types to be managed across an extended time period.

HR01.comments: Total Rewards Statements are standard functionality. They combine compensation categories like base, merit, bonus, equity, allowances, and benefits. Statements can include payroll actuals and use templates for branding. HR administrators can manage statement creation without programming. Compensation overviews are also available on worker profiles. Additionally, Workday supports Total Rewards templates to display a breakdown of base pay, bonuses, health and other benefits, stock, commissions, payroll earnings/deductions, and custom fields.

HR02.comments:
Total Rewards statements allow organizations the ability to express employee investment to the workforce. Workday delivers a configurable Total Rewards statement. You can configure the Total Rewards Statement with options, such as comparison between actual and target, naming overrides, and decimal precision control that can make this statement truly individual to your organization's needs.

Workday meets this requirement through a combination of two powerful features:

    ● For ongoing, up-to-date visibility (especially for new hires), the live Total Rewards Statement is automatically available based on configuration rules.
    ● For formal, point-in-time issuance (like an annual refresh tied to a compensation cycle), the Compensation Review Statement can be released on a pre-configured schedule.

HR03.comments: Workday enables the setup of step-based salary ranges and utilizes time in range to determine when the employee should be moved to the next step. Progression rules allow you to create eligibility conditions for specific step increases. Workday delivers a report that will identify employees who are ready for their next step and will allow the withholding of the step increase if appropriate. Managers can also receive notifications in My Tasks when an employee is about to progress to the next step, wherein they can approve or withhold progression. Finally, Workday can consider other factors when it comes to an employee's targeted step progression date, such as adjusting the date based on an employee's time in a nonpay status, or preserving the target date if the employee has a compensation change.

HR04.comments:
The Workday platform provides a comprehensive and integrated framework to handle all three of those requirements. It is designed to manage large-scale compensation events with flexibility, control, and clear communication.
1. Managing and Applying Cost of Living Adjustments (COLA)
Workday handles mass pay changes like COLAs through a controlled and auditable business process, typically initiated as a mass action.
2. Supporting Calibration Outside of Standard Metrics. This is a core strength of Workday's Compensation Review Process. The platform is built to support managerial discretion and calibration, rather than forcing strict adherence to formulas.

    ● During a compensation review cycle (for merit, bonus, and/or stock), managers are presented with a configurable grid of their employees.
    ● The system can provide a suggested merit increase based on standard metrics (like performance rating and position in range/compa-ratio). However, these are typically configured as guidelines, not hard-enforced rules.
    ● Managers have the ability to override the system's suggestion and enter a different amount, allowing them to apply their own judgment. They can often be required to enter a comment to justify the deviation, ensuring the decision is documented.
    ● Calibration Tools:
        ● Budget Pools: Managers are allocated budgets. They can distribute their pool across their team as they see fit, as long as they do not exceed their total budget.
        ● Additional Adjustment Pools: You can configure separate budget pools for "Additional Adjustments" or "Promotions." This gives managers a separate purse of money to use for discretionary increases outside of the standard merit budget, which is ideal for addressing equity issues, retaining top talent, or rewarding special contributions.
        ● Visual Analytics: The calibration screen includes analytics that update in real-time, showing the manager how their proposed changes compare to the guidelines and how they are tracking against their budget.


3. Generating Printable Compensation Statements

Workday provides a powerful tool for creating and distributing personalized, printable compensation statements at the end of a review cycle.

    ●  The Compensation Review Statement is a formal PDF document that is generated for each employee included in the compensation review process.
    ●  The layout of these statements is fully customizable using BIRT (Business Intelligence and Reporting Technology).

HR05.comments:
Workday's Business Process Framework delivers standard business processes across the entire functional spectrum. These business processes are used as starting points for customers who then make configuration changes to meet their business requirements. Some examples of these configurable business processes are Change Job (transfer, promotion, etc.), Request Compensation Change, Request a One-Time Payment, etc. These processes provide an "always on" audit trail of all the actions taken.

The Workday platform is designed with an integrated architecture where staffing changes and compensation are intrinsically linked. The system not only applies compensation changes during a promotion but also uses the same event to automatically update eligibility for other compensation components like bonus plans.
