Users can create their own custom dashboards and reports and through a robust security model share them with people in the organization.

BG38.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides sophisticated control mechanisms to review and manage budget tolerances and overrides, ensuring effective governance across the planning process. The platform allows organizations to define acceptable tolerance levels for budget changes, which automatically flag or require higher-level approval for any submission that exceeds the predetermined thresholds. Furthermore, while the system supports necessary manual overrides for distributed amounts or driver-based calculations, every instance of an override is systematically tracked with an unalterable audit trail, capturing the user, the change, and the rationale. This combination of automated tolerance enforcement and transparent override tracking is a significant differentiator, giving Port of Portland the necessary balance of planning flexibility and strict financial control.

BG39.comments:
Workday delivers standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. Furthermore, Workday Adaptive Planning provides highly robust and dynamic capabilities for comparing budgets, actuals, and forecasts directly within a single, integrated reporting and analysis environment. The platform’s unified data model allows organizations to instantly pull and juxtapose multiple versions, such as the initial budget, the latest rolling forecast, and month-to-date actual results, enabling immediate variance analysis without time-consuming data exports or manual reconciliations. Organizations gain the ability to drill down into the variances to understand their root causes and drive informed course correction, a significant advantage over tools that require separate consolidation steps for each data set. This continuous comparison and real-time visibility are key differentiators, supporting agility and accuracy in managing financial performance.

BG40.comments: Workday allows you to define your exact fiscal schedule. You can roll up your days into weeks, months, quarters, and years. These weeks can be tailored to meet specific organization and industry needs, e.g., 5/5/4. The configuration would enable you to run financial statements that cross fiscal years (e.g., rolling 12 months), year over year, quarter over quarter, midmonth, etc. Workday additionally supports the use of both standard and alternate fiscal schedules as appropriate allowing for the consolidation and associated reporting of a parent/subsidiary combination with different subsidiaries.

BG41.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides seamless automation for the import and refresh of actuals data, which is foundational for timely and accurate budget versus actual reporting and includes robust support for pre- and post-audit views. This is achieved through established and maintainable integrations with source general ledger and operational systems, ensuring actual data is consistently pulled into the Workday solution without manual intervention or data mapping complexity. The platform’s version control and audit log functionality allow organizations to maintain and report on distinct views of the data, capturing the original imported actuals and any subsequent, auditable adjustments (pre- and post-audit) within a single, unified environment. This continuous, automated data flow and clear versioning are critical differentiators, ensuring that the Port of Portland always has a current, transparent, and defensible comparison for performance management.

BG42.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports robust variance analysis workflows, incorporating configurable spot-check thresholds that are invaluable for reviews prior to an accounting close or formal review cycles. The platform's powerful workflow engine allows organizations to set specific variance tolerances based on dollar amounts, percentages, or dimensional intersections, automatically flagging and routing items that exceed these thresholds to the appropriate personnel for investigation and commentary. This capability allows finance teams to proactively focus their attention on the most material variances, ensuring issues are addressed and documented promptly before the final closing process, thereby accelerating the close and enhancing the integrity of the financial statements. This proactive, controlled approach to variance management is a significant differentiator, providing far better governance than relying on manual, post-close exception reports.

BG43.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the essential ability to reconcile differences between the adopted budget and subsequent forecasted budgets seamlessly for both audit and external reporting purposes. The platform achieves this through its robust version management capabilities, where the original adopted budget is locked as an official version, and all subsequent forecasts are maintained as separate, trackable versions. Organizations gain the ability to instantly generate reports that clearly show period-by-period variances between these two versions, including the drivers of change, all within a fully auditable environment that captures every modification and commentary. This unified approach eliminates the manual reconciliation pain points common in disparate systems, ensuring that Port of Portland can always trace the evolution of its financial plan with complete transparency and confidence for stakeholders and auditors.

BG44.comments: Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. You can configure alerts to send relevant business information to users based on most conditions for which you can create a report in Workday. The alerts are driven off the Workday reporting framework which can be run immediately or on schedule basis. The alerts can drive notifications to the Workday Inbox, to e-mail, or to a mobile device.

BG45.comments: Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. BG46.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the use of Excel-based templates for budget imports and bulk data uploads, providing a familiar and efficient entry method for users, particularly during initial setup or for complex, detailed data sets. The platform offers a seamless mechanism to define and download standardized Excel templates that are directly mapped to the underlying dimensional model, ensuring data integrity upon upload. Organizations gain the ability to leverage their existing data gathering processes while eliminating the manual reconciliation and mapping errors common with other systems, as the intelligent import feature handles the translation from spreadsheet to structured model automatically. BG47.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides comprehensive support for configurable data validation and a robust audit trail specifically for Excel-based imports, ensuring data quality and governance are maintained even when leveraging external tools. Before data is committed to the model, the platform runs configurable validation rules that check for data format compliance, correct dimensionality, and adherence to defined business rules, automatically alerting the user to errors that need correction in the Excel file. Critically, every successful import, along with the source file and details of the user and timestamp, is logged in a complete, unalterable audit trail, providing full traceability for all data inputs, including those uploaded in bulk. This dual capability of pre-import validation and detailed post-import auditing is a major differentiator, providing confidence that all financial data, regardless of entry method, is accurate and defensible.

BG49.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning comprehensively supports the comparison of current budget proposals against performance targets and historical budget data, which is essential for comprehensive budget review packages. The platform's integrated modeling and reporting environment allows organizations to seamlessly incorporate and display multiple planning versions, such as the mandated strategic targets, the prior year's adopted budget, and the latest forecast, alongside the current submission. This capability enables reviewers to instantly assess the strategic alignment and efficiency of the proposed budget, making variance analysis against both historical trends and forward-looking goals straightforward and transparent. Unlike legacy tools that require complex data manipulation to achieve such comparisons, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures all these critical data points are readily accessible and consistently linked for efficient, data-driven decision-making.

BG50.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports collaborative review workflows and meticulous version tracking, which are central to its design as a modern, enterprise performance management solution. The platform allows organizations to define multi-step, sequential, or parallel review and approval workflows that automatically route budget or forecast submissions to the relevant stakeholders for input, commentary, and sign-off, eliminating the bottleneck of email-based reviews. Crucially, every step, data change, and approved milestone is captured through robust, granular version tracking, providing an unalterable audit trail that ensures data governance and transparency throughout the entire collaborative process. This seamless integration of workflow and version control accelerates decision-making while providing the confidence that all changes are documented and traceable.

BG51.comments: Fully supported. Workday provides the ability to track budget amendments, transfers, type of change, reason and with Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities, who requested the change as well as date and time stamp is always recorded.

BG52.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning fully supports the creation and monitoring of comprehensive, multi-year capital budget plans, including the critical functionality to enforce financial caps over a five-year horizon. This is achieved through the platform's specialized capital planning module, which allows users to define projects, track their lifecycle, and assign specific spending limits or caps across fiscal years and dimensional categories. Organizations gain the ability to model the impact of different funding scenarios and timing of expenditures while the system proactively monitors adherence to these predefined multi-year constraints. Unlike systems that require manual tracking of long-range limits, Workday Adaptive Planning ensures capital plans remain aligned with strategic financial policy and within established appropriation boundaries automatically.

BG54.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the critical ability to accurately compare actual capital additions against the approved capital budget and automatically calculate variances, ensuring rigorous control over your fixed asset spending. The platform seamlessly integrates actual capital expenditure data from your asset management or general ledger systems, allowing for a real-time comparison against the specific project, asset, and period defined in the adopted capital budget version. Organizations gain the ability to immediately identify over- or under-spending, investigate the root causes of variances, and take corrective action, all within the same unified system. This automated, granular variance tracking capability provides superior financial governance and is a key differentiator when compared to systems that rely on manual, post-period reconciliations.

BG55.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides the comprehensive ability to compare approved funds requests against both the adopted capital budget and the actual capital additions through its integrated planning and analysis platform. The platform's capital module allows for the detailed tracking of approved funding requests as a distinct version or data set, which can then be instantly juxtaposed with the official capital budget for commitment variance analysis. Furthermore, the seamless integration of actual expenditure data enables continuous monitoring to ensure that spending against these approved requests aligns with the physical asset additions, providing a full circle of control from initial request to final asset acquisition. This superior capability for multi-point comparison is a key differentiator, delivering complete financial transparency and governance over the entire capital expenditure lifecycle.

BG56.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning’s robust capital planning module fully supports the ability to enter and enforce capital spending caps and compare these constraints directly against actual expenditures at both the portfolio and division levels. This is achieved by leveraging the platform’s dimensional modeling to define and track spending limits by any criteria, such as a portfolio of projects or a specific division, ensuring granular control and accountability. Organizations gain the ability to monitor compliance with these hard limits in real time as actuals flow in from the general ledger, automatically calculating and reporting variances at the strategic level. This level of flexible, multi-dimensional capping and real-time comparison is a key differentiator, providing Port of Portland the necessary governance to manage long-term capital resources effectively and avoid overspending.

BG57.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning comprehensively supports the assignment of multiple funding sources, such as debt, grants, or operating cash, to individual capital projects and rigorously tracks spending against each specific source. This critical functionality is achieved by leveraging the platform’s powerful dimensional model, which allows funding sources to be configured as distinct elements linked directly to capital projects and their associated spending. Organizations gain the ability to accurately monitor and report on the consumption of specific funding pools in real time, ensuring compliance with grant restrictions or debt covenants throughout the project lifecycle. This superior ability to manage and reconcile complex, multi-source funding within the planning system is a key differentiator, providing complete visibility and control over restricted capital resources.

BG59.comments: Yes, fully supported. Unlike legacy systems that rely on static, annual budgets, Workday Adaptive Planning handles long-term capital forecasting with native time-intelligence features that ensure continuity and accuracy without complex workarounds or manual adjustments. This capability enables the Port of Portland to manage project lifecycles, understand cash flow requirements across multiple fiscal periods, and easily report on portfolio performance with configurable dashboards and reports tailored for capital steering committees.

BG60.comments: Workday supports managing project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets. A streamlined process lets you manage project costs from acquisition through to identification of assets within the project, and finally to capitalization of these assets.

Organizations benefit from a continuous planning environment where budget owners can input data, see the immediate impact on financial statements, and run what-if scenarios interactively without needing to wait for batch processing or manual consolidations. This real-time capability accelerates the budget cycle, drives more informed decision-making, and represents a significant differentiator from systems that rely on slow, periodic updates.

BG63.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning provides robust, native capabilities for consolidating budgets across all departments, legal entities, and reporting dimensions within the Port of Portland structure. Unlike competitor systems that require complex scripting or manual data blending, this seamless consolidation ensures that every user is always planning and reporting on the most current and accurate consolidated financial picture. This allows the finance team to accelerate the close and planning cycle, focusing their efforts on strategic analysis rather than data integration and validation.

BG75.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning incorporates intelligent planning and predictive analytics capabilities that proactively identify budget categories at risk of exceeding defined thresholds, providing a significant advantage over reactive reporting tools. Leveraging machine learning algorithms, the platform analyzes historical trends and current planning data to generate predictive forecasts, flagging potential variances and risks before they materialize, allowing the Port of Portland to take timely corrective action. Furthermore, the system includes native, configurable alerting functionality that automatically notifies key stakeholders via email or within the application when a designated risk threshold or variance percentage is breached. This integration ensures seamless compliance and simplified reporting for complex schedules like 425, 270, and 271, as the platform eliminates the need for manual data manipulation by drawing directly from the unified financial model. Unlike standalone grant management tools, Workday provides a consolidated view that bridges the gap between operational grant tracking, capital investment decisions, and statutory financial reporting.

BG78.comments: Workday Adaptive Planning effectively addresses the requirement for clear mapping between project costs and reimbursable amounts through its integrated dimensional modeling and flexible document attachment capabilities. While Workday Adaptive Planning is a planning and analytics solution, its integration capabilities, particularly with Workday Financial Management or other core ERP systems, allow for the attachment and verification of supporting payment documentation directly to the planning and actuals data, ensuring a clear audit trail and compliance with grant or funding requirements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. Flexible reporting prompting settings and drilldowns allow for flexible querying of activity and transactions to answer relevant questions relative to spending or spend attributes.

CD13.comments: Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds.

CD20.comments:
Workday:
Workday supports manual and automated journal entries, including validation, reconciliation, and audit trails of imported data using web services, delivered integration tools, Workday’s Accounting Journal Connector, or Consolidation Data Capture. 

SymPro:
SymPro can generate journal entries for investments transactions and send those entries to Workday for posting.

CD21.comments: Supported. Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. Asset transactions automatically update the General Ledger in real time at the point of transaction approval. This facilitates coordination between purchasing and accounting to help ensure that items that should be capitalized are created as assets. Workday enables you to share asset costs across multiple worktags, providing you with a way to register assets from invoices or receipts to share costs, depreciation, and lifecycle events.

CD22.comments:
Workday:
Workday delivers unique capabilities that allow customers the ability to create and define their own APIs. If only a subset of fields within Workday services are needed for a specific integration, then a custom API that contains only those needed fields can be built. This API can then be consumed by Workday or any third-party integration tool.

SymPro:
SymPro Cash - Integration with third party applications are provided with API based and file based connections based on availability.

CD23.comments:
Workday:
Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Consolidated cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed. With Workday’s composite reports you can build a single, matrix-style report from multiple data sources, take advantage of spreadsheet-like capabilities to create reports that perform column, row, and cell calculations and control report formatting style.

SymPro:
SymPro Cash Management

CD24.comments:
Workday:
Supported. Workday enables you to assign cash flow categories on bank statement lines and external cash flows to use when generating cash positioning reports. This assists in the normalization of data across bank statement formats to support simplified reporting. These can also be used to identify the reason for cash movements both for automated and user entered journals to assist in the creation of direct and indirect cash flow reporting.

SymPro Cash Management

CD25.comments:
Workday:
Workday's Accounting Center allows transactional data from third-party systems including treasury systems to be ingested, enriched, and transformed to create automated accounting journals that are posted to the GL. Customers can manage and track transactions from source systems in batches from ingestion to summarized accounting.

SymPro:
SymPro Cash Management

CD26.comments:
Workday:
Workday provides electronic bank reconciliation to match bank statement lines to operational transactions. You can create matching rules and condition rules to associate with a bank account, and the system can automatically match, suggest a match, or create an ad hoc bank transaction. Workday also offers exception handling and audit trail reporting.

SymPro:
SymPro Cash Management

CD27.comments:
Workday:
All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility.

SymPro:
Audit logs by user are standard in SymPro

CD28.comments:
Workday:
Workday provides configurable validation rules that create either a warning or an error message. An error must be corrected before the expense report can be submitted. A warning can be processed and the warning becomes part of the transaction. Warnings are viewable during the approval process, can be used to route expense reports, and are viewable in the reporting.

Each budget can be configured to control or not control spending activity. For example, further business process configuration options allow a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist. 

SymPro:
SymPro Cash Management- Configurable in BI tool

CA02.comments: Workday captures and manages a broader set of data than traditional accounting systems to support an expanded view of business. Workday enables customers to combine various data sources such as actuals, budgets, external data from third-party systems, statistics, and headcount, into live multi-dimensional reports that users can format, drill down into, and act on all within Workday. Organizations can significantly streamline their chart of accounts for a faster, more accurate financial close. Workday can model multiple operating entities, organizations, or business units to easily complete intercompany transactions, eliminations, allocations, adjustments, journal line matching, and consolidated reporting.

CA04.comments: Workday allows you to define your exact fiscal schedule down to the day. You can roll up your days into weeks, months, quarters, and years. These weeks can be tailored to meet specific organization and industry needs, e.g., 5/5/4. The configuration would enable you to run financial statements that cross fiscal years (e.g., rolling 12 months), year over year, quarter over quarter, midmonth, etc. Workday additionally supports the use of both standard and alternate fiscal schedules as appropriate allowing for the consolidation and associated reporting of a parent/subsidiary combination with different subsidiaries.

CA05.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework manages all journal entry and edits, providing control over who can approve journals and manage posting. Workday's audit capabilities track who, when, and what was entered or changed on journals automatically.

CA08.comments: Supported. Workday allows organizations to define their organizational structure, including departments, divisions, business units, and other groupings of employees. This structure can be easily modified to reflect changes in the organization, such as mergers, acquisitions, or reorganizations.

CA09.comments: Workday uses worktags as business dimensions. Inspired by the Web 2.0 concept of tagging, a worktag is a label assigned (i.e., tagged) to a business event, thus describing the event and enabling multi-dimensional classification, search, aggregation, reporting, and analysis of business information. You can configure your own custom worktags such as campaign or channel. In addition, any custom organizations you configure, such as line of business, product line, or brand, are also available as worktags.

CA10.comments: Workday provides the ability to post in detail and in real time directly from operational areas like customer invoice entry, supplier invoice entry, expenses, etc. to the General Ledger. In addition, all Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level. Unlike other cloud based financial software packages, this action does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

CA11.comments: Workday captures transactions as business events and uses an accounting engine to create accounting entries based upon user-defined rules. Users can define multiple sets of Accounting Rules for business events such as Business Assets, Revenue Recognition, and Lease Accounting to generate Accounting entries according to different accounting standards such as US GAAP, IFRS, Regulatory, Local GAAP, and Cash. All these accounting entries would be available for reporting.

CA15.comments: Workday provides drill-down capabilities from financial statements to underlying transaction details such as journal entries, invoices, and receipts. Unlike other cloud-based financial software, Workday does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

CA16.comments: Workday supports multi-book reporting. Within Workday, you have the option to display financial statement data by book. This allows users to view their books side by side (e.g., GAAP vs. Tax vs. IFRS vs. Local GAAP). Transactions are entered against book codes which roll up into books to facilitate financial reporting.

CA17.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system including transaction entry and approval such as journal entries. In addition, Workday supports the upload of backup documentation that you can attach directly to the entry or the ability to include a link to an external document repository where that information can be found.

CA18.comments: Workday supports project budgets to forecast costs and revenue by project. Each budget can be configured to control or not control spending activity, which can be a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist.

CA19.comments: Workday supports capital projects. You can manage project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets.

CA20.comments: Workday Projects enable you to define and plan your project. Establish a project work plan with phases, tasks, and milestones to track progress over time. Automatically create or update a downstream project plan for quote phases and tasks after a project has already been created. You can initiate a Budget for cost, revenue, and targeted margin. Roll up multiple work areas under larger initiatives or work hierarchies.

CA22.comments: Workday supports the creation of billable, non-billable, and capital projects. You can track project time costs and spend on projects, project tasks, or non-project work.

CA23.comments: Workday supports project budgets that can be configured to control spending activity. Business process configuration options allow a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist.

CA24.comments: Workday supports managing project costs from project-tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project costs into depreciable business assets.

CA26.comments: Workday provides various ledgers to accommodate pre-encumbrances, encumbrances, and actuals.

CA27.comments: Workday supports multiple accounting methods, including fund accounting, by using book codes and custom reporting to track fund balances, restrictions and interfund transfers in compliance with GASB standards. Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements.

CA28.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required. Define allocations, organize them by how often and in what order they should run. Allocate based on a fixed source amount, fixed rate amount, or by ledger/plan structure.

CA29.comments: Workday provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created. Workday enables you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required.

CA30.comments: Workday Supports Multi-level or dependent allocations. The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and will alert the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensures the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA31.comments: Workday supports multi-level or dependent allocations. The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and alerts the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensuring the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA33.comments: Workday can allow you to find the data for Work Orders but does not provide functionality of Work Orders

CA34.comments: Workday supports a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules. We enable you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required. A single allocation definition can be used to group multiple worktag types allowing allocations to be performed by worktags.

CA35.comments: Workday enables you to define allocations and organize them by how often and in what order they should run. We support a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules.

CA36.comments: Workday gives you greater visibility into project costs with project capitalization. A streamlined process lets you manage project costs from acquisition through to identification of assets within the project, and finally to capitalization of these assets. Workday also automates the WIP/CIP and other accounting entries along the way. This enables you to identify your project costs and delay recognition of these expenses by recording them as long-term assets.

CA38.comments: Workday lets you run allocations in a “draft” status to view the financial impact through your reports WITHOUT having to post/un-post the transactions. You can also run pro-forma allocations at any time to provide visibility into fully loaded numbers on demand.

CA39.comments: Supported. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. Users can run a receivables aging by either operational aging (ages by transaction date) or financial aging (ages by accounting date). Additionally, users can have the aging basis be by either due date or invoice date.

CA40.comments:
Workday provides the ability to add unlimited notes and attachments to a customer’s record. We also break up the security capability to add a note (versus add and/or update the customer record) and another permission to edit any notes entered.

When you view a customer invoice, you have visibility into all hold, collection, and dispute information.

CA41.comments: Workday provides Dunning Letter capabilities to assist organizations with late customer invoice management by providing auto-generated reminder letters, which can be emailed. Workday provides users the ability to automatically calculate and create invoices for interest and/or late fees related to past-due customer invoices. The delivered customer statement can be run in two formats. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories.

CA42.comments: While the provided text does not explicitly mention security deposits, Workday's robust functionality around customer payments, cash application, and receivables control accounts suggests that it can be configured to track customer security deposits. The ability to record deposits, apply cash across ledgers, and manage receivables control accounts provides a foundation for tracking security deposits. Furthermore, the system's document ID generation capabilities can be used to create unique identifiers for each security deposit transaction.

CA43.comments: Workday supports the following payment methods: cash, check, direct debit, EFT, wire, and credit/debit card. Workday also provides a Customer Payment Lockbox integration to import Bank Administration Institute (BAI) lockbox data into Workday from an external endpoint.

CA44.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. You can also offset multiple invoices and adjustments that aren't tied in a single transaction by recording zero-amount customer payments.

CA45.comments: Workday does not have a Customer portal currently

CA46.comments: Workday does not provide the POS system but can certainly bring the data in

CA47.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. Workday even delivers a specific business process to govern and audit assets transferred.

CA49.comments:
Supported. You can manage project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets.

Project assets are buckets that are specific to each capital project, which capture separate, ongoing costs of a capital project that is in progress. You can associate multiple projects assets with a project to track cost over the life of a project. When all costs are in from transactions tagged to the capital project, you can convert each project asset to a business asset to realize cost and start depreciation. Each asset may have a different depreciation schedule.

CA50.comments:
Workday enables you to correct the useful life of an asset if the asset is capitalized with an incorrect life or there's a change in its estimated useful life. The transaction effective date when you correct useful life is always the acquisition date. The transaction effective date to change the useful life of an asset can be any date during the life of an asset.

The Asset Useful Life Update Event business process routes the transaction for review and approval. Workday also creates depreciation adjustment transactions for applicable useful life changes when ledger periods have processed depreciation and depreciation expenses should be a different amount.

CA51.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. Workday enables you to apply large volumes of payments automatically against multiple invoices based on remittance information via a system of payment application rules. You can configure customer payment rule sets and link them to active bank accounts, and then schedule payment jobs for eligible bank accounts with unreconciled bank statement lines. Workday supports the ability to configure invoice prefixes, customer mapping tables, and multiple bank accounts, among others, to populate associated instances. This automates the process of recording customer payments with remittance advice and customer deposits from the bank statement lines.

CA52.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Reconciliation rules are configured to tell Workday how to reconcile the items using condition rules. A condition rule consists of one or more logical statements used to check for matching entries using logical comparison operators AND/OR. Condition rules are then specified in reconciliation rules, which specify the reconciliation reason and the action to take. The action could be to automatically match, suggest a match or create an ad hoc bank transaction for a first notice item (e.g., bank fees). This reduces manual effort as you only have to take care of the exception not covered by the reconciliation rules which provides more flexibility, transparency, and accuracy.

CA53.comments: Supported. With Workday, you can automatically import electronic bank statements in order to reconcile bank statement lines with system transactions for multiple bank accounts. Delivered integrations support both the BAI2 and ISO 20022 statement formats. If the bank does not support BAI2 or ISO 20022, you can build a custom integration to import the statement data. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Additionally, exception reporting is available for unmatched or out-of-tolerance transactions.

CA54.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday also provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. Workday delivers a specific business process to govern and audit assets transferred.

CA55.comments: Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Accounting "workpaper" reports such as trial balance, ledger detail, account analysis, and reconciliations provide analysis and control. Consolidated profit and loss (P&L), balance sheet, and cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed.

CA56.comments: Workday supports automated receipt processing across various channels, including ACH, credit card, wire, and lockbox. Our system provides the ability to match payments to open invoices using configurable rules such as amount, reference number, and customer ID. For unmatched items, Workday offers exception-handling workflows to ensure proper resolution.

CA57.comments: Workday supports manual entry of invoices, cash receipts, and adjustments. For customer invoices, you can create them manually, load them directly into Workday, copy existing invoices, or create them from customer invoice proposals. Supplier invoices can also be created by manual entry or by copying existing invoices. Journals can be created by manual entry or copying an existing journal.

CA58.comments:
Supported. Workday allows you to create a template for a recurring journal with a start and end date and generate new journals using this template for a user-specified timeframe. You may also specify a reversal date on recurring journals. Please note that with this template, once it has been populated the recurring journals will automatically get recorded in the appropriate timeframe that are specified in the template. Different from other solutions that recommend using a spreadsheet and uploading recurring journals each period, in Workday users do not need to remember to upload the journals each period as journal generation will happen automatically.

Custom validations can also be added to recurring journal entries to ensure that all requirements for data input are met prior to posting.

CA59.comments: Receipts can be processed efficiently for goods, services, project-based services and contingent worker time sheets on purchase order and supplier contract lines. Workday supports both a decentralized and centralized receiving process on both the desktop and mobile application. New accounts can be loaded into the system via a spreadsheet if the volume warrants it. The Ad Hoc Project Transaction feature allows users to create billable (time, expenses, and supplier invoices) transactions from external source.

CA63.comments: To minimize the movement of data, latency, and reconciliation that goes with it, Workday's technology includes a reporting and analytics platform in the same environment you consolidate, transact, and reconcile. Unlike point solutions, our approach allows you to load, transform and manage source data directly in the Reconciliation and Consolidation environment. In Workday, this provides you seamless access to financial details at the level of granularity defined by what YOU need, without maintaining drill paths and security between multiple third-party environments.

CA64.comments: Workday can integrate very well with other systems as long as the other system can be integrated with.

CA65.comments: Workday provides dynamic and flexible integration capabilities that enable customers to extend the functionality of the platform. With open APIs, web services and delivered integration templates, Workday facilitates sharing information with systems that are relevant for maintaining and managing your projects. An integration with MasterWorks, a third-party system, can be configured to meet this requirement.

CA67.comments: Workday is a very open system for integrations. Integrations are typically supported as long as the external system can be integrated with.

CA68.comments: An integration would need to be configured to bring costs to projects for additional capitalization.

CA70.comments: Workday allows you to cost project time directly from payroll, where payroll is allocated across projects charged in the timesheet. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data where you choose which compensation components to include in the cost rate and you choose what burdening percentage (if any) to uplift the rate by. Burdening percentage is defined by the same project rate rules.

GM01.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities ensures all history is tracked in the system. This includes grant records across fiscal years, enabling backward-looking audits and compliance lookbacks.

GM06.comments: Workday allows you to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. You can also reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds.

GM07.comments: Workday supports defining multiple fiscal schedules, allowing you to configure each grant’s fiscal year with specific start and end dates. This enables budget tracking aligned to the grant’s fiscal year, independent of the Port's fiscal year.

GM08.comments: Alerts/notifications are sent to team members as they are assigned, approach, and miss milestones.

GM09.comments: Workday delivers several standard reports to give you greater insight into projects. Financial reports such as Labor Cost Summaries and Worker Allocations provide the details needed to show staff time charges down to the individual time entry level.

GM10.comments: Workday provides visibility for teams into projects and the milestones related to any given initiative. Stakeholders will get visibility into next steps, milestones yet to be completed, when they were completed and will be notified along the way. Everything within Workday can be tracked, captured and reported on in our reporting part of the solution, leveraging graphs, charts and dashboards which is all available in real time.

GM11.comments: Workday provides a mechanism to allocate funding from the top-level portfolio to the children projects and project portfolios. Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds.

GM13.comments: Available with integration of finalized grant transactions from Workday

GM15.comments: Workday provides three ways to cost project labor: standard cost rates, burdened cost rates, and direct payroll. Standard cost rate sheets allow you to cost labor according to three methods: named worker (most specific), project role (least specific), and project rate rule. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data. Workday allows you to cost project time directly from payroll, where payroll is allocated across projects charged in the timesheet.

GM16.comments: Workday supports automated accruals for Purchase Order lines and Supplier Contract receipt lines that have not been invoiced. With receipt accruals, you can easily accrue all supplier contract or purchase order lines that aren't fully invoiced giving you better insight into the goods and services your organization has received, but that have not yet been invoiced. Accrual entries can be set to automatically reverse in the next accounting period. Receipts that have not been invoiced are fully reportable.

GM17.comments: Workday provides multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. With Workday's Business Process Framework and audit always on capabilities, all changes are tracked with date and time stamps.

GM18.comments: Workday provides the ability to tag reports. You can apply tags to individual reports and to report groups. When you generate an individual report or report group, Workday applies the output tag to each individual report. Tagging reports enables you to easily filter and access reports. You can have tags that are ‘fixed’ (i.e., user defined) and tags that are ‘variable’ (i.e., ‘period’ would tag the report with the specific period you are running the report for), giving you flexibility to configure them to your specific business needs. Worktags are configurable. Each customer can configure up to 15 custom worktags. Additionally, Workday delivers over 100 worktags out of the box. For each of the delivered worktags, Workday presets where you can use it; therefore, you do not have to sort through inapplicable worktags when entering data. You can configure how to use related worktags so that the related worktag types available make sense for your business.

GM22.comments: Workday provides three ways to cost project labor: standard cost rates, burdened cost rates, and direct payroll. Burdened cost rates are based on compensation data and you choose which compensation components to include in the cost rate and you choose what burdening percentage (if any) to uplift the rate by. Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements.

GM23.comments: Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source.

GM24.comments: Workday’s multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. Workday streamlines the multi-funding requirements by reducing the time spent with automation, improves accuracy, and provides consistent results from one reporting source. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. The ability to track project time and expense is supported. Capture expenses and purchase orders related to a project or project task.

GM25.comments: Workday provides the ability to define validation rules to ensure compliance with expense reporting policies. Validation rules can result in a warning or they can prevent the user from submitting the expense report until the policy violation is corrected. Expense reports that contain validation warnings/errors are flagged so you can monitor policy non-compliance and optionally route for additional review and audit.

GM28.comments: Workday provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created. Lease accounting in Workday automatically generates lease transactions from supplier contracts. You can then create financial reporting, which can help with compliance.

GM29.comments: Workday provides enhanced commitment accounting on procurement card transactions by automatically liquidating encumbrances on purchase orders and contracts associated with procurement cards. This increases the accuracy of spend obligation balances and reduces manual processing. Your organization can configure budget check to run at any point, or at multiple times, in the procurement card transaction verification business process.

GM30.comments: Workday provides standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. Reporting in Workday is based on real-time reporting on transactions. When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information, enabling a comparison of real-time actuals balances against approved budgets. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

GM31.comments: Workday provides various ledgers to accommodate pre-encumbrances, encumbrances, and actuals.

GM33.comments: Opportunity Projects enable you to start planning for projects before they are sold. Opportunity Projects allow you to plan for future staffing, generate financial forecasts, and convert them later to sold standard projects. Workday allows you to distinguish between opportunity projects and sold projects for ease of prioritizing resources and understanding project pipeline.

GM38.comments:
Workday's Business Process Framework supports any number of approval levels for a given transaction or activity. Approval and review steps can be configured based upon your organization's business needs and roles such as Cost Center Manager, Grant Manager, Manager, Management Chain as well as numerous other roles.

Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required.

GM39.comments: Alerts/notifications are sent to team members as they are assigned, approach, and miss milestones. Workday provides the ability to create and generate alerts and notifications with the Workday Alerts Framework. You can configure alerts to send relevant business information to users based on most conditions for which you can create a report in Workday. The alerts can drive notifications to the Workday Inbox, to e-mail, or to a mobile device.

GM40.comments: Workday provides a Message Centre facility to allow Sourcing Managers to send messages to suppliers, suppliers to submit questions to the sourcing team, and sourcing managers to respond to supplier questions. Throughout the lifecycle of the Sourcing project, a Team Chat facility is provided to allow internal stakeholders and the procurement team to communicate and collaborate, with all messages retained against the project for audit purposes. During active sourcing events, a live chat facility is provided to allow sourcing managers to communicate quickly with suppliers. During RFx events and other projects, sourcing managers can define project milestones and assign them to stakeholders so everyone knows what they are expected to do, by when, and the completion status can be tracked.

GM41.comments: Workday provides configurable validation rules that create either a warning or an error message when charging to the wrong project code. Validation rules can result in a warning, or they can prevent the user from submitting the expense report until the policy violation is corrected. Expense reports that contain validation warnings/errors are flagged so you can monitor policy non-compliance and optionally route for additional review and audit. Workday provides the ability to track project time and expense.

GM42.comments: Workday provides dynamic and flexible integration capabilities that enable customers to extend the functionality of the platform. With open APIs, web services and delivered integration templates, Workday facilitates sharing information with systems that are relevant for maintaining and managing your projects. APIs are also provided to integrate with external Project Management solutions

GM46.comments:
Workday delivers standard reports out of the box related to budgets and performance against budgets, including comparisons of budget to actuals, and variance calculations. The delivered reports provide the ability to generate and display budget to actual results both at a company / legal entity level, or by organizations such as cost center / region / division, etc. Additional reports can also be configured where desired. Existing results can be copied and leveraged as a starting point for new reports.

Reporting in Workday is based on real-time reporting on transactions. When transactions are approved, they are immediately posted and reflected in reports. All reports will reflect current, up-to-date information, enabling a comparison of real-time actuals balances against approved budgets. Reports also provide the ability to drill down to see detailed transactions.

Workday does not provide a delivered, out-of-the-box integration specifically for SharePoint to retrieve and display documents directly within Workday records.

However, Workday does offer a feature called Workday Drive, which provides pre-built integrations with other major cloud document management systems:

    ● Box
    ● Dropbox
    ● Google Drive
    ● Microsoft OneDrive for Business

With these delivered integrations, you can link files from your chosen storage provider directly to records in Workday. The documents remain stored in the external drive, but they are accessible in context from the relevant Workday pages.

EH09.comments:
Workday has the ability to do a file upload or API call. Email and other entries items would require a workaround approach. You can still achieve a similar result by using a common workaround:

    1. Create a Custom URL Field:
        ● An administrator can use the Maintain Custom Fields task to add a new field to the relevant business objects (e.g., Asset, Work Order, Incident).
        ● The Field Type for this custom field should be set to Hyperlink.
        ● You can name the field something intuitive, like "SharePoint Document" or "Project Folder."

    2. Link Documents Manually:
        ● Users would then navigate to the specific document or folder in your SharePoint instance and copy its URL.
        ● They would paste this URL into the new custom hyperlink field on the corresponding record in Workday (e.g., on the specific asset's page).

    3. Accessing the Document:
        ● This creates a direct link from the Workday record to the document in SharePoint.
        ● When a user clicks the link in Workday, it will open the document directly from SharePoint in a new browser tab.


Advanced Custom Integration

For a more seamless experience than a manual URL link, a custom integration could be developed using Workday Studio. This would be a more complex solution requiring development resources to build a custom connection between Workday and the SharePoint API, but it could potentially automate the process of linking and retrieving documents.
