CM02.comments:
Workday tracks notes, files, involved parties, and conversations using safety incidents functionality. Workday Help, our case management tool, facilitates conversations between employees and internal experts to manage the input, allocation, and resolution of HR-related cases.

Throughout the entire Workday platform, all business processes that impact a worker's record capture the full audit trail for each update. This means customers can easily see the date and timestamp of every user and the updates they made for each step in the business process. 

Workday provides the ability to reopen closed cases, and all actions are captured for audit purposes. Here’s a breakdown of how it works:

Reopening a Closed Case

    ● Employee/Case Creator: An employee can reopen a case they created within seven days of it being resolved. If a case creator adds a comment to a resolved case, the assigned Case Solver is notified and can choose to reopen it.
    ● Case Solver: A Case Solver can reopen a case up to seven days after it has been closed.
    ● Automatic Reopening: The system does not automatically reopen a case when a creator adds a new message.

Audit Tracking

Workday maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all case activities. To review the audit history of a case, you can use the following general procedure:

    1. Locate the case in question.
    2. From the related actions menu of the case, select Audits > View Audit Trail.
    3. You can then specify a date range to view all changes, including who made the change and when.

This audit trail includes, but is not limited to:

    ● Case creation
    ● Comments and internal notes
    ● Status changes (e.g., from 'Resolved' to 'In Progress')
    ● Changes to the assigned solver or service team

It's important to note that Workday retains all case data indefinitely for legal and auditing purposes.

CM03.comments:
Workday provides a mechanism to correct records for a business process that has already been completed, such as a closed case. This is done using the Correct action.

It is important to distinguish this from reopening a case. Reopening a case changes its status and allows the regular workflow to continue. The Correct action is an administrative function used to fix erroneous information on a transaction that is already considered complete, without changing its completed status.

CM04.comments:
Workday Help is a case management solution for HR teams consisting of two main elements: the Knowledge Base and the Case Management capability. Cases can be classified according to defined categories and sub-categories, which are managed by the customer. HR teams have access to previous enquiries and can produce reports to identify trends so that they can improve self-service information and add new content. For example, if an employee has an issue not covered by standard COVID-19 information, they can raise a case. Once HR has completed the case, they can decide whether to add those circumstances to the existing documentation. Once the case is resolved, a survey can be sent to measure employee experience and provide qualitative feedback. Workday Talent Optimization includes a configurable template for tracking disciplinary actions. Actions can be initiated by a manager, can follow your defined business process, and can include required steps and notifications, as well as e-signatures by the employee and manager.

Workday Case Management is highly configurable and allows you to create and track a wide variety of case types, including those related to performance issues, workplace concerns, and accommodations.

Key Configuration Components

The system's flexibility comes from the ability to define custom Case Types and Case Categories. This allows you to tailor the case management system to the specific needs of your organization.

    ● Case Types: This is the highest-level classification for a case. You can create distinct case types for different HR functions. For example, you could create case types such as:
        ● Employee Relations
        ● Performance Management
        ● Reasonable Accommodations
        ● Benefits Inquiry
        ● Payroll Discrepancy

CM05.comments:
Workday provides Mass Actions functionality to allow data to be collected and reported in an excel-like Worksheet within Workday, mass updates applied and then leverage the Workday Business Process Framework to trigger any required business process approvals, actions etc. Workday also provides Enterprise Interface Builders (aka EIBs) to provide excel-like templates to allow data to be collected and reported outside of Workday, mass updates applied and then import back into Workday, where the Workday Business Process Framework can again trigger any required business process approvals, actions etc.

Workday does not offer a standard feature to perform a wide range of bulk actions like status changes, reassignments, and archiving on multiple cases simultaneously using a single spreadsheet upload.

However, Workday does have a powerful Mass Actions framework that utilizes Worksheets (a spreadsheet-like interface) to process events in bulk for specific business processes. This capability is primarily used for HCM transactions, such as:

    ● Change Organization Assignments
    ● Terminate Employee
    ● End Contingent Worker Contract
    ● Change Job

While this framework exists, Case Management is not listed as a business process that is enabled for this general Mass Actions functionality.

Alternative for Bulk Approvals

For the specific action of approving cases, Workday does provide a bulk option:

    ● Bulk Approve from Inbox: A user can select multiple approval tasks directly in their My Tasks inbox and approve them all at once.
    ● Approve Business Process EIB: You can use an Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB) to upload a spreadsheet containing the IDs of multiple business process events (like cases) to approve them in bulk. This requires the user running the EIB to be a valid approver for the tasks.

These methods are limited to approvals and do not support other bulk actions like changing a case status to "In Progress," reassigning cases to a different team, or archiving.

CM06.comments:
Workday provides robust functionality for customers to create their own configurable case types and custom fields directly within the tenant, without needing to engage Workday or another vendor for assistance.

This self-service capability is a core part of the Workday platform and is managed through the use of Custom Objects.

CM07.comments: Workday provides a unique employee ID for every employee, and this ID remains an attribute for that employee, regardless of their location, status, or changes to their profile. Workday also offers a Unified Duplicate Management Framework to configure match criteria to surface potential duplicates for a candidate.

CM08.comments:
Workday has extensive capabilities for reporting and analysis on an employee's various group memberships and roles, which can be used for classification and analysis. This is achieved through Workday's flexible organizational structures and its powerful reporting framework.

Tracking Employee Workgroups

In Workday, an employee's "workgroup" can be represented by several different objects, all of which are reportable:

    ● Organizational Roles: Every employee belongs to a Supervisory Organization, and their position is associated with other organization types like Cost Centers, Locations, and Regions. You can report on all of these assignments.
    ● Workteams: You can use Workteams to create and track flexible, cross-functional groups of people that span geographic and organizational boundaries. This is a leading practice for tracking project teams, squads, or Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). You can report on Workteam membership and an employee's status within the team (e.g., Active, Inactive).
    ● Committees: Workday allows you to formally track membership in various types of committees, such as corporate, regulatory, or special subcommittees.
    ● Talent Pools: You can group employees into Talent Pools based on specific criteria for development, succession planning, or special projects. Membership in these pools is fully reportable.

Tracking Representation Status

An employee's representation status is typically tracked through their assigned roles and security group memberships.

    ● Assignable Roles: You can report on the specific roles a worker holds within any organization, such as Manager, HR Partner, or Matrix Manager. The standard report Role Assignments for Worker provides a detailed overview of these assignments.
    ● Security Group Membership: You can easily report on which user-based and role-based security groups an employee belongs to. This is useful for understanding a user's access rights and their functional role within the system.

Reporting and Analysis

Workday provides a robust set of tools to pull this information for analysis:

    ● Standard Reports: Workday offers numerous standard reports to view this data, including: 
    ● Custom Reporting: You can create powerful custom reports using data sources like All Active Workers or Workers for HCM Reporting. 

By combining these objects in a single custom report, you can create detailed datasets for classification, auditing, and analysis.

CM09.comments:
Workday provides the ability to manually enter and override employee demographic data, which is particularly useful for managing records of former employees or individuals who were not part of an integrated data load.

This is primarily managed through the Former Worker object and associated tasks.

CM10.comments:
Workday is designed to maintain a complete history for each employee, and this includes all cases associated with their record, even after they have left the organization.

How It Works

The ability to maintain this history is based on a core concept in Workday's architecture: the persistence of the Worker Profile.

    ● Permanent Worker Record: When an employee is hired, a permanent worker record is created. All transactions and associated data, including any cases created by or for that employee, are linked to this single record.
    ● Termination Process: When an employee leaves the organization, their status is changed from "Active" to "Terminated." The worker record itself is not deleted. It is preserved with its entire history.
    ● Retained Case History: Because the case data is tied to the persistent worker record, the full case history remains accessible. Authorized users, such as HR partners or case managers, can still view and report on cases associated with a terminated employee, subject to their security permissions.

Distinction for Former Workers

It's important to distinguish between employees terminated within Workday and "Former Workers":

    ● Terminated Employees: These are individuals who were active in Workday and were subsequently terminated. Their complete history, including cases, is stored in Workday.
    ● Former Workers: These are individuals who left the organization before Workday was implemented. Typically, only a basic set of their historical data is loaded into Workday's "Former Worker" object. Any case history for these individuals would reside in the legacy system that was in use prior to Workday.

In summary, for any employee who has had an active profile in Workday, the system ensures that their entire case history is maintained and accessible on their record indefinitely, providing a complete and auditable trail of all interactions.

CM11.comments: Workday delivers various options for handling non-employees, including hiring the individual into Workday as a Contingent Worker and setting up their employee sub-type as a non-employee.

CM13.comments: Yes, there are multiple configuration options for cases and Workday Help to align with your business needs. Case Management Flags, Case Statuses, Help Case SLAs and Service Teams can be configured to track case status, history, and aging.

CM14.comments: Workday Help enables HR to receive, process and track enquiries. The employee or HR person can attach attachments, contextual insights or internal notes to the case, as well as tag anyone else who needs to be involved.

CM15.comments:
In Workday, this capability is managed through Workday Help, which leverages the Business Process Framework to control the lifecycle of a case.

You can configure the business process to define steps and routing for case intake, assignment, and resolution.

Workday Help enables HR to receive, process, and track enquiries. Cases can be classified according to defined categories and sub-categories, which are managed by the customer. Workday Help is supported by our Business Process Framework ensuring customer rules and processes are followed in each case.

CM16.comments:
Workday Human Capital Management provides an Integration Framework as part of the unique, adaptable Workday foundation, to allow data to be easily imported and exported between Third Party systems, such as Recruiting/Applicant Tracking systems. Workday provides core connectors and templates to provide a basis for integration to be configured (during deployment), which can therefore allow internal candidates to be provided access with Third Party Recruiting/ATS systems, allow job and compensation data to be shared etc., as well allow for the upload of Candidate Hires into Workday. As the ATS has managed the candidate job application, Workday is able to import the Candidate information as data fields (vs a document file) into a Pre-Hire Worker record with Workday (Workday provides this worker type to specifically manage the stage between Hire and Official Start Date) and automatically converts the Pre-Hire to an Employee worker record upon official start date. Note: When using Third Party ATS systems, all Candidate data, history and documents are retained within the Third Party ATS (not Workday).

Workday is designed to be the single source of truth for all employee data, and it has robust capabilities for importing and managing demographic and personal information. This is a fundamental part of any Workday implementation.

Here’s how Workday handles the integration and import of demographic data for employee records:

Key Integration Methods

Workday provides several methods to import employee data, each suited for different scenarios:

    1. Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): This is the most common tool for bulk data loading.EIBs use a pre-formatted spreadsheet template (based on a corresponding web service) that you populate with your employee data. You then upload this spreadsheet into Workday, and the EIB process creates or updates the employee records. 
    2. Workday Studio: This is a more powerful, developer-oriented tool for complex integrations. Workday Studio is an integrated development environment (IDE) that allows you to build sophisticated integrations with custom logic, transformations, and connections to multiple sources or destinations. 
    3. Core Connectors: These are pre-built integration templates designed for specific third-party systems or standards. Core Connectors provide a pre-configured starting point for common integrations, such as those for benefits carriers or payroll providers. You configure the connector to meet your specific requirements.

CM17.comments: Yes, Workday Help, our case management tool, enables companies to manage the input, allocation and resolution of HR-related cases by facilitating those conversations between employees and internal experts. This is a case management solution that ties in with the full power of the Workday platform. Workday enables the attachment of any electronic document to an employee’s record. There are two means of attaching documents to workers within Workday: Workday enables the attachment of documents to a worker's record as a general attachment. When attached as a general attachment, the document is not saved in association with a specific business process. Alternatively, a document can be attached within a business process. Documents attached within a specific business process will be shown in association with the business process that they support. In either case, all documents will be shown in the worker’s document file and security can be setup by document category to determine who has access to view the document for the worker. There are no limitations on the number of documents on an employee's record.

CM18.comments:
Workday provides a Document Framework, allowing a library of documents to be managed (across various document types) that can be associated with worker profiles and/or included to support any business process. Workday also allows for easy upload of external documents to worker profiles. All Workday documents leverage the always on audit functionality as part of the Workday Business Process Framework. All Documents that a worker views and/or accepts are visible within the worker profile, with the actual document, user login and date/time that the document was added.

Workday Help is designed to allow for the management of documents throughout the entire lifecycle of a case, even after it has been created or closed.

This functionality ensures that a complete and accurate record is maintained, allowing users and solvers to add relevant information or documentation as it becomes available.

How to Manage Documents on a Case

Here are the steps to add or manage documents on an existing case:

    1. Navigate to the Case: Access the relevant case by searching for it or selecting it from a worklet or report.
    2. Select the Documents Tab: On the case record, click on the Documents tab. This will display all documents currently attached to the case.
    3. Add or Manage Documents:
        ● To Add a New Document: Click the Add button. You will be prompted to upload the file from your computer and can add comments or tags if needed.
        ● To Manage an Existing Document: Use the related actions icon next to a document in the list to perform actions such as viewing, downloading, or deleting it (permissions permitting).


Key Considerations

    ● Lifecycle Access: Documents can be added by the case creator, the assigned solver, or any other user with the appropriate permissions at any stage, including when the case is In Progress or Closed.
    ● Audit Trail: All document uploads and changes are tracked in the case history, providing a complete audit trail of who added what and when.
    ● Security: The ability to view, add, or manage documents on a case is controlled by the Functional Areas for Case Management security domain in the System functional area. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access sensitive attachments.

Even after a case is closed and most of its fields (like category or status) are no longer editable, the system still permits adding comments and documents. This is crucial for situations where follow-up information or related documentation needs to be recorded with the original case for future reference.

CM19.comments:
Yes, there are multiple configuration options for cases and Workday Help to align with your business needs. The following can be modified and reported on: Case Management Flags, Case Statuses, Help Case SLAs, and Service Teams. The service team or individual within a service team, user-defined cases statuses (scheduled, unscheduled, etc.) prioritization, and response time based on SLAs may all be reported on to capture who might be responding to cases, their frequency, their volumes, and SLA/response times.

Workday provides a suite of standard, out-of-the-box reports for case management that cover the key operational metrics listed.

These are often referred to as Standard Reports in Workday. They are pre-built and delivered with the Workday Help application to provide immediate value and insight without requiring any initial configuration.

Standard Reports for Case Management

You can expect to find standard reports that cover the following areas:

    ● Open/Closed Case Counts: Reports like "Cases by Status" provide a quick overview of how many cases are currently in an "Open," "In Progress," "Closed," or other custom status. This is fundamental for tracking overall workload and backlog.
    ● Staff Assignment: You will find reports such as "Cases by Solver Group" or "Cases by Assignee" that show how cases are distributed across different teams and individual agents. This is essential for monitoring workload balance and identifying potential bottlenecks.
    ● Volume by Category: Reports like "Cases by Category" allow you to see which types of issues are being reported most frequently. This is a critical input for trend analysis, helping you identify areas where more training, documentation, or process improvement may be needed.

CM20.comments:
Workday delivers the ability to report across all data held in the system. Reports detailing trends by department, issue type, and representation status can be generated as needed in Workday.

With Workday you have the ability to create custom reports that analyze trends across various dimensions is a core strength of the Workday platform. Workday is not limited to pre-defined reports; it provides a powerful, built-in Report Writer that allows you to build sophisticated analyses from the ground up to meet your specific needs.

CM21.comments:
Dashboard functionality is standard in Workday. Workday provides over 100 dashboards with the full Workday solution. In addition, customers can create an unlimited number of custom dashboards. Organizations can easily add new default analytics, called Worklets to any dashboard. Individual users can then configure additional reports onto the dashboard.

A key strength of the Workday platform is its ability to create highly configurable dashboards that provide real-time operational metrics. This is not a rigid, pre-built feature but a flexible framework that allows you to tailor dashboards to the specific needs of your audience, such as HR leaders, legal teams, or case managers.

This is achieved by combining two of Workday's most powerful tools: the Workday Report Writer and Dashboards.

CM22.comments:
Workday Human Capital Management provides country-specific diversity data definitions (such as ethnicity, personal identifiers), as well as gender and compensation grade profiles, to allow for country specific reporting requirements alongside other worker data such as employment type and management level. Reporting compensation in relation to worker demographics (i.e. ethnicity, age group, and gender) is seamless.

Workday is not just a system with a set of pre-built reports; it is a comprehensive reporting platform that allows you to create highly configurable reports to match the specific layouts, data points, and logic of any central system.

Workday's ability to serve as the "single source of truth" relies on its capability to present that truth in whatever format is required for consumption by other systems or for comparison with legacy data.

Here is a breakdown of the tools and concepts that enable this functionality:

1. The Workday Report Writer

This is the core engine for all reporting in Workday. It is a powerful, built-in tool that allows you to create custom reports from scratch without needing to know SQL or have database administration skills.

2. Calculated Fields

This is the most powerful tool for ensuring a report can match the logic of another system. Calculated fields allow you to create new, custom fields within Workday without programming. You can derive data, transform existing data, and apply business logic.

3. Custom Report Layout and Formatting

Once you have the right data (using standard and calculated fields), the Report Writer gives you full control over the presentation:

4. Integration-Readiness

These highly configurable reports are the foundation of Workday's integration capabilities.

    ● Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): You can use a custom report as the data source for an outbound EIB. This allows you to generate a file (e.g., a CSV) with the exact columns, headings, and data formats required by a downstream "central system" and deliver it on a schedule.
    ● XSLT Transformations: For more complex formatting requirements, you can apply an XSLT transformation to the report's XML output to create a file in virtually any text-based format.

By combining the Report Writer, Calculated Fields, and control over output formatting, you can build demographic reports in Workday that are not just similar to, but are a precise, field-for-field replica of, the reports from your central system.

CM23.comments:
Workday delivers a non-employee role with minimized access already configured based on best practices. The customer has the ability to modify this role as needed to meet their organization policies and needs. Non-employee roles might include roles (with appropriate security access/limitations) for contingent workers (contractors, subcontractors, etc.), retirees, external learners, and external applicants.

Workday is designed to be highly configurable and can absolutely be set up to operate in an "internal-only" mode for case management.

By default, Workday's security is restrictive. Access to any task or data must be explicitly granted. Therefore, achieving an internal-only model is a matter of not granting self-service access to the general employee population, rather than having to disable pre-existing features.

Here is how you would configure Workday Help to operate exclusively for internal teams (like HR, Legal, Payroll, etc.) to create and manage cases among themselves or on behalf of employees, without any employee-facing features.

CM24.comments:
Workday leverages role-based security to determine access to different functionalities and information. Security can be configured to trigger against roles and groups to ensure each user has relevant access.

The ability to restrict case access based on user roles is a fundamental and powerful capability of Workday's security framework, especially as it applies to Workday Help.

This is not achieved through a single setting but by combining several components of Workday's security architecture to create a robust, contextual, and role-based access model.

Here is a breakdown of how this is accomplished:

Core Components for Role-Based Case Security

    1. Case Categories: This is the primary element used to drive security. You create distinct categories for different types of cases (e.g., "HR Policy Inquiry," "Payroll Discrepancy," "IT Support Request," "Legal Investigation"). Each category can have its own unique security configuration.
    2. Security Groups: These groups define the "roles" or populations of users. Workday uses many types of security groups, but for this purpose, the most common are:
        ● Role-Based Security Groups: These are dynamic groups that include all users assigned to a specific role (e.g., all "HR Partners," all "Managers," all "Payroll Administrators").
        ● User-Based Security Groups: These are manually created lists of specific individuals. This is ideal for small, static teams like a "Legal Department" or an "Ethics Committee."

    3. Domain Security Policies: This is where the configuration comes together. Domains represent a set of related tasks or data (e.g., View: Case, Manage: Case Documents, Reassign: Case). You configure the security policy for a domain to specify which security groups have access, and you can make this access conditional on the Case Category.

CM25.comments:
Workday has robust security capabilities that allow for the creation and handling of fully private cases, accessible only to designated personnel, such as a legal team.

This is a critical function for managing sensitive employee relations, investigations, or other confidential matters. The privacy is not a separate feature but is configured using Workday's standard, powerful security framework.

CM26.comments: Workday provides "always on auditing" throughout the entire system. All change-effecting actions are tracked within Workday by default, and the audit trails for any object within Workday can be accessed for further review by individuals with the correct security permissions. Workday does not provide a way to “turn-off” audit trails within the system, and Workday customers do not have to take any additional steps to enable and/or maintain the audit trails. They are maintained and provided automatically as part of your Workday subscription.

CM27.comments: Workday enables you to manage the lifecycle of help cases through case purging. This allows you to permanently delete cases to comply with your organization's data retention policies. While Workday provides the tools to define and execute retention rules, the process itself is manual and not fully automated.

SC01.comments:
The Workday platform is fundamentally designed as a system of record, and it fully supports the requirement to maintain historical schedule and time data for long-term periods to meet legal, union, and internal policy needs.

Workday's architecture ensures that this data is not only stored but is also auditable and accessible. The retention period is not limited by the system; data is retained indefinitely unless you explicitly choose to purge it.

Workday provides the flexibility to meet your data retention requirements. Here is a summary of how historical schedule data is managed and how you can configure retention.

Data Retention in Workday

Workday does not impose a maximum retention period for customer data, including historical schedule records. Your organization has control over how long data is stored in the tenant. Data is retained for the lifetime of the tenant unless your organization actively purges it using Workday's data management tools.

This approach allows you to define your own data retention policies in line with legal, union, and grievance process requirements.

SC02.comments:
Workday maintains a full history of all events including position assignment and compensation changes. Workday is an always-on audit application. Also, Workday Time Tracking supports retroactive worked time and time entries, as well as Time Offs and Leave of Absence.

This capability is a cornerstone of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are all part of a single system, it can seamlessly track historical changes and automatically calculate the financial impact of those changes with full auditability.

SC03.comments:
Workday Scheduling provides a range of configurable scheduling settings that allow various warnings and/or errors for various conditions to be triggered. Examples include minimum breaks between shifts, minimum shift length, maximum shift length, maximum daily hours, maximum weekly hours, required meals and breaks, consecutive days and overtime thresholds.

This is a fundamental capability of Workday's Human Capital Management (HCM) and Time Tracking/Absence platform. The system is designed to create, assign, and maintain a detailed and historical record of each employee's standard work schedule.

This functionality is critical, as it forms the foundation for numerous other processes, including payroll, leave accruals, and resource planning.

SC04.comments:
Workday provides visibility for all worker and business process changes as part of the always-on audit controls. Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time and Workday Scheduling which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs actual time reporting. Workday Time Tracking leverages the worker data already held within Workday, including the Core Compensation Pay Rates, Absence Plans and Job Data, which allows this data to be available for base rates natively.

This capability is a core strength of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are not separate systems but are all part of a single platform, it provides unparalleled real-time visibility and seamless reconciliation between scheduled work, exceptions, and final pay.

This real-time, integrated process eliminates the manual reconciliation work common in systems with separate time and payroll modules, significantly reducing errors and ensuring that employees are paid accurately based on their approved schedules and exceptions.

SC05.comments:
Workday's business process framework supports the complete cycle for probation periods. This includes the ability to configure specific conditions for probationary periods based on contract duration, type of employment, previous employment, region, collective agreements and more. Specific business processes are delivered for Manage Probation Period, Pre-Hire Probation Period Details, and Review Probation Period.

Workday can support this requirement through a combination of standard features and configurable components. While the standard probation period functionality is typically based on a set duration (e.g., 90 days), you can use calculated fields and alerts to create a process based on actual hours worked.

Standard Probation Period Functionality

Workday's native probation period feature allows you to track a period of time from an employee's hire or start date. You can configure alerts to notify managers and other stakeholders when a standard, duration-based probation period is coming to an end.

However, this standard feature is based on a calendar duration (e.g., days, months) and does not natively track a cumulative total of hours worked.

Leading Practice for Hours-Based Probation

To meet the requirement of a probation period based on actual hours worked, you can leverage the power of Workday Time Tracking and Calculated Fields. This approach involves creating a mechanism to count the hours and trigger a notification once the threshold is met.

SC06.comments:
Yes, the system can track continuing education units and certifications within the worker profile in Workday Core HCM. Workday Learning supports configurable Unit Types, which allows customers to define their accreditation units (Professional Development Units, CEUs, Credits, certification units) within the properties of a course with flexibility.

Workday is well-equipped to track both continuing education requirements and certifications. This is primarily managed through the Talent and Performance and Workday Learning modules.

SC07.comments:
Workday Scheduling, Time Tracking, and Absence Management work as a unified system. Time off requests and approvals update balances dynamically. Schedules integrate with timekeeping for variance reporting and accurate payroll.

These capabilities represent a core and fundamental strength of the Workday platform. Workday's single architecture, where HCM, Scheduling, Time Tracking, Absence, and Payroll all exist within one system, is specifically designed to provide this seamless integration and real-time visibility.

Workday's architecture is designed to provide seamless integration between scheduling, timekeeping, and absence management to ensure data accuracy and streamline payroll processes.

Integration of Scheduling, Timekeeping, and Payroll

Workday provides a single, unified system for Workforce Management that includes Workday Scheduling, Workday Time Tracking, and Workday Payroll. This integration ensures a smooth and accurate flow of data.

    ● Schedule to Timecard: When schedules are created in Workday Scheduling, they serve as the baseline for an employee's timecard in Workday Time Tracking. As employees report their time (e.g., via clock-in/clock-out or manual entry), the system automatically compares the actual hours worked against the scheduled hours.
    ● Variance Tracking: This direct comparison allows the system to identify and flag schedule variances in real-time. Examples include:
        ● Overtime: Time worked beyond the scheduled shift or weekly threshold.
        ● Unscheduled Absences: A "no-show" for a scheduled shift.
        ● Early/Late Punches: Deviations from the scheduled start and end times.

    ● Flow to Payroll: Once timecards are approved by a manager, the accurately calculated hours—including all variances, overtime, and shift differentials—flow directly into Workday Payroll. This eliminates the need for manual data transfer, significantly reduces the risk of payroll errors, and creates a clear audit trail.
    ● Reporting: Because all data resides within the same system, you can generate real-time reports and dashboards that provide visibility into scheduled vs. actual hours, overtime costs, and other key labor metrics.

Vacation Requesting and Leave Balance Integration

Workday also provides tight integration between vacation requests, managed in Workday Absence Management, and employee leave balances.

SC08.comments:
Workday supports effective dated tracking of union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. These elements are attributes of the employee and can be used throughout the entire system for compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, and reporting. Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for assigning Workday Scheduling tags based on specific qualifications, assessments, and/or experience. A worker or position may be associated with a wide variety of attributes such as job profile, location, cost center, manager, default weekly hours, shift, and status.

This is a fundamental principle of Workday's architecture and a primary benefit of its unified platform. The scheduling system is not a separate module that needs to be integrated with HCM; it is an intrinsic part of the same system, operating on the same single source of truth.

This seamless access to core employee data is what allows Workday to make intelligent, compliant, and efficient scheduling decisions, rather than just placing names in time slots.

SC09.comments:
Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time, and scheduling, which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs. actual time reporting. Workday can manage shift differential eligibility by setting up standard schedules, for example, based on geography (country) or other criteria. These shifts can have different pay rates based on the shift or base plus a premium for hours worked on a shift or eligible times of day. Shift differential calculations are configured to ensure workers working in the specified timeframes get the right level of pay. Multiple shift differential calculations can be combined, for example, when different pay rates apply from 10pm to 12am and from 12am to 4am.

Workday doesn't just integrate its scheduling, timekeeping, and payroll systems; they are all components of a single, unified system operating on one source of employee data. This distinction is crucial because it means there is no "transmission" or "transfer" of data between separate systems. Instead, it's a seamless, real-time flow of information within one platform.

SC10.comments:
While Workday does not offer pre-built, certified connectors specifically for Vector Solutions or InTime, it provides a robust and flexible integration platform that can be used to build custom connections to these or any other third-party systems.

If you find that the native Workday Scheduling capabilities do not fully meet your Public Safety requirements, you can develop a custom integration to exchange schedule, assignment, and availability data.

Workday Integration Tools

Workday offers several tools to facilitate custom integrations. The choice of tool depends on the complexity, data volume, and real-time needs of the integration.

    ● Workday Studio: This is Workday's most powerful integration tool, providing a graphical development environment for building complex, custom integrations. Workday Studio would be the leading practice for creating a sophisticated, bidirectional integration with systems like InTime or Vector Solutions. It can handle complex data transformations, custom logic, and error handling.
    ● REST and SOAP APIs: Workday provides a comprehensive library of web services that allow external systems to securely access and update Workday data. You can develop a custom solution that calls Workday's APIs to:
        ● Get Data: Pull worker information, availability, leave requests, and other relevant data from Workday to send to the external scheduling system.
        ● Put Data: Push approved schedules, assignments, and actual hours worked from the external system back into Workday for timekeeping and payroll processing.

    ● Workday Cloud Connect: This is a framework for building packaged integrations. While there isn't a pre-built Cloud Connect package for these specific vendors, the underlying technology could be used by a development team to create a reusable connector.
    ● Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): EIBs are a user-friendly tool for getting data into and out of Workday. They are ideal for simpler, bulk data transfers that can be scheduled to run on a recurring basis. An EIB could be used for less time-sensitive data exchanges, such as sending a daily file of approved hours from the external system to Workday.

In summary, if you need to integrate with Vector Solutions or InTime, it is technically feasible. The project would involve a custom development effort utilizing Workday's integration tools, with Workday Studio and the extensive API library being the most likely components of a successful solution.

SC11.comments:

Workday ensures employees have valid certifications and correct credentials for their roles by issuing and renewing certifications based on successful completion of learning content. Certification rules allow configuration of required learning content and completion frequency. Workday provides reporting capabilities around active, inactive, and expiring certifications.

Workday Scheduling is designed to help organizations create, manage, and publish schedules for their workers. Key features that support this include:

    ● Position-Based Scheduling: Schedules can be assigned directly to positions, which is useful for roles with consistent shift coverage needs.
    ● Publishing: Once schedules are finalized, they can be published, making them visible to employees through their Workday profiles and the Workday mobile app.
    ● Flexibility: The tool is designed to support the scheduling needs of diverse teams, from hourly workers in retail or facilities to salaried staff with more fixed schedules.

SC18.comments:

Workday's scheduling solutions are designed to handle complex scheduling requirements, including 24/7 coverage with varied shift lengths and start/end times. This is managed through a combination of Work Schedule Calendars, Workday Scheduling, and the optional Workday Labor Optimization add-on.

    ● Workday Scheduling: This solution is ideal for organizations with hourly workers who have relatively static or consistent schedules. It allows you to create schedules manually or automatically based on previous periods. The system validates schedules against business rules, regulatory requirements, and worker availability, preferences, and qualifications.
    ● Workday Labor Optimization: For businesses with more fluctuating labor demands, this AI-powered add-on to Workday Scheduling optimizes schedules to ensure the right workers with the right skills are in the right place at the right time. It helps balance business needs, labor costs, and worker preferences.

SC19.comments:

Workday's scheduling functionality is centered on assigning schedules to individual workers rather than assigning a shift to a team or squad as a single, collective unit. However, you can achieve efficient team-based scheduling and coverage management with it.

    ● Provides fundamental tools to create and manage schedules

    ● Assigns shifts by taking into account availability, preferences, regulatory rules but doesn’t automatically adjust the structure of the shifts themselves
    ● Provides tools to allow managers or scheduling administrators to manage/adjust/edit schedules seamlessly

    ● Provides visibility into gaps in coverage and unassigned shifts

Workday Scheduling provides capabilities to manage coverage and overtime when backfilling is required.

SC21.comments:
Workday Scheduling engages workforce managers and operations leads, by providing an easy to use, dynamic scheduling tool to allow schedules to be quickly generated and to handle dynamic schedule changes for emergencies. Managers can use the My Team's Schedule report to make immediate, ad-hoc changes directly on the schedule calendar. This allows them to:

    ● Instantly add or edit shifts to deploy workers to an incident.
    ● Cancel existing shifts to reassign personnel from their regular duties.
    ● Find available and qualified workers to fill critical gaps.

These changes are temporary overrides that don't affect the workers' base schedules. You can use Schedule Tags (e.g., "Emergency Response") on these shifts to simplify tracking, reporting, and applying premium pay for the incident. If additional staff are needed, managers can use the system to identify qualified and available workers. The Replace Worker functionality can suggest suitable replacements for backfilling non-critical roles, considering factors like availability and qualifications.

You can create and apply specific Schedule Tags to these dynamic shifts (e.g., "Emergency Response," "Mutual Aid," "Incident Command"). This is critical for:

    ● Reporting: Easily track and report on all hours and costs associated with a specific incident.
    ● Payroll: Automatically apply premium pay or different pay rules for hours worked during the declared emergency.
    ● Automated Notifications: When a manager adds or changes a worker's shift, the system can automatically send a notification to the worker's mobile device or Workday inbox, ensuring rapid communication of the new assignment. 

Real-time insights also allow workforce managers to react quickly to labor cost and productivity KPI impacts as schedules are being determined and/or worked.

To ensure leadership coverage during all operational hours through rotating command duty officers, you can leverage Calendar Events in Workday. This approach is effective for creating complex, rotating schedules that do not follow a standard weekly pattern.

Here is a general procedure to set this up:

    1. Create a Calendar: Establish a new calendar specifically for the command duty officer schedule.
    2. Create a Calendar Event: For the first shift in the rotation, create a calendar event. This event will represent the 'on-duty' period for a specific officer or role.
    3. Define Recurrence: Within the calendar event, set up the recurrence pattern. This is the key step to automate the rotation. You can define the cycle length (e.g., every 20 or 30 days) and the duration of the pattern.
    4. Assign Workers: Assign the relevant command duty officers to this calendar. Their schedules will then reflect the rotating events you have created.

This method allows you to create a complete, recurring cycle for the rotating duty, which can be set up with or without a specific end date.

Workday supports complex, crew-based scheduling for organizations like fire departments through its Workday Scheduling module, which is part of the broader Workforce Management solution.

Here is a brief explanation of how the system addresses your specific requirements:

    ● Crew-Based Scheduling and Apparatus Assignment: The system allows you to define specific "posts" or "jobs" that can represent an apparatus (e.g., Engine 1, Truck 2). You can then create schedules that assign entire crews or teams to these specific posts for their shifts.
    ● Shift-Based Rotation: Workday Scheduling is designed to handle complex rotations. You can configure rotating shift patterns and apply them to your crews, ensuring that teams are correctly assigned to their apparatus and location according to the established rotation.
    ● Location Tracking: By assigning crews to an apparatus/post, which is tied to a work location, the system effectively tracks where each team is scheduled to be during their operational hours.
    ● Assignment to Multiple Apparatus: The system is flexible, allowing for individuals to be assigned to different posts or apparatus. For example, a firefighter could be scheduled on an engine for one shift and a rescue unit for another, depending on qualifications and operational needs. This is often managed through the Scheduling to Posts functionality.

These capabilities, combined with Time Tracking and Absence Management, provide a centralized solution for managing schedules, tracking time, and ensuring proper staffing levels across all apparatus and locations.

Note: Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

SC24.comments:
Workday can forecast vacation hours to support processes like annual vacation bidding. The system includes a Projected Balance feature. This allows an employee or manager to view a time off balance as of a future date.

When a future date is selected (e.g., the end of the next year), Workday calculates the total time off that will be available on that date. This calculation includes all future accruals that are scheduled to occur between the present and the selected future date.

This gives employees a clear view of their full annual entitlement, allowing them to confidently bid on vacation slots for the entire year, even for time they have not yet technically accrued.

Workday provides visibility for all eligible Time Off plans and associated balances. Time Off/Vacation balances are dynamically updated as time off/vacation is requested/approved and based on effective dates, so a worker could determine what their vacation balance will be as of a future date less already approved vacation dates.

SC25.comments:
With shift bidding, an unlimited amount of workers can bid on the same shift from the Open Shift Board and managers can decide which worker to select to cover the shift.

Workday supports shift bidding, which can be configured to meet the needs of organizations like Public Safety where seniority is a key factor in awarding shifts.

The feature, known as Open Shift Bidding, allows workers to bid on available shifts posted on the Open Shift Board. Managers are then able to review the bids and select a worker to cover the shift.

A key aspect for Public Safety is that when a manager reviews the bids, the system provides details about each worker's seniority. This allows the manager to make an informed decision and award the shift based on established seniority rules. It is important to note that the system provides the seniority information to the manager, who then makes the final selection; it does not automatically award the shift to the most senior bidder.

Workday Scheduling provides the flexibility to create a wide variety of shift configurations to meet different operational needs.

Here’s how it supports the patterns you mentioned:

    ● Rotating Schedules: These are managed using Schedule Patterns. You can define a sequence of work shifts that repeats over a specified number of weeks, and then assign this pattern to workers.
    ● Split Shifts: You can define a Work Shift to include non-contiguous blocks of time. For example, you can create a single shift for a day that includes a work block from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM and another from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM.
    ● Fixed Assignments: This is a standard capability where workers are assigned a consistent schedule that does not change week to week. This can be managed by assigning a simple, non-rotating Schedule Pattern or by directly assigning shifts.

In addition to these, you can also use scheduling tags to identify workers with specific skills for certain shifts and define labor requirements to ensure adequate coverage.

Based on different dimensions, (e.g. roles, location, company division) different time entry types can be made available.

SC35.comments:
Workday Scheduling settings allows for enforcing of specific organizational rules, such as minimum/maximum shift lengths and required breaks/meals per shift, as well as worker preferences. Workday Time Tracking managers can proactively track approaching overtime and overtime reached by employees.

Yes, that is a core capability of Workday's workforce management solution, which combines Workday Scheduling and Workday Time Tracking.

Here is how the system addresses those specific compliance and fatigue management needs:

    ● Overlapping Shifts: When creating or editing schedules, Workday automatically flags shifts that overlap for the same worker, helping managers avoid scheduling conflicts.
    ● Overtime Triggers: You can configure scheduling validations and time calculations to manage overtime.
        ● Daily and Weekly Overtime Thresholds: You can set thresholds for the maximum number of hours a worker can be scheduled in a day or week. When a schedule exceeds this limit, Workday flags the excess hours as overtime.
        ● Time Calculations: For payroll purposes, you can create Standard Overtime Calculations in Workday Time Tracking. These calculations automatically identify and tag hours that exceed daily, weekly, or biweekly thresholds, ensuring accurate overtime pay.

    ● Excessive Hours and Fatigue Management:
        ● Maximum Shift Length: You can define the maximum duration for a single shift.
        ● Minimum Rest Between Shifts: You can set a required minimum rest period between scheduled shifts to help prevent worker fatigue.
        ● Maximum Working Days: You can specify the maximum number of consecutive days a worker can be scheduled to work in a week.


These rules are configured as Scheduling Validations and can be applied to specific groups of workers using eligibility rules. When a manager builds a schedule that violates one of these rules, Workday displays a validation error, alerting the manager so they can make corrections.

SC36.comments:
Workday supports the effective dated tracking union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. As attributes of the employee, these elements can then be used throughout the entire system for things like compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, reporting, etc. Workday also provides Workday Scheduling settings that can allow for enforcing of specific Workday Scheduling rules, including maximum hours worked per day (with alerts for associated overtime). Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

Workday provides the flexibility to configure and enforce complex union-negotiated rules through a combination of its scheduling and time-tracking features.

    ● Schedule Validations: This is the core enforcement engine. You can build rules that prevent managers from publishing a schedule that violates union policies, such as assigning a shift to an unqualified worker, scheduling insufficient rest time, or exceeding weekly hour limits.
    ● Shift Assignments and Bidding: Eligibility for shifts and bidding is managed by checking worker attributes. Validations can be configured to ensure a worker has the required qualifications, certifications, or seniority before they can be assigned to or bid on a specific shift.
    ● Overtime Rotation: Workday uses Time Calculations to continuously track accumulated overtime for each worker. Managers then use custom reports, sorted by these hours (e.g., from least to most), to ensure they offer overtime to the correct person in the rotation, adhering to equalization rules.
    ● Call-Out Procedures: Similar to overtime, you can create custom reports that act as the official "call-out list." These reports are configured to order employees based on specific union rules (e.g., lowest overtime hours, last call-out date), ensuring managers follow the negotiated procedure for unscheduled needs.

SC37.comments:
Workday provides a range of configurable scheduling settings that allow various warnings and/or errors for various conditions to be triggered. Examples include minimum breaks between shifts, minimum shift length, maximum shift length, maximum daily hours, maximum weekly hours, required meals and breaks, consecutive days and overtime thresholds.

Workday handles mandatory holdovers and voluntary shift fills by combining schedule tags with custom reporting to ensure fairness and compliance.

    ● Tracking the Events:
        ● Mandatory Holdovers: When a worker is required to stay past their shift, a manager makes an ad-hoc edit to their schedule, extending the shift and applying a "Mandatory Holdover" schedule tag.
        ● Voluntary Fills: When a vacancy is filled by an employee who volunteers, the shift they pick up (often from the Open Shift Board) is marked with a "Voluntary Fill" schedule tag.

    ● Ensuring Equitable Distribution:
The system continuously tracks the hours associated with these tags for each employee. To ensure equitable distribution, you create custom reports that serve as live "equalization lists." These reports rank employees based on the specific union rules, such as:
        ● Lowest total overtime hours.
        ● Fewest mandatory holdovers year-to-date.
        ● Seniority or rotation order.


When a manager needs to assign a holdover or offer voluntary overtime, they consult this ranked report to identify the correct employee. This process provides a clear, data-driven, and auditable method for complying with union agreements. The tags also ensure the hours are paid at the correct rate in Workday Payroll and can also carry over into Workday Finance for visibility into labor costs for the organization (if desired).

SC38.comments:
Workday provides configurable time calculation rules and time totals, which can allow worked time to be split into relevant time codes according to union provisions or department-specific policies. 

Workday provides the functionality to track and balance overtime assignments to ensure fair distribution, although it is not a single, automated feature but rather a configured process combining several Workday modules. The process primarily leverages Workday Time Tracking and Workday Reporting.

SC39.comments:
Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for the assigning of Workday Scheduling tags to be assigned (and un-assigned) based on specific qualifications, assessments and/or experience.

Workday restricts shift assignments based on certifications and training status by seamlessly integrating employee qualifications from Workday HCM with its Workday Scheduling module.

Here is a brief description of the process:

    1. Maintain Certifications in HCM: All employee certifications and qualifications (e.g., CPR, firearms, specific equipment training) are maintained within the employee's profile in Workday HCM. This includes tracking issuance and expiration dates, ensuring the data is always current.
    2. Define Requirements on the Shift: When creating a schedule, supervisors can define the specific certifications or skills required for a particular shift or role. For example, a shift can be configured to require an active "Firearms Qualification" certification.
    3. Automated Matching and Restriction: The Workday Scheduling engine uses this data to enforce the rules automatically:
        ● During Schedule Generation: The system will only consider and assign employees who hold the necessary, unexpired certifications for that specific shift.
        ● Finding Replacements: If a supervisor needs to fill a vacant shift, the system will only suggest available employees who meet the defined certification requirements.


This ensures that only qualified and compliant personnel are assigned to roles that require specific training or credentials, preventing manual errors and maintaining operational readiness.

SC40.comments:
Workday's Absence Management provides a comprehensive view into a worker's use of various absence plans, ranging from vacation and sick plans to defined leaves of absence. By taking advantage of Workday's robust calculation engine, Workday Absence Management enables organizations to define and manage both their accrued time off and leave of absence plans. Workday’s Absence Management can be used seamlessly with Workday Payroll or as part of Workday HCM.

Workday's Absence Management enables workers to submit absence requests that can follow a defined path for approval. Workers and managers can view worker's leave balances and the history of their leave requests through self-service. Once a request is approved the worker's balance is automatically decremented, and the leave information is shared with payroll for calculation purposes.

Workday's Absence Management provides the following high-level functionality:

    ● Highly flexible and configurable set of rules to define eligibility criteria
    ● Configurable accrual policies and accrual limits
    ● Configurable negative balance limits
    ● Configurable rules for carryover allowances
    ● Flexible calculation period schedules

Workers are able to:

    ● Create absence or time off requests through self-service on a desktop or mobile device
    ● View time off balances through self-service, including calculation of balances as of a future date
    ● View history of time off or absence requests
    ● View approved team time off
