PM01.comments:
IBM TRIRIGA is a comprehensive platform that does track a wide range of master data, including lease terms, balances, and security deposits. It also manages property-related data such as tax information, including R numbers, port- or tenant-paid taxes, and drainage fees. Additionally, TRIRIGA can handle certificates of insurance (COI). This information is stored in a centralized database and can be accessed by authorized users across various modules within the software, thus enhancing efficiency and data accuracy. It's designed to provide a holistic view of a company's real estate portfolio, facilitating better decision-making and strategic planning.

“R Number” or property reference number is a custom field within the Property or Building record.

PM02.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the capability to track customer billing activity across different locations and business units. It offers a robust financial management module that can manage complex billing scenarios, including multi-location and multi-business unit billing. 
TRIRIGA provides both rolled-up and drill-down views of billing activities, offering users the flexibility to view summary-level information or to delve into specifics, depending on their needs. This functionality allows for efficient monitoring of billing activities, ensuring accurate invoicing, and facilitating thorough analysis of financial performance.
The system's integrated nature allows for seamless access to this billing data from other modules, such as lease accounting or space management, providing a comprehensive view of associated data and driving better decision-making.

PM03.comments: TRIRIGA offers extensive functionality and customization options, a portal specifically designed for customers to view billing information and contracts requires additional configuration and potentially the development of custom workflows and integrations.

PM04.comments: Custom Customer Portal with link to payment app (Paypal, Venmo, etc…)

PM05.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the capability to track contact details and roles associated with each tenant or lease. The system allows you to store and manage contact information for various parties involved in a lease. This includes details like names, email addresses, phone numbers, and more.

Furthermore, you can assign specific roles to these contacts, such as legal contact, billing contact, or facilities lead. This helps in organizing and streamlining communication, ensuring that the right person is contacted for specific matters related to the lease.

In terms of logging communication history, TRIRIGA doesn't inherently have this feature. However, through its document management and workflow capabilities, you can maintain a record of correspondence by attaching emails, letters, and other communication to the relevant lease or tenant record. This way, all communication related to a particular lease or tenant is readily accessible and searchable, thereby maintaining a de facto communication history.

PM06.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does offer functionality to manage broker records. It enables you to track various details related to brokers, such as contact information, commission terms, and associated lease deals.

When it comes to lease deals, TRIRIGA allows you to link brokers to specific leases, providing a clear overview of who is involved in which transactions. This facilitates better management of broker relationships and commission structures.

Moreover, TRIRIGA's financial management module can be used to manage payment milestones related to broker commissions. You can set up rules and triggers in the system to alert when payments are due based on predefined lease event milestones, such as lease start or renewal dates.

As with many TRIRIGA features, the exact implementation can depend on your organization's specific configuration and usage of the system. It's recommended to consult with your TRIRIGA administrator or IBM representative to understand the full scope of available functionality and how it can be tailored to meet your needs.

PM07.comments: Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) logbook feature. It has the ability to manage and track interactions, communications, and activities related to tenants, brokers, and other stakeholders. The system maintains a record of calls, emails, meetings, and other forms of communication tied to specific individuals or leases. Additionally, TRIRIGA offers tools for managing leads, tracking opportunities, and handling sales pipelines, similar to traditional CRM systems. This functionality can be particularly useful for organizations involved in real estate leasing or sales.

PM09.comments: IBM TRIRIGA does provide support for tracking and reporting related to environmental assessments, including BREEAM scores, for applicable assets or facilities. However, this capability is typically achieved through the use of custom fields and workflows, rather than being a built-in, out-of-the-box feature. TRIRIGA's flexibility allows users to define custom properties to capture BREEAM scores or other sustainability metrics. These custom fields can then be incorporated into reports, dashboards, and other data presentations, enabling you to monitor and analyze environmental performance across your portfolio. Furthermore, TRIRIGA's Workflow and Business Intelligence components can be leveraged to automate score tracking and generate automated reports. For instance, you could set up a workflow to prompt facility managers to input BREEAM scores upon completion of an assessment, or use BI tools to create customized reports showing trends or comparisons across BREEAM-certified buildings.

PM10.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have robust capabilities to manage lease options such as renewals, expansions, and early terminations, including the ability to track key dates and conditions, and manage stakeholder visibility.
1. Key Dates: TRIRIGA's Lease Management module allows you to set up and track significant lease events and dates, such as commencement, expiration, and option exercise dates. These can be configured to trigger alerts or automated workflows, ensuring important deadlines aren't missed.
2. Conditions: The system lets you detail and associate various conditions related to lease options. This could include specific terms and clauses, financial considerations, or even regulatory requirements. This information can be linked to the appropriate lease documents, enhancing traceability and compliance.
3. Stakeholder Visibility: TRIRIGA supports role-based access control, allowing you to manage who within your organization or among external stakeholders (like tenants or brokers) has visibility into specific lease details, including options. This ensures that the right people have the right level of access at the right time. For instance, a facilities manager might have visibility into day-to-day lease operations, while a financial analyst might focus on fiscal implications of lease options.
4. Automated Workflows: TRIRIGA's workflow engine can automate tasks related to lease options. For example, it can automatically update lease statuses, notify relevant stakeholders, or initiate financial processes when a lease option is exercised.

PM11.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does offer the capability to configure building units and lease spaces with structured hierarchies and clearly defined spatial relationships. This functionality is a core part of TRIRIGA's spatial management features.
1. Hierarchical Structures: TRIRIGA allows users to create a hierarchical structure for floor plans or building layouts. This means that you can group spaces together at varying levels of granularity, such as by entire buildings, floors, wings, or individual suites. This hierarchical organization can reflect the physical layout of a building and can be customized to suit your specific needs.
2. Spatial Relationships: TRIRIGA can represent and manage spatial relationships between different areas of a building. You can define contiguous spaces, adjacent rooms, or shared areas, all of which can be visualized on floor plans. This capability supports a clear understanding of how spaces relate to each other within a building, which is crucial for tasks like space allocation, facility management, and move management.
3. Floor Plans and Visualization: TRIRIGA includes tools for importing and maintaining digital floor plans. These plans can be annotated with spatial information, such as room numbers or equipment locations. The system can also render these plans graphically, allowing users to visualize and navigate the spatial relationships between different areas.
4. Customization: TRIRIGA's spatial management features are highly configurable. You can define the way spaces and hierarchies are represented, and you can tailor the system to accommodate unique organizational requirements or industry-specific standards.

PM12.comments: Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the capability to set lease and billing details by location and by property within a location.

PM13.comments: TRIRIGA's Real Estate module allows you to store and manage multiple addresses for each lease. This feature is particularly useful when a lease requires transactions or communications to be sent to different addresses.

PM14.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support high-volume lease creation through various methods designed to streamline and automate the process, especially for leases with multiple units or recurring structures.
1. Batch Entry: TRIRIGA allows users to manually create multiple leases at once in a batch process. This feature is beneficial when setting up a large number of leases with similar characteristics.
2. Import Templates: TRIRIGA provides the ability to import lease data from external sources like spreadsheets using CSV files or other delimited text formats. You can create and save import templates to standardize the format and speed up the process for recurring imports.
3. Pre-configured Setup Rules: TRIRIGA's workflow and configuration tools can be used to create setup rules for recurring lease structures. Once established, these rules can automate the creation of lease records with predefined settings, significantly reducing the time and effort required for high-volume lease setup.
4. Mass Update and Modification: TRIRIGA also supports mass updates and modifications to lease records, allowing you to apply changes to multiple leases simultaneously. This can be particularly useful when dealing with periodic adjustments, such as annual rent reviews or utility charge updates.
5. Integration Capabilities: For extremely high-volume scenarios, TRIRIGA can integrate with external systems to automate lease data exchange. This could involve integrating with a company's existing real estate or finance systems to pull lease data directly into TRIRIGA.

PM15.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support sequential leasing of units to multiple tenants over time without causing data conflicts or reuse restrictions through its Real Estate module, which is designed to handle complex, multi-tenant leasing scenarios efficiently.
1. Lease Terms and Dates: TRIRIGA allows you to define separate start and end dates for each tenant's occupancy of a unit, accurately capturing the sequential nature of the leasing arrangement.
2. Space Management: The system can manage the availability and assignment of spaces across different lease periods. Once a tenant's lease term ends, the space can automatically become available for leasing to a new tenant at a later date without the risk of double-booking or data conflicts.
3. Automated Workflows: TRIRIGA's workflow capabilities can be configured to automate the space reallocation process. When one lease ends, a workflow can trigger the preparation of the space for the next tenant, including sending notifications, updating space records, and initiating any necessary maintenance or move management activities.

PM16.comments: Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support overlapping occupancy of a unit or space by multiple tenants during transitions or short-term arrangements, including temporary reuse scenarios TRIRIGA can accommodate lease periods that overlap, which is typical in scenarios where one tenant is moving out while another is moving in. This can be managed by setting distinct start and end dates for each tenant's occupancy.

PM17.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support lease transitions where a new tenant begins occupancy before the prior tenant's contract has fully terminated. This scenario, often referred to as a "lease-up" or "overlap," can be managed effectively within TRIRIGA's Real Estate module.
    Flexible Start and End Dates: TRIRIGA allows for setting distinct start and end dates for each tenant's occupancy, accommodating situations where one tenant's lease runs concurrently with another's.
    Transition Management: The system supports the coordination of lease transitions, including the preparation of spaces for new tenants and the handover from departing tenants. This can involve scheduling maintenance, coordinating with service providers, and managing any associated lease liabilities.
    Space Allocation: TRIRIGA maintains precise records of space allocation, ensuring that each tenant is correctly linked to the appropriate space during their occupancy, including periods of overlap.

PM18.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support the termination of only part of a lease, such as a subset of rooms or square footage, while keeping the rest active. This capability is managed through TRIRIGA's Lease Management module, which offers flexibility in handling partial lease terminations.
    Sub-leasing: TRIRIGA allows for the creation of sub-leases, where a portion of a tenant's space is leased to a sub-lessee. This effectively terminates the original tenant's lease for that specific space segment.
    Partial Termination: TRIRIGA can accommodate scenarios where only a portion of a lease is terminated, while the remainder continues under the original lease terms. This can be managed by updating the lease record to reflect the reduced space or square footage under the original lease agreement
    Space Management: The system enables precise tracking of space allocations, ensuring that the correct space is associated with the appropriate lease terms at any given time. Even in cases of partial termination, TRIRIGA can clearly indicate which parts of a space are still under active lease and which have been terminated or sub-leased.
    Billing Adjustments: TRIRIGA's financial management tools can handle billing adjustments resulting from partial lease terminations. This includes prorating rent and other charges according to the revised space allocation.

PM19.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support the classification of leases by various criteria, including business unit, lease type, and configurable tags. This categorization capability is a key feature of TRIRIGA's Real Estate  module, supporting reporting, workflow assignment, and complexity scoring.
    Business Unit Classification: TRIRIGA allows you to associate leases with specific business units, enabling you to manage and report on lease portfolios in alignment with your organization's structure.
    Lease Type Classification: The system supports the definition and application of different lease types, such as ground leases, terminal leases, or easements. This classification can drive specific workflows, reporting, and analytics tailored to each lease type.
    Configurable Tags: TRIRIGA's extensible data model allows for the creation of custom tags or categories to further classify leases according to your organization's needs. These tags can represent anything from environmental criteria to strategic importance, facilitating targeted reporting and decision-making.
    Workflow Assignment: Based on lease classifications, TRIRIGA can automatically route lease-related tasks to the appropriate business units or teams. For example, complex leases might be directed to a specialized review process, while routine leases follow a standard approval path.

PM20.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the ability to handle perpetual or long-term leases with special statuses, such as easements or year-to-year occupancy. TRIRIGA's Lease Management module is flexible and extensible enough to accommodate a wide variety of lease arrangements, including those that are non-standard or have unique characteristics.
    Customizable Lease Types: TRIRIGA supports the definition of custom lease types, beyond the standard annual or month-to-month leases. Users can create lease types tailored to specific needs, such as perpetual leases or easements.
    Special Status Indicators: Lease records in TRIRIGA can be marked or categorized with special statuses, flags, or tags to highlight unique attributes. For instance, an easement lease might be identified with a specific indicator to ensure it's recognized and treated differently in workflows, reports, and analyses.
    Extended Terms: TRIRIGA can handle long-term lease durations beyond typical annual or multi-year terms. This includes perpetual leases, which theoretically continue indefinitely unless explicitly terminated.
    Year-to-Year Occupancy: TRIRIGA's lease terms and renewal capabilities can accommodate year-to-year or periodic occupancy arrangements. These leases can be set up to automatically renew on an annual basis until terminated, with each renewal period clearly tracked.

PM21.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support the definition and tracking of landlord versus tenant maintenance responsibilities using configurable rules or assignment tables per lease or asset. This capability is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Maintenance Management module, which integrates closely with the Lease Management module to provide comprehensive facilities management support.
    Configurable Responsibility Rules: TRIRIGA allows you to define rules or logic that determine maintenance responsibilities based on various factors, including lease terms, asset types, and specific clauses outlined in lease agreements. These rules can be customized to align with your organization's standards or the unique terms of individual leases.
    Assignment Tables: You can set up assignment tables within TRIRIGA to explicitly map maintenance responsibilities. For example, you could create a table that lists which party is responsible for which types of maintenance tasks, such as HVAC repairs, plumbing, or structural repairs, for different asset types or lease scenarios.
    Lease-Level and Asset-Level Assignments: Responsibility assignments can be defined at both the lease level and the individual asset level within TRIRIGA. This granular approach ensures accurate tracking of maintenance obligations, regardless of whether they pertain to an entire building, a specific floor, or individual spaces or equipment.

PM22.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA provides the capability to configure custom fields and document metadata without requiring vendor customization, and it supports searching by specific fields such as operating agreements or leases of improved space. This functionality is available through TRIRIGA's extensible data model and robust search and reporting capabilities.
    Custom Fields: TRIRIGA allows users to add custom fields to various entities, including leases, properties, and tenants. These custom fields can be configured based on lease type, property class, or business unit. For example, you could add a custom field to capture specific details about a lease, such as the presence of an operating agreement or the nature of the space (improved or unimproved).
    Metadata Configuration: TRIRIGA supports the association of metadata tags or categories with documents, enabling you to classify and categorize lease-related documents according to predefined criteria. These tags can be based on lease type, property characteristics, or any other relevant attribute, facilitating organized document management and searching.
    Extensible Data Model: TRIRIGA's data model is designed to be extensible, meaning that users can define new entities, attributes, and relationships without needing to modify the underlying system code. This extensibility empowers organizations to tailor TRIRIGA to their specific requirements without relying on vendor customization services.

PM23.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the ability to route contract documents and drafts to relevant teams, including legal, risk, environmental, and finance, with built-in audit trails and status tracking. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine and Document Management features.
1. Workflow Engine: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine enables the automated routing of documents and tasks to designated reviewers or approvers based on predefined rules or business processes. You can create workflows tailored to your organization's requirements, ensuring that contract documents are directed to the appropriate teams (e.g., legal for review of lease terms, finance for budgetary considerations, risk for compliance checks, and environmental for sustainability evaluations).
2. Task Management: Within TRIRIGA's workflows, tasks can be assigned to specific individuals or teams, along with due dates and escalation procedures. This ensures that contract reviews are completed in a timely and orderly manner, with clear accountability.
3. Audit Trails: TRIRIGA maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all actions taken on contract documents, including viewings, edits, approvals, and rejections. This audit trail captures details such as who performed the action, when it was done, and what changes were made, providing an immutable record of document handling and review processes.
4. Status Tracking: TRIRIGA's workflow engine supports real-time tracking of document statuses and review progress. This allows stakeholders to monitor the contract review process, identify bottlenecks, and take corrective actions as needed.
5. Document Management: TRIRIGA's robust document management capabilities enable secure storage, version control, and easy retrieval of contract documents. Documents can be linked to specific leases, properties, or other entities within TRIRIGA, ensuring context and facilitating integrated management of real estate data.

PM24.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the ability to manage and track parallel contract drafts with placeholders, redlines, and version histories. This capability is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Document Management and Workflow Engine features.
1. Version Control: TRIRIGA's document management system supports version control, allowing users to create, save, and manage multiple versions of contract drafts. Each new version can be saved with a descriptive name or number, and the system retains a complete version history, including date and time stamps, and the user who created the version.
2. Placeholders and Redlines: TRIRIGA enables the insertion of placeholders or redlines within contract drafts to denote sections requiring attention or negotiation. These markups can be saved as part of the document's version history, capturing the evolution of the contract during the drafting process.
3. Parallel Drafts: With TRIRIGA's flexible workflow capabilities, you can manage parallel contract drafts simultaneously. Different versions or drafts can be routed through separate review workflows, ensuring that multiple parties can work on different iterations of a contract concurrently without interfering with each other's work.
4. Workflow Engine: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine can be configured to automate the review, approval, and revision processes for contract drafts. This includes setting up specific review tasks for different teams (e.g., legal for contract terms, finance for budgetary considerations) and managing the progression of drafts through various stages of completion.
5. Compare Versions: TRIRIGA's document management system allows users to compare different versions of a contract side-by-side, highlighting additions, deletions, and modifications. This feature simplifies the process of identifying changes and resolving discrepancies between drafts.
6. Audit Trails: As with all document activities in TRIRIGA, contract draft management is accompanied by an audit trail that documents who made changes, when, and what exactly was altered. This ensures transparency and accountability throughout the drafting and negotiation process.

PM25.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the ability to manage standard and custom lease templates, including clause libraries and pre-approved exhibit structures, primarily through its Lease Administration module.
1. Lease Templates: TRIRIGA allows you to create and manage lease templates, which serve as pre-defined outlines of lease terms and conditions. These templates can include standard boilerplate language, as well as variable fields that can be populated with specific lease details when a new lease is created.
2. Clause Libraries: TRIRIGA supports the creation of a clause library, a repository of standardized lease clauses that can be pulled into lease documents as needed. This library can be organized by clause type (e.g., rent, maintenance, termination), making it easy to find and insert appropriate clauses into lease drafts.
3. Customization: The lease templates and clause library in TRIRIGA can be customized to align with your organization's lease policies, legal requirements, or industry standards. This includes the ability to add, edit, or remove clauses, as well as to modify the overall structure of lease documents.
4. Pre-approved Exhibits: TRIRIGA enables the creation and management of pre-approved exhibits, such as building standards, service level agreements, or environmental commitments. These exhibits can be associated with specific lease templates or clauses, ensuring consistency and compliance across your lease portfolio.
5. Template-Driven Lease Creation: Using TRIRIGA's lease templates, you can quickly generate lease documents by populating template variables with specific lease details. This saves time and reduces the risk of errors compared to manually drafting each lease from scratch.
6. Version Control and Audit Trail: Like other document management features in TRIRIGA, lease templates and clause libraries benefit from version control and an audit trail. This ensures that changes to standard lease language are tracked, and previous versions are retained for reference or rollback if necessary.

PM26.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the capability to initiate leases from both business development scenarios (ingestion of generic CRM records) and tenant renewal workflows. This functionality is supported through TRIRIGA's integration capabilities and workflow engine.
1. Business Development (CRM Ingestion): TRIRIGA can be integrated with various CRM systems to ingest lease-related data from generic CRM records. This integration allows for the automatic creation of lease records in TRIRIGA when new lease opportunities are identified in the CRM. The integration can be customized to map CRM fields to TRIRIGA lease attributes, ensuring a smooth transfer of information.
2. Lease Initiation from CRM: Once integrated, lease initiation from a CRM involves selecting a relevant opportunity or lead record, specifying the applicable TRIRIGA lease template, and initiating the lease creation process. This can be achieved via a custom button or action within the CRM system, which invokes the corresponding workflow in TRIRIGA.
3. Tenant Renewal Workflows: TRIRIGA's workflow engine can be configured to automate lease renewal processes. When a lease renewal is due, the system can automatically create a new lease record based on the existing lease's terms, update the lease start and end dates, and route the renewal for approval through a pre-defined workflow. This workflow can involve relevant stakeholders such as leasing managers, legal reviewers, and executives.
4. Data Synchronization: TRIRIGA’s integration with CRM systems ensures data consistency by synchronizing updates between the two platforms. Thus, changes made in either system (e.g., updating lease terms in TRIRIGA or notes in the CRM) are reflected in the other system, maintaining an accurate and up-to-date view of lease-related information. requirements.

PM27.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support internal pre-approval workflows for lease proposals, including structured deal analysis, stakeholder routing, and executive sign-off. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine and customizable lease proposal management features, including support for Business Analysis Transaction Sheets (BATS), executive summaries, Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs), and Letters of Intent (LOIs).
1. Workflow Engine: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine allows you to design and implement custom workflows tailored to your organization's lease proposal approval processes. These workflows can include various stages, such as deal analysis, stakeholder review, and executive approval, with conditional branching and escalation procedures.
2. Deal Analysis: Within TRIRIGA, you can perform structured deal analysis by incorporating custom fields, calculations, and data validation rules into lease proposal records. This enables consistent evaluation of lease terms and financial implications across proposals.
3. Stakeholder Routing: TRIRIGA's workflow engine can route lease proposal documents and associated tasks to relevant stakeholders based on predefined rules. This ensures that the appropriate team members, such as leasing managers, legal reviewers, and financial analysts, have the opportunity to review and approve or reject the proposal.
4. Executive Sign-off: For lease proposals requiring executive approval, TRIRIGA can be integrated with electronic signature solutions (e.g., DocuSign, Adobe Sign) to facilitate the execution of BATS, NDAs, and LOIs. These documents can be attached to lease proposal records in TRIRIGA and routed for digital signatures as part of the workflow.
5. Executive Summaries: TRIRIGA's lease proposal management features support the creation of concise executive summaries, providing key details and recommendations to executives for swift decision-making.
6. NDAs and LOIs: TRIRIGA enables the attachment, tracking, and management of NDAs and LOIs within lease proposal records. This ensures that all necessary confidentiality and intent agreements are in place before proceeding with final lease negotiations.
7. Audit Trail and Compliance: TRIRIGA maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all lease proposal activities, including document versions, task assignments, and approval workflows. This ensures transparency, accountability, and compliance with internal governance and external regulations.

PM28.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the ability to initiate contract drafts with minimal required fields and progressively require additional information based on the contract's status. This capability is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine and customizable document management features.
1. Minimal Required Fields: When creating a new contract draft in TRIRIGA, you can specify a set of minimal required fields, such as the contract type, related parties (e.g., lessor and lessee), and a brief description. This initial level of detail is sufficient to kickstart the contract drafting process without overwhelming the user with too many data entry requirements.
2. Progressive Disclosure: TRIRIGA's document management and workflow features support progressive disclosure of information based on the contract's status. As the contract progresses through different stages (e.g., Draft, Legal Review, Execution), the system can dynamically prompt users to provide additional details or documentation relevant to that stage. For example, during the Legal Review phase, the system might request specific clauses or terms to be reviewed or updated.
3. Customizable Workflows: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine allows you to design and implement custom workflows tailored to your organization's contract drafting and approval processes. These workflows can include conditional branching, task assignments, and escalation procedures, ensuring that the appropriate stakeholders are involved and that necessary information is collected at the right time.
4. Document Assembly: TRIRIGA's document assembly capabilities enable the creation of contract templates with placeholders for variable data. As the contract draft progresses through different stages, these placeholders can be automatically populated with relevant information, such as parties' details, financial terms, or specific clauses.
5. Version Control and Audit Trail: TRIRIGA maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all contract-related activities, including document versions, task assignments, and approval workflows. This ensures transparency, accountability, and compliance with internal governance and external regulations.

PM29.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does have the capability to generate structured lease abstracts that summarize key terms, dates, obligations, financial conditions, and location details, including street and building numbers. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module and reporting tools.
1. Lease Abstract Generation: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module includes features for generating lease abstracts or summaries. These abstracts can be customized to include specific lease terms, such as start and end dates, key financial information (e.g., rent amounts, expense escalations), obligations (e.g., maintenance responsibilities, improvement allowances), and location details (e.g., street address, building number).
2. Configurable Content: The content of lease abstracts in TRIRIGA can be tailored to your organization's requirements. This includes the selection of which lease terms and details to include, as well as the format and presentation of the abstract. Abstracts can be generated in various output formats, such as PDF, Word, or web pages.
3. Dynamic Content: TRIRIGA's reporting tools leverage dynamic content that pulls information directly from lease records, ensuring that lease abstracts are always up-to-date with the latest lease terms and details. As lease information is updated in TRIRIGA, the lease abstracts can automatically reflect these changes.
4. Integration with Other Modules: Lease abstracts generated in TRIRIGA can be linked to related records in other modules, such as financials, maintenance, or space management. This integration enables users to access comprehensive, contextual information from a single source, improving decision-making and operational efficiency.
5. Mass Generation: TRIRIGA's reporting features allow for the mass generation of lease abstracts, enabling users to produce summary reports for entire portfolios or subsets of leases. This capability is particularly useful for portfolio-level analysis, compliance reporting, or strategic planning.

PM30.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA can be configured to allow the assignment of Port of Portland signatories and tracking of delegated authorities within its Lease Administration and Workflow Engine modules.
1. Signatory Assignment: TRIRIGA allows for the assignment of specific signatories, such as those from the Port of Portland, to lease documents. This can be accomplished by configuring the appropriate roles or user groups within TRIRIGA and associating them with the relevant lease documents during the workflow process.
2. Delegated Authorities: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine supports the concept of delegated authorities, enabling you to set up workflows where certain tasks or approvals can be delegated to other team members. For example, a lease administrator might be authorized to execute leases up to a certain dollar amount, after which higher-level approval is required.
3. Customizable Workflows: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine allows you to design and implement custom workflows tailored to your organization's requirements. These workflows can include specific steps for assigning signatories and managing delegated authorities, ensuring that lease documents are routed appropriately and that approval thresholds are respected.
4. Audit Trail and Compliance: TRIRIGA maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all lease-related activities, including signatory assignments and delegated authorities. This ensures transparency, accountability, and compliance with internal governance and external regulations, such as those imposed by the Port of Portland.
5. Integration with External Systems: TRIRIGA can be integrated with external systems, such as the Port of Portland's own systems, to exchange lease-related information and facilitate seamless collaboration and compliance.

PM31.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA is designed to define and manage properties, units, and leases to support integrated financial reporting by location and asset. This capability is facilitated by TRIRIGA's comprehensive data model and reporting tools, which can link lease-related information to billing activity, maintenance history, capital planning needs, and the responsible property manager.
1. Property and Unit Management: TRIRIGA enables the definition and management of properties and units, capturing relevant attributes such as location, size, and condition. This information can be used to generate financial reports and analytics at the property or unit level, providing granular insights into lease portfolios.
2. Lease Management: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module supports the management of lease records, including key financial terms, obligations, and dates. By linking lease records to specific properties and units, TRIRIGA ensures that financial reporting can be conducted at the appropriate level of detail.
3. Billing Activity Integration: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module integrates with lease records to track and report on billing activity. By associating lease terms with actual billing data, TRIRIGA enables the generation of financial reports that accurately reflect revenue recognition, expense allocation, and other billing-related metrics.
4. Maintenance History and Capital Planning: TRIRIGA's Maintenance Management module integrates with lease records to track maintenance activities and costs throughout the lease term. By linking maintenance records to specific properties, units, and leases, TRIRIGA supports capital planning efforts, helping organizations anticipate and budget for future maintenance and capital expenditure needs.
5. Property Manager Assignment: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration and Maintenance Management modules allow for the assignment of responsible property managers to specific properties, units, or leases. This ensures that relevant stakeholders are accountable for managing lease-related activities and facilitates efficient communication and collaboration.

PM32.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to track available properties, office suites, and land parcels in a centralized, searchable database with availability timelines. This functionality is primarily facilitated by TRIRIGA's Space Management and Lease Administration modules.
1. Centralized Database: TRIRIGA's Space Management module maintains a centralized database of all leased and vacant spaces within your portfolio, including properties, office suites, and land parcels. This database serves as a single source of truth for space-related information, ensuring consistency and accuracy across your organization.
2. Availability Status: Each space record in TRIRIGA can be marked as available or occupied, and additional details can be provided to describe the availability, such as whether the space is currently being marketed or held for future use.
3. Searchable Database: TRIRIGA's space management features provide robust search and filter capabilities, enabling users to quickly locate available spaces based on various criteria, such as location, size, or property type. This search functionality facilitates efficient space allocation and tenant placement.
4. Availability Timelines: TRIRIGA allows you to define and track availability timelines for spaces. This includes specifying the start and end dates of an availability period, as well as any intermediate availability windows (e.g., for subleases or seasonal occupancy). By associating availability timelines with space records, TRIRIGA supports accurate forecasting and planning of space utilization.
5. Space Allocation and Marketing: TRIRIGA's space management features support the allocation of available spaces to prospective tenants, including the creation of lease proposals, tracking of marketing activities, and management of tenant inquiries and applications.
6. Integration with Lease Administration: TRIRIGA's Space Management module integrates with the Lease Administration module, ensuring that space availability information is reflected in lease records and financial reports. This integration supports accurate revenue recognition, lease administration, and space optimization efforts.

PM33.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to determine and display property availability based on current lease end dates, renewal status, termination status, and planned vacancy periods. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Space Management and Lease Administration modules.
1. Current Lease End Dates: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module maintains up-to-date lease end dates for each lease record. By cross-referencing these dates with space records in the Space Management module, TRIRIGA can determine which spaces will become available upon lease expiration.
2. Renewal Status: TRIRIGA allows you to track lease renewal status, including whether renewal options have been exercised, are under negotiation, or have been declined. This information helps TRIRIGA accurately predict space availability in the future, taking into account potential lease continuations.
3. Termination Status: For spaces subject to early termination or lease buy-out, TRIRIGA enables tracking of termination status and associated timelines. This enables the system to factor in potentially earlier-than-expected vacancies when determining overall property availability.
4. Planned Vacancy Periods: TRIRIGA's Space Management module allows for the definition of planned vacancy periods, such as those resulting from renovations, repositioning, or strategic hold periods. These planned vacancies are factored into TRIRIGA's availability calculations, providing a comprehensive view of future space availability.
5. Space Availability Calculation: TRIRIGA's algorithms consider lease end dates, renewal status, termination status, and planned vacancy periods to calculate and display space availability. This calculation can be performed at various levels, such as individual properties, buildings, or entire portfolios, enabling users to understand availability trends and make informed decisions.
6. User Interface and Reporting: TRIRIGA's user interface and reporting tools provide visual representations of space availability, including charts, graphs, and interactive maps. These interfaces enable users to easily interpret and act upon space availability data, facilitating space allocation, marketing, and strategic planning.

PM34.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does distinguish between various lease statuses, including 'closed,' 'terminated,' 'month-to-month,' 'year-to-year,' 'start and end dates,' and 'pulled over,' particularly when finance activities are still pending. This distinction is managed through TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module, which supports a comprehensive set of lease statuses and provides the ability to track finance-related activities alongside lease status changes.
1. Lease Statuses: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module supports a range of lease statuses, such as:
   - Closed: A lease that has ended, and all financial activities are complete.
   - Terminated: A lease that has been terminated before its natural end, potentially involving early termination fees, buy-outs, or other compensatory arrangements.
   - Month-to-Month: A lease with no fixed end date, renewing on a monthly basis until cancellation by either party.
   - Year-to-Year: Similar to month-to-month but with an annual renewal cycle.
   - Start and End Dates: A lease with specified commencement and conclusion dates, which may or may not have reached its end date.
   - Pulled Over: A lease whose term has been extended beyond the original end date, often due to negotiations or other reasons.
2. Pending Finance Activities: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module allows you to track finance-related activities, such as billing, payments, and accruals, even when a lease is not yet considered "closed." This ensures that all financial aspects of a lease are accounted for, regardless of its status.

PM35.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support the definition and tracking of Common Area Maintenance (CAM) program rules and the generation of tenant cost estimates for reconciliation, in coordination with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) billing processes. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Lease Administration and Financial Management modules.
CAM Rules: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module allows you to define and manage CAM rules. These rules can include allocation methods (e.g., square footage, sales, or equalization), budgetary forecasts, and historical data for comparison. By setting up these rules, TRIRIGA ensures that CAM charges are calculated consistently and accurately across your lease portfolio.

PM36.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA does support forecasting Common Area Maintenance (CAM) fees for prospective or renewing leases based on historical billing, square footage, or other allocation methods. Additionally, TRIRIGA can manage reserve funds for CAM. This functionality is facilitated by TRIRIGA's Lease Administration and Financial Management modules.
1. CAM Fee Forecasting: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module allows you to forecast CAM fees for prospective or renewing leases using historical billing data, square footage, or other allocation methods. By analyzing past CAM charges and applying historical trends, TRIRIGA can generate accurate fee projections for new or renewed leases.
2. Allocation Methods: TRIRIGA supports various allocation methods for CAM fees, such as:
   - Square Footage: Allocating CAM charges proportionally based on the tenant's occupied square footage.
   - Sales: Allocating CAM charges based on the tenant's proportion of gross sales within the property.
   - Equalization: Distributing CAM charges equally among tenants, with adjustments for any significant disparities in actual costs.
3. Integration with Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module integrates with the Lease Administration module to ensure accurate representation of CAM charges in financial processes. This integration supports the generation of invoices, tracking of payments, and management of CAM-related reserve funds.
4. Reserve Fund Management: TRIRIGA can help manage CAM reserve funds by tracking fund balances, allocating contributions, and monitoring usage. This support enables organizations to plan for future maintenance and repair expenses, ensuring that sufficient funds are available when needed.
5. Workflow Automation: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine can automate CAM-related processes, such as the generation of CAM charge invoices, tenant statements, and reserve fund allocation. This automation reduces manual effort and potential errors, while ensuring timely and accurate CAM billing and reserve fund management.

PM37.comments: Yes, IBM TRIRIGA's Real Estate Administration module maintains a record of past CAM cost allocations for each unit or tenant. This historical data is crucial for understanding trends, validating budgets, and supporting lease negotiations.

PM38.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA's Strategic Facilities Planning (SFP) module enhances the organization's ability to perform scenario-based planning and cost-benefit analysis for asset retention or reinvestment decisions. SFP is an optional module that builds upon TRIRIGA's core capabilities in space management, capital project management, and financial management.
1. Scenario Planning: The SFP module provides tools for creating and evaluating different scenarios related to space utilization, capital investments, and operational strategies. This includes modeling various reinvestment options, such as renovations, expansions, or divestitures, and assessing their potential impact on the organization's real estate portfolio.
2. What-If Analysis: SFP's what-if analysis capabilities enable users to model and compare different scenarios, such as varying space allocation, investment levels, or operational strategies. This allows for a data-driven evaluation of alternative courses of action before making final decisions on asset retention or reinvestment.
3. Integrated Data: SFP leverages data from TRIRIGA's core modules, such as Space Management, Capital Project Management, and Financial Management, to provide a holistic view of the organization's real estate portfolio. This integration ensures that cost, revenue, and cash flow estimates associated with different scenarios are accurate and up-to-date.
4. Portfolio Optimization: SFP's portfolio optimization tools support the identification of underperforming assets, redundant spaces, or inefficient utilization patterns. By analyzing these factors, SFP helps organizations prioritize reinvestment opportunities that maximize returns and align with strategic objectives.
5. Cost-Benefit Analysis: SFP facilitates cost-benefit analysis by allowing users to weigh the costs of different scenarios against their expected benefits, such as improved space utilization, increased revenue, or reduced expenses. This analysis can help inform asset retention or reinvestment decisions by providing a data-driven perspective on the potential return on investment (ROI).

PM39.comments:
IBM TRIRIGA tracks and reports capital expenditures (CapEx) and depreciation schedules by property or asset location primarily through its Capital Project Management and Financial Management modules. Here's an overview of how these features contribute to tracking and reporting CapEx and depreciation:
1. Capital Project Management: TRIRIGA's Capital Project Management module is used to plan, track, and manage capital projects, including the associated expenses. Key features include:
   - Project Budgeting: Users can create and manage project budgets, including initial estimates, approved changes, and actual expenditures.
   - Expense Tracking: The module allows for the recording of expenses incurred during capital project execution, categorized by type (e.g., materials, labor, equipment) and allocated to specific projects.
   - Forecasting: TRIRIGA enables users to forecast future expenses based on historical data, project plans, and other relevant factors.
2. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module integrates with the Capital Project Management module to provide a comprehensive view of CapEx and depreciation. Key features include:
   - Chart of Accounts: TRIRIGA's chart of accounts can be configured to include specific accounts for capital expenditures and depreciation, ensuring accurate financial reporting.
   - Journal Entries: The module supports the creation and posting of journal entries to record CapEx and depreciation expenses, maintaining an audit trail of financial transactions.
   - Depreciation Scheduling: TRIRIGA allows users to define depreciation schedules based on the asset's useful life, recovery period, and depreciation method (e.g., straight-line, declining balance).

PM40.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to recognize revenue by accounting period based on lease terms and amortization rules, primarily through its Lease Administration and Financial Management modules.
1. Lease Administration: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module maintains detailed lease records, including start and end dates, rent amounts, and escalation schedules. This information is crucial for accurately recognizing revenue on an ongoing basis.
2. Revenue Recognition Rules: TRIRIGA allows users to define revenue recognition rules based on accounting standards (e.g., ASC 842, IFRS 16) and organizational policies. These rules dictate when and how rental income should be recognized, considering factors such as lease term length, payment schedules, and any non-standard lease provisions.
3. Amortization Rules: TRIRIGA supports the application of amortization rules to lease-related expenses, such as initial direct costs (IDC) and above/below-market lease adjustments. These rules ensure that expenses are recognized over the appropriate periods, aligning with accounting principles and internal policies.
4. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module integrates with the Lease Administration module to support the application of revenue recognition and amortization rules. Key features include:
   - Journal Entries: TRIRIGA enables users to create and post journal entries to record rental income and related expenses, applying the defined revenue recognition and amortization rules.
   - Period-End Processing: The module supports period-end processing, automatically calculating and posting revenue and expense recognition based on the established rules and lease terms.

PM41.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA provides data analytics capabilities to support capital forecasting and planning, financial analysis, and pro forma analysis. These capabilities are primarily facilitated by TRIRIGA's Financial Management, Capital Project Management, and Lease Administration modules, along with its reporting and analytics tools.
1. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module includes robust reporting and analytics tools that support financial analysis. Key features include:
   - Financial Statements: TRIRIGA can generate standard financial statements, such as balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements, providing insights into the organization's financial health and performance.
   - Trend Analysis: The module supports the analysis of historical financial data, enabling users to identify trends, make comparisons, and benchmark performance against industry standards or internal targets.
2. Capital Project Management: TRIRIGA's Capital Project Management module offers tools for capital forecasting and planning:
   - Capital Budgeting: Users can create and manage capital budgets, including initial estimates, approved changes, and actual expenditures, supporting forecasting and resource allocation.
   - Cash Flow Projections: The module enables users to project future cash flows for capital projects, considering factors such as timing, funding sources, and potential risks.
3. Lease Administration: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module supports pro forma analysis by providing detailed lease data, which can be used to model different scenarios and assess their financial impact:
   - Lease Data Export: TRIRIGA allows users to export lease data to external tools (e.g., Excel) for pro forma analysis, enabling the creation of "what-if" scenarios and assessing their potential impact on financial metrics.
   - Pro Forma Reporting: The module includes reporting capabilities to display pro forma financial statements, considering hypothetical lease modifications, such as rent adjustments, term extensions, or new lease agreements.

PM42.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA automates rent escalation schedules, including fixed increases, percentage-based increases, and Consumer Price Index (CPI) or flat-rate adjustments, with effective dates and audit history. This functionality is primarily supported by TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module.
1. Rent Escalation Rules: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module allows users to define rent escalation rules for each lease, including:
   - Fixed Increases: Users can specify a fixed amount by which the rent will increase at predetermined intervals (e.g., annually).
   - Percentage-Based Increases: TRIRIGA supports the definition of percentage-based rent escalations, with options to apply the increase to base rent, additional rent (e.g., common area maintenance), or both.
   - CPI or Flat-Rate Adjustments: The module enables the configuration of rent escalations tied to the CPI or a predefined flat-rate adjustment, ensuring that rent increases are tied to relevant economic indicators or organizational policies.
2. Effective Dates: TRIRIGA's rent escalation rules can be associated with specific effective dates, ensuring that rent adjustments occur precisely when scheduled. This feature supports both one-time and recurring escalations, accommodating various lease term structures.
3. Audit History: TRIRIGA maintains an audit trail of all changes made to rent escalation rules, including the user who made the change, the date and time of the modification, and the previous and current values. This audit history supports transparency, accountability, and compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
4. Integration with Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module integrates with the Financial Management module, ensuring that rent escalations are accurately reflected in financial records and reports. This integration supports the automation of journal entries, period-end processing, and financial statement generation.

PM43.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to allocate indirect or shared costs, such as utilities, staff time, insurance, and taxes, across properties based on configurable drivers like square footage, lease terms, and usage. This functionality is primarily facilitated by TRIRIGA's Space Management and Financial Management modules.
1. Space Management: TRIRIGA's Space Management module maintains detailed records of individual properties, including space attributes, lease terms, and occupancy information. This data serves as the foundation for allocating indirect or shared costs.
2. Cost Allocation Drivers: TRIRIGA allows users to define cost allocation drivers, such as:
   - Square Footage: Allocate costs based on the amount of space occupied by each tenant or property.
   - Lease Terms: Distribute costs according to the terms of each lease, considering factors like lease duration, renewal options, or early termination provisions.
   - Usage: Allocate costs based on actual usage, such as energy consumption or service requests, when available.
3. Cost Distribution Rules: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module enables users to create cost distribution rules, specifying how indirect or shared costs should be allocated across properties and tenants. These rules can be based on the aforementioned drivers or other relevant factors.
4. Integration with Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Space Management and Financial Management modules integrate to ensure that cost allocation rules are applied accurately in financial records and reports. This integration supports the generation of journal entries, period-end processing, and financial statement generation.

PM44.comments:
Yes, the IBM TRIRIGA modules and features that have the ability to allocate shared utility and maintenance costs across multiple tenants or units using configurable cost allocation methods such as square footage, percentage share, fixed fee, or usage-based inputs are primarily the following:
1. Real Estate Management: TRIRIGA's Real Estate Management module provides the foundation for managing lease and property data, including tenant information, lease terms, and space attributes. This module is crucial for understanding the context in which cost allocation takes place.
2. Space Management: TRIRIGA's Space Management module maintains detailed records of individual properties, including space attributes, lease terms, and occupancy information. This module is specifically designed for managing space-related data and is integral to cost allocation processes, as it allows for the precise identification of spaces subject to cost allocation.
3. TREES (Total Energy and Real Estate Environmental System): Although TREES is primarily focused on energy and environmental management, it also plays a role in cost allocation. TREES can track energy and utility consumption at the space level, providing data that can be used as an input for usage-based cost allocation methods.
4. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module is responsible for handling the financial aspects of cost allocation, including the creation and management of cost distribution rules, the generation of journal entries, and period-end processing. This module integrates with the Space Management module to ensure that cost allocation rules are applied accurately in financial records and reports.

PM45.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to route calculated tenant allocations for internal review and approval prior to invoicing, including visibility into allocation methods and editable overrides where permitted. This functionality is primarily facilitated by TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine and Financial Management module.
1. Workflow Engine: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine enables the creation of custom workflows that dictate how tenant allocations are routed for review and approval. These workflows can be configured to:
   - Route allocations for review by specific individuals or teams based on their roles or responsibilities.
   - Provide visibility into the allocation methods and underlying data used to calculate tenant shares.
   - Allow for editable overrides where permitted, giving reviewers the ability to adjust allocations as needed before final approval.
2. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module integrates with the Workflow Engine to ensure that tenant allocations are managed within the appropriate financial context. This integration supports the generation of journal entries, period-end processing, and financial statement generation.
3. Approval Framework: TRIRIGA's Approval Framework complements the Workflow Engine by providing a structured process for routing and tracking approvals. This framework ensures that tenant allocations receive proper oversight and adhere to organizational policies and procedures.
4. Audit Trail and Compliance: TRIRIGA maintains a comprehensive audit trail for all tenant allocation-related activities, including review and approval processes. This audit trail supports transparency, accountability, and compliance with internal governance and external regulations.

PM46.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to initiate invoice requests or generate tenant billing entries based on approved allocation results, and to link those entries to the originating shared expense. This functionality is primarily facilitated by TRIRIGA's Financial Management module and Workflow Engine.
1. Financial Management: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module manages the creation and processing of financial transactions, including invoice requests and tenant billing entries. After tenant allocations have been reviewed and approved, the module can:
   - Generate billing entries based on the approved allocation results.
   - Link these entries to the originating shared expense, ensuring traceability and accurate financial reporting.
2. Workflow Engine: TRIRIGA's Workflow Engine can be configured to automate the initiation of invoice requests or billing entries based on approved allocations. This automation streamlines the billing process and reduces manual effort.
3. Integration with External Systems: TRIRIGA can be integrated with external invoicing and accounting systems, allowing for seamless transfer of billing data and supporting the generation of invoices in those systems.

PM47.comments:
Yes, IBM TRIRIGA has the ability to store contract drafts and executed agreements in a centralized, searchable repository through its Document Management module. This capability enables efficient organization, retrieval, and management of contract-related documents.
1. Document Management: TRIRIGA's Document Management module serves as a centralized repository for storing and managing various types of documents, including contract drafts and executed agreements. This module provides tools for organizing, categorizing, and searching documents based on metadata, such as document type, lease, or property.
2. Lease and Contract Links: TRIRIGA's document management capabilities integrate with its Lease Administration module, allowing users to attach contracts and related documents directly to lease records. PM51.comments:
While IBM TRIRIGA offers a wide range of out-of-the-box (OOTB) reports, there isn't a specific set of OOTB reports dedicated solely to reporting lease information, expirations, and terminations to county assessors for property tax processing, including tenant responsibilities and drainage fees. 
Here are some examples of OOTB reports that can be utilized or modified to support property tax reporting:
1. Lease Summary Report: This report provides an overview of lease-related data, including lease start and end dates, tenants, and lease-specific terms. While it doesn't directly address tenant responsibilities for property taxes or drainage fees, it can be customized to include these details.
2. Space Utilization Report: This report offers insights into space occupancy, availability, and tenancy. It can be modified to include lease expiration and termination dates, supporting the identification of leases nearing their conclusion.
3. Financial Overview Report: TRIRIGA's Financial Management module includes various financial reports that track expenses and allocations. These reports can be customized to display tenant responsibilities for property taxes and drainage fees.
4. Tenant Information Report: This report provides details about tenants, including contact information and lease-related data. It can be modified to include specific lease terms relevant to property tax processing, such as tenant-paid taxes or drainage fees.
5. Property Information Report: TRIRIGA's Space Management module includes reports that detail property attributes and occupancy. These reports can be customized to include lease-specific data, such as lease expiration dates and tenant responsibilities for property taxes.
Please remember that while I strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, it's essential to confirm these features and configurations with your TRIRIGA administrator or IBM representative, as specific implementations may differ based on your organization's unique setup.

Also, it's worth noting that while TRIRIGA's built-in analytics capabilities are robust, some advanced analytics, such as machine learning or predictive modeling, may require additional tools or custom development, depending on your specific needs and requirements.

Lastly, the extent of customization and report creation may be limited by your organization's internal policies and TRIRIGA's configuration. It's important to consult with your TRIRIGA administrator or IBM representative to understand the specific capabilities and limitations in your environment.

TRIRIGA's Lease Administration module enables the assignment of terminal use agreements to specific leases or spaces, regardless of the underlying property. This ensures that terminal use agreements are properly linked to the relevant lease records and tracked throughout the lease lifecycle.

AP01.comments: Workday's single data model unifies AP, GL, and procurement, removing the need for manual interfacing. Transactions automatically update the General Ledger in real time upon approval, facilitating coordination between purchasing, accounts payable and accounting to ensure proper unity. All Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level.

AP04.comments: Workday streamlines invoice review and editing with field annotations and interactive highlights in scanned PDFs. Users can easily compare the scanned file and the Workday invoice using the pop-out attachment or split-screen view. Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level. Unlike other cloud-based financial software packages, this action does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

AP06.comments: Workday supports 3-way invoice matching to purchase orders and receipts. Matching rules and conditions can be defined to govern when 3-way match is required. Workday can perform a three-way match between the Purchase Order, Receipt, and Supplier Invoice. Matching enables you to identify exceptions on supplier invoices, ensuring compliance across your procurement activities.

AP07.comments: Supported. Workday helps you identify and manage disputed invoices, invoices in collection, and invoices on hold. Invoices can be marked as on hold, in dispute, in collection, or as any combination. When you view a customer invoice, you have visibility into all hold, collection, and dispute information.

AP09.comments: Supplier invoices can be entered manually, copied from an existing invoice, or created from a purchase order, contract, or receipt. Invoices can also be submitted by a supplier via self-service or created from a Workday customer invoice. Additionally, invoices can be imported from other systems or via EDI or OCR.

AP10.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework provides auditable tracking and, with audit always on capabilities, date and time stamps are always recorded. Workday's Expense Protect functionality, an intelligent auditing tool leveraging machine learning, reviews 100% of the expenses in real-time and provides insights on those expense lines or reports with anomalies requiring special attention. Validation rules create warnings/errors for discrepancies and can be configured with conditional logic.

AP11.comments: Workday fully supports the creation of recurring supplier invoices, either fixed or variable amounts, with all the relevant supplier and invoice information including frequency type and first/last invoice date or total amount. You can also define supplier invoice contracts which will generate invoices according to a user-defined schedule. With Workday Contracts you can automate billing using templates for various types of products or services, including fixed amount, prepaid amount including visibility to utilization, recurring billing, fixed term, subscription, usage based billing, variable amount where the contract amounts represent estimates, project fixed fee, project Time & Expense, value based project, alternate contracts, set worktag dimension as a billing rate rule and create a billing rate rule using contract rate sheets. You also define the billing schedule as single, recurring with equal or custom amounts or milestone-driven. You can create a consolidated billing schedules across all customer contract lines

AP12.comments: Workday provides the ability to allocate the cost of each line item on a business document to multiple accounting distribution lines through our line split details. Splits can be allocated by quantity or spend amount/percentage.

AP13.comments: Workday enables you to hold supplier invoices from payment. You can specify reasons to hold supplier invoices and supplier invoice adjustments from payment.

AP14.comments: You can define, at the supplier contract level, a discount percentage applicable on the supplier catalog. When creating a purchase order, the price will be derived from the contract, including the discount. For prompt payment, discounts may be calculated using Payment Terms. Discounts may also be entered as a negative value (-) for specific lines within the purchase order.

AP15.comments: Supported. When you Void/Cancel a payment in Workday the related Supplier Invoices are set back to Unpaid/Approved status so a new payment can be issued.

AP17.comments: Supported. You can create an invoice adjustment referenced to the original invoice to change the amount due from the customer and create the required accounting. You can decrease the amount due as a credit memo (e.g., for returns, damaged merchandise, price adjustments, and so forth). You can also increase the amount due as a debit memo.

AP22.comments: Supported. Workday includes allocated freight, tax, and other charges for the on-hand value of purchased items. AP25.comments: The system provides the ability to print a sequence of checks for payments and allows you to change the check number if the Workday check number and the number on your preprinted check stock get out of sync. You can also create manual checks.

AP27.comments: Supplier Invoices can be generated from and matched against purchase orders, supplier contracts, receipts, timesheets, and task logs. AP28.comments: Workday provides a unified settlement engine to manage settlement for all transaction types, which enables you to gain visibility to all approved and unpaid items. AP29.comments: Supported. If there was an error made on entry, an invoice can be changed with a related action any time before it is paid and will re-enter the business process upon submission. AP33.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system including: Transaction entry and approval such as journal entries and supplier invoices; Reference data such as customers, cost centers, and expense types; Hierarchy/organization structures; Business process/workflow definitions; Edit and validation rules and Security definition and role assignment. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility over things such as the Vendor or Item Primary records, companies, organizational structures, account roll-ups, etc. Auditing is always on.

AP42.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework supports configurable approval workflows, including those for non-PO items. Approval steps can be based on amount, spend categories, project, cost center, or any number of other related business attributes.

AP43.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework is highly configurable and helps prevent business processes from being delayed if an Approver isn't available or doesn't respond within a time range. Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments managing the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. The business process framework within Workday is highly configurable and can use any values on the invoice or invoice line to drive required approvals and documentation before an invoice can be released for payment.

AP46.comments: Workday delivers an automated business process for settlements/payments. This process manages the required review and approval steps for individual invoice payments or payment batches. AP49.comments: Workday enables you to track and view 1099 data for suppliers. You can set up 1099 categories and then designate suppliers as 1099 suppliers. Workday provides the ability to create tax authority and tax categories and specify as withholding.

AP50.comments: Supported. On each supplier you have the possibility to set Supplier status, segmentation or supplier risk. AP51.comments: Workday delivers aging reports that can be run as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. You can also specify a specific translation type for multi-currency agings. These delivered reports can display payables aging at any summarization i.e., by supplier or by transaction, and provide the ability to drill into the details to see the underlying invoices and take action. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. Users can run a receivables aging by either operational aging (ages by transaction date) or financial aging (ages by accounting date). Additionally, users can have the aging basis be by either due date or invoice date. Workday provides users to be able to create custom aging reports as well using delivered report-writing capabilities.

AP52.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to track and view 1099 data for suppliers. We deliver print layouts that support printing 1099 MISC, 1099 NEC, and 1096 forms, with the flexibility to print on blank paper or on pre-printed IRS forms.
