You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. Workday also provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created.

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Debt Management - Debt service payments can be allocated to different porjects or funds

CD15.comments:
Workday:

Supported

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Investment System - Investments can be segegrated by bond issue

CD20.comments:
Workday:

Workday supports manual and automated journal entries, including validation, reconciliation, and audit trails of imported data using web services, delivered integration tools, Workday’s Accounting Journal Connector, or Consolidation Data Capture.

CD21.comments: Supported. Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements. You can reclassify procurement commitments and obligations to different funding sources, enabling you to automatically apply project spending to the correct funds. Asset transactions automatically update the General Ledger in real time at the point of transaction approval. This facilitates coordination between purchasing and accounting to help ensure that items that should be capitalized are created as assets. Workday enables you to share asset costs across multiple worktags, providing you with a way to register assets from invoices or receipts to share costs, depreciation, and lifecycle events.

CD22.comments:
Workday:

Supported. Workday delivers unique capabilities that allow customers the ability to create and define their own APIs. If only a subset of fields within Workday services are needed for a specific integration, then a custom API that contains only those needed fields can be built. This API can then be consumed by Workday or any third-party integration tool.

CD23.comments:
Workday:

Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Consolidated cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed. With Workday’s composite reports you can build a single, matrix-style report from multiple data sources, take advantage of spreadsheet-like capabilities to create reports that perform column, row, and cell calculations and control report formatting style.

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Cash Management

CD24.comments:
Workday:

Supported. Workday enables you to assign cash flow categories on bank statement lines and external cash flows to use when generating cash positioning reports. This assists in the normalization of data across bank statement formats to support simplified reporting. These can also be used to identify the reason for cash movements both for automated and user entered journals to assist in the creation of direct and indirect cash flow reporting.

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Cash Management

CD25.comments:
Workday:

Summarized accounting journals can be integrated directly into Workday Core Financials. For transaction level detail, Workday's Accounting Center allows transactional data from third-party systems including treasury systems to be ingested, enriched, and transformed to create automated accounting journals that are posted to the GL. Customers can manage and track transactions from source systems in batches from ingestion to summarized accounting.

CD26.comments:
Workday:

Workday provides electronic bank reconciliation to match bank statement lines to operational transactions. You can create matching rules and condition rules to associate with a bank account, and the system can automatically match, suggest a match, or create an ad hoc bank transaction. Workday also offers exception handling and audit trail reporting.

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Cash Management

CD27.comments:
Workday:

All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system. In addition to audit of all transactions and processes, Workday provides an audit of all meta-data attributes used in the financial and HR application to ensure appropriate control and visibility.

Sympro:

Supported. Audit logs by user are standard in SymPro

CD28.comments:
Workday:

Workday provides configurable validation rules that create either a warning or an error message. An error must be corrected before the expense report can be submitted. A warning can be processed and the warning becomes part of the transaction. Warnings are viewable during the approval process, can be used to route expense reports, and are viewable in the reporting.

Each budget can be configured to control or not control spending activity. For example, further business process configuration options allow a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist. 

Sympro:

Supported via SymPro Cash Management

CP01.comments: Contracts in Workday can have start and end dates, whatever the duration of the contract. They can be amended at any point to extend the life of the contract. They can also have multiple lines for different goods or services. Receipts can be processed efficiently for goods, services, project-based services and contingent worker time sheets on purchase order and supplier contract lines. Workday supports both a decentralized and centralized receiving process on both the desktop and mobile application. Workday Strategic Sourcing workflow management capabilities helps organizations seamlessly move through the various stages of the Source to Contract workflow.

CP03.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework is flexible and allows for configuration based on various conditions. This enables you to configure internal policies and procedures for approvals, including those related to federal funding.

CP05.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework supports any number of approval levels for a given transaction or activity. Approval and review steps can be configured based upon your organization's business needs and roles such as Cost Center Manager, Grant Manager, Manager, Management Chain as well as numerous other roles. Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required.

CP06.comments: Workday Strategic Sourcing workflow management capabilities helps organizations seamlessly move through the various stages of the Source to Contract workflow. Also, with Workday, you can create custom fields for suppliers, purchase orders, and supplier contracts to meet your organization's specific needs. By leveraging our tools feature for creating custom objects, you can define custom fields and custom prompt lists.

CP07.comments: Workday supports robust detailed item level descriptions. Workday provides the ability to allocate the cost of each line item on a business document to multiple accounting distribution lines through our line split details. Splits from requisitions carry over to the purchase order line-level, and during purchase order creation, Workday provides the ability to add, remove, or modify line-level splits.

CP100.comments: Workday enables users to search for purchase information using PO number, requisition number, department name, and other user-defined fields (Worktags). Worktags are custom dimensions that can be defined to meet specific reporting and tracking needs, offering flexible criteria for information retrieval.

CP11.comments: Workday Strategic Sourcing provides a number of mechanisms designed to facilitate internal and external collaboration around sourcing events and other procurement activities, including a Message Center, Team Chat, Supplier Chat and Milestones.

CP119.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

Administrators can set granular permissions to control who can view, edit, or collaborate on specific documents, ensuring only authorized users access sensitive information. OnBase integrates with authentication systems like Active Directory, LDAP, and SAML2, and applies data encryption at rest and in transit. Detailed audit trails, versioning, and automated records management provide transparency and compliance, while network access controls and the principle of least privilege further reduce risk. OnBase is certified for major compliance standards (SOC2, ISO 27001), supporting confidentiality and privacy requirements in both cloud and on-premises deployments.

Workday:

Supported

CP120.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

OnBase foldering allows for a combination of automatic and manual folder creation. This includes parent folders, and an unlimited nesting of child folders. Each new contract can have a template of folders created automatically but then allow for manual creation of additional folders as necessary. Users can add, edit, and manage documents within these folders, ensuring all related materials are centrally stored and easily accessible. Version control and audit trails track changes and maintain document integrity, while permissions ensure only authorized users can modify content.

Workday:

Supported

CP121.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

Every edit, comment, and approval is automatically logged with user, timestamp, and detailed notes, ensuring a transparent record of negotiations and decision-making. Users can attach rationale or supporting documentation directly to contract records, and workflow tools prompt for required explanations or approvals at key stages. All negotiation communications, change justifications, and approval notes are securely stored alongside the contract file, making them easily retrievable for future audits or contract renewals.

Workday:

Supported

CP122.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

Each time a document is edited or updated, OnBase automatically creates a new version while preserving previous versions for reference and audit purposes. Users can view, compare, or restore earlier versions as needed. Version history includes details such as who made changes and when, supporting compliance and effective collaboration both internally and with external parties.

Workday:

Supported

CP123.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

As documents are created or received in OnBase, they are indexed with metadata called keywords. One of the primary purposes of keywords is to create associations between documents and between data in different enterprise systems. This ensures all related documentation is centrally organized and easily accessible throughout the procurement process.

Workday:

Supported

CP124.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

Administrators can manage standardized clauses or provisions centrally, so when a change is made, it automatically propagates across all relevant templates and documents. This ensures consistency, reduces manual effort, and minimizes the risk of outdated or conflicting language in contract documents, supporting compliance and efficient contract management.

Workday:

Supported

CP125.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

The Hyland for Workday integration enables any type of document, including word processing files, spreadsheets, and scanned images, to be linked to any transaction in Workday Financials and Workday HCM. This is performed by mapping OnBase keywrd data to relevant Workday field dat, enabling consistent, reusable, and fully supported integrations

Workday:

Attachments may be added to requisitions. The maximum attachment size is 30720 KB (30 MB), and the maximum image size is 10240 KB (10 MB). The organization's Workday tenant can be configured to either allow all file attachment types or allow ONLY specific file attachment types (e.g., allow only .PDF, JPG, etc.).

CP126.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

OnBase is not relianct on file path locations. All content is accessible using the unique document ID or using queries for different keywords assigned to each document. This approach ensures that all documents remain securely accessible within the platform, reducing the risk of broken links or inaccessible files.

Workday:

Supported

CP13.comments: When publishing your RFP you will have the option to build out your evaluation, you can add sections, scoring questions, long answer questions, short answer questions, multiple choice questions. Once the RFP has been returned your sourcing team can score while looking at supplier responses, ie, looking at pricing and financials while scoring on the same page through a side bar. Once scoring has been completed the system will calculate a percentage across all suppliers aggregated the opinion of all your stakeholders to show you the results from your scoring side by side to ultimately make your decision.

CP138.comments: Yes, however Workday does offer own sourcing module called Strategic Sourcing.

CP142.comments: Yes, we offer a public API for resources within Workday Strategic Sourcing that would allow for integration with 3rd party systems. The API supports both import and export. Please see https://apidocs.workdayspend.com/index.html for further information about our API.

CP18.comments:
Workday enables you to create commodity codes that group similar types of goods and services, and associate spend categories as well as purchase and catalog items to commodity codes. This helps to ensure data integrity and improve search for items based on standard commodity code structures.

Workday Strategic Sourcing enables you to create request for quote (RFx) business documents so that suppliers can bid on procurement goods and services. An email is sent to suppliers inviting them to participate in your RFx event. We also have public bidding options which allow for a link to be discovered by any prospective supplier and proactively sign up for Workday Strategic Sourcing.

CP19.comments: You will have access to see when the supplier accessed the bid, submitted the bid and/or revised the bid.

CP20.comments: The Workday Supplier Portal allows suppliers to review and maintain contact details and supplier classifications. You can create commodity codes that group similar types of goods and services. You can also define your own structure or use an industry-based standard taxonomy, such as the National Institute of Governmental Purchasing Commodity Services Code (NIGP).

CP24.comments: RFx can be edited, canceled, and closed with some restrictions at each stage.

CP26.comments: The Workday Strategic Sourcing application allows Customers to grant users access to their projects and contracts in pre-defined security policies by assigning them to various security groups. Security Groups can grant either full or read-only access to bids and  contracts.

CP29.comments: While Workday does not have embedded pre-conference functionality, you can track this data as part of the project (internal tracking) that is associated with the bid.

CP30.comments: Workday supports electronic receipt of supplier bid responses on the supplier portal, eliminating manual tasks and increasing accuracy. Electronic delivery of RFQ documents and notifications via email is supported, and users can compare bid responses side by side and more easily award suppliers with a winning bid.

CP33.comments: Workday Strategic Sourcing provides a Message Centre facility to allow Sourcing Managers to send messages to suppliers, suppliers to submit questions to the sourcing team, and sourcing managers to respond to supplier questions. Team Chat is provided to allow internal stakeholders and the procurement team to communicate and collaborate, with all messages retained against the project for audit purposes. Supplier Chat a live chat facility is provided to allow sourcing managers to communicate quickly with suppliers. All supplier chat messages are retained against the project for audit purposes.

CP34.comments: You can create numerous contract agreement types that support your business needs. Examples include but are limited to leases, maintenance agreements, purchasing contracts, blanket purchase orders, rights, prepaids, and subscriptions.

CP36.comments: Workday has the ability to associate funding sources with contracts and purchase orders; however, the system does not yet dynamically adjust required clauses or forms based on agency. This is schedule to be part of future release in Workday's Contract Lifecycle Management module.

CP45.comments: You may how to edit (manage) format for Purchase Order numbering sequence. Workday can also deliver controls on Purchase Orders by using details about the person entering the purchase order (such as company , job title, cost center that a worker belongs to) as well as control based on what is being purchased (type of item, spend category, dollar value) can drive control over if a employee can attempt to enter a purchase order for a item and then routing for review and approval.

CP47.comments: Workday supports single-supplier or multi-supplier contracts with specified items, payment terms, and other details. Requisitions can be automatically sourced against contracts to ensure optimal pricing. Workday also offers punchout catalog capabilities with support for multi-supplier punchouts. Users can check out items from multiple suppliers in the same shopping cart.

CP52.comments: Alerts/notifications are sent to team members as they are assigned, approach, and miss milestones. Workday's Business Process Framework is highly configurable and helps prevent business processes from being delayed. Workday enables you to automatically renew supplier contracts as they expire, making supplier management easier and more efficient. You can also notify your supplier contract specialists about those expiring contracts and renewal processing results. Workday also delivers a Find Expiring Supplier Contracts report that returns all expiring supplier contracts.

CP58.comments:
Change orders can be processed against a purchase order at any point during the purchase order life cycle, up until the purchase has been marked as Closed. From a purchase order, the user can select the related action of Create Change Order to enter the desired changes and submit the change order for processing. You can create sourcing rules to perform budget checks on change orders.

Change orders can also be created automatically with approved changes recorded via PO Acknowledgements.

In addition, you can submit and/or get change orders through Web Service operations. The service will make modifications on the header, line or split levels of issued purchase orders, with some exceptions.

CP73.comments: The Workday Supplier Portal allows suppliers to provide onboarding documents. All supplier-submitted documents and changes to supplier data can be routed for approval or review to the appropriate individuals within your organization before being accepted.

CP74.comments: Workday allows you to set Supplier status, segmentation, or supplier risk on each supplier. There's also the option to set tags, such as 'preferred'. Supplier custom fields can also be created and stored within the supplier profile to further assist with supplier categorization.

CP75.comments: This occurs within your approved vendor base of on-contract vendors or self-registered vendors.

CP78.comments: This can be supplemented using our partner Relish Data Assure which will support additional validations.

CP79.comments: Workday Supplier Administrators can give suppliers access to their Workday Supplier Portal, an online collaboration space that encourages supplier self-service. When provided access, suppliers can manage their business data - add or modify their own supplier data including alternate business names, business addresses, contact information, supplier classification, and settlement banking information. Once submitted, change requests are routed to an internal user for review and approval before being implemented. Additionally, Customers have access to an audit log in the form of changes for projects and contracts. There also is a Supplier Profile history log.

CP86.comments: Yes Workday can allow for definition of  a format.  Workday can also deliver controls on Purchase Orders by using details about the person entering the purchase order (such as company , job title, cost center that a worker belongs to) as well as control based on what is being purchased (type of item, spend category, dollar value) can drive control over if a employee can attempt to enter a purchase order for a item and then routing for review and approval.

CP89.comments: Because Workday's quoting tool is embedded within a single solution, data can be used to automatically create a requisition and does not require OCR.

CP90.comments: You can configure purchase order types to categorize purchase orders according to your procurement and sourcing needs. You can also use purchase order types to source purchase orders from matching requisition types, report on purchase orders, create custom validations and display, on the printed (.PDF) Purchase Order, supplier-facing language and instructions including Shipping Terms, Shipping Instructions, Comments, and Messages.

CP91.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework supports approval levels for transactions. Approval steps can be configured based on your organization's business needs and roles. Each step in a business process can also be configured with Step Conditions to control when that approval or review step is required, such as exceeding a threshold.

CP99.comments: Workday gives you control to specify which goods/services are presented and thus available to users during requisitioning. You can grant access to goods and services based on criteria related to the worker as well to the worker's location to ensure that requesters are presented with options that are relevant to their job and their location when creating requisitions.

CA02.comments: Workday captures and manages a broader set of data than traditional accounting systems to support an expanded view of business. Workday enables customers to combine various data sources such as actuals, budgets, external data from third-party systems, statistics, and headcount, into live multi-dimensional reports that users can format, drill down into, and act on all within Workday. Organizations can significantly streamline their chart of accounts for a faster, more accurate financial close. Workday can model multiple operating entities, organizations, or business units to easily complete intercompany transactions, eliminations, allocations, adjustments, journal line matching, and consolidated reporting.

CA04.comments: Workday allows you to define your exact fiscal schedule down to the day. You can roll up your days into weeks, months, quarters, and years. These weeks can be tailored to meet specific organization and industry needs, e.g., 5/5/4. The configuration would enable you to run financial statements that cross fiscal years (e.g., rolling 12 months), year over year, quarter over quarter, midmonth, etc. Workday additionally supports the use of both standard and alternate fiscal schedules as appropriate allowing for the consolidation and associated reporting of a parent/subsidiary combination with different subsidiaries.

CA05.comments: Workday's Business Process Framework manages all journal entry and edits, providing control over who can approve journals and manage posting. Workday's audit capabilities track who, when, and what was entered or changed on journals automatically.

CA08.comments: Supported. Workday allows organizations to define their organizational structure, including departments, divisions, business units, and other groupings of employees. This structure can be easily modified to reflect changes in the organization, such as mergers, acquisitions, or reorganizations.

CA09.comments: Workday uses worktags as business dimensions. Inspired by the Web 2.0 concept of tagging, a worktag is a label assigned (i.e., tagged) to a business event, thus describing the event and enabling multi-dimensional classification, search, aggregation, reporting, and analysis of business information. You can configure your own custom worktags such as campaign or channel. In addition, any custom organizations you configure, such as line of business, product line, or brand, are also available as worktags.

CA10.comments: Workday provides the ability to post in detail and in real time directly from operational areas like customer invoice entry, supplier invoice entry, expenses, etc. to the General Ledger. In addition, all Workday reports have navigation inherently built in allowing for drill through to the original source and drill down to the most granular level. Unlike other cloud based financial software packages, this action does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

CA11.comments:
Visual Lease:

Visual Lease will continue to fully support the GASB accounting for leases along with any changes or announcements.

Workday:

Workday captures transactions as business events and uses an accounting engine to create accounting entries based upon user-defined rules. Users can define multiple sets of Accounting Rules for business events such as Business Assets, Revenue Recognition, and Lease Accounting to generate Accounting entries according to different accounting standards such as US GAAP, IFRS, Regulatory, Local GAAP, and Cash. All these accounting entries would be available for reporting.

CA12.comments:
Visual Lease:

Visual Lease fully supports GASB 87 and 96.

CA15.comments: Workday provides drill-down capabilities from financial statements to underlying transaction details such as journal entries, invoices, and receipts. Unlike other cloud-based financial software, Workday does not require navigating through multiple tables with predefined rigid drill paths. This is performed in Workday with a single click.

CA16.comments: Workday supports multi-book reporting. Within Workday, you have the option to display financial statement data by book. This allows users to view their books side by side (e.g., GAAP vs. Tax vs. IFRS vs. Local GAAP). Transactions are entered against book codes which roll up into books to facilitate financial reporting.

CA17.comments: All Workday business processes are fully auditable and track the who, what, when, and why of every change made to the system including transaction entry and approval such as journal entries. In addition, Workday supports the upload of backup documentation that you can attach directly to the entry or the ability to include a link to an external document repository where that information can be found.

CA18.comments: Workday supports project budgets to forecast costs and revenue by project. Each budget can be configured to control or not control spending activity, which can be a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist.

CA19.comments: Workday supports capital projects. You can manage project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets.

CA20.comments: Workday Projects enable you to define and plan your project. Establish a project work plan with phases, tasks, and milestones to track progress over time. Automatically create or update a downstream project plan for quote phases and tasks after a project has already been created. You can initiate a Budget for cost, revenue, and targeted margin. Roll up multiple work areas under larger initiatives or work hierarchies.

CA22.comments: Workday supports the creation of billable, non-billable, and capital projects. You can track project time costs and spend on projects, project tasks, or non-project work.

CA23.comments: Workday supports project budgets that can be configured to control spending activity. Business process configuration options allow a hard stop for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist, or a warning for transactions where sufficient budget does not exist.

CA24.comments: Workday supports managing project costs from project-tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project costs into depreciable business assets.

CA26.comments: Workday provides various ledgers to accommodate pre-encumbrances, encumbrances, and actuals.

CA27.comments:
Visual Lease:

Visual Lease supports the creation of the appropriate fund journal entries for the tracking and posting to these funds

Workday:

Workday supports multiple accounting methods, including fund accounting, by using book codes and custom reporting to track fund balances, restrictions and interfund transfers in compliance with GASB standards. Workday's multiple-funding source feature allows users to define funding source rules to optimize the distribution of available funds in accordance with funding agreements.

CA28.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required. Define allocations, organize them by how often and in what order they should run. Allocate based on a fixed source amount, fixed rate amount, or by ledger/plan structure.

CA29.comments: Workday provides a robust allocation engine which provides the ability to allocate various costs to final cost objectives with accounting entries automatically being created. Workday enables you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required.

CA30.comments: Workday Supports Multi-level or dependent allocations. The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and will alert the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensures the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA31.comments: Workday supports multi-level or dependent allocations. The system keeps track of the status of the dependencies and alerts the user if an allocation is dependent on another allocation rule. The user can run the allocation as a group and the system will process the dependencies in the proper order, ensuring the accuracy and consistencies on the allocation amount.

CA33.comments: Workday can allow you to find the data for Work Orders but does not provide functionality of Work Orders

CA34.comments: Workday supports a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules. We enable you to automatically allocate overhead, costs, revenues, and more using worktags to provide whatever level of granularity is required. A single allocation definition can be used to group multiple worktag types allowing allocations to be performed by worktags.

CA35.comments: Workday enables you to define allocations and organize them by how often and in what order they should run. We support a wide range of allocation methods, with any basis/driver, and give users complete control over ordering and organizing their rules.

CA36.comments: Workday gives you greater visibility into project costs with project capitalization. A streamlined process lets you manage project costs from acquisition through to identification of assets within the project, and finally to capitalization of these assets. Workday also automates the WIP/CIP and other accounting entries along the way. This enables you to identify your project costs and delay recognition of these expenses by recording them as long-term assets.

CA38.comments: Workday lets you run allocations in a “draft” status to view the financial impact through your reports WITHOUT having to post/un-post the transactions. You can also run pro-forma allocations at any time to provide visibility into fully loaded numbers on demand.

CA39.comments: Supported. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories. Users can run a receivables aging by either operational aging (ages by transaction date) or financial aging (ages by accounting date). Additionally, users can have the aging basis be by either due date or invoice date.

CA40.comments:
Workday provides the ability to add unlimited notes and attachments to a customer’s record. We also break up the security capability to add a note (versus add and/or update the customer record) and another permission to edit any notes entered.

When you view a customer invoice, you have visibility into all hold, collection, and dispute information.

CA41.comments: Workday provides Dunning Letter capabilities to assist organizations with late customer invoice management by providing auto-generated reminder letters, which can be emailed. Workday provides users the ability to automatically calculate and create invoices for interest and/or late fees related to past-due customer invoices. The delivered customer statement can be run in two formats. You can run a customer aging as of any date and using any user-defined age categories.

CA42.comments: While the provided text does not explicitly mention security deposits, Workday's robust functionality around customer payments, cash application, and receivables control accounts suggests that it can be configured to track customer security deposits. The ability to record deposits, apply cash across ledgers, and manage receivables control accounts provides a foundation for tracking security deposits. Furthermore, the system's document ID generation capabilities can be used to create unique identifiers for each security deposit transaction.

CA43.comments: Workday supports the following payment methods: cash, check, direct debit, EFT, wire, and credit/debit card. Workday also provides a Customer Payment Lockbox integration to import Bank Administration Institute (BAI) lockbox data into Workday from an external endpoint.

CA44.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. You can also offset multiple invoices and adjustments that aren't tied in a single transaction by recording zero-amount customer payments.

CA45.comments:
Partially Supported + integration with POS system for payment

Workday has a secure customer-facing portal for invoice lookup and payment research, which also allows for collection reporting. For payment, Workday can link out to 3rd party POS systems / websites. Invoice matching is automated through the application process/engine inside of workday, which is not customer-facing.

CA46.comments: Workday does not provide the POS system but can certainly bring the data in

CA47.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. Workday even delivers a specific business process to govern and audit assets transferred.

CA49.comments:
Supported. You can manage project costs from project tagged transactions, to the association of those transactions with capital assets in the project, to capitalization of those project cost into depreciable business assets.

Project assets are buckets that are specific to each capital project, which capture separate, ongoing costs of a capital project that is in progress. You can associate multiple projects assets with a project to track cost over the life of a project. When all costs are in from transactions tagged to the capital project, you can convert each project asset to a business asset to realize cost and start depreciation. Each asset may have a different depreciation schedule.

CA50.comments:
Workday enables you to correct the useful life of an asset if the asset is capitalized with an incorrect life or there's a change in its estimated useful life. The transaction effective date when you correct useful life is always the acquisition date. The transaction effective date to change the useful life of an asset can be any date during the life of an asset.

The Asset Useful Life Update Event business process routes the transaction for review and approval. Workday also creates depreciation adjustment transactions for applicable useful life changes when ledger periods have processed depreciation and depreciation expenses should be a different amount.

CA51.comments: Workday simplifies the payment application process by leveraging embedded machine learning algorithms to automatically match customer payments to invoices and adjustments. Workday enables you to apply large volumes of payments automatically against multiple invoices based on remittance information via a system of payment application rules. You can configure customer payment rule sets and link them to active bank accounts, and then schedule payment jobs for eligible bank accounts with unreconciled bank statement lines. Workday supports the ability to configure invoice prefixes, customer mapping tables, and multiple bank accounts, among others, to populate associated instances. This automates the process of recording customer payments with remittance advice and customer deposits from the bank statement lines.

CA52.comments: Supported. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Reconciliation rules are configured to tell Workday how to reconcile the items using condition rules. A condition rule consists of one or more logical statements used to check for matching entries using logical comparison operators AND/OR. Condition rules are then specified in reconciliation rules, which specify the reconciliation reason and the action to take. The action could be to automatically match, suggest a match or create an ad hoc bank transaction for a first notice item (e.g., bank fees). This reduces manual effort as you only have to take care of the exception not covered by the reconciliation rules which provides more flexibility, transparency, and accuracy.

CA53.comments: Supported. With Workday, you can automatically import electronic bank statements in order to reconcile bank statement lines with system transactions for multiple bank accounts. Delivered integrations support both the BAI2 and ISO 20022 statement formats. If the bank does not support BAI2 or ISO 20022, you can build a custom integration to import the statement data. Workday enables you to match bank statement lines to your operational transactions for automatic bank statement reconciliations. You can create a rule set consisting of different matching rules, condition rules, and matching methods to associate with a bank account. Additionally, exception reporting is available for unmatched or out-of-tolerance transactions.

CA54.comments: Supported. You can transfer business assets between different organizations, locations, and/or workers. Workday also provides the ability to perform mass transfers of assets as well as a special “return to storage” designation. CA55.comments: Workday delivers the ability to create financial reports for statutory reporting, tracking financial operations performance, and generating comparison and consolidation reports. Accounting "workpaper" reports such as trial balance, ledger detail, account analysis, and reconciliations provide analysis and control. Consolidated profit and loss (P&L), balance sheet, and cash flow statements meet financial statement needs in base and reporting currencies. User-defined, real-time query provides insight whenever and wherever it's needed.

CA56.comments: Workday supports automated receipt processing across various channels, including ACH, credit card, wire, and lockbox. Our system provides the ability to match payments to open invoices using configurable rules such as amount, reference number, and customer ID. For unmatched items, Workday offers exception-handling workflows to ensure proper resolution.

CA57.comments: Workday supports manual entry of invoices, cash receipts, and adjustments. For customer invoices, you can create them manually, load them directly into Workday, copy existing invoices, or create them from customer invoice proposals. Supplier invoices can also be created by manual entry or by copying existing invoices. Journals can be created by manual entry or copying an existing journal.

CA58.comments:
Supported. Workday allows you to create a template for a recurring journal with a start and end date and generate new journals using this template for a user-specified timeframe. You may also specify a reversal date on recurring journals. Please note that with this template, once it has been populated the recurring journals will automatically get recorded in the appropriate timeframe that are specified in the template. Different from other solutions that recommend using a spreadsheet and uploading recurring journals each period, in Workday users do not need to remember to upload the journals each period as journal generation will happen automatically.

Custom validations can also be added to recurring journal entries to ensure that all requirements for data input are met prior to posting.

The Ad Hoc Project Transaction feature allows users to create billable (time, expenses, and supplier invoices) transactions from external source.

CA63.comments: To minimize the movement of data, latency, and reconciliation that goes with it, Workday's technology includes a reporting and analytics platform in the same environment you consolidate, transact, and reconcile. SC01.comments:
The Workday platform is fundamentally designed as a system of record, and it fully supports the requirement to maintain historical schedule and time data for long-term periods to meet legal, union, and internal policy needs.

Workday's architecture ensures that this data is not only stored but is also auditable and accessible. The retention period is not limited by the system; data is retained indefinitely unless you explicitly choose to purge it.

Workday provides the flexibility to meet your data retention requirements. Here is a summary of how historical schedule data is managed and how you can configure retention.

Data Retention in Workday

Workday does not impose a maximum retention period for customer data, including historical schedule records. Your organization has control over how long data is stored in the tenant. Data is retained for the lifetime of the tenant unless your organization actively purges it using Workday's data management tools.

This approach allows you to define your own data retention policies in line with legal, union, and grievance process requirements.

SC02.comments:
Workday maintains a full history of all events including position assignment and compensation changes. Workday is an always-on audit application. Also, Workday Time Tracking supports retroactive worked time and time entries, as well as Time Offs and Leave of Absence.

This capability is a cornerstone of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are all part of a single system, it can seamlessly track historical changes and automatically calculate the financial impact of those changes with full auditability.

SC03.comments:
Workday Scheduling provides a range of configurable scheduling settings that allow various warnings and/or errors for various conditions to be triggered. Examples include minimum breaks between shifts, minimum shift length, maximum shift length, maximum daily hours, maximum weekly hours, required meals and breaks, consecutive days and overtime thresholds.

This is a fundamental capability of Workday's Human Capital Management (HCM) and Time Tracking/Absence platform. The system is designed to create, assign, and maintain a detailed and historical record of each employee's standard work schedule.

This functionality is critical, as it forms the foundation for numerous other processes, including payroll, leave accruals, and resource planning.

SC04.comments:
Workday provides visibility for all worker and business process changes as part of the always-on audit controls. Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time and Workday Scheduling which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs actual time reporting. Workday Time Tracking leverages the worker data already held within Workday, including the Core Compensation Pay Rates, Absence Plans and Job Data, which allows this data to be available for base rates natively.

This capability is a core strength of Workday's unified architecture. Because Workday's HCM, Time Tracking, and Payroll modules are not separate systems but are all part of a single platform, it provides unparalleled real-time visibility and seamless reconciliation between scheduled work, exceptions, and final pay.

This real-time, integrated process eliminates the manual reconciliation work common in systems with separate time and payroll modules, significantly reducing errors and ensuring that employees are paid accurately based on their approved schedules and exceptions.

SC05.comments:
Workday's business process framework supports the complete cycle for probation periods. This includes the ability to configure specific conditions for probationary periods based on contract duration, type of employment, previous employment, region, collective agreements and more. Specific business processes are delivered for Manage Probation Period, Pre-Hire Probation Period Details, and Review Probation Period.

Workday can support this requirement through a combination of standard features and configurable components. While the standard probation period functionality is typically based on a set duration (e.g., 90 days), you can use calculated fields and alerts to create a process based on actual hours worked.

Standard Probation Period Functionality

Workday's native probation period feature allows you to track a period of time from an employee's hire or start date. You can configure alerts to notify managers and other stakeholders when a standard, duration-based probation period is coming to an end.

However, this standard feature is based on a calendar duration (e.g., days, months) and does not natively track a cumulative total of hours worked.

Leading Practice for Hours-Based Probation

To meet the requirement of a probation period based on actual hours worked, you can leverage the power of Workday Time Tracking and Calculated Fields. This approach involves creating a mechanism to count the hours and trigger a notification once the threshold is met.

SC06.comments:
Yes, the system can track continuing education units and certifications within the worker profile in Workday Core HCM. Workday Learning supports configurable Unit Types, which allows customers to define their accreditation units (Professional Development Units, CEUs, Credits, certification units) within the properties of a course with flexibility.

Workday is well-equipped to track both continuing education requirements and certifications. This is primarily managed through the Talent and Performance and Workday Learning modules.

SC07.comments:
Workday Scheduling, Time Tracking, and Absence Management work as a unified system. Time off requests and approvals update balances dynamically. Schedules integrate with timekeeping for variance reporting and accurate payroll.

These capabilities represent a core and fundamental strength of the Workday platform. Workday's single architecture, where HCM, Scheduling, Time Tracking, Absence, and Payroll all exist within one system, is specifically designed to provide this seamless integration and real-time visibility.

Workday's architecture is designed to provide seamless integration between scheduling, timekeeping, and absence management to ensure data accuracy and streamline payroll processes.

Integration of Scheduling, Timekeeping, and Payroll

Workday provides a single, unified system for Workforce Management that includes Workday Scheduling, Workday Time Tracking, and Workday Payroll. This integration ensures a smooth and accurate flow of data.

    ● Schedule to Timecard: When schedules are created in Workday Scheduling, they serve as the baseline for an employee's timecard in Workday Time Tracking. As employees report their time (e.g., via clock-in/clock-out or manual entry), the system automatically compares the actual hours worked against the scheduled hours.
    ● Variance Tracking: This direct comparison allows the system to identify and flag schedule variances in real-time. Examples include:
        ● Overtime: Time worked beyond the scheduled shift or weekly threshold.
        ● Unscheduled Absences: A "no-show" for a scheduled shift.
        ● Early/Late Punches: Deviations from the scheduled start and end times.

    ● Flow to Payroll: Once timecards are approved by a manager, the accurately calculated hours—including all variances, overtime, and shift differentials—flow directly into Workday Payroll. This eliminates the need for manual data transfer, significantly reduces the risk of payroll errors, and creates a clear audit trail.
    ● Reporting: Because all data resides within the same system, you can generate real-time reports and dashboards that provide visibility into scheduled vs. actual hours, overtime costs, and other key labor metrics.

Vacation Requesting and Leave Balance Integration

Workday also provides tight integration between vacation requests, managed in Workday Absence Management, and employee leave balances.

SC08.comments:
Workday supports effective dated tracking of union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. These elements are attributes of the employee and can be used throughout the entire system for compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, and reporting. Workday Core HCM is able to track qualifications and expiry dates (to ensure workers are always available for rostering) and allows for assigning Workday Scheduling tags based on specific qualifications, assessments, and/or experience. A worker or position may be associated with a wide variety of attributes such as job profile, location, cost center, manager, default weekly hours, shift, and status.

This is a fundamental principle of Workday's architecture and a primary benefit of its unified platform. The scheduling system is not a separate module that needs to be integrated with HCM; it is an intrinsic part of the same system, operating on the same single source of truth.

This seamless access to core employee data is what allows Workday to make intelligent, compliant, and efficient scheduling decisions, rather than just placing names in time slots.

SC09.comments:
Workday provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time, and scheduling, which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs. actual time reporting. Workday can manage shift differential eligibility by setting up standard schedules, for example, based on geography (country) or other criteria. These shifts can have different pay rates based on the shift or base plus a premium for hours worked on a shift or eligible times of day. Shift differential calculations are configured to ensure workers working in the specified timeframes get the right level of pay. Multiple shift differential calculations can be combined, for example, when different pay rates apply from 10pm to 12am and from 12am to 4am.

Workday doesn't just integrate its scheduling, timekeeping, and payroll systems; they are all components of a single, unified system operating on one source of employee data. This distinction is crucial because it means there is no "transmission" or "transfer" of data between separate systems. Instead, it's a seamless, real-time flow of information within one platform.

SC10.comments:
While Workday does not offer pre-built, certified connectors specifically for Vector Solutions or InTime, it provides a robust and flexible integration platform that can be used to build custom connections to these or any other third-party systems.

If you find that the native Workday Scheduling capabilities do not fully meet your Public Safety requirements, you can develop a custom integration to exchange schedule, assignment, and availability data.

Workday Integration Tools

Workday offers several tools to facilitate custom integrations. The choice of tool depends on the complexity, data volume, and real-time needs of the integration.

    ● Workday Studio: This is Workday's most powerful integration tool, providing a graphical development environment for building complex, custom integrations. Workday Studio would be the leading practice for creating a sophisticated, bidirectional integration with systems like InTime or Vector Solutions. It can handle complex data transformations, custom logic, and error handling.
    ● REST and SOAP APIs: Workday provides a comprehensive library of web services that allow external systems to securely access and update Workday data. You can develop a custom solution that calls Workday's APIs to:
        ● Get Data: Pull worker information, availability, leave requests, and other relevant data from Workday to send to the external scheduling system.
        ● Put Data: Push approved schedules, assignments, and actual hours worked from the external system back into Workday for timekeeping and payroll processing.

    ● Workday Cloud Connect: This is a framework for building packaged integrations. While there isn't a pre-built Cloud Connect package for these specific vendors, the underlying technology could be used by a development team to create a reusable connector.
    ● Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB): EIBs are a user-friendly tool for getting data into and out of Workday. They are ideal for simpler, bulk data transfers that can be scheduled to run on a recurring basis. An EIB could be used for less time-sensitive data exchanges, such as sending a daily file of approved hours from the external system to Workday.

In summary, if you need to integrate with Vector Solutions or InTime, it is technically feasible. The project would involve a custom development effort utilizing Workday's integration tools, with Workday Studio and the extensive API library being the most likely components of a successful solution.

SC11.comments:
Workday provides a fully integrated solution to validate training and certification status for shift eligibility by leveraging the seamless connection between Workday Scheduling, Workday Learning, and the Talent and Performance module.

Workday ensures employees have valid certifications and correct credentials for their roles by issuing and renewing certifications based on successful completion of learning content. Certification rules allow configuration of required learning content and completion frequency. Workday provides reporting capabilities around active, inactive, and expiring certifications.

SC12.comments:
While Workday does not provide pre-built, certified integrations for the wide array of specialized first responder software and applications (such as Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) or Records Management Systems (RMS)), it is designed with a powerful and flexible integration platform to make these connections possible through custom development.

The leading practice for integrating Workday with specialized third-party systems is to leverage Workday's suite of integration tools to build a solution tailored to your specific needs. This allows you to use best-of-breed applications for specific first responder functions while maintaining Workday as the system of record for all HR, time, and payroll data.

The leading practice is to use Workday as the system of record for all employee data, including absences and availability, and to build an integration that allows the EAM to query this information in real-time.

SC16.comments: Workday provides hard-stop alerts for Workday Scheduling settings where needed. This can prevent a user from adding a shift if rules are breached. Approvals for Open Shift Board requests also allows Managers to see the impact for approving additional shifts.

HR01.comments: Total Rewards Statements are standard functionality. They combine compensation categories like base, merit, bonus, equity, allowances, and benefits. Statements can include payroll actuals and use templates for branding. HR administrators can manage statement creation without programming. Compensation overviews are also available on worker profiles. Additionally, Workday supports Total Rewards templates to display a breakdown of base pay, bonuses, health and other benefits, stock, commissions, payroll earnings/deductions, and custom fields.

HR02.comments:
Total Rewards statements allow organizations the ability to express employee investment to the workforce. Workday delivers a configurable Total Rewards statement. You can configure the Total Rewards Statement with options, such as comparison between actual and target, naming overrides, and decimal precision control that can make this statement truly individual to your organization's needs.

Workday meets this requirement through a combination of two powerful features:

    ● For ongoing, up-to-date visibility (especially for new hires), the live Total Rewards Statement is automatically available based on configuration rules.
    ● For formal, point-in-time issuance (like an annual refresh tied to a compensation cycle), the Compensation Review Statement can be released on a pre-configured schedule.

HR03.comments: Workday enables the setup of step-based salary ranges and utilizes time in range to determine when the employee should be moved to the next step. Progression rules allow you to create eligibility conditions for specific step increases. Workday delivers a report that will identify employees who are ready for their next step and will allow the withholding of the step increase if appropriate. Managers can also receive notifications in My Tasks when an employee is about to progress to the next step, wherein they can approve or withhold progression. Finally, Workday can consider other factors when it comes to an employee's targeted step progression date, such as adjusting the date based on an employee's time in a nonpay status, or preserving the target date if the employee has a compensation change.

HR04.comments:
The Workday platform provides a comprehensive and integrated framework to handle all three of those requirements. It is designed to manage large-scale compensation events with flexibility, control, and clear communication.

1. Managing and Applying Cost of Living Adjustments (COLA)

Workday handles mass pay changes like COLAs through a controlled and auditable business process, typically initiated as a mass action.

2. Supporting Calibration Outside of Standard Metrics

This is a core strength of Workday's Compensation Review Process. The platform is built to support managerial discretion and calibration, rather than forcing strict adherence to formulas.

    ● During a compensation review cycle (for merit, bonus, and/or stock), managers are presented with a configurable grid of their employees.
    ● The system can provide a suggested merit increase based on standard metrics (like performance rating and position in range/compa-ratio). However, these are typically configured as guidelines, not hard-enforced rules.
    ● Managers have the ability to override the system's suggestion and enter a different amount, allowing them to apply their own judgment. They can often be required to enter a comment to justify the deviation, ensuring the decision is documented.
    ● Calibration Tools:
        ● Budget Pools: Managers are allocated budgets. They can distribute their pool across their team as they see fit, as long as they do not exceed their total budget.
        ● Additional Adjustment Pools: You can configure separate budget pools for "Additional Adjustments" or "Promotions." This gives managers a separate purse of money to use for discretionary increases outside of the standard merit budget, which is ideal for addressing equity issues, retaining top talent, or rewarding special contributions.
        ● Visual Analytics: The calibration screen includes analytics that update in real-time, showing the manager how their proposed changes compare to the guidelines and how they are tracking against their budget.


3. Generating Printable Compensation Statements

Workday provides a powerful tool for creating and distributing personalized, printable compensation statements at the end of a review cycle.

    ●  The Compensation Review Statement is a formal PDF document that is generated for each employee included in the compensation review process.
    ●  The layout of these statements is fully customizable using BIRT (Business Intelligence and Reporting Technology).

HR05.comments:
Workday's Business Process Framework delivers standard business processes across the entire functional spectrum. These business processes are used as starting points for customers who then make configuration changes to meet their business requirements. Some examples of these configurable business processes are Change Job (transfer, promotion, etc.), Request Compensation Change, Request a One-Time Payment, etc. These processes provide an "always on" audit trail of all the actions taken.

The Workday platform is designed with an integrated architecture where staffing changes and compensation are intrinsically linked. The system not only applies compensation changes during a promotion but also uses the same event to automatically update eligibility for other compensation components like bonus plans.

HR06.comments: Workday supports off-cycle compensation changes, across the board changes, and compensation review process events. Pay change requests can be initiated by users with the requisite security access and is supported by the Workday Business Process Framework (BPF). The Workday BPF allows you to tailor the process to include relevant approvals, embed analytics for decision support, and validations as desired. If necessary, customers can also bulk load compensation changes using Workday’s Enterprise Interface Builder (EIB) integration tool.

HR07.comments:
Workday Advanced Compensation facilitates tactical, in-cycle scenario planning for managers to plan how to use their budget. When an annual process like a merit or bonus cycle is launched, managers receive a planning grid for their team. Within this grid, they can:

    ● Model Different Awards: Enter various percentage increases or bonus amounts for individual employees to see the immediate impact.
    ● Get Real-Time Calculations: The system instantly recalculates new salaries, compa-ratios, and the total spend for their team.
    ● Ensure Budget Conformance: Managers can see how their proposed awards track against their allocated budget in real-time, allowing them to adjust scenarios to stay within their target.

This is essentially modeling within a pre-defined budget for the immediate compensation cycle.


How Adaptive Planning Strengthens This
Workday Adaptive Planning provides the strategic, high-level financial modeling that happens before the Advanced Compensation cycle even begins. It strengthens the process by allowing leadership to:

    ● Model Large-Scale "What-If" Scenarios: Analyze the long-term financial impact of major changes, such as adjusting salary ranges across the company, introducing new incentive plans, or modeling workforce growth and promotions.
    ● Create the Budget: The primary function is to forecast and set the overall compensation budget. The outputs of these strategic models in Adaptive Planning are what determine the budgets that are ultimately loaded into Advanced Compensation for managers to execute against.

In short, Adaptive Planning is used to strategically decide what the budget should be, while Advanced Compensation is used to tactically plan how to spend that budget.

HR08.comments:
Workday supports the effective dated tracking union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. As attributes of the employee, these elements can then be used throughout the entire system for things like compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, reporting, etc. Workday provides configurable eligibility rules for Time Off Plans, as well as conditional calculation rules within the Time Off Plan, that can allow for variations of accruals based on criteria such as Employee type and/or location. Flexible compensation plans such as bonus and stock plans can support a wide variety of short, medium, and long-term incentives. Eligibility rules determine who is included in each plan. These rules are highly configurable and can be as broad or narrow as needed by leveraging data and attributes available in the system.

This is a fundamental and powerful capability of the Workday platform. Workday's compensation framework is built on a robust, rule-based engine that allows you to automate and enforce eligibility for any compensation program with a high degree of precision and control.

HR09.comments:
Workday supports the effective dated tracking of union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. As attributes of the employee, these elements can then be used throughout the entire system for things like compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, reporting, etc.

Workday is designed from the ground up to handle complex, global compensation requirements, and its architecture excels at managing numerous, diverse compensation plans concurrently, including the distinct structures needed for union and non-union employee populations.

HR10.comments:
Workday manages spot bonuses through a configurable Business Process (BP), typically the Request One-Time Payment BP. This creates a seamless, controlled process from initiation to payment.

    1. Initiation and Approval Routing:
        ● A manager initiates the process, selecting the employee and the bonus amount.
        ● The BP automatically routes the request for all necessary approvals. This routing is highly configurable; for example, bonuses under $500 might only need the direct manager's approval, while awards over $500 automatically route to the department head and HR for additional review.

    2. Linkage to Division-Level Budgets:
        ● The spot bonus plan itself is configured with funded award pools. These pools are directly linked to specific organizations, such as a division or cost center.
        ● When a manager initiates a bonus, the system knows which budget pool to draw from based on the employee's assigned organization.

    3. Validation to Prevent Overspending:
        ● This is the critical control step. Before the bonus can be submitted or approved, the BP validates the award amount against the real-time balance of the linked budget pool.
        ● If the award exceeds the available funds, the system can be configured to trigger a "hard stop" (an error that prevents submission) or a "soft stop" (a warning that allows submission but may require additional approvals).


Once fully approved, the bonus amount automatically flows to Workday Payroll for processing, ensuring a complete and auditable end-to-end workflow.

Workday enables the attachment of any electronic document to an employee’s record. There are two means of attaching documents to workers within Workday. In either case, all documents will be shown in the worker’s document file and security can be setup by document category to determine who has access to view the document for the worker. HR103.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

OnBase manages access to content in the same manner as Workday manages access to data - using a combination of user groups and role-based access. Access is controlled via a secure identity provider, such as Active Directory, Azure AD, or SAML-based SSO. Permissions are assigned using role-based access controls (RBAC) based on job function, department, or other customer-defined attributes, ensuring users only access the data and features necessary for their role.

Workday:

Yes, Workday supports this functional requirement.

Of course. Your statement is correct. Workday's entire architecture is built on the principle of managing personnel records through a secure, centralized, and role-based access model. This is a fundamental aspect of the system's design and not just an add-on feature.

Here’s a breakdown of how Workday accomplishes this:

1. Centralized System of Record

Workday operates as a single, unified system for all HR data. This means that from the moment a candidate is hired until they are terminated, their entire personnel record—including personal information, job history, compensation, performance reviews, benefits, and absence data—is stored in one place. This eliminates data silos and ensures that all stakeholders are working from the same source of truth.

2. Secure, Role-Based Access Model

Workday’s security is not based on individuals but on the roles they hold within the organization. This ensures that access is consistent, scalable, and auditable.

HR104.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

OnBase Records Management will manage all personnel file retention in accordance with employee exit dates and retention policies. The system automates retention schedules, ensuring that files are securely maintained for the required period and disposed of appropriately when retention requirements are met, supporting compliance and reducing administrative burden. OnBase supports the ability to put holds on files and supports the use of certificates of destruction to prove that files were properly destroyed and purged from the system.

Workday:

Workday Recruiting leverages the always-on audit controls within Workday, allowing all applicant data to be retained as history and used in find candidate searches, candidate pools and recruiting campaigns. Where in-country requirements require applicants to be asked whether their application details can be retained by the employer, Workday provides delivered functionality to allow the applicant to give their consent (and if they don't give consent and wish for their details to be deleted, candidate purge functionality provides the ability to de-identify the applicant details, whilst providing required data for recruiting reports).

Workday is designed to manage the retention and purging of personnel files in accordance with company policies and legal requirements, which are often tied to a worker's exit date.

Workday provides a powerful and configurable framework that puts the customer in control of their own data retention lifecycle. The primary tool for this is the Purge Person Data task.

HR105.comments:
Hyland OnBase:

OnBase, by Hyland, is recommended as the integrated document/content management system for the Port of Portland. The Hyland for Workday integration is Certified Built on Workday, which provides the seamless access users need from Workday with the security, controls, and features of an industry-leading content management solution. Workday:

HR11.comments:
Workday provides robust tools for simulating and modeling compensation changes. This functionality is primarily handled through the Advanced Compensation module, specifically within the Compensation Review Process.

Here’s a brief explanation of how the system supports this:

    ● Compensation Review Process: This is the core feature for managing company-wide compensation events like merit increases, bonus awards, and stock grants. It allows HR users and managers to work within a dedicated space to propose and review changes.
    ● Modeling and Simulation:
        ● Sandbox Environment: For extensive "what-if" scenarios, a common leading practice is to use a Workday Sandbox tenant. You can refresh the sandbox with production data, and then run various compensation scenarios without impacting the live environment. This allows you to see the full downstream effects of proposed changes.
        ● Compensation Review Grid: Within a live or sandbox compensation review process, managers and HR partners can enter proposed changes into a configurable grid. The system will dynamically calculate the impacts of these changes on budgets and compensation pools in real-time. This allows for immediate feedback and adjustments.
        ● Calculations Engine: Workday's calculation engine is a powerful tool that can be used to model complex scenarios. You can create custom calculations to simulate how changes in one area (like a performance rating) might affect another (like a bonus payout).

    ● Budget and Pool Management: During the compensation review process, you can establish budgets and pools. As you model changes, the system tracks expenditures against these budgets, providing a clear picture of the financial impact.
    ● Pay Equity Analysis: By using Workday's reporting and analytics capabilities in conjunction with the compensation review process, you can analyze the impact of proposed changes on pay equity. You can create reports that show pay distribution by various demographics and identify any potential disparities before finalizing the changes.

In summary, Workday enables HR users to simulate compensation changes by leveraging the Compensation Review Process in a sandbox environment and using the dynamic calculations within the compensation grid to model the impacts on budgets and analyze pay equity. events.

Workday provides Leave of Absence functionality with a delivered task for requesting a leave of absence, which can be initiated by workers, managers and/or administrators. Supported by the Workday Business Process Framework, associated approvals, notifications, questionnaires, reports, to-dos, checklists and even web services and integrations can be automatically triggered to support the process. Steps within the business process can be conditional, allowing for variation in process steps where needed (e.g. perhaps by Leave of Absence type, location or job role). The Leave Of Absence plan can be flagged should any effects for Payroll, Other Absences, Benefits, Stock, Continuous Service or Talent be required. Compensation Reviews can be automated from an eligibility and calculation of allocations perspective also. Workday clearly shows workers as "On Leave" within the worker profile, within searches and reports and within the Org Chart and provides delivered reports to allow for easy tracking of workers "On Leave". Leveraging the Notifications & Alerts Framework, users can be notified upon the approach of anticipated return dates, to submit the Return from Leave of Absence request, which is also a delivered task with a delivered business process, to allow the specific tasks and actions associated with returns from extended absences to be managed. Actions and Notifications are provided to users via their Workday My Tasks (can also be pushed to email and received as in-app notifications via the Workday Mobile App and Workday App for Microsoft Teams).
