Recruiters can schedule interviews in Workday and send interview questionnaires to the team based on those interview dates. Interview scheduling is available with Outlook 365 or Google Calendar. You can configure a simplified interview experience with minimal steps for interviewers without a calendar integration, and Workday will also propose the convenient scheduling options for you to choose from.  Applicant self-scheduling is enabled for interviews.

TA26.comments:
Workday Recruiting allows Recruiters to create calendar schedules with time slots and locations that are shared with candidates in their candidate home page. If a candidate is invited to interview, a task appears where they are asked to select a time and date that is convenient for them. The interview slot selected automatically updates in the recruiter's interview report. 

The system's ability to "lock" a schedule is centered on the internal interview team's availability, especially when using a calendar integration.

    ● Calendar Integration: Workday integrates with Microsoft Outlook 365 and Google Calendar. When a recruiter schedules an interview, the system sends calendar invitations directly to the internal interviewers. This action reserves the time slot on the interviewers' calendars, effectively "locking" it for the interview.
    ● Centralized Management: The recruiter or interview scheduler manages the entire process within Workday. The schedule is considered fixed unless the recruiter initiates a change through the Reschedule or Cancel actions.
    ● Candidate View: Candidates do not have their calendars directly accessed or locked. Instead, they are notified of the confirmed interview details and can view their upcoming schedule on their Candidate Home portal.

Workday is designed to keep recruiters and the hiring team informed of scheduling changes and updates.

    ● Schedule Changes: If a recruiter needs to reschedule or cancel an interview, initiating this action in Workday automatically triggers updated calendar invitations to all relevant interviewers. This ensures the internal team's calendars are updated in near real-time.
    ● Interviewer Responses: When using a calendar integration, the interview scheduler can receive email notifications when interviewers accept or decline the meeting invitations. This provides timely feedback on the team's availability.
    ● Candidate Reminders: You can configure automated reminders to be sent to candidates via email or SMS before their scheduled interview. While the system does not send a dynamic calendar file to the candidate, it ensures they are kept informed of the confirmed date and time.
    ● Recruiter Visibility: Recruiters have full visibility into the interview process. They are notified of interviewer responses and manage all changes from within Workday, allowing them to react quickly to any scheduling conflicts.

TA27.comments:
Workday enables interactive, two-way communication with candidates via Candidate Conversational Messaging for SMS. This functionality is an enhancement of the broader Workday Messaging framework. Two-way candidate communications are key to an informed and efficient recruitment process. While it is important for recruiters to have the ability to initiate a conversation with a candidate, in particular cases, the information returned through the candidate’s response is even more pivotal to the success of a hire. 

A wide array of communication mediums can be utilized to message back-and-forth with the candidate at different points throughout the process depending on the organization size, location and culture. The one commonality across all of these channels is the importance of tracking these communications for compliance and also allowing for full visibility across the entire hiring team to enable information-sharing and collaboration. 

The requirements of this feature are to:

    ● Send SMS communication to candidates
    ● Provide the candidate with the ability to reply to an SMS message

In essence, this capability allows for more agile and engaging recruitment by enabling real-time, threaded SMS conversations directly within the Workday platform.

Workday is in the process of acquiring Paradox which will bring significant functionality in this area. Press release here: https://newsroom.workday.com/2025-08-21-Workday-Signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Paradox,-the-AI-Company-Redefining-the-Frontline-Candidate-Experience?refCamp=701VT00000UCjJZ 

Paradox is currently a certified partner with a pre-built API on the Marketplace.

TA28.comments: Standard functionality supported by Workday Recruiting. A complete history of the applicant flow and candidate status, for every application the candidate has made, is available on the candidate profile. All steps throughout the process are configurable via the business process framework and can include steps such as Review, Screen, Assessment, Interview, Offer etc.

TA29.comments:
Workday's single platform has redefined how offers and contracts are generated ensuring that core HCM, Recruiting and candidate data merge seamlessly to create desired correspondence. Generated offers include compensation guidelines which will flow from approved Positions, Offer, to Hire. The offer process can include approval steps, including the Compensation and Benefits team. The offer process supports flexibility in the offer negotiation process. Candidates have the opportunity to view and accept extended offers in the Candidate Home, electronic signature is supported with Adobe Sign or DocuSign. Additional correspondence can be set up as part of the recruiting business process and can be automatically sent to the candidate; for example, you may have an additional questionnaire at the time of screening a candidate. In addition, with Candidate Conversational Messaging, you can exchange SMS messages with candidates during recruitment. This provides cross-team visibility of candidate conversations and reduces the need for third-party systems and potential for data compliance issues by keeping your data within Workday. Workday also enables you to attach documents when you send internal offers for approval. You can control who can view and edit attachments and easily access attached documents from the candidate or worker profile. This increases efficiency by reducing the need to send documents separately.

The system's capabilities are centered on the Offer business process. You can configure this business process to include specific compensation-related subprocesses, such as:

    ● Propose Compensation Offer
    ● Request One-Time Payment Offer
    ● Request Stock Grant Offer

By embedding these as steps within the main Offer business process, you ensure that all components of a candidate's proposed compensation package are reviewed and approved by the designated individuals (e.g., compensation partners, hiring managers) before an offer letter is generated. You can configure approval steps and condition rules within the business process to route the offer to the appropriate reviewers based on your organization's policies.

TA30.comments:
Workday Docs enables you to create templates for Offer Letters, Employee Contracts and Agreements, enabling you to easily create and edit templates, insert data fields, and apply condition rules. You can also include tables, images, additional formatting, built-in headers and footers, and preview as you build. Workday supports integrations to electronic signature vendors like Adobe e-sign or DocuSign if an e-signature is needed. Workday also provides the ability to upload and share documents with candidates. Documents can be stored against a candidate/worker profile once signed and completed.

Key capabilities include:

    ● Template Creation: Design, create, and preview dynamic offer letter templates using Workday Docs. These templates can automatically pull in data specific to the candidate, such as name, title, location, salary, and start date.
    ● Automation: The offer process can be automated. For instance, an offer letter can be generated automatically when a candidate is moved to the Offer stage in the recruiting process.
    ● Consolidated Task Management: For high-volume recruiting, you can consolidate up to 200 individual offer tasks into a single workbook for more efficient processing.
    ● Efficient Renegotiation: You can easily update offer details, such as compensation terms or start date, and regenerate the letter. Workday maintains a complete version history, providing a clear audit trail of all changes.
    ● Candidate Interaction: Through the external career site, candidates can accept or decline offers, upload required documents, and complete forms.
    ● Attachments: You can attach supplemental documents to an offer, such as a benefits summary or a copy of the employee handbook.
    ● Integrated Process: The offer process is seamlessly integrated with other Workday functions, including Compensation, Job Requisitions, and Employment Agreements. For example, you can combine salary, bonus, and stock grant components into a single offer task.

TA31.comments:
Workday supports the ability to manually enter background check and screening results as part of the overall job application process. In addition, integrations can be leveraged to trigger the screening process with a vendor and have the results of the screening automatically fed back to Workday. Bidirectional integration with key background check and pre-employment vendors is supported by Workday Recruiting.

We have several certified partners with pre-built APIs on the Workday Marketplace: https://marketplace.workday.com/en-US/listing?attr=20379&page=1&locale=en-US#q=background%20check (https://marketplace.workday.com/en-US/listing?attr=20379&page=1&locale=en-US#q=background%20check)

TA32.comments:
Workday Onboarding helps organizations extend a warm welcome to new and pre-hires. You can provide access to onboarding activities before the worker's start date to help them feel welcome and transition comfortably into their new surroundings.

Workday's ability to deliver onboarding tasks prior to a new hire's start date is a key feature of its unified system, managed through the Hire Business Process.

Here is a brief explanation of how it works:

Once a candidate accepts their offer, they are moved into a "Pre-Hire" status within Workday. They are no longer just a candidate, but not yet an active employee. This unique status allows the system to interact with them in a controlled way.

    1. Triggering Pre-Boarding:
The Hire Business Process is configured to include steps that are specifically assigned to the "Pre-Hire" before their official start date. These steps are automatically triggered once the recruiting and offer stages are complete.
    2. Delivering Tasks to the Candidate Home:
The Pre-Hire receives an email notification prompting them to log back into their Candidate Home portal (the same site they used to apply for the job). Upon logging in, they will find a new set of tasks assigned to them.
    3. Types of Pre-Start Date Tasks:
Workday can deliver a variety of digital tasks, forms, and checklists, including:
        ● Review Documents: Presenting the final offer letter, a welcome packet, or key policies for early review.
        ● Complete Questionnaire: A powerful tool for collecting information that would normally be handled with paper forms. This can be used for things like confirming a mailing address, gathering emergency contact details, or noting equipment preferences (e.g., Mac vs. PC).
        ● "To Do" Checklists: Simple tasks that can include instructions and links, such as a prompt to "Explore our company benefits portal" or "Watch a welcome video from our CEO."


This pre-start date capability allows organizations to complete a significant amount of administrative work ahead of time. It creates a more engaging and welcoming experience for the new hire and ensures they are ready to be productive from day one, rather than spending their first day filling out paperwork.

TA33.comments:
Workday’s Business Process Framework (BPF) makes it easy to support different business processes for different parts of the organization and for those processes to change over time as your business evolves. Customers may configure these processes to alter the flow, which may include notifications, approvals and other actions such as integration with other systems throughout the processes.

Workday supports department-specific onboarding processes by adding conditional steps to the main Onboarding business process.

The system uses Condition Rules to check a new hire's department (their Supervisory Organization). Based on the department, the workflow automatically triggers additional tasks or even entire sub-processes.

There are two primary ways this is done:

    1. Conditional "To Do" Steps: For simple tasks, a rule is added to the main onboarding process. For example: If the new hire is in the 'Marketing' department, then assign the 'To Do' task: "Request Access to Adobe Creative Cloud."
    2. Conditional Sub-Processes: For more complex, multi-step departmental onboarding, a separate business process (a "sub-process") is created. For example: If the new hire is in the 'Clinical Staff' department, then initiate the "Clinical Onboarding" sub-process, which includes steps for credential verification, lab coat fitting, and specialized system access.

This ensures that new hires automatically receive a tailored onboarding experience with all relevant corporate and department-specific tasks assigned to them.

TA34.comments:
Workday tailors onboarding workflows using conditional logic within the Onboarding business process.

This is achieved by creating Condition Rules that check an employee's data for specific criteria. Think of it as an "if-then" statement for the workflow.

For example:

    ● If a new hire belongs to a Union, then the business process automatically adds union-specific steps, such as a "To Do" to acknowledge the collective bargaining agreement or a "Review Documents" step for the union benefits guide.
    ● If the new hire is an Admin employee (and not in a union), then the workflow automatically skips the union-specific steps and instead presents a different set of tasks relevant to their role.

This ensures each employee automatically receives a relevant onboarding experience with the correct tasks, documents, and notifications based on their specific classification.

Workday supports the effective dated tracking union and/or collective bargaining membership on workers. As attributes of the employee, these elements can then be used throughout the entire system for things like compensation eligibility, time calculation and payroll rules, security, reporting, etc.

TA35.comments:
Onboarding and Hiring is business process enabled. Within the business process framework, customers can create notifications and trigger notifications to other departments/users as needed to automate downstream impacts for actions, integrations, to-do, notifications, approvals, etc. including looping facilities, IT and security into the process without there being any disconnect.

Workday automates onboarding notifications and provisioning tasks with the configurable Hire Business Process (BP). This BP acts as a central workflow engine that orchestrates all downstream activities. You can adjust this BP at any time as you grow.

Here’s how it works:

The Hire Business Process is a sequence of steps that executes when a new employee is hired. You can insert specific notification and integration steps that trigger automatically upon the completion of a key stage, such as the final approval of the hire.

1. Automated Notifications and Tasks:

    ● For IT:
        ● A "To Do" step can be added to the business process that is routed to the "IT Onboarding" security group. This task appears in their Workday inbox with dynamic details like: "Provision laptop and accounts for [New Hire Name], starting on [Start Date] at [Location]."
        ● Alternatively, an integration step can be configured to automatically call an external system like ServiceNow or Jira, creating a provisioning ticket with all the necessary new hire data, requiring zero manual intervention.

    ● For Facilities:
        ● Similar to IT, a "To Do" is automatically generated and assigned to the "Facilities Team" role. The task instructs them to prepare a workspace, desk, and any physical access requirements for the new hire at their specific business site.

    ● For Payroll:
        ● Payroll is inherently integrated. The completion of the Hire BP itself is the primary trigger. The moment the hire is final, the employee's record becomes active, and their compensation data is immediately available to the Payroll module for the next pay run.
        ● The BP also launches the new hire's Onboarding tasks, which include system-guided steps for them to complete their tax elections and direct deposit information, feeding that data directly and securely into the payroll system.


This business process-driven approach eliminates manual email chains and handoffs, ensures data accuracy by using information directly from the hire event, and creates a complete, auditable trail of all onboarding activities.

TA36.comments: Yes, this is a field for preferred name that can be populated and/or corrected at any time. The system will capture the legal name, but change anything in Workday to the preferred name.

TA37.comments:
Workday manages document uploads through the Onboarding business process, which assigns tasks to new hires prompting them to upload required files (e.g., I-9 documentation, signed offer letters).

When a document is uploaded, it is assigned a specific Document Category. Access is then controlled by Workday's Role-Based Security model. This means permissions are not granted to individuals, but to roles (like Hiring Manager, HR Partner, or the employee themselves).

For example, an HR Partner's role can be granted permission to view the "I-9 Documentation" category, while the Hiring Manager's role is not. This ensures managers can see that the onboarding task is complete but cannot access the sensitive underlying document, enforcing need-to-know access automatically.

Workday's Document Framework delivers a library of configurable and securable document types, allowing workers to easily upload documents to their worker profile or as requested as information as part of a business process (e.g. upload a copy of passport when updating passport details). Documents can be categorized and secured by type to ensure sensitive data is protected at all times.

TA38.comments:
Yes, this functionality is supported. 

Workday can drive provisioning (and de-provisioning) of Workday accounts (and using integration steps in business processes - non-Workday accounts or Active Directory) directly from business processes such as Hire, Transfer. Promotion, Secondment, Exit etc.

TA39.comments:
Workday's single platform has redefined how offers and contracts are generated ensuring that core HCM, Recruiting and candidate data merge seamlessly to create desired correspondence. Generated offers include compensation guidelines which will flow from approved Positions, Offer, to Hire.

Upon offer acceptance, the Hire business process automatically converts the candidate record into an employee record.

Data from the offer—such as position, job title, location, and compensation details (salary, bonus)—directly populates the new employee's Core HR profile.

Workday Payroll then reads this Core HR data in real-time to calculate pay. The process also assigns a Pay Group based on eligibility rules and triggers Onboarding tasks for the new hire to submit their tax and bank details. This unified process ensures data accuracy and eliminates manual entry.

TA40.comments:
Onboarding can be used for contingent workers as well as employees and can be extended to your new starters before their start date (pre-employees and pre-contingent workers). In addition, you can incorporate onboarding steps for different types of internal movements (cross-boarding) such as a location change, promotion, secondment, or international assignment.

Workday’s Business Process Framework (BPF) makes it easy to support different business processes for different parts of the organization and for those processes to change over time as your business evolves. Pre-configured workflows are delivered for many processes, including both Hires and Termination. Customers may configure these processes to alter the flow, which may include notifications, approvals and other actions such as integration with other systems throughout the processes. 

Optional steps within the Hire business process include Propose Compensation, Budget Check, Background Check, Review of Hire by an HR and/or Benefits Partner, Assign Pay Group, Edit Service Dates, Create/Enable Workday Account for Onboarding/Self-service, notifications or “To-Do’s” for managers, Automated Web-Services and Integrations to third party systems, send tasks to IT for asset provisioning, or send notifications or tasks to the employee to be completed. Each step can include conditional logic and validation rules to fire off the appropriate step in the workflow based on the type of hire and criteria you define in your Hire Business Process.

TA41.comments:
Workday’s Business Process Framework enables you to manage all steps required, delivering a tailored message to a specific audience - employee, contingent worker, international assignment - through a specific onboarding experience. Workday security is inherited, by role, such as employee, manager, administrator, executive, to deliver the proper welcome. Workers are assigned roles during onboarding, and those roles deliver the proper steps, and are completed in the desired order, by the desired party, within the appropriate time periods, and based on any conditional logic required including language. Workday also provides an onboarding status report that provides real time visibility into the progress of your onboarding employees. 

The process flow outlines the "type" of worker (employee versus contingent), the "conditions" (global requirements), and various stakeholders for review/approval based on defined rules. Inclusive to this process is the due dates, required versus optional steps, and integrations to sources needed to complete provisioning activities. This rich business process is comprehensive to completion across all Workday products.

Workday supports assignment, notification, and tracking of required training. Expiry as well as due dates are supported. Advanced expiry logic also ensures you can have a different expiry date for different audiences. Required Learning conveniently displays for the end user on the Workday Home page as well as the Learning home page, ordering based on Due Date, and alert notifications can be auto-delivered to employees, managers, and others when required training has not yet been completed. 

Workday's onboarding capabilities include forms management, where customers can configure various forms such as employment policies and agreements. Workday will track that the worker reviewed the forms and can also require an acknowledgement or e-signature. You can issue and renew certifications to learners based on their successful completion of associated learning content. Certifications improve learner engagement by encouraging skill development and help to ensure compliance in your organization. Workday provides full reporting capabilities around active, inactive, and expiring certifications. Through the Onboarding business process checklist can be added and distributed to appropriate security groups, providing completion status visibility to HR and hiring managers.

TA42.comments:
The onboarding dashboard can present personalized welcome messages, videos, links to information, a directory of helpful contacts and people to meet, as well as the list of onboarding activities they need to complete.

Workday embeds media like welcome videos directly into the Onboarding business process in two primary ways:

    ● As a "To Do" Task: The simplest method is to add a "To Do" to the new hire's onboarding checklist. This task contains a direct hyperlink to your video, which can be hosted on your intranet or a video-sharing site.
    ● Through Workday Learning: For a more robust and trackable approach, you can use Workday Learning. The video is uploaded as a digital course, and the onboarding process is configured to automatically enroll new hires. This allows you to report on who has completed the training.

TA43.comments:
Workday Benefits is a unified, automated system for managing your entire benefits portfolio. Its core is a powerful rules engine that uses live Core HR data (like job profile, location, and employee type) to determine eligibility for specific plans.

How it Works:
The system is event-driven. A Benefit Event, such as a new hire, a qualifying life event (e.g., marriage), or the annual Open Enrollment period, triggers a business process. This process guides the employee through a self-service enrollment experience in the Benefits and Pay Hub. Once elections are complete, Workday automatically calculates costs, sends deductions and credits to Workday Payroll, and transmits enrollment data to providers, often using pre-built Cloud Connect for Benefits (CCB) integrations.

Programs Supported:
Workday is highly configurable and supports a wide array of programs, including:

    ● Health Care: Medical, Dental, and Vision plans.
    ● Insurance: Life, Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D), and Disability.
    ● Retirement Savings: Defined contribution plans like 401(k) and 403(b).
    ● Spending Accounts: Health Savings Accounts (HSA) and Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
    ● Additional Benefits: A flexible category for wellness programs, commuter benefits, tuition assistance, and other unique offerings.

Workday Cloud Connect for Benefits helps extend Workday core business applications through prebuilt connections to third-party solutions and services unlike legacy vendors which often build integrations as needed. Each integration is deployed in the Workday cloud, giving you all the benefits of a simplified and accelerated integration process as well as market best practices and years of Workday experience. All provided content is managed, maintained, and supported by Workday. The ever-expanding Cloud Connect Packages and Connectors catalog includes Benefits, HCM, Payroll, Financials, and Student.

With the upcoming releases, some significant improvements are coming through Workday Wellness including new APIs to partners, data insights, guidance, AI and more. We can provide some recording about Workday Wellness.

TA44.comments:
The system's ability to activate HR-required permissions during onboarding is a native and automated function of Workday's Role-Based Security model, which is integrated directly with the Business Process Framework (BPF).

Here is the step-by-step explanation:

1. Permissions are Not Assigned to Individuals, but to Roles:
In Workday, permissions (e.g., "view compensation," "approve time off," "edit personal information") are not assigned to a person by name. Instead, they are bundled into Security Groups. The most common type is a Role-Based Security Group. For example, all permissions a manager needs are assigned to the "Manager" security group. All permissions an employee needs for self-service are in the "Employee as Self" security group.

2. The "Hire" Business Process Assigns Roles:
The activation of permissions is triggered by the completion of the Hire business process, which is the final stage of onboarding. When the Hire process is successfully executed:

    ● The individual is officially converted from a pre-hire to an active employee.
    ● The system places them into a specific Position within a Supervisory Organization.
    ● This act of placing them in the organizational structure automatically assigns them the roles associated with that position (e.g., "Manager," "HR Partner," "Accountant").

3. Automatic and Instantaneous Permission Inheritance:
The moment the Hire process completes and the employee is assigned their roles, they automatically inherit all the permissions associated with the security groups linked to those roles.

Example:
When a new manager is hired, the Hire business process completes on their start date. At that exact moment:

    ● They are assigned the "Manager" role for their team.
    ● They instantly and automatically gain access to the "Manager" security group's permissions, allowing them to see their direct reports' data, initiate compensation changes, and approve requests.

This automated, event-driven approach ensures that permissions are granted precisely when they are needed and are always appropriate for the employee's current role. It eliminates the need for manual intervention by IT or HR to provision access, significantly reducing security risks and administrative overhead.

TA45.comments:
This capability is a core function of the configurable Business Process Framework (BPF), which underpins all major workflows in the system.

Here’s how it works:

Every major transaction, such as a Create Job Requisition or Hire, is a configurable business process composed of sequential steps (e.g., approvals, reviews). Workday allows you to configure automated Notifications on any step of that process.

1. Configuration and Automation:
The notifications are configured directly within the business process definition. For each step, you can define when a notification should trigger:

    ● On Entry: When the task arrives in someone's inbox.
    ● On Exit: When the step is successfully completed (e.g., an approval is given).
    ● If Overdue: If the task is not completed within a specified time.

2. Targeting Relevant Stakeholders:
The system uses a role-based security model to direct notifications to the right people. You don't send a notification to a specific person's name; you send it to a Role associated with the transaction. Examples include:

    ● Hiring Manager: The manager of the position being hired.
    ● HR Partner: The HR representative for the supervisory organization.
    ● Recruiter: The recruiter assigned to the requisition.
    ● Role-Based Groups: You can create specific groups like "IT Provisioning" or "Facilities Team" and assign them roles within the business process.

3. Dynamic and Actionable Content:
The content of each notification is fully configurable. You can write custom text and, more importantly, embed dynamic data from the business process itself. For example, a notification to IT upon a successful hire could automatically include the new hire's Name, Start Date, Location, and Job Title, providing all the necessary information to provision equipment without manual intervention.

Example Workflow:
When a Job Requisition receives its final approval:

    1. A notification is automatically sent to the Hiring Manager and Recruiter stating, "Requisition [Req ID] for [Position Title] has been approved and is now open for sourcing."
    2. When a candidate is hired and the Hire business process completes:
        ● IT gets a notification: "Provision laptop and accounts for [New Hire Name], starting [Start Date] at [Location]."
        ● Facilities gets a notification: "Prepare workspace for [New Hire Name] at [Location]."


This framework ensures that all stakeholders are informed in real-time with the exact information they need, triggered automatically by the system as the process moves forward.

TA46.comments:
Workday Cloud Connect for Learning (CCLRN), allows you to manage content, tracking, and vendor integrations in one place. Your learners can easily access different content catalogs without ever leaving Workday. Workday CCLRN brings together multiple content types, such as audio, video, and text, from a variety of third-party sources including LinkedIn Learning, Coursera, Harvard ManageMentor, OpenSesame, Pluralsight, Skillsoft Percipio and Udemy Business. This allows your learners to easily access a comprehensive catalog of curated content that updates automatically based on productized integrations to these publishers, and allows you to surface third-party content alongside your own to enrich the learning catalog and end user experience.

Because Workday Learning is the native Learning Management System (LMS) within the Workday platform, the process of retrieving, validating, and syncing completion records is seamless and automated, eliminating the need for complex integrations.

Once the audience is defined, you launch a Learning Campaign. The campaign assigns the specific bias training course to every member of the dynamic audience you created. You can set due dates, configure automatic reminders for overdue training, and track the overall progress of the campaign. 

Records are automatically updated. When an employee completes the course within Workday Learning, the system doesn't need to "retrieve" the record. The completion is inherently valid because the employee, the course, and the enrollment all exist within the same system. The moment the course is finished, Workday automatically creates a permanent Learning History record on that employee's worker profile instantaneously.

This provides managers, HR partners, and compliance officers with real-time visibility into training completion. They can run reports directly in Workday to see who has or has not completed required training by department, role, or any other dimension, ensuring accountability and simplifying compliance tracking.

TA47.comments:
With HCM & Finance, Workday leverages position management functionality and the Workday Business Process Framework as part of the Workday foundation, to provide specific data and associated business process tasks. As Workday is a single system, Workday can provide visibility across all positions, position status, headcount budget and FTE and provides a delivered headcount and FTE reporting to provide real-time visibility into position funding and status:

Workday’s HCM, Position Control, and Financials modules are not separate systems but are built on a single, unified data model. This eliminates the need for traditional batch integrations and enables real-time data sharing.

    1. Linking Positions to Budgets with Worktags:
When a Position is created or edited within the Position Control module, it is tagged with financial dimensions known as Worktags. The most critical Worktag in this process is the Cost Center, which directly links the position to a specific budget managed within Workday Financials. 
    2. Real-Time Budget Check on Staffing Events:
When a manager or HR professional initiates a staffing business process—such as Create Job Requisition for a vacant position or Hire an employee into a position—Workday automatically performs a real-time budget check. The system instantly queries the associated Cost Center's budget in the Financials module to verify that sufficient funds are available.
    3. Commitment Accounting (Encumbrances):
Upon successful budget validation, the system automatically creates a payroll encumbrance. This is a financial entry that reserves or "commits" the projected salary and benefit costs for the position for the remainder of the fiscal period. 
    4. Dynamic Status Synchronization:
The financial status is always in sync with the position's HR status:
        ● When the position is filled, the encumbrance is systematically liquidated, and actual payroll expenses begin to post against the budget with each pay run.
        ● If a position is frozen or closed, the associated encumbrance is automatically released, and the committed funds are returned to the available budget balance in real-time.


This ensures that if a position is unfunded, the hiring process can be automatically stopped before an offer is even made. It provides managers and finance leaders with complete, up-to-the-minute visibility through reports that combine headcount data, position status, and budget-versus-actuals, preventing budget overruns and enabling proactive financial management of the workforce.

TA48.comments:
Since Workday Payroll resides in the same data store as Workday HCM, enabling HR and payroll to collaborate and manage end-to-end-processes that cross both domains. The automatic population of Core HR and Payroll records begins the moment a candidate electronically accepts a job offer within Workday Recruiting. This acceptance is the trigger that initiates the Hire business process, which orchestrates the seamless transfer of data.

Here is a breakdown of the automated data flow:

    1. Candidate to Employee Conversion: The Hire business process is fundamentally designed to convert the existing candidate record into a new, active employee record. This prevents data loss and eliminates the need for duplicate data entry.
    2. Core HR Population:
        ● Personal Data: Information the candidate provided during their application is automatically transferred to the new employee's personal data record.
        ● Job & Position Data: Details from the approved job requisition and the offer letter are used to place the new hire within the organizational structure. This includes their assigned Supervisory Organization, Position (if using position management), Job Profile, business site (location), and hire date.
        ● Compensation Data: The specific compensation details from the accepted offer—such as salary, hourly rate, grade, and any specified allowance or bonus plans—are automatically populated in the employee's comp profile within Core HR.

    3. Payroll Module Setup:
        ● Compensation for Payroll: The compensation data established in Core HR serves as the direct, foundational input for the Payroll module to begin calculating gross pay.
        ● Pay Group Assignment: Workday automatically assigns the new employee to the correct Pay Group (e.g., US Bi-weekly Salaried, UK Monthly) based on pre-configured eligibility rules. 
        ● Onboarding for Payroll Data: The Hire process also kicks off the Onboarding business process. This is a critical step that generates tasks for the new employee, prompting them to complete essential payroll information directly in Workday. This typically includes filling out federal and state tax election forms (like a W-4) and entering their bank account details for direct deposit.


This automated, cross-application process is a core strength of Workday's unified system. It ensures data integrity, reduces the risk of manual errors, and accelerates the time it takes for a new hire to be fully and accurately established in the HR and payroll systems.

TA49.comments:
Workday has partnerships with several background check vendors that have pre-built integrations with Workday Recruiting. Additionally, Workday provides a generic Background check connector that can be configured to work with other Background check providers. 

There are several certified partners for background checks with pre-built APIs to Workday that can be seen here: https://marketplace.workday.com/en-US/listing?locale=en-US&refCamp=701VT00000UCjJZ#q=background%20check (https://marketplace.workday.com/en-US/listing?locale=en-US&refCamp=701VT00000UCjJZ#q=background%20check) 

Dynamic business process parameters can automatically advance or disposition a candidate based on condition rules. The bi-directional integration ensures the background check information is associated to the candidates profile.

TA50.comments:
Workday Cloud Connect for Benefits facilitates reporting and payments to third-party benefit providers. Cloud Connect for Benefits provides a catalog of packaged integrations that connect to benefits providers, giving HR organizations the ability to evaluate, select and offer the most appropriate plans for their workforce. Workday currently manages over 300 vendor integrations of approximately 20 different products and can add additional vendors as needed.

Workday Benefits is a comprehensive solution designed to help you manage your organization's complete range of benefit programs. It allows for the configuration and administration of everything from health and insurance plans to retirement and wellness programs, all within a single, unified system.

The core of Workday Benefits is its powerful and flexible rules-based engine, which automates eligibility, enrollment, and event processing. This reduces manual effort and helps ensure compliance.


Key Capabilities
    ● Configurable Benefit Plans: You can create and manage a wide variety of benefit plans. This includes the ability to define coverage targets, set up providers, and establish eligibility rules.
    ● Enrollment Management: Workday supports the entire enrollment lifecycle. This includes:
        ● Enrollment for new hires.
        ● Processing employee-initiated life events (e.g., marriage, birth of a child).
        ● Automated events triggered by changes in employee data (e.g., a change in job profile or location).
        ● A comprehensive Open Enrollment process that can be configured to meet your organization's specific needs.

    ● Dependents and Beneficiaries: The system allows for the creation and management of dependents and beneficiaries, including the ability to track verification status and designate legal trusts.
    ● Integration: Workday Benefits is designed to work seamlessly with other Workday modules, most notably Workday Payroll, to ensure that deductions and contributions are processed accurately. It also offers pre-built integrations to third-party benefit providers through Cloud Connect for Benefits (CCB).
    ● Employee Experience: The Benefits and Pay Hub provides employees with a single, intuitive location to view their current benefits, make elections, and access related pay information. Enrollment is also fully supported on the Workday Mobile app.
    ● Compliance and Reporting: The system includes functionality to support regulatory requirements, such as the Affordable Care Act (ACA) in the United States, and provides robust reporting capabilities.

PR76.comments:
Yes, this functionality is supported.

Workday allows you to create Time entry validations which are warnings or errors that prevent workers from entering or submitting invalid time entries. During time entry, Workday executes validations after time calculations when a worker clicks OK to enter time. Validations help workers catch time entry errors before you run payroll.

Yes. Workday Time Tracking provides configurable time tags and allows for mass upload of time tag values (as this information is generally held in Third Party systems). This can allow Employees to enter time against time tags for tasks and/or projects. Alternatively, Workday Projects can be used to provide project time, costing and billing functionality, with all projects and tasks available natively for time entry in Workday Time Tracking.

PR78.comments:
Yes, The system provides this capability through the use of Time Code Groups and Time Tracking Eligibility Rules. This allows you to create a curated list of available pay codes (called Time Entry Codes in Workday) that appears on an employee's timesheet, ensuring they can only select and enter time against codes for which they are eligible.

Time entry codes describe the types of time workers can enter. Time code groups control who can use a set of time entry codes. Workers can use the time entry codes in their time code group as well as the default time entry code assigned to their time entry template. Based on different dimensions (e.g. roles, location, company division) different time entry types can be made available.

Workday Scheduling and Workday Time Tracking deployed together provide seamless capability between scheduled vs actual time worked. Workday Time Tracking can auto-generate timesheets based on scheduled time from Workday Scheduling and can allow for the break-down of hours to be paid by Workday Payroll or Third Party Payrolls.

Workers can use the time entry codes in their time code group as well as the default time entry code assigned to their time entry template. PR82.comments: Workday is able to reflect all regional bank holiday calendars which then is used for both, Absence management and Time Tracking. Time calculations use these holiday calendars to calculate the correct overtime for an employee population when an employee works on a bank/public holiday.

PR83.comments: Workday provides a robust and highly configurable framework for sending reminders and alerts related to timecards. This functionality is managed primarily through the Workday Business Process Framework, which allows you to build notifications directly into the time entry and approval workflows.

PR84.comments: Workday delivers a business process framework that is fully configurable by your organization to meet your specific requirements, and goes beyond simple approvals. The time entry business process supports different workflow routing and approvals for different organizations or employee groups, or to matrix or cost center managers, based on specific criteria.

PR85.comments: Yes, Workday has robust capabilities to enforce time submission deadlines and manage exceptions, ensuring that payroll is processed accurately and on time. This is primarily handled through a combination of Period Schedules, Time Entry Validations, and configurable Business Processes.

PR86.comments: Workday provides "always on auditing" throughout the entire system. All change-effecting actions are tracked within Workday by default, and the audit trails for any object within Workday can be accessed for further review by individuals with the correct security permissions. Workday does not provide a way to “turn-off” audit trails within the system, and Workday customers do not have to take any additional steps to enable and/or maintain the audit trails. They are maintained and provided automatically as part of your Workday subscription.

PR87.comments: Yes. Workday Time Tracking provides exception reporting for attendance (Employees not clocked-on on a scheduled day) and configurable time validation messages that can be provided for exception reporting. Workday provides a single reporting framework to allow delivered reports to be easily copied and modified to meet particular needs and the ability to easily configure new reports from the vast range of Workday delivered reporting types. Reports can be scheduled, included within dashboards and included as push notifications. In addition, the Daily Time Exceptions feature enables managers to view and act on daily time exceptions on their mobile device.

Workday Time Tracking provides a single system for HCM, compensation, absence, time and Workday Scheduling which provides visibility and reporting across all Workday data for auditing purposes and allows for scheduled vs actual time reporting. Workday Time Tracking provides visibility for workers that are late to clock-in for their scheduled shift.

Workday can handle timesheets for capturing and processing individual employee work hours as well as for capturing time for multiple employees in a supervisory organization, including the tracking of project time and hours billed to different cost centers. Workday Timesheets can be routed through the business process framework (workflow) for approvals. An employee can enter their time on the timesheet and submit the timesheet in the Workday system (using a browser or mobile app). The timesheet will then be directed for approval or through the chain of approval that you determine using the business process framework. The person approving the timesheet has the option to edit, deny, send back the timesheet to the employee for edits, and approve the timesheet one by one or in bulk. Unlimited approval levels supported.

Yes, Workday Time Tracking is designed with a powerful calculation engine and rules framework to provide flexibility, configurability and control. Organizations can configure Time Capture Templates, Time Calculations, Leave Accruals, Validations, Approval Processes and Exception Reporting.
